sql: SELECT * FROM track WHERE Location = 'Fontana, CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the full details for the race track located in Fontana, CA.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM race WHERE Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and date for all races belonging to the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT Name, Year_Opened FROM track ORDER BY Year_Opened DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and opening year of each track, ordered from the most recently opened to the oldest.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and seating capacity for each racetrack.

sql: SELECT Name FROM race;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each race recorded in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each race name and its corresponding track name by joining races to their respective tracks.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each track's name along with the total number of races that have occurred at that track.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Seating) FROM track GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each track location along with the total combined seating capacity for all tracks in that location.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of racetracks that opened after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Chicagoland Speedway';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all races that have taken place at the 'Chicagoland Speedway' track.

sql: SELECT AVG(Seating) FROM track;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average seating capacity across all tracks.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of tracks that have hosted at least one race.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the track that has hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating > 80000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of races that are held at high-capacity tracks with more than 80,000 seats.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Track_ID FROM race AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of race tracks that have never hosted a race.

sql: SELECT Name, Seating FROM track WHERE Seating = (SELECT MAX(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and seating capacity of the racetrack with the most seats.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Year_Opened < 2000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of races held at tracks that were opened before the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race WHERE Class = 'DP/GT');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of tracks that have hosted races for the 'DP/GT' class.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Seating) FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race);
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average seating capacity for all tracks that have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating = (SELECT MIN(T3.Seating) FROM track AS T3 JOIN race AS T4 ON T3.Track_ID = T4.Track_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of races held at the track with the smallest seating capacity among all tracks that host races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating > (SELECT AVG(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location for races held at tracks with a seating capacity that is greater than the average across all tracks.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race) UNION SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 1960;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique names of all tracks that have either hosted a race or were opened before 1960.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race GROUP BY Track_ID HAVING count(*) = 1) EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Location = 'Joliet, IL';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of tracks that have hosted exactly one race, excluding any tracks located in 'Joliet, IL'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T2 WHERE T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID AND T2.Class = 'DP/GT');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all tracks that have hosted a 'DP/GT' class race.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened > 1990 AND Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM race) INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Seating > 70000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of large-capacity tracks (over 70,000 seats) that also opened after 1990 and have hosted a race.

sql: SELECT ID, name, salary FROM instructor WHERE dept_name = 'Cybernetics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, and salary for each instructor in the 'Cybernetics' department.

sql: SELECT title, dept_name, credits FROM course ORDER BY credits DESC;
result_summary: Returns 200 rows listing each course's title, its department name, and its credit value, ordered from the highest number of credits to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT building FROM department;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows providing a unique list of all buildings that house a department.

sql: SELECT building, room_number, capacity FROM classroom WHERE capacity > 10;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing classrooms with a capacity greater than 10, showing the building, room number, and capacity for each.

sql: SELECT dept_name, budget FROM department ORDER BY budget DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the three departments with the largest budgets, ordered from highest to lowest budget.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, COUNT(T2.course_id) AS number_of_courses FROM department AS T1 JOIN course AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name GROUP BY T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each department name along with the total count of courses offered by that department.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.i_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.name = 'McKinnon';
result_summary: Returns 31 rows listing the names of all students who are advised by the instructor named 'McKinnon'.

sql: SELECT dept_name, AVG(salary) FROM instructor GROUP BY dept_name HAVING AVG(salary) > 60000;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing departments where the average instructor salary is greater than $60,000, showing each department's name and its specific average salary.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T1.dept_name FROM course AS T1 WHERE T1.course_id IN (SELECT T2.course_id FROM prereq AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 79 rows listing the title and department name for every course that serves as a prerequisite for another course.

sql: SELECT course_id, COUNT(ID) AS num_students FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2003 GROUP BY course_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each course ID from the Spring 2003 semester along with the total number of students enrolled in it.

sql: SELECT building, SUM(capacity) AS total_capacity FROM classroom GROUP BY building;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each building along with the total combined classroom capacity for that building.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name, T2.dept_name FROM teaches AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.year = 2004;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique instructors who taught a course in the year 2004, showing their name and department.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.year FROM teaches AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID GROUP BY T2.ID, T1.year HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.semester) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing instructor names and the specific years in which they taught courses across more than one semester.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN course AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.dept_name = 'Biology';
result_summary: Returns 713 rows listing the unique names of students who have taken at least one course in the Biology department.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM course AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT T2.course_id FROM section AS T2 WHERE T2.course_id = T1.course_id AND T2.semester = 'Fall') AND EXISTS (SELECT T3.course_id FROM section AS T3 WHERE T3.course_id = T1.course_id AND T3.semester = 'Spring');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the titles of courses that are offered in both the Fall and Spring semesters.

sql: SELECT name, dept_name, salary FROM instructor AS T1 WHERE salary > (SELECT avg(T2.salary) FROM instructor AS T2 WHERE T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name);
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing instructors who earn more than the average salary within their respective departments, showing each instructor's name, department name, and salary.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 WHERE T1.dept_name = 'History' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN course AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.dept_name = 'History';
result_summary: Returns 72 rows listing the names of all students majoring in History who have also taken at least one course offered by the History department.

sql: SELECT name FROM instructor WHERE dept_name = 'Cybernetics' EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM teaches AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the instructor from the 'Cybernetics' department who is not currently teaching any courses.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, T1.total_salary FROM (SELECT dept_name, SUM(salary) AS total_salary FROM instructor GROUP BY dept_name) AS T1 ORDER BY T1.total_salary DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the highest total instructor salary expenditure, along with that calculated total amount.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, T1.budget, SUM(T2.salary) AS total_salaries FROM department AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name GROUP BY T1.dept_name, T1.budget HAVING SUM(T2.salary) > T1.budget;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying departments where the total of instructor salaries exceeds the department's budget, listing the department name, its budget, and the calculated total salary expenditure.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year JOIN instructor AS T4 ON T3.ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.name = 'McKinnon';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: WITH CourseCounts AS (SELECT course_id, COUNT(ID) AS num_students FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2003 GROUP BY course_id) SELECT T2.title, T1.num_students FROM CourseCounts AS T1 JOIN course AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id ORDER BY T1.num_students DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing course titles and their student enrollment counts for the Fall 2003 semester, ordered from the most to least popular course.

sql: SELECT name FROM student WHERE dept_name = 'Statistics' UNION SELECT name FROM instructor WHERE dept_name = 'Statistics';
result_summary: Returns 91 rows listing the combined names of all students and instructors who belong to the 'Statistics' department.

sql: SELECT T1.course_id, COUNT(T1.ID) FROM takes AS T1 WHERE T1.semester = 'Spring' AND T1.year = 2003 GROUP BY T1.course_id HAVING COUNT(T1.ID) > (SELECT avg(T2.cnt) FROM (SELECT count(ID) AS cnt FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2003 GROUP BY course_id) AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing courses from the Spring 2003 semester with an enrollment count higher than the average for that term, showing each course ID and its total number of students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID JOIN section AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id AND T3.sec_id = T4.sec_id AND T3.semester = T4.semester AND T3.year = T4.year WHERE T2.building <> T4.building;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows listing the unique names of instructors who teach at least one course in a building different from their own department's building.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for all pilots with United States nationality.

sql: SELECT Model, Order_Year, Manufacturer FROM aircraft ORDER BY Order_Year ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each aircraft's model, order year, and manufacturer, sorted chronologically from the earliest order year.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Rank, Age FROM pilot ORDER BY Age ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, rank, and age of the single youngest pilot in the dataset.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM aircraft;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of aircraft.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Manufacturer FROM aircraft;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each unique manufacturer of aircraft in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each pilot's name along with the total count of their associated pilot records.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the distinct aircraft manufacturers and models that have an associated pilot flying record.

sql: SELECT Order_Year, COUNT(*) FROM aircraft GROUP BY Order_Year;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each order year alongside the total number of aircraft ordered in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Join_Year, T2.Date FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T1.Join_Year >= 2000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name, join year, and record date for pilots who joined in or after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(Pilot_ID) FROM pilot GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each nationality along with the total count of pilots from that country.

sql: SELECT Position, AVG(Age) FROM pilot GROUP BY Position;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each pilot position along with the average age of pilots holding that position.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Date < '2005-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct aircraft manufacturer and model that were flown before January 1st, 2005.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all pilots who have flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS RecordCount FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name ORDER BY RecordCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single pilot with the most records, showing their name and the total count of their associated records.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Age < 40;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique aircraft manufacturers and models that have been flown by pilots under the age of 40.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Pilot_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Pilot_ID FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Order_Year = 1992);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of pilots who have not flown any aircraft that were ordered in the year 1992.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Model) AS DistinctModelsFlown FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each pilot's name alongside a count of the distinct aircraft models they have flown.

sql: SELECT T3.Manufacturer, AVG(T1.Rank) AS AverageRank FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T3.Manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each aircraft manufacturer alongside the average rank of the pilots who have flown their aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Aircraft_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the pilot who has flown more than one distinct aircraft.

sql: SELECT T3.Manufacturer, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS RecordCount FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T3.Manufacturer ORDER BY RecordCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single aircraft manufacturer with the most pilot records, along with the total count of those records.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Join_Year > T3.Order_Year;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of pilots who have flown at least one aircraft that was ordered before the pilot's join year.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' AND T3.Fuel_Propulsion = 'Diesel';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of pilots from the United States who have experience flying diesel-powered aircraft.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Team FROM pilot WHERE Team = 'Bradley' UNION SELECT Pilot_name, Team FROM pilot WHERE Team = 'Fordham';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the pilots and their corresponding team names for those on the 'Bradley' or 'Fordham' teams.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Order_Year = (SELECT MIN(Order_Year) FROM aircraft);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of pilots who have flown the oldest aircraft, as determined by the earliest order year.

sql: SELECT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Aircraft_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Pilot_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the manufacturer and model for an aircraft that has been flown by more than one distinct pilot.

sql: SELECT Name, "Home Town" FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and home town for each person in the dataset.

sql: SELECT * FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing all available details for a perpetrator incident that occurred in South Korea, including IDs, date, location, and casualty figures.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight FROM people WHERE Height = 6.4;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and weight of a person who is 6.4 feet tall.

sql: SELECT Location, Killed FROM perpetrator ORDER BY Killed DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the location of perpetrator incidents and the number of people killed, ordered from the highest number of fatalities to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person with a People_ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed + T2.Injured AS Total_Victims FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each perpetrator's name and the total number of their victims, calculated by summing the killed and injured counts.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the total count of perpetrators from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1."Home Town" FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'China';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and hometowns of perpetrators associated with the country 'China'.

sql: SELECT Country, SUM(Killed) FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING SUM(Killed) >= 32;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing countries with a total of 32 or more fatalities, along with the total number of people killed for each of those countries.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator WHERE Year > 1990);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people identified as perpetrators in incidents that occurred after 1990.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Killed ASC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing perpetrator names and the number of people they killed, ordered in ascending order from the fewest killed to the most.

sql: SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average weight for all people from 'Los Angeles, CA'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed = (SELECT MAX(Killed) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the perpetrator with the highest number of killings.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the perpetrator who has killed more people than the average for all perpetrators.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Injured) FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Injured) > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying perpetrators who have injured more than 30 people, showing each perpetrator's name and the total number of individuals they injured.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all individuals who are not identified as perpetrators in any incident.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 6.3 UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > 210;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of individuals who are either taller than 6.3 feet or weigh more than 210 pounds.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 6.3 INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Weight > 200;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of people who are both taller than 6.3 feet and weigh more than 200 pounds.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 6.3 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of people taller than 6.3 feet, excluding those whose home town is Los Angeles, CA.

sql: SELECT T3.Country, T3.AvgKilled FROM (SELECT Country, AVG(Killed) AS AvgKilled, COUNT(*) AS NumActs FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country) AS T3 WHERE T3.NumActs >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country that has at least one recorded perpetrator act, along with the average number of people killed per act for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM perpetrator AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Injured > 10);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of perpetrators who have injured more than 10 people.

sql: SELECT T1."Home Town", MAX(T2.Killed) FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1."Home Town";
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each home town along with the maximum number of people killed by a single perpetrator from that town.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Killed FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(T3.Height) FROM people AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of perpetrators who are taller than the average person, along with the number of people they killed.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, T1.Location, T1.Killed FROM perpetrator AS T1 WHERE T1.Killed = (SELECT MAX(T2.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, each identifying the perpetrator incident with the maximum number of casualties for a given country, showing the country, location, and number killed.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > T2.Injured AND T2.Year > 1950;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of perpetrators and the location of incidents that occurred after 1950 and resulted in more fatalities than injuries.

sql: SELECT IATA, name, City FROM airport WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IATA code, name, and city for all airports located in the United States.

sql: SELECT name, Incorporated_in FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Joint Venture';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and country/region of incorporation for all companies classified as 'Joint Ventures'.

sql: SELECT Vehicle_Flight_number, Date, Altitude FROM flight WHERE Pilot = 'Thompson';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the vehicle flight number, date, and altitude for each flight piloted by 'Thompson'.

sql: SELECT name, Group_Equity_Shareholding FROM operate_company WHERE Group_Equity_Shareholding > 50;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows identifying companies with a majority group equity shareholding (over 50%), showing each company's name and its specific shareholding percentage.

sql: SELECT name, Principal_activities FROM operate_company ORDER BY name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing operating companies and their principal activities, sorted by company name in reverse alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each vehicle's flight number and the name of the corresponding operating company.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.Vehicle_Flight_number FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each flight's vehicle flight number along with the name of its associated airport.

sql: SELECT Pilot, COUNT(*) FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each pilot and the total number of flights they have flown.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.Altitude) FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each airport's name alongside the average altitude of all flights associated with that airport.

sql: SELECT Pilot, COUNT(DISTINCT airport_id) FROM flight GROUP BY Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each pilot along with a count of the distinct airports they have visited.

sql: SELECT T2.name, MAX(T1.Velocity) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each operating company alongside the maximum flight velocity recorded for any of its flights.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.Vehicle_Flight_number, T1.Date FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Incorporated_in = 'China';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, flight number, and date for all flights operated by companies that are incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT Pilot, Vehicle_Flight_number FROM flight WHERE Altitude = (SELECT MAX(Altitude) FROM flight);

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 1;

sql: SELECT Vehicle_Flight_number, Pilot, Velocity FROM flight WHERE Velocity > (SELECT AVG(Velocity) FROM flight);

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'Netherlands';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Pilot FROM flight WHERE company_id IN (SELECT id FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Joint Venture');

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Principal_activities, T3.City FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T2.Altitude > 0;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and principal activities of airline companies, along with the airport city for each of their active flights (where altitude is greater than zero).

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name, T3.Country FROM airport AS T3 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T3.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T1 ON T2.company_id = T1.id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'Hong Kong';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 LEFT JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T1.Type = 'Corporate' AND T2.id IS NULL;

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM operate_company AS T1 LEFT JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each operating company's name along with the total count of flights they operate.

sql: SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Iceland' UNION SELECT T1.Vehicle_Flight_number FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Incorporated_in = 'China';

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.avg_vel FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN (SELECT company_id, AVG(Velocity) AS avg_vel FROM flight GROUP BY company_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id ORDER BY T2.avg_vel DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the airline company with the highest average flight velocity, listing the company's name and that calculated average.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Netherlands' GROUP BY T1.Pilot HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.airport_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM airport WHERE Country = 'Netherlands');

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Altitude = 3650 EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T1.Altitude <> 3650;

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Place FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan';

sql: SELECT * FROM body_builder WHERE Total > 315.0;

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people ORDER BY Weight DESC;

sql: SELECT Snatch, Clean_Jerk FROM body_builder WHERE People_ID = 3;

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Name = 'Ty Conklin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the person found by searching for 'Ty Conklin'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Total DESC;

sql: SELECT AVG(Snatch), AVG(Clean_Jerk), AVG(Total) FROM body_builder;

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Birth_Place) FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of unique birthplaces recorded for all people.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Snatch FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Snatch = 140.0;

sql: SELECT T2.Birth_Place, COUNT(T1.Body_Builder_ID) FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each birthplace along with the total number of bodybuilders from that location.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN body_builder AS T1 ON T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID WHERE T1.Total = 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of bodybuilders whose total score or lift amount is exactly 315.0.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Weight > 80.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and total score for each bodybuilder weighing over 80.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and total scores for bodybuilders who have a score greater than the average of all bodybuilders.

sql: SELECT T2.Birth_Place, MAX(T1.Total) FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Birth_Place;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each unique birthplace alongside the maximum total score achieved by a bodybuilder from that location.

sql: SELECT T2.Birth_Place FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Birth_Place HAVING COUNT(T1.Body_Builder_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the distinct birthplaces that are associated with at least one bodybuilder.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total / T2.Weight FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Total / T2.Weight DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each bodybuilder's name and their calculated strength-to-weight ratio, ordered from the highest ratio to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM body_builder AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID);

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Snatch FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan' ORDER BY T1.Snatch DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-ranked bodybuilder from 'Port Huron, Michigan' based on their Snatch lift, showing the athlete's name and lift amount.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Snatch > (SELECT AVG(Snatch) FROM body_builder) AND T1.Clean_Jerk > (SELECT AVG(Clean_Jerk) FROM body_builder);

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder WHERE Total > 315.0) UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Glenview, Illinois';

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Total DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and total score for the bodybuilder with the second-highest total.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total = (SELECT Total FROM body_builder WHERE People_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of body builders and their total lift amount, filtered to show only those who have the same total as the body builder with ID 2.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Weight) FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average weight of bodybuilders who can lift a total weight greater than 315.0.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY (T1.Clean_Jerk - T1.Snatch) ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the bodybuilder with the smallest difference between their Clean & Jerk and Snatch lift weights.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total < (SELECT MAX(Total) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and total scores for all body builders who did not achieve the maximum total score.

sql: SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE country = 'USA';

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE lname = 'Gibbons';

sql: SELECT title FROM Papers WHERE paperID = 200;

sql: SELECT lname FROM Authors ORDER BY lname ASC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the last names of all authors, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT authID, instID FROM Authorship WHERE paperID = 200;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the author ID and institution ID for all authors associated with the specific paper identified by paperID 200.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname, COUNT(T2.paperID) FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID;

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.authID) FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT T3.title FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T2.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.lname = 'Hinze';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1;

sql: SELECT T1.title, COUNT(T2.authID) FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each paper's title along with the total number of authors for that paper.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.fname, T3.lname FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T2.authID = T3.authID WHERE T1.name = 'University of Oxford';

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID WHERE T1.authID = 51;

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.instID) > 1;

sql: SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname, T4.fname, T4.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID AND T1.authID < T2.authID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T1.authID = T3.authID JOIN Authors AS T4 ON T2.authID = T4.authID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all unique pairs of authors who have co-authored at least one paper, showing the first and last names for each author in the pair.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID HAVING COUNT(T2.paperID) > (SELECT CAST(COUNT(paperID) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT authID) FROM Authorship);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of an author who has written a number of papers greater than the overall average number of papers per author.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM (SELECT paperID, COUNT(authID) AS Cnt FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID ORDER BY Cnt DESC LIMIT 1) AS T1 JOIN Papers AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID;

sql: SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T2.authID = T3.authID WHERE T1.country = 'UK' EXCEPT SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T2.authID = T3.authID WHERE T1.country = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of authors who are affiliated with institutions in the UK but not with any institutions in the USA.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.authOrder = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.authOrder = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of an author who has been credited as both a first author and a second author across different publications.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T4.title FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN (SELECT instID, MAX(authOrder) AS max_o FROM Authorship GROUP BY instID) AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID AND T2.authOrder = T3.max_o JOIN Papers AS T4 ON T2.paperID = T4.paperID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing the institution name and the corresponding paper title for cases where an author from that institution is listed last on the paper's author list.

sql: SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE instID IN (SELECT T2.instID FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.lname = 'Gibbons');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the institution associated with any author whose last name is 'Gibbons'.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors EXCEPT SELECT T4.fname, T4.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T1.authID = T3.authID JOIN Authors AS T4 ON T2.authID = T4.authID WHERE T3.lname = 'Hinze' AND T4.lname <> 'Hinze';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of all authors who have not co-authored a paper with any author named 'Hinze'.

sql: SELECT T3.country, AVG(T4.num_authors) FROM Inst AS T3 JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT instID, paperID FROM Authorship) AS T2 ON T3.instID = T2.instID JOIN (SELECT paperID, COUNT(*) AS num_authors FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID) AS T4 ON T2.paperID = T4.paperID GROUP BY T3.country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country alongside the average number of authors on papers associated with institutions from that country.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.authOrder = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.authOrder != 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of authors who have only ever been the primary author on their publications and never a co-author.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.authID) > (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.authID) FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID WHERE T1.name = 'University of Oxford');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T2.authID = T3.authID WHERE T1.country = 'USA' UNION SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.paperID = 200 AND T1.authOrder = 1;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of all authors from US-based institutions, combined with the name of the first author for the paper with ID 200.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_Deaths FROM storm ORDER BY Number_Deaths DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing storm names and the number of deaths they caused, ordered from the most deadly storm to the least.

sql: SELECT Name, Dates_active FROM storm WHERE Max_speed = 972;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and active dates for storms that reached a maximum speed of exactly 972.

sql: SELECT Storm_ID, Name, Damage_millions_USD FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD = 13.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID, name, and damage amount for all storms that caused exactly $13.0 million in damages.

sql: SELECT Region_name FROM region WHERE Region_code = 'DZ';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing the name of the region that corresponds to the region code 'DZ'.

sql: SELECT Storm_ID, Region_id FROM affected_region WHERE Number_city_affected >= 15.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the storm and the region it impacted for cases where 15 or more cities were affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each storm's name alongside the name of a region it affected, creating a complete list of storm-region pairs.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Region_id) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name and providing a count of the number of regions it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each storm by name and the total number of cities it affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Damage_millions_USD > 10.75;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of storms and the corresponding regions they affected, filtered to only include storms that caused over $10.75 million in damages.

sql: SELECT T2.Region_name, COUNT(T1.Storm_ID) AS storm_count FROM affected_region AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id GROUP BY T2.Region_name ORDER BY storm_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the region affected by the highest number of storms, showing the region's name and its total storm count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Damage_millions_USD FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that affected a specific region (Region ID 1) and the corresponding financial damage in millions of USD for each storm.

sql: SELECT T2.Region_name FROM affected_region AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T1.Storm_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the region affected by the storm with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Number_Deaths > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique regions that have been affected by a storm resulting in at least one fatality.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Max_speed FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID HAVING COUNT(Region_id) > 1) AND T1.Max_speed > (SELECT AVG(Max_speed) FROM storm);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and maximum speed of storms that impacted multiple regions and had a speed greater than the overall average of all storms.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of storms that affected Region 1 but did not affect Region 3.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, SUM(T1.Number_Deaths) AS total_deaths FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id GROUP BY T3.Region_name ORDER BY total_deaths DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the region with the highest total number of storm-related deaths, along with that death count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 WHERE T1.Storm_ID IN (SELECT T2.Storm_ID FROM affected_region AS T2 WHERE T2.Region_id = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T3.Storm_ID FROM affected_region AS T3 WHERE T3.Region_id = 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the storm that has affected both region 1 and region 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Number_city_affected) AS total_cities FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T1.Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT Damage_millions_USD FROM storm WHERE Name = 'Two') GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing storms that caused more financial damage than the storm named 'Two', along with the total number of cities each of those storms affected.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID NOT IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of regions that have only been affected by non-fatal storms (storms with zero recorded deaths).

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, AVG(T2.Number_city_affected) FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Max_speed = 972 GROUP BY T3.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average number of cities affected per region for storms that reached a maximum speed of exactly 972.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T1.Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT AVG(Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm) AND T3.Region_name = 'Algeria';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the storm that affected the Algeria region and caused a higher-than-average amount of damage in millions of USD.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Storm_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Storm_ID FROM affected_region);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that have no recorded affected regions.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, SUM(T1.Number_Deaths) AS TotalDeaths, SUM(T1.Damage_millions_USD) AS TotalDamage FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id GROUP BY T3.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each geographical region along with the total number of deaths and the total financial damage (in millions of USD) caused by storms in that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Damage_millions_USD FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID WHERE T2.Region_id = 1 AND T1.Damage_millions_USD = (SELECT MAX(T3.Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm AS T3 JOIN affected_region AS T4 ON T3.Storm_ID = T4.Storm_ID WHERE T4.Region_id = 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and damage amount for the single most destructive storm that affected a specific region (Region ID 1).

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, T1.Name, (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Region_id) FROM affected_region WHERE Storm_ID = T1.Storm_ID) - 1 AS Other_Regions_Affected FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each region affected by a storm, the storm's name, and a count of how many other regions were impacted by that same storm.

sql: SELECT Film_ID, Title FROM film WHERE Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM cinema WHERE Capacity > 1000;

sql: SELECT Title, Original_air_date FROM film ORDER BY Title ASC;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Date FROM schedule;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique date present in the schedule.

sql: SELECT Name, Openning_year FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year >= 2012;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and opening years of cinemas established in 2012 or later.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each cinema's name along with the total count of films on its schedule.

sql: SELECT Date, AVG(Price) FROM schedule GROUP BY Date;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the average scheduled price for each distinct date.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Capacity), COUNT(Cinema_ID) FROM cinema GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, one for each location, showing the total combined seating capacity and the total number of cinemas within that location.

sql: SELECT T3.Title FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Codling';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of all films scheduled to be shown at the cinema named 'Codling'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each film title and the corresponding count of its scheduled showings.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and location of the cinema that has a scheduled showing on May 21st.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Show_times_per_day FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 Jun';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a film and its number of daily show times for all films scheduled on June 21st.

sql: SELECT Location FROM cinema WHERE Capacity = (SELECT MAX(Capacity) FROM cinema);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the location of the cinema with the maximum seating capacity.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the cinema that showed more than one film on May 21st.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID NOT IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Date = '21 Jun');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of all films that are not scheduled to be shown on June 21st.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Show_times_per_day) FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of daily show times for all films directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 Jun';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films scheduled to be shown on '21 May' but not on '21 Jun'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Price) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and average ticket price for each cinema that is showing two or more different films.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T2.Date FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the film title and its scheduled date for all screenings with a ticket price higher than the overall average price.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' AND T2.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM schedule WHERE Date = '21 May');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of films scheduled on '21 May' that have the highest ticket price for that day.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Title != 'The Case of the Smart Dummy' AND T2.Date = (SELECT T4.Date FROM film AS T3 JOIN schedule AS T4 ON T3.Film_ID = T4.Film_ID WHERE T3.Title = 'The Case of the Smart Dummy');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of any film scheduled to play on the same date as 'The Case of the Smart Dummy'.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Title, T2.Date FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN schedule AS T4 ON T2.Date = T4.Date JOIN film AS T3 ON T4.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T1.Film_ID < T3.Film_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of different films scheduled to be shown on the same day, providing both film titles and their shared screening date.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Price = 12.99 UNION SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T3.Capacity > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the unique titles of films that are either priced at $12.99 or are being shown in a cinema with a capacity greater than 1000.

sql: SELECT T.Location FROM (SELECT T1.Location, SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS total_shows FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Location) AS T ORDER BY T.total_shows DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location with the highest total number of daily movie showings across all of its cinemas.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T3.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner' AND T1.Capacity > (SELECT AVG(Capacity) FROM cinema) GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a cinema with an above-average capacity that is showing a film directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and price of phones available on the 'Sprint' carrier.

sql: SELECT Name, Carrier FROM phone WHERE Memory_in_G = 128;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and carrier for all phones that have 128 GB of memory.

sql: SELECT * FROM market WHERE District = 'Alberta';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing all details, including Market ID, number of employees, number of shops, and ranking, for the market located in the 'Alberta' district.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone ORDER BY Price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each phone's name and price, ordered from the most to the least expensive.

sql: SELECT District, Num_of_employees FROM market WHERE Num_of_shops > 10;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the district and the number of employees for market areas that contain more than 10 shops.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Num_of_stock FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each phone model's name and its corresponding quantity in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.District, COUNT(T2.Phone_ID) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district along with the total count of phones marketed within that district.

sql: SELECT T1.District, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district along with the total number of phones in stock for that district.

sql: SELECT Carrier, AVG(Price) FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each phone carrier along with the average price of phones for that carrier.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.District FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing phone names and the corresponding market districts where each phone is sold.

sql: SELECT Ranking, AVG(Num_of_employees) FROM market GROUP BY Ranking;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each market ranking alongside the average number of employees for companies within that ranking.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of phones that are sold in markets with a ranking of 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM phone);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and price for all phones that are priced higher than the average phone price.

sql: SELECT T1.District, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing districts with a total phone stock greater than 1000, showing each district's name and its corresponding total stock count.

sql: SELECT T1.District FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING count(T2.Phone_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the districts that have more than one phone model available across their associated markets.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T1.District = 'Alberta' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T1.District = 'New Brunswick';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of phones available in the 'Alberta' district that are not also available in the 'New Brunswick' district.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Market_ID IN (SELECT Market_ID FROM market WHERE Num_of_employees > 1965);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of phones sold in markets that have more than 1965 employees.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T1.District = 'Alberta' INTERSECT SELECT T3.Name FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T1.District = 'British Columbia';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.District FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District ORDER BY SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single district with the highest total number of phones in stock across all of its markets.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Market_ID) FROM phone AS T1 LEFT JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each phone by name along with a count of the number of markets in which it is available.

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Phone_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Phone_ID FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID WHERE T1.Ranking > 20);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of phones sold exclusively in high-ranking markets (those with a ranking of 20 or less).

sql: SELECT Carrier FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier HAVING AVG(Price) > 400;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the phone carrier whose average phone price is greater than 400.

sql: SELECT T3.District, MAX(T1.Price) FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID GROUP BY T3.District;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each market district along with the highest price of a phone available in that district.

sql: SELECT District FROM market WHERE Market_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Market_ID FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Carrier = 'Sprint');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the districts for markets that do not offer any phones from the carrier 'Sprint'.

sql: SELECT T1.Memory_in_G, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.Memory_in_G;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each phone memory capacity (in GB) along with the total number of units in stock for that capacity.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the customer ID and name for every customer in the database.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE Customer_name = 'Ellsworth Paucek';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details (customer ID and name) for the customer named 'Ellsworth Paucek'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT policy_type_code FROM Available_Policies;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique codes for each available policy type.

sql: SELECT Service_name FROM Services WHERE Service_ID = 6;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the service that has a Service ID of 6.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID, Effective_Date FROM Claims ORDER BY Effective_Date ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each claim's ID and its effective date, ordered chronologically from the earliest to the most recent claim.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who have a 'Property Insurance' policy.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, count(T2.Policy_ID) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer by name and the total count of insurance policies they hold.

sql: SELECT T3.Service_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID WHERE T1.Customer_name = 'Ellsworth Paucek';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the service name associated with an incident filed by the customer 'Ellsworth Paucek'.

sql: SELECT T1.Service_name, count(T2.FNOL_ID) FROM Services AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID GROUP BY T1.Service_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each service name along with the total number of initial loss reports associated with that service.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T2.Policy_ID FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Date_Opened LIKE '2016%';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of customers and their associated policy IDs for all policies that were opened in the year 2016.

sql: SELECT Settlement_ID, Settlement_Amount FROM Settlements WHERE Settlement_Amount > (SELECT avg(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and amount for each settlement with a value greater than the overall average settlement amount.

sql: SELECT SUM(T2.Settlement_Amount) FROM Claims AS T1 JOIN Settlements AS T2 ON T1.Claim_ID = T2.Claim_ID WHERE T1.Effective_Date > '2014-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total settlement amount for all claims that became effective after January 1st, 2014.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name HAVING count(T2.Policy_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who hold more than one policy.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID WHERE T3.Service_name = 'Change a policy';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of customers who have a settled claim that originated from a 'Change a policy' service request.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, sum(T4.Settlement_Amount) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T4 ON T3.Claim_ID = T4.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each customer by name and the total settlement amount paid out across all of their insurance claims.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.policy_type_code FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Claims AS T2 ON T1.FNOL_ID = T2.FNOL_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T1.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique types of policies that have had an associated insurance claim filed.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, sum(T4.Settlement_Amount) AS TotalAmount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T4 ON T3.Claim_ID = T4.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name ORDER BY TotalAmount DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top customers with the highest total settlement amounts across all their claims, ordered from largest to smallest amount.

sql: SELECT Customer_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all customers who have never filed a claim (First Notification of Loss).

sql: SELECT T1.Service_name FROM Services AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID GROUP BY T1.Service_name ORDER BY count(T2.FNOL_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the service name associated with the most "First Notification of Loss" events.

sql: SELECT T1.policy_type_code, avg(T5.Settlement_Amount) FROM Available_Policies AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T3 ON T2.Customer_ID = T3.Customer_ID AND T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T3.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID GROUP BY T1.policy_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each policy type code alongside the average claim settlement amount for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_name HAVING count(T2.Policy_ID) >= 2 AND sum(CASE WHEN T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who hold two or more policies, where at least one of those policies is 'Property Insurance'.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name, T4.policy_type_code, T5.Service_name, T6.Settlement_Amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T3.FNOL_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T4 ON T2.Policy_ID = T4.Policy_ID JOIN Services AS T5 ON T2.Service_ID = T5.Service_ID JOIN Settlements AS T6 ON T3.Claim_ID = T6.Claim_ID WHERE T2.FNOL_ID = 532;
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the customer name, policy type, service involved, and the final settlement amount for the claim tied to a specific First Notification of Loss (FNOL ID 532).

sql: SELECT T3.policy_type_code, sum(T5.Settlement_Amount) FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T1 JOIN Claims AS T2 ON T1.FNOL_ID = T2.FNOL_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T1.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID JOIN Customers_Policies AS T4 ON T1.Policy_ID = T4.Policy_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T2.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID GROUP BY T3.policy_type_code HAVING sum(T5.Settlement_Amount) > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows that calculate the total settlement amount for each policy type, showing only those types where the total payout exceeds 1000.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Services AS T3 ON T2.Service_ID = T3.Service_ID JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T2.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID WHERE T3.Service_name = 'Change a policy' AND T5.Settlement_Amount > (SELECT avg(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance' UNION SELECT T1.Customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T2.Service_ID = 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of customers who either hold a 'Property Insurance' policy or have filed a claim associated with service ID 6.

sql: SELECT staff_name FROM Staff WHERE other_staff_details = 'Manager';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of staff members who are designated as managers.

sql: SELECT part_name, chargeable_amount FROM Parts WHERE chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and chargeable amount for all parts that are designated as chargeable.

sql: SELECT company_name, company_address FROM Third_Party_Companies WHERE company_type = 'Maintenance Contractor';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and address for all third-party companies that are classified as a 'Maintenance Contractor'.

sql: SELECT skill_code, skill_description FROM Skills ORDER BY skill_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each skill's unique code and full description, ordered alphabetically by the skill code.

sql: SELECT asset_details, asset_make, asset_model FROM Assets WHERE asset_acquired_date > '2018-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details, make, and model for any asset acquired after January 1, 2018.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.company_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last name of each maintenance engineer, along with the name of the third-party company they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.skill_id) FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id GROUP BY T1.engineer_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing the first and last name of each maintenance engineer along with the total count of their skills.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T2.company_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.supplier_company_id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the details for each asset alongside the name of the third-party company that supplied it.

sql: SELECT T3.part_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T3 ON T2.part_id = T3.part_id WHERE T1.asset_make = 'PT';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all parts used in assets manufactured by 'PT'.

sql: SELECT T2.staff_name, COUNT(T1.fault_log_entry_id) FROM Fault_Log AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.recorded_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T2.staff_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each staff member's name along with the total count of fault logs they have recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T3.company_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Contracts AS T2 ON T1.maintenance_contract_id = T2.maintenance_contract_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T3 ON T2.maintenance_contract_company_id = T3.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing asset details alongside the name of the third-party company responsible for that asset's maintenance contract.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, COUNT(T2.part_id) FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Asset_Parts AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id GROUP BY T1.asset_details;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each asset's details along with a total count of its component parts.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.company_name FROM Engineer_Visits AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.company_id WHERE T1.fault_status = 'Waiting';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and company of each maintenance engineer assigned to a fault that currently has a 'Waiting' status.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.company_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T4 ON T1.company_id = T4.company_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Electrical';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first name, last name, and company name of all maintenance engineers who possess the 'Electrical' skill.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.part_name FROM Fault_Log AS T1 JOIN Assets AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T4 ON T3.part_id = T4.part_id WHERE T1.fault_description = 'system error';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of parts that are components of assets which have reported a 'system error'.

sql: SELECT T1.company_name, COUNT(T2.engineer_id) FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T1.company_name HAVING COUNT(T2.engineer_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing third-party companies that employ more than one maintenance engineer, showing each company's name and its total number of engineers.

sql: SELECT T3.skill_description FROM Part_Faults AS T1 JOIN Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T2 ON T1.part_fault_id = T2.part_fault_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T1.fault_description = 'Top Parts Wrong';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions of skills required to fix the part fault known as 'Top Parts Wrong'.

sql: SELECT asset_id FROM Asset_Parts INTERSECT SELECT asset_id FROM Fault_Log;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of assets that are both associated with parts and have had a fault logged.

sql: SELECT T1.engineer_id FROM Engineer_Skills AS T1 JOIN Skills AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id WHERE T2.skill_description = 'TV, Video' EXCEPT SELECT T1.engineer_id FROM Engineer_Skills AS T1 JOIN Skills AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id WHERE T2.skill_description = 'Electrical';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs of engineers who are skilled in 'TV, Video' but not in 'Electrical'.

sql: SELECT T3.part_name, COUNT(*) AS fault_count FROM Fault_Log_Parts AS T1 JOIN Part_Faults AS T2 ON T1.part_fault_id = T2.part_fault_id JOIN Parts AS T3 ON T2.part_id = T3.part_id GROUP BY T3.part_name ORDER BY fault_count DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id JOIN Assets AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id WHERE T3.asset_details = 'dell laptop3';
