sql: SELECT College_ID, Name, College_Location FROM college WHERE Leader_Name = 'Ousame Tounkara';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the College ID, name, and location for the college led by 'Ousame Tounkara'.

sql: SELECT Member_ID, Name, College_ID FROM member WHERE Country = 'Canada';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, and associated college ID for all members from Canada.

sql: SELECT Round_ID, Member_ID, Decoration_Theme FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the first-place winner of a round, listing the round ID, the winning member's ID, and their decoration theme.

sql: SELECT Member_ID, Country, College_ID FROM member WHERE Name = 'Jack Nicklaus';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the member ID, country, and college ID for the member named 'Jack Nicklaus'.

sql: SELECT Name, Leader_Name, College_Location FROM college ORDER BY Name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each college's name, its leader's name, and its location, ordered by college name in reverse alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(Member_ID) FROM member GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each country along with the total number of members from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Decoration_Theme, T2.Rank_in_Round FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Round_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each member's name, decoration theme, and rank specifically from the first round of the competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Round_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each member's name along with the total count of rounds they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the names of colleges that have more than one member, along with the total count of members for each of those colleges.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS MemberName, T2.Name AS CollegeName FROM member AS T1 LEFT JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each member's name and their corresponding college's name, including members who may not be assigned to a college.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'United States' GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each college and the total count of its members who are from the 'United States'.

sql: SELECT Decoration_Theme FROM round GROUP BY Decoration_Theme HAVING COUNT(Member_ID) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all unique decoration themes that have been assigned to at least one member.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS MemberName, T2.Name AS CollegeName, T3.Decoration_Theme FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T3.Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing members who ranked first in a round, showing their name, their college's name, and the decoration theme of the round they won.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Rank_in_Round < (SELECT AVG(Rank_in_Round) FROM round);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of members who achieved a rank in at least one round that was better than the overall average rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Leader_Name FROM college AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member AS T2 JOIN round AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T2.College_ID = T1.College_ID AND T3.Rank_in_Round = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and leader's name of colleges that have had at least one member achieve a first-place rank in a competition round.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member WHERE Country = 'United States' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Rank_in_Round = 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and country of members from the 'United States' who have never finished a round with a rank of 2.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member WHERE Country = 'Canada' UNION SELECT Name, College_Location FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the names and countries of all members from Canada, plus an additional entry for the 'Saskatchewan School' and its location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM member AS T2 WHERE T2.College_ID = T1.College_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of colleges that have more than one member.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T2.College_Location = 'Ottawa' AND T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from colleges in Ottawa who have participated in a round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1) INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from the 'United States' who have also won at least one round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT COUNT(T3.Round_ID) FROM round AS T3 WHERE T3.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID) AS Round_Count FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.College_ID = (SELECT College_ID FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from 'Saskatchewan School' and the total count of rounds each member has played.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE College_ID = (SELECT College_ID FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School') AND Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round <= 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members from the 'Saskatchewan School' who have achieved a rank of 1st or 2nd in a round.

sql: SELECT T1.Decoration_Theme, AVG(T1.Rank_in_Round) FROM round AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Decoration_Theme HAVING AVG(T1.Rank_in_Round) <= 3;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the decoration themes with an average rank of 3 or less, along with their specific average rank for each round.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, MIN(T3.Rank_in_Round) AS Best_Rank FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID GROUP BY T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each country along with the best (lowest) rank achieved by any member from that country.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member WHERE Country <> 'United States' AND Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and country of any international member (not from the United States) who has participated in a round.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Type FROM store;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and type for every store in the dataset.

sql: SELECT product, dpi FROM product;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each product name along with its corresponding DPI (dots per inch) resolution.

sql: SELECT District_name, City_Area FROM district ORDER BY City_Area DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing all districts and their respective city areas, ordered from the largest city area to the smallest.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Area_size FROM store WHERE Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and area size for the top-ranked store.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE interface = 'USB 2.0';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the product name and color pages-per-minute speed for all products that utilize a 'USB 2.0' interface.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, count(T2.Product_ID) FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each store name and the total count of distinct products available at that store.

sql: SELECT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Store_Name = 'Miramichi';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the products available at the 'Miramichi' store.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, count(T2.Store_ID) FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each district's name along with the total count of stores located within that district.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.District_ID = T3.District_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each store name alongside the name of its corresponding district.

sql: SELECT avg(Area_size) FROM store WHERE Type = 'Village Store';

sql: SELECT T3.product FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T1.Ranking > 2;

sql: SELECT sum(City_Population) FROM district WHERE District_ID IN (SELECT District_ID FROM store_district);
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total population of all cities across districts that contain a store.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name HAVING count(T2.Store_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a district that contains more than one store.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Store_Name ORDER BY count(T2.Product_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the store that carries the highest number of products.

sql: SELECT T5.product FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN store_product AS T4 ON T3.Store_ID = T4.Store_ID JOIN product AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.product_id WHERE T3.Type = 'City Mall' AND T1.City_Population > 100000;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the products sold in 'City Mall' type stores located in districts with a city population over 100,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN store_district AS T3 ON T1.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.District_ID = T4.District_ID WHERE T4.District_name LIKE '%District%' GROUP BY T1.Store_Name HAVING count(T2.Product_ID) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of stores that are located in a district with 'District' in its name and carry more than two products.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.District_name ORDER BY avg(T3.Area_size) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the district that contains the stores with the largest average area size.

sql: SELECT T4.product FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN store AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN store_product AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID JOIN product AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.product_id GROUP BY T4.product_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.District_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of products that are sold in at least two different districts.

sql: SELECT Store_Name FROM store WHERE Ranking = 1 AND Store_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Store_ID FROM store_district AS T1 JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID WHERE T2.District_name = 'Bahawalnagar District');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a top-ranked store that is not located in the 'Bahawalnagar District'.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, avg(T2.c) FROM store AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Store_ID, count(*) AS c FROM store_product GROUP BY Store_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each store type along with the average number of products offered by stores of that type.

sql: SELECT T1.District_name, count(T4.Product_ID) FROM district AS T1 LEFT JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID LEFT JOIN store_product AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID LEFT JOIN product AS T4 ON T3.Product_ID = T4.product_id AND T4.dpi = 600.0 GROUP BY T1.District_name;

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.pages_per_minute_color > 28.0 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Store_Name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_product AS T2 ON T1.Store_ID = T2.Store_ID JOIN product AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.product_id WHERE T3.pages_per_minute_color <= 4.0;

sql: SELECT T1.District_name FROM district AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T2 ON T1.District_ID = T2.District_ID JOIN store AS T3 ON T2.Store_ID = T3.Store_ID WHERE T3.Type IN ('City Mall', 'Village Store') GROUP BY T1.District_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T3.Type) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the district that contains stores of both the 'City Mall' and 'Village Store' types.

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Name, T2.Store_Name, T3.District_name FROM store AS T1 JOIN store_district AS T_d1 ON T1.Store_ID = T_d1.Store_ID JOIN store AS T2 JOIN store_district AS T_d2 ON T2.Store_ID = T_d2.Store_ID JOIN district AS T3 ON T_d1.District_ID = T3.District_ID WHERE T_d1.District_ID = T_d2.District_ID AND T1.Store_ID < T2.Store_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a pair of different stores located in the same district, listing the names of both stores and their shared district.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Number_of_product_category FROM store AS T1 WHERE T1.Number_of_product_category > (SELECT avg(T2.Number_of_product_category) FROM store AS T2 WHERE T2.Type = T1.Type);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and product category count for stores that carry more product categories than the average for their specific store type.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_type_code = 'Clothes';

sql: SELECT department_name FROM Departments WHERE dept_store_id = 5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the department located in the store with an ID of 5.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'New';

sql: SELECT T2.store_name, COUNT(T1.department_id) FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Department_Stores AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_id = T2.dept_store_id GROUP BY T2.store_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each department store by name, along with the total number of departments it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_price > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T2.supplier_name, SUM(T1.total_value_purchased) FROM Product_Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T2.supplier_name;

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id WHERE T3.department_name = 'marketing';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all staff members who are assigned to the 'marketing' department.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T2 ON T3.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id WHERE T2.supplier_name = 'Tesco';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and price for all products supplied by the supplier 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T3.product_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name alongside the total count of products they have purchased across all of their orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T3.product_id = (SELECT product_id FROM Products WHERE product_name = 'black jeans');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who has purchased the product 'black jeans'.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, COUNT(T2.product_id), SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_id HAVING COUNT(T2.product_id) >= 1 AND SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) > 10000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing significant suppliers, specifically those with at least one product and a total purchase value over 10,000, along with their name, product count, and total purchase value.

sql: SELECT T5.dept_store_chain_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id JOIN Department_Stores AS T4 ON T3.dept_store_id = T4.dept_store_id JOIN Department_Store_Chain AS T5 ON T4.dept_store_chain_id = T5.dept_store_chain_id WHERE T1.staff_name = 'Tom';

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_id IN (SELECT address_id FROM Supplier_Addresses);

sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed';

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_price > (SELECT AVG(T2.product_price) FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.product_type_code = T1.product_type_code) AND T1.product_type_code = 'Clothes';

sql: SELECT T2.dept_store_chain_name, T3.store_count FROM Department_Store_Chain AS T2 JOIN (SELECT dept_store_chain_id, COUNT(*) AS store_count FROM Department_Stores GROUP BY dept_store_chain_id) AS T3 ON T2.dept_store_chain_id = T3.dept_store_chain_id WHERE T3.store_count > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each department store chain that has at least one store, along with the total count of stores for that chain.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name, T2.job_title_code FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.department_id IN (SELECT department_id FROM Departments WHERE dept_store_id IN (SELECT dept_store_id FROM Department_Stores WHERE dept_store_chain_id = 1));

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id ORDER BY T1.customer_name, T4.product_price DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the products each customer has ordered, sorted alphabetically by customer name, with each customer's products further sorted from most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers UNION SELECT supplier_name, supplier_phone FROM Suppliers;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows creating a unified contact list by combining customer names and emails with supplier names and phone numbers.

sql: SELECT T1.address_details FROM Addresses AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.payment_method_code = 'Direct Debit';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the address details of all customers who use 'Direct Debit' as their payment method.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'PartFilled';

sql: SELECT Artist_ID, Artist, Age FROM artist WHERE Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the ID, name, and age of artists who are older than 30.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Artist = 'Ophiolatry';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the famous titles attributed to the artist 'Ophiolatry'.

sql: SELECT Song, Weeks_on_Top FROM volume ORDER BY Weeks_on_Top DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing songs and the number of weeks each spent at the top, ordered from the most weeks to the fewest.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Category = 'Best Song';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of music festivals that have a 'Best Song' award category.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Result FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each music festival that has received a 'Nominated' result.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist's name and the total number of volumes they have released.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 3.0;

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID;

sql: SELECT T1.Music_Festival, T2.Song FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID;

sql: SELECT Result, COUNT(*) FROM music_festival GROUP BY Result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each festival result category (e.g., 'Awarded', 'Nominated') and the total number of festivals that fall into each category.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Music_Festival = '34th England Academy Prize';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the artist who performed at the '34th England Academy Prize' music festival.

sql: SELECT Category FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of music festival categories that have occurred more than once.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist GROUP BY Artist HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artists who appear more than once in the database, indicating potential duplicate entries.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the distinct artists who have won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT Artist, Age FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top >= 3.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of any artist who has had a volume stay at the top of the charts for three or more weeks.

sql: SELECT T2.Song, T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Category = 'Best Lyrics' AND T3.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the songs and their corresponding artists that were nominated for the 'Best Lyrics' award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Famous_Release_date = '2009' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the artists who had a famous release in 2009 and were also nominated for a music festival award.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Artist_ID IN (SELECT T2.Artist_ID FROM volume AS T2 JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age of all artists who have won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who has released a volume but has never received an 'Awarded' result at a music festival.

sql: SELECT Song FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top = (SELECT MAX(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song or songs that spent the most weeks at the top.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist, COUNT(T3.Volume_ID) FROM (SELECT avg(Age) AS avg_age FROM artist) AS T1, artist AS T2 JOIN volume AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Age > T1.avg_age GROUP BY T2.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing artists who are older than the average artist age, along with the total count of volumes for each.

sql: SELECT Category FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the single most common music festival category, based on the number of occurrences in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Song, Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top > (SELECT AVG(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the song title and artist ID for all songs that have spent an above-average number of weeks at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM volume GROUP BY Artist_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the artist who has produced the highest number of volumes.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Famous_Title FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Famous_Release_date = 'November 2007' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM volume AS T2 WHERE T2.Artist_ID = T1.Artist_ID AND T2.Weeks_on_Top = 1.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artist and their famous title, filtering for works released in November 2007 by artists who have had a volume spend exactly one week at the top.

sql: SELECT Name, Headquarters FROM company WHERE Industry = 'Banking';

result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of people who work for companies with a market value greater than 200 billion.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.People_ID) FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Company_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.People_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the company with the most employees, along with the total count of its employees.

sql: SELECT Industry, AVG(Market_Value_in_Billion) FROM company GROUP BY Industry HAVING COUNT(Company_ID) > 0 AND AVG(Market_Value_in_Billion) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing industries where the average company market value exceeds 150 billion, showing each industry alongside its calculated average market value.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 LEFT JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Company_ID IS NULL;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Sales_in_Billion > 400 UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of people who are either from the United States or are employed by a company with sales exceeding $400 billion.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a person from the United States who also works in the Banking industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.People_ID = T3.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Company_ID HAVING AVG(T3.Age) > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM people);

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, COUNT(T2.course_id) FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first name, last name, and city for each person by matching people to their corresponding address.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Candidates AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.candidate_id;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T3.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students enrolled in the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT asessment_outcome_code, COUNT(*) FROM Candidate_Assessments GROUP BY asessment_outcome_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each assessment outcome code ('Pass', 'Fail') and the total count of candidate assessments that resulted in each outcome.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.email_address FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.city = 'Linnealand';

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.student_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each course name along with the total count of unique students who have attended that course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.person_id HAVING count(T2.course_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who are registered for more than one course.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.course_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 WHERE T1.student_id = T2.student_id AND T1.course_id = T2.course_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student and course for a registration that has no corresponding attendance record.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T5.city FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass' AND T5.city = 'South Minnie';

sql: SELECT T2.course_name FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_name ORDER BY count(T1.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the course with the highest number of student registrations.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.student_id JOIN Courses AS T5 ON T4.course_id = T5.course_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' AND T5.course_name = 'French';

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.date_from FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T3 ON T2.candidate_id = T3.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T4 ON T1.person_id = T4.person_id WHERE T3.asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, last name, and address start date for a candidate who failed their assessment.

sql: SELECT avg(T3.num_courses) FROM (SELECT T1.student_id, count(T1.course_id) AS num_courses FROM Student_Course_Attendance AS T1 GROUP BY T1.student_id) AS T3;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id GROUP BY T1.person_id HAVING count(T2.address_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name, T4.course_name FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id AND T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN People AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.person_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T1.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T1.registration_date = T2.date_of_attendance;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name, last name, and course name for a student who attended a course on the exact day they registered for it.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidates AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all unique individuals who are identified as either a student or a candidate.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM People AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.person_id = T3.person_id WHERE T2.assessment_date > T3.date_from;

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all available size codes and their corresponding descriptions, sorted alphabetically by the size description.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each dog's name alongside the first and last name of its corresponding owner.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code WHERE T1.name = 'Mavis';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the breed name for the dog named 'Mavis'.

sql: SELECT T2.size_description, COUNT(T1.dog_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Sizes AS T2 ON T1.size_code = T2.size_code GROUP BY T2.size_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each dog size category along with the total number of dogs belonging to that size.

sql: SELECT T2.treatment_type_description, AVG(T1.cost_of_treatment) FROM Treatments AS T1 JOIN Treatment_Types AS T2 ON T1.treatment_type_code = T2.treatment_type_code GROUP BY T2.treatment_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each treatment type's description along with the average cost for that treatment.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Professionals AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'VAC';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique first and last names of professionals who have administered a treatment of the type 'VAC'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, COUNT(T2.dog_id) FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id WHERE T1.state = 'Vermont' GROUP BY T1.owner_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first name of each dog owner from Vermont along with a count of how many dogs they own.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code WHERE T1.abandoned_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and breed for each dog that has been marked as abandoned.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.dog_id) FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id GROUP BY T1.owner_id HAVING COUNT(T2.dog_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying owners who have more than one dog, listing their first and last name along with the total count of dogs they own.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.treatment_id) FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id GROUP BY T1.dog_id HAVING COUNT(T2.treatment_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of dogs that have received more than one treatment, along with a count of their total treatments.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Professionals AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id WHERE T2.cost_of_treatment = (SELECT MAX(cost_of_treatment) FROM Treatments);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the professional who administered the single most expensive treatment.

sql: SELECT T2.breed_name, T3.size_description, AVG(T1.weight) AS average_weight FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code JOIN Sizes AS T3 ON T1.size_code = T3.size_code GROUP BY T2.breed_name, T3.size_description;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows calculating the average weight for each dog breed, broken down by its size description.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS T3 ON T2.professional_id = T3.professional_id WHERE T3.state = 'Wisconsin';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Dogs WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Dogs);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names and ages of dogs who are older than the average age of all dogs in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.breed_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Breeds AS T2 ON T1.breed_code = T2.breed_code GROUP BY T2.breed_name HAVING AVG(T1.age) > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Dogs);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the dog breed whose average age is greater than the overall average age of all dogs.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Owners UNION SELECT first_name, last_name FROM Professionals;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the unique first and last names of all individuals from a combined list of owners and professionals.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'EXAM' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.dog_id = T2.dog_id WHERE T2.treatment_type_code = 'VAC';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of dogs that have received an exam but have not received a vaccination.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id WHERE T2.age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM Dogs);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of the owners of the oldest dog or dogs in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.treatment_type_code) AS num_treatment_types FROM Professionals AS T1 JOIN Treatments AS T2 ON T1.professional_id = T2.professional_id GROUP BY T1.professional_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.treatment_type_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and a count of distinct treatment types for each professional who has performed more than one type of treatment.

sql: SELECT T2.breed_name FROM Breeds AS T2 JOIN Dogs AS T1 ON T2.breed_code = T1.breed_code JOIN Treatments AS T3 ON T1.dog_id = T3.dog_id GROUP BY T2.breed_name ORDER BY AVG(T3.cost_of_treatment) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the dog breed with the highest average cost of treatment.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Owners AS T1 WHERE T1.owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Dogs WHERE breed_code = 'BUL') AND T1.owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Dogs WHERE size_code = 'LGE');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the first and last names of owners who have at least one dog of the 'BUL' breed and at least one large-sized dog.

sql: SELECT * FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, providing a complete list of all artists along with their country, gender, and preferred genre.

sql: SELECT song_name FROM song WHERE rating = 8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all songs that have a rating of exactly 8.

sql: SELECT artist_name, country FROM artist ORDER BY country;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist and their country, sorted alphabetically by country.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM song;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each unique country that appears in the song database.

sql: SELECT g_name FROM genre WHERE most_popular_in LIKE '%Sylhet%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the music genre that is most popular in locations containing 'Sylhet'.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, COUNT(T2.f_id) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN files AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name GROUP BY T1.artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each artist's name along with the total count of their associated files.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.artist_name, T2.rating FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name WHERE T2.rating = '8';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the song name, artist name, and genre rating for songs belonging to genres with a rating of '8'.

sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM artist GROUP BY country HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing countries that have more than one artist, along with the total count of artists for each of those countries.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.gender, T3.file_size FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T1.f_id = T3.f_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each song's name along with the gender of its artist and the song's file size.

sql: SELECT song_name, rating FROM song WHERE rating > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing songs with a rating higher than the overall average rating, showing each song's name and its specific rating.

sql: SELECT T1.preferred_genre, AVG(T2.rating) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh' GROUP BY T1.preferred_genre;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the average song rating for each preferred music genre among artists from Bangladesh.

sql: SELECT T1.g_name FROM genre AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.g_name = T2.genre_is WHERE T2.resolution > 500;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of genres that contain high-resolution songs (where resolution is greater than 500).

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN genre AS T3 ON T2.genre_is = T3.g_name WHERE T3.rating > '7' GROUP BY T1.artist_name HAVING count(T2.song_name) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of unique artists who have at least one song in a genre with a rating greater than 7.

sql: SELECT artist_name, country FROM artist AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name AND T2.rating = 8);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name and country of artists who have at least one song with a rating of 8.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE f_id IN (SELECT f_id FROM files WHERE formats = 'mp4');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and artist for each song that is available in the 'mp4' file format.

sql: SELECT T.artist_name, T.avg_rating FROM (SELECT artist_name, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM song GROUP BY artist_name) AS T WHERE T.avg_rating > 6;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artists whose average song rating is greater than 6, along with their calculated average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T2.gender, T3.formats, T4.rating FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T1.f_id = T3.f_id JOIN genre AS T4 ON T1.genre_is = T4.g_name WHERE T2.gender = 'Female';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing songs by female artists, providing the song's name, the artist's gender, the song's file format, and its genre's rating.

sql: SELECT artist_name, country FROM artist WHERE gender = 'Male' UNION SELECT artist_name, country FROM song WHERE rating > 7;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and country of artists, comprising a combined list of all male artists and any artist associated with a song rated higher than 7.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' INTERSECT SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE rating > 6;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an artist who is from Bangladesh and has a song with a rating greater than 6.

sql: SELECT g_name FROM genre EXCEPT SELECT genre_is FROM song WHERE rating < 8;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of music genres where all associated songs have a rating of 8 or higher.

sql: SELECT artist_name FROM song GROUP BY artist_name HAVING AVG(rating) > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists whose average song rating is higher than the overall average rating for all songs.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.num_songs FROM artist AS T1 JOIN (SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) as num_songs FROM song GROUP BY artist_name) AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing artists from Bangladesh along with a count of the total number of songs for each artist.

sql: SELECT song_name, artist_name FROM song WHERE resolution > (SELECT avg(resolution) FROM song) AND f_id IN (SELECT f_id FROM files WHERE formats = 'mp4');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and artists of songs available in 'mp4' format that have a higher-than-average resolution.

sql: SELECT T2.g_name, T2.rating FROM song AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_is = T2.g_name WHERE T1.rating = (SELECT MAX(rating) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the genre name and its associated rating for the song that has the highest rating in the dataset.

sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) FROM song WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' GROUP BY artist_name UNION SELECT artist_name, COUNT(*) FROM song WHERE country = 'India' GROUP BY artist_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artists from Bangladesh and India, along with the count of their songs in their respective country.

sql: SELECT Product_Name, Product_Price FROM Products WHERE Product_Type_Code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and price for all products that are classified as 'Food'.

sql: SELECT Party_Details FROM Parties ORDER BY Party_Details;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the details for each political party, ordered alphabetically by the party's details.

sql: SELECT Event_Type_Code, Finance_ID, Location_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the event type code, finance ID, and location ID for all events classified as a 'Trade Show'.

sql: SELECT Other_Details FROM Assets WHERE Other_Details LIKE '%Cars%';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for any assets that have the word 'Cars' in their description.

sql: SELECT Product_Name, Product_Price FROM Products ORDER BY Product_Price DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the most expensive products and their prices, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT Event_Type_Code, COUNT(Event_ID) FROM Events GROUP BY Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each event type code and the total count of events associated with that type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Product_Name, T1.Product_Price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique names and prices for all products that have been featured in at least one event.

sql: SELECT T2.Role_Code FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Party_Details = 'European People''s Party';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the role codes for all events in which the 'European People's Party' was involved.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, COUNT(T2.Party_ID) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the event type and the total count of participating parties for each individual event.

sql: SELECT T2.address_details FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.Address_ID = T2.Address_ID WHERE T1.Event_Type_Code = 'Press Conferenc';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the address details for all events that are categorized as 'Press Conference'.

sql: SELECT Product_Name, Product_Price FROM Products WHERE Product_Price > (SELECT AVG(Product_Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing products whose price is above the average product price, showing each product's name and its specific price.

sql: SELECT T1.Other_Details, COUNT(T2.Event_ID) FROM Locations AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID GROUP BY T1.Other_Details;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing location details and the corresponding total count of events for each.

sql: SELECT T5.Product_Name FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T3.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T1.Party_Details = 'European Free Alliance' AND T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all products associated with events organized by the 'European Free Alliance' party.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code IN ('Organizer', 'Participant') GROUP BY T1.Party_ID, T1.Party_Details HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Role_Code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the details of parties that have served as both an 'Organizer' and a 'Participant' across different events.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Type_Code, T3.Product_Name, T3.Product_Price FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID WHERE T3.Product_Price = (SELECT MAX(T5.Product_Price) FROM Products_in_Events AS T4 JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T4.Event_ID = T1.Event_ID);
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, identifying the most expensive product for each event by listing the event's type code, the product's name, and its price.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID FROM Agreements AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Event_ID HAVING count(T1.Document_ID) > 1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Event_ID FROM Assets_in_Events AS T2 JOIN Assets AS T3 ON T2.Asset_ID = T3.Asset_ID WHERE T3.Other_Details = 'Transportation Cars';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID for any event that has both multiple agreement documents and involves 'Transportation Cars' as an asset.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Participant';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code, SUM(T3.Product_Price) AS TotalProductValue FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Product_ID = T3.Product_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code HAVING SUM(T3.Product_Price) > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying high-value events where the total value of all associated products exceeds $3000, listing the event's ID, type code, and the calculated total product value.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Type_Code FROM Events AS T1 WHERE T1.Location_ID = (SELECT Location_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_ID = 13) AND T1.Event_ID != 13;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the event ID and type code for any other event that occurred at the same location as the event with ID 13.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Product_Name, T4.address_details FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Product_ID = T2.Product_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.Address_ID = T4.Address_ID JOIN Finances AS T5 ON T3.Finance_ID = T5.Finance_ID WHERE T5.Other_Details = 'Good';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing unique product names and the addresses of events where they appeared, filtered to include only those events with a 'Good' financial status.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Products_in_Events AS T4 ON T3.Event_ID = T4.Event_ID JOIN Products AS T5 ON T4.Product_ID = T5.Product_ID WHERE T5.Product_Type_Code = 'Clothes' AND T2.Role_Code = 'Participant';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details of the unique party that participated in an event involving 'Clothes' products.

sql: SELECT AVG(ProductCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T2.Product_ID) AS ProductCount FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Products_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the average number of products per event.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, COUNT(T1.Party_ID) FROM Parties_in_Events AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Event_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Party_ID) > (SELECT AVG(PartyCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Party_ID) AS PartyCount FROM Parties_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID));
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying events with an above-average number of attending parties, showing each event's ID and its corresponding party count.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND T3.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show' UNION SELECT T1.Party_Details FROM Parties AS T1 JOIN Parties_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T2.Role_Code = 'Participant' AND T3.Event_Type_Code = 'Press Conferenc';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the details of parties that have either organized a 'Trade Show' or participated in a 'Press Conference'.

sql: SELECT T2.Other_Details, COUNT(T1.Event_ID) AS EventCount FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID GROUP BY T2.Other_Details ORDER BY EventCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the location detail that is associated with the most events, along with the total event count.

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population FROM city WHERE Regional_Population > 8700400;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and regional populations of cities with more than 8.7 million people.

sql: SELECT * FROM match WHERE Competition = 'Friendly match';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with complete details for any match classified as a 'Friendly match'.

sql: SELECT GDP FROM city WHERE City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the city of Shanghai.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Competition FROM match;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique competition name recorded in the match data.

sql: SELECT Jan FROM temperature WHERE City_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the January temperature for the specific city with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2008;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the city that hosted an event in the year 2008.

sql: SELECT T1.City, COUNT(T2.Match_ID) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city along with the total count of matches hosted in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.City, (T2.Jan + T2.Feb + T2.Mar + T2.Apr + T2.Jun + T2.Jul + T2.Aug + T2.Sep + T2.Oct + T2.Nov + T2.Dec) / 11.0 FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each city and its average monthly temperature, calculated by summing the temperatures for 11 months (excluding May) and dividing by 11.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of cities that hosted a match during the '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' competition.

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total regional population of all cities that hosted an event in the year 2009.

sql: SELECT T3.Date, T3.Score FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T1.City = 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the date and final score for all matches that took place in the city of 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )'.

sql: SELECT T2.Year, AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T2.Year;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each event year alongside the average GDP of the corresponding host cities for that year.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City GROUP BY T1.City HAVING COUNT(T2.Match_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that has hosted more than one match.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City ORDER BY T1.GDP DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and GDP of the host city with the highest Gross Domestic Product.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Competition FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T1.Regional_Population > 8004680;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique type of competition that has been hosted in a city belonging to a region with a population greater than 8 million.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Score = '1-0' AND T1.GDP > 700;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a city with a GDP greater than 700 that hosted a match ending in a '1-0' score.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T2.Jan < (SELECT AVG(Jan) FROM temperature);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of cities where the January temperature is below the average January temperature of all cities.

sql: SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = 'Friendly match' EXCEPT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T3.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that hosted a 'Friendly match' but did not also host a match for the '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification'.

sql: SELECT T3.Competition, MAX(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID GROUP BY T3.Competition;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each competition along with the maximum regional population of any city that hosted a match for that competition.

sql: SELECT City FROM city WHERE GDP > (SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city) AND City_ID IN (SELECT Host_City FROM hosting_city);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of host cities that have a Gross Domestic Product (GDP) greater than the average GDP of all cities.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN hosting_city AS T3 ON T1.City_ID = T3.Host_City WHERE T2.Jan < T2.Dec;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the unique host cities that have a warmer temperature in December than in January.

sql: SELECT City FROM city WHERE GDP > 700 INTERSECT SELECT T1.City FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the city that has both a GDP exceeding 700 and has also served as a host city.

sql: SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city WHERE City_ID IN (SELECT T1.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID WHERE T2.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification');

sql: SELECT T1.Governor FROM party AS T1 WHERE T1.Party_ID IN (SELECT T2.Party FROM election AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Party HAVING COUNT(T2.Election_ID) >= 2);
