sql: SELECT Delegate FROM election EXCEPT SELECT T1.Delegate FROM election AS T1 JOIN county AS T2 ON T1.District = T2.County_Id WHERE T2.County_name = 'Howard';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all delegates, excluding those who represent Howard county.

sql: SELECT * FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0 AND Party_ID IN (SELECT Party FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the political parties from 1998 that participated in an election, along with their slate of candidates for various state and federal offices.

sql: SELECT Delegate, First_Elected FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Appropriations' AND First_Elected = (SELECT MIN(First_Elected) FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Appropriations');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the most senior delegate(s) on the 'Appropriations' committee by listing their name and earliest election year.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and price for every product.

sql: SELECT Name, Founder FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Tokyo';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and founder of the manufacturer headquartered in Tokyo.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Products WHERE Price = 240;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of products that have a price of exactly 240.

sql: SELECT Name, Revenue FROM Manufacturers ORDER BY Revenue DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all manufacturers and their corresponding revenue, ordered from highest to lowest revenue.

sql: SELECT Name, Headquarter FROM Manufacturers WHERE Founder = 'Owen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and headquarters of the manufacturer founded by 'Owen'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS ProductName, T2.Name AS ManufacturerName FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows, listing each product's name and its corresponding manufacturer's name.

sql: SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average price calculated across all products.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Code) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with a count of the total number of products they produce.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the average price of their products.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price > 150;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of manufacturers who produce products costing more than $150.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, MIN(T1.Price), MAX(T1.Price) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each manufacturer's name along with the minimum and maximum price of the products they offer.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T2.Revenue > 50.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of products made by manufacturers with revenue exceeding 50.0.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and price for products that are more expensive than the average price of all products.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers that produce more than one product.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS ProductName, T2.Name AS ManufacturerName FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most expensive product, providing its name and the name of its manufacturer.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Manufacturer IN (SELECT Code FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Tokyo');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of products from manufacturers headquartered in Tokyo.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Manufacturers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS T2 WHERE T2.Manufacturer = T1.Code AND T2.Price > 200);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers who produce at least one product with a price greater than 200.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING AVG(T1.Price) > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers whose average product price is higher than the overall average price across all products.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Manufacturers WHERE Code NOT IN (SELECT Manufacturer FROM Products WHERE Name = 'Memory');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of manufacturers who do not produce any products named 'Memory'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Products WHERE Price > 200 UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T2.Name = 'Sony';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of products that either have a price greater than 200 or are manufactured by Sony.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price > 200 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price < 150;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a manufacturer that produces both high-priced (over $200) and low-priced (under $150) products.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Manufacturers WHERE Revenue > 50.0 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Name = 'Memory';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of manufacturers with revenue over 50.0, excluding any that produce a product named 'Memory'.

sql: SELECT T1.ManufacturerName, T1.AvgPrice FROM (SELECT T2.Name AS ManufacturerName, AVG(T3.Price) AS AvgPrice FROM Manufacturers AS T2 JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.Code = T3.Manufacturer GROUP BY T2.Name) AS T1 WHERE T1.AvgPrice > 150;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of manufacturers whose average product price is greater than 150, along with that calculated average price.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Revenue FROM Manufacturers AS T1 WHERE T1.Revenue > (SELECT AVG(T2.Revenue) FROM Manufacturers AS T2 WHERE T2.Headquarter = T1.Headquarter);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Headquarter, COUNT(T1.Code) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Manufacturers AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer = T2.Code WHERE T1.Price > 120 GROUP BY T2.Headquarter HAVING COUNT(T1.Code) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each manufacturer headquarter along with the total count of their products that are priced over 120.

sql: SELECT name, age, job FROM Person WHERE city = 'new york city';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, age, and job of a person living in 'new york city'.

sql: SELECT name, age, city FROM Person WHERE job = 'engineer';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, age, and city for the person identified as an engineer.

sql: SELECT name, friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE year = 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the friendship pair (a person's name and their friend's name) that was recorded in year 10.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE age > 35;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of each person who is older than 35.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT job FROM Person;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique job title held by people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.friend;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of each person who is also recorded as being a friend to someone else.

sql: SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each person by name along with the total count of their friends.

sql: SELECT job, AVG(age) FROM Person GROUP BY job;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, with each row listing a job title and the corresponding average age for people with that job.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age, T2.friend FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows that detail friendship pairings, showing each person's name and age alongside the name of one of their friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.friend = 'Bob';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the person who is friends with 'Bob'.

sql: SELECT T1.city, T2.friend FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T1.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the city of each male person and the name of their corresponding friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name, MAX(T2.age) FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each person's name alongside the age of their oldest friend.

sql: SELECT name, age, city FROM Person WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Person);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, age, and city for individuals whose age is greater than the overall average age.

sql: SELECT T1.name AS Person1, T3.friend AS PotentialFriend FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name JOIN PersonFriend AS T3 ON T2.name = T3.name WHERE T1.name <> T3.friend;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows of potential friend recommendations, matching an initial person (`Person1`) with a recommended individual (`PotentialFriend`) who is a "friend of a friend".

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PersonFriend AS T2 WHERE T2.name = T1.name AND T2.year > 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and ages of people who have had at least one friendship for more than 5 years.

sql: SELECT T1.job, COUNT(*) FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name GROUP BY T1.job HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each job title alongside the total count of friendships held by all people with that job.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM PersonFriend AS T1 WHERE T1.friend IN (SELECT T2.name FROM Person AS T2 WHERE T2.job = 'engineer');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a person who has a friend working as an engineer.

sql: SELECT name, city FROM Person WHERE city = 'new york city' UNION SELECT name, city FROM Person WHERE job = 'engineer';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and cities for all people who either live in 'new york city' or have the job title of 'engineer'.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE gender = 'male' INTERSECT SELECT name FROM PersonFriend WHERE year > 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying a male person who has also been a friend for more than 10 years.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE gender = 'male' EXCEPT SELECT name FROM PersonFriend WHERE friend = 'Zach';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a male individual who is not considered a friend of 'Zach'.

sql: SELECT T1.city, T1.avg_age FROM (SELECT city, AVG(age) AS avg_age FROM Person GROUP BY city) AS T1 WHERE T1.avg_age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the cities where the average age of the population is greater than 30, along with that calculated average age.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name GROUP BY T1.name, T1.age HAVING MIN(T2.year) < 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of people whose earliest friendship began before year 10.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE city = 'austin' AND age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Person WHERE city != 'austin');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of each person from Austin who is older than the average age of people living in all other cities.

sql: SELECT T1.job FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name GROUP BY T1.job ORDER BY AVG(T2.year) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the job title held by people who, on average, have the most recently formed friendships.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE name NOT IN (SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT * FROM Participants WHERE Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing all participant details for those identified by the type code 'Organizer'.

sql: SELECT Service_Type_Code FROM Services WHERE Service_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the service type code for the specific service with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Events WHERE Event_Details = 'Success';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all events that were successful.

sql: SELECT Participant_Details FROM Participants WHERE Participant_ID = 9;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the participant details for the specific participant with an ID of 9.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Event_Details FROM Events;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct type of event detail recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T2.Service_Type_Code FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID WHERE T2.Service_Type_Code = 'Death Proof';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the event ID for all events that used the 'Death Proof' service type.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, COUNT(T2.Participant_ID) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each event's ID along with the total count of participants registered for that event.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, T2.Event_ID FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID WHERE T1.Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details for each participant classified as an 'Organizer' and the ID of the event they are involved with.

sql: SELECT T2.Service_Type_Code, T1.Event_Details, count(*) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID GROUP BY T2.Service_Type_Code, T1.Event_Details;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each combination of service type and event detail (e.g., 'Success', 'Fail'), along with the total number of events that occurred for that specific pairing.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details for each participant registered in event number 3.

sql: SELECT T2.Event_ID, T2.Event_Details FROM Participants_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T1.Participant_ID = 26;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the ID and details for all events in which participant ID 26 took part.

sql: SELECT Participant_Type_Code, COUNT(*) FROM Participants GROUP BY Participant_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each participant type code and the total number of participants belonging to that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Details = 'Success';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the details of participants who took part in any event categorized as 'Success'.

sql: SELECT Event_ID FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID HAVING count(Participant_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing the Event IDs for all events that have more than one registered participant.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 WHERE T1.Participant_ID IN (SELECT T2.Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events AS T2 WHERE T2.Event_ID = 3);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details for each participant who attended the event with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T4.Service_Type_Code, T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID JOIN Services AS T4 ON T3.Service_ID = T4.Service_ID WHERE T1.Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the details for participants identified as 'Organizers' and the corresponding service type for the events they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T1.Service_Type_Code FROM Services AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Events AS T2 WHERE T2.Service_ID = T1.Service_ID AND T2.Event_Details = 'Fail');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the service type codes for services that have experienced at least one event with a 'Fail' status.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details, T3.Num_Events FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Participant_ID, count(*) AS Num_Events FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Participant_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Participant_ID = T3.Participant_ID WHERE T3.Num_Events > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing active participants, who have taken part in at least one event, along with the total count of events they have participated in.

sql: SELECT Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events WHERE Event_ID IN (SELECT Event_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Details = 'Success') UNION SELECT Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events WHERE Event_ID IN (SELECT Event_ID FROM Events WHERE Event_Details = 'Fail');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique IDs of all participants who took part in events that resulted in either a 'Success' or a 'Fail'.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Details = 'Success' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID JOIN Events AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Details = 'Fail';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the details of participants who have been involved in a 'Success' event but never in a 'Fail' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events AS T1 JOIN Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T2.Event_Details = 'Success' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events AS T1 WHERE T1.Event_ID IN (SELECT Event_ID FROM Events WHERE Service_ID = 5);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of participants who were involved in a successful event and also participated in an event related to service ID 5.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, T1.Event_Details FROM Events AS T1 WHERE T1.Service_ID = (SELECT Service_ID FROM Services WHERE Service_Type_Code = 'Death Proof');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the event ID and details for all events associated with the 'Death Proof' service type.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Type_Code, count(T1.Participant_ID) FROM Participants AS T1 WHERE T1.Participant_ID IN (SELECT Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events) GROUP BY T1.Participant_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that provide a count of participants for each participant type, including only those who are registered in an event.

sql: SELECT T1.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Participant_ID = T2.Participant_ID GROUP BY T1.Participant_ID, T1.Participant_Details HAVING COUNT(T2.Event_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the details of participants who have registered for more than one event.

sql: SELECT T2.Service_Type_Code, count(T1.Event_ID) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Service_ID = T2.Service_ID WHERE T1.Event_Details = 'Success' GROUP BY T2.Service_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each service type code along with the total count of successful events associated with that service type.

sql: SELECT subject_id, subject_name FROM Subjects;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all available academic subjects along with their corresponding unique IDs.

sql: SELECT personal_name, family_name, login_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors WHERE login_name = 'jmckenzie';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the personal name, family name, and login name for the specific course author or tutor identified by the login name 'jmckenzie'.

sql: SELECT login_name, personal_name, family_name FROM Students WHERE personal_name = 'Wilson';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the login name, personal name, and family name for any student whose personal name is 'Wilson'.

sql: SELECT course_name, course_description FROM Courses;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and description for each available course in the catalog.

sql: SELECT registration_id, date_test_taken FROM Student_Tests_Taken WHERE test_result = 'Pass';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the registration ID and the date the test was taken for all student tests that resulted in a 'Pass'.

sql: SELECT T2.subject_name, COUNT(T1.course_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Subjects AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id GROUP BY T2.subject_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each subject name along with the total number of courses offered for that subject.

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name, T2.family_name, COUNT(T1.course_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T2.author_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each author's first and last name, along with a count of the total number of courses they have created.

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name, T2.family_name FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T1.course_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all students enrolled in the course with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name, COUNT(T1.student_id) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each course name along with the total count of students enrolled in that course.

sql: SELECT personal_name, family_name, date_of_registration FROM Students WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', date_of_registration) = '2015';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the full name and registration date for all students who signed up during the year 2015.

sql: SELECT course_name FROM Courses WHERE subject_id = (SELECT subject_id FROM Subjects WHERE subject_name = 'Computer Science');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all courses offered within the 'Computer Science' subject.

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T2.author_id HAVING COUNT(T1.course_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the personal names of all authors and tutors who have created at least one course.

sql: SELECT T3.subject_name, COUNT(T1.student_id) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id GROUP BY T3.subject_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each subject name alongside the total count of student enrollments for that subject.

sql: SELECT T3.personal_name, T3.family_name FROM Student_Tests_Taken AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.registration_id = T2.registration_id JOIN Students AS T3 ON T2.student_id = T3.student_id WHERE T1.test_result = 'Pass';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of students for each test they have passed.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name, COUNT(T1.student_id) AS NumberOfStudents FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_id ORDER BY NumberOfStudents DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most popular course, listing its name and the total number of students enrolled.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Courses AS T2 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T2.author_id = T1.author_id);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the personal and family names of course authors whose courses have at least one student enrolled.

sql: SELECT login_name FROM Students EXCEPT SELECT login_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the login names of students who are not also registered as course authors or tutors.

sql: SELECT login_name FROM Students UNION SELECT login_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the unique login names for all users, combining individuals from both the Students and Course Authors/Tutors tables.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T1.subject_id = (SELECT subject_id FROM Subjects WHERE subject_name = 'Computer Science') AND T3.test_result = 'Fail';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.personal_name, T2.family_name, T3.course_name, T1.date_of_completion FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T1.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.date_of_completion IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing students who have completed a course, showing their full name, the course name, and the specific date of completion for each enrollment.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T3.test_result = 'Pass' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.registration_id) FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T1.registration_id = T3.registration_id GROUP BY T2.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each course name alongside its corresponding student test pass rate percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T2.date_of_enrolment FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id ORDER BY T2.date_of_enrolment ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the first student to enroll in a course, showing their full name and the specific date of their enrolment.

sql: WITH SubjectAuthorCounts AS (SELECT T1.subject_id, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.author_id) AS NumAuthors FROM Courses AS T1 GROUP BY T1.subject_id) SELECT T2.subject_name, T1.NumAuthors FROM SubjectAuthorCounts AS T1 JOIN Subjects AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each subject name alongside the total count of unique authors who have created courses for it.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name, AVG(JULIANDAY(T1.date_of_completion) - JULIANDAY(T1.date_of_enrolment)) AS AvgDaysToComplete FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.date_of_completion IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T2.course_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each course name and the average number of days students take to complete it, calculated from their enrollment to completion dates.

sql: SELECT T3.personal_name, T3.family_name FROM Student_Course_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Students AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.student_id WHERE T2.subject_id = (SELECT subject_id FROM Subjects WHERE subject_name = 'Computer Science') GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(T1.registration_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the full names of students who are enrolled in at least one 'Computer Science' course.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of all scientists in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the name of each project and the corresponding hours required for its completion.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN = 152934485;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the scientist identified by the specific Social Security Number 152934485.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Hours > 200;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of projects that required more than 200 hours to complete.

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects ORDER BY Hours DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing all projects and their associated hours, ordered from the project with the most hours to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the scientist assigned to the 'AeH1' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Project) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist by name and the total count of projects they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique names of projects that have at least one person assigned to them.

sql: SELECT SUM(T3.Hours) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T1.Name = 'Michael Rogers';

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours > (SELECT AVG(Hours) FROM Projects);

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Scientist) FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.Project GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T3.Name = 'Winds: Studying Bernoullis Principle';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the scientist assigned to the 'Winds: Studying Bernoullis Principle' project.

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of scientists who are assigned to either project 'AeH1' or 'AeH3'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'AeH1' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project = 'Ast3';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Hours) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T3.Hours) > 200;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing scientists who have worked more than 200 total hours across all projects, showing each scientist's name and their corresponding sum of hours.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Code = (SELECT Project FROM AssignedTo GROUP BY Project ORDER BY COUNT(Scientist) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the project that has the most scientists assigned to it.

sql: SELECT T1.Scientist, T2.Scientist FROM AssignedTo AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Project = T2.Project AND T1.Scientist < T2.Scientist;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying all unique pairs of scientists who are assigned to work on the same project.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T3.Hours > 150;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing the unique names of scientists who have worked on projects that required more than 150 hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo AS T2 JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T2.Scientist = T1.SSN AND T3.Hours > 250) THEN 'High-Hour Project' ELSE 'Standard-Hour Project' END AS Status FROM Scientists AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each scientist's name and a status indicating whether they are assigned to any project exceeding 250 hours ('High-Hour Project') or only to standard-hour projects.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and age for each captain in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all ships registered under the Panamanian flag.

sql: SELECT Name, Rank FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and rank of all captains who hold the rank of 'Midshipman'.

sql: SELECT Name, Built_Year FROM Ship ORDER BY Built_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and build year of each ship, ordered from the newest to the oldest.

sql: SELECT * FROM captain WHERE Name = 'Captain Sir Henry Langford';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing all details for the captain named 'Captain Sir Henry Langford'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each captain alongside the name of their corresponding ship.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each distinct captain rank and the total number of captains holding that rank.

sql: SELECT Built_Year, COUNT(*) FROM Ship GROUP BY Built_Year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each construction year alongside the total number of ships built in that year.

sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS REAL)) FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average age calculated across all captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'Panamax';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.age FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = 1997;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of captains whose ships were built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Class FROM captain;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique classes of ships that have a captain.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS REAL) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS REAL)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age for each captain who is older than the average age of all captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Flag, COUNT(T2.Captain_ID) FROM Ship AS T1 JOIN captain AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Flag;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Ship AS T1 WHERE T1.Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of ships commanded by a captain with the rank of 'Midshipman'.

sql: SELECT T1.Rank, AVG(CAST(T1.age AS REAL)) FROM captain AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Rank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each captain rank along with the average age for captains holding that rank.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID = (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Name = 'HMS Gorgon');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the captains who commanded the ship 'HMS Gorgon'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Cutter';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all captains who are not in the 'Cutter' class.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Built_Year FROM Ship AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM captain AS T2 WHERE T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID AND CAST(T2.age AS REAL) > 40);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and built year for all ships that are commanded by at least one captain over the age of 40.

sql: SELECT Class, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Class HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying ship classes with more than one captain, showing the class name and the corresponding count of captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Name LIKE 'Captain%';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of captains whose recorded name starts with the title 'Captain' and the corresponding names of the ships they command.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Rank = 'Midshipman' UNION SELECT Name, Type FROM Ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows creating a combined list of Midshipman-ranked captains (showing their name and age) and Panamanian-flagged ships (showing their name and type).

sql: SELECT * FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = (SELECT MIN(Built_Year) FROM Ship);

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.age FROM (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = 1997) AS T1 JOIN captain AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for each captain of a ship that was built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT Rank FROM captain GROUP BY Rank ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most frequently occurring rank among all captains.

sql: SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won';

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Festival_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for the festival with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Year FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the festival name and year corresponding to nominations that resulted in a win.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival by name, along with the total count of artwork nominations for that festival.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, COUNT(T1.Festival_ID) FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artwork type alongside the total count of festival nominations received for that type.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM artwork AS T2 WHERE T2.Artwork_ID IN (SELECT T1.Artwork_ID FROM nomination AS T1 WHERE T1.Result = 'Nominated');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all artworks that received a 'Nominated' result.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of festivals that have received more than one nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each artwork and the name of the festival where it was nominated.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type, T3.Festival_Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2007 AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artwork that won a festival award in 2007, detailing the artwork's name, its type, the festival's name, and the winning result.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE T1.Artwork_ID IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Nominated') AND T1.Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for an award but did not win.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Num_of_Audience > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and audience counts for all festivals with an audience size greater than the average.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, T2.Year FROM festival_detail AS T2 WHERE T2.Festival_ID IN (SELECT T1.Festival_ID FROM nomination AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Festival_ID HAVING COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and year of festivals that received nominations for more than one artwork.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T2 JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Artwork_ID = T1.Artwork_ID AND T3.Chair_Name = 'Charles Coody');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type of artworks that were nominated for a festival chaired by 'Charles Coody'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Festival_Name = 'Panasonic Awards';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and type of artworks that were nominated for the 'Flower Awards' but were not nominated for the 'Panasonic Awards'.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.Result = 'Won' THEN 1 END) AS Wins, COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.Result = 'Nominated' THEN 1 END) AS Nominations FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each artwork type along with the total count of its wins and nominations.

sql: SELECT T3.Chair_Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'I Know' AND T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the festival chair where the artwork 'I Know' won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE T1.Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of artworks that are either of the 'Program Music/Variety Show' type or have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Festival_ID IN (SELECT T2.Festival_ID FROM nomination AS T2 JOIN artwork AS T3 ON T2.Artwork_ID = T3.Artwork_ID WHERE T3.Type = 'Presenter Music/Variety Show');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the festival where an artwork of the type 'Presenter Music/Variety Show' was nominated.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, T1.Year, T2.Nomination_Count FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Festival_ID, COUNT(*) as Nomination_Count FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID ORDER BY T2.Nomination_Count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival's name, year, and its total number of nominations, ordered from the festivals with the most nominations to the least.

sql: SELECT T3.Festival_Name, COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID GROUP BY T3.Festival_Name HAVING AVG(T3.Num_of_Audience) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing festivals with an average audience greater than 150, along with the total count of artworks nominated for each festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' AND T2.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and type for the artwork that won an award at the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate;

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, Date FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate = 0.25;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Support_rate FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Candidate_ID) FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Sex = 'M';

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll');

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Date = 'Feb 12–15, 2007' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Date = 'Feb 13–14, 2007';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of candidates who participated in the 'Feb 12–15, 2007' event but did not participate in the 'Feb 13–14, 2007' event.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' UNION SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Support_rate > 0.2;

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source ORDER BY AVG(Support_rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single poll source that has the highest average candidate support rate across all its polls.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Date_of_Birth = (SELECT MAX(T2.Date_of_Birth) FROM people AS T2 JOIN candidate AS T3 ON T2.People_ID = T3.People_ID);

sql: SELECT T1.Poll_Source, T3.Name, T1.Support_rate FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.People_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(T2.Support_rate) FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.Poll_Source = T1.Poll_Source);

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Weight FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Weight > (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people);

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Support_rate > T1.Oppose_rate AND T1.Unsure_rate < (SELECT AVG(Unsure_rate) FROM candidate);

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY (T1.Support_rate - T1.Oppose_rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
