sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY Allergy ORDER BY COUNT(StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most common allergy among students, determined by the highest count of affected individuals.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY COUNT(T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single academic major that has the highest number of students with reported allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Cat' AND T1.city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, and age of a student from the city with code 'BAL' who is allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING count(*) = (SELECT max(C) FROM (SELECT count(T3.Allergy) AS C FROM Has_Allergy AS T3 GROUP BY T3.StuID));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of the student(s) with the highest number of recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Shellfish';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of students who either live in the city with code 'BAL' or have a shellfish allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.Sex = 'F' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all female students who have allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have a known allergy, but are not allergic to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 28 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have a food-related allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T1.Age > 18 AND T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of students over the age of 18 who have a recorded food allergy.

sql: SELECT T3.AllergyType, count(T1.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy GROUP BY T3.AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each allergy type along with the total count of students diagnosed with that allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T1.StuID = T2.StuID AND T2.Allergy = 'Tree Pollen');
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are recorded as having a 'Tree Pollen' allergy.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_description, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all available products classified as 'Cutlery', showing each item's name, description, and daily hire cost.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers ORDER BY date_became_customer DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer's first name, last name, and the date they joined, ordered from the most recent customer to the oldest.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id, amount_payable FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the booking ID, customer ID, and payable amount for all bookings with a 'Confirmed' status.

sql: SELECT * FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the complete records for all payments that were made in cash.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Products_Booked WHERE returned_late_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of products that were returned late.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's first and last name, along with a count of the total number of bookings they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.daily_hire_cost, T2.booked_count FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.returned_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the product name, daily hire cost, and the quantity booked for each completed hire transaction where the product has been returned.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T2.booking_status_code, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.first_name = 'Rosalee';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the booking status and product name for all items booked by the customer named 'Rosalee'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount_payable) FROM Bookings GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(booking_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each customer ID alongside the total payable amount summed across all of their bookings.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE daily_hire_cost > (SELECT AVG(daily_hire_cost) FROM Products_for_Hire);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the product name and daily hire cost for all products with a hire cost above the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.good_or_bad_customer, SUM(T2.amount_payable) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' GROUP BY T1.good_or_bad_customer;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total amount payable from all bookings made by customers classified as 'good'.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_status_code, AVG(T2.amount_paid) FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE T1.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' GROUP BY T1.booking_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average amount paid across all bookings that have a 'Confirmed' status.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T1.date_became_customer FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T2.returned_damaged_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first name, last name, and sign-up date for all customers who have returned a damaged booking.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_type_code FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products_Booked AS T2 WHERE T2.product_id = T1.product_id AND T2.returned_late_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and type code for each product that has been returned late on at least one occasion.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Payments AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T3.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card' AND T1.good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers classified as 'good' who have made a payment using a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.TotalBookedAmount FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN (SELECT product_id, SUM(booked_amount) AS TotalBookedAmount FROM Products_Booked GROUP BY product_id) AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id ORDER BY T2.TotalBookedAmount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product name alongside its total booked amount, ordered from the highest total amount to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.amount_payable, T2.coupon_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id JOIN Bookings AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.amount_payable < T2.coupon_amount;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows identifying customers whose booking cost was less than the value of their assigned discount coupon, showing each customer's name, the booking amount, and the coupon's value.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.booking_id, T1.amount_payable FROM Bookings AS T1 WHERE T1.amount_payable > (SELECT AVG(T2.amount_payable) FROM Bookings AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying individual bookings where the amount paid was greater than that customer's own average booking amount, listing the customer ID, booking ID, and the specific amount paid.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer ID and first name for customers who are designated as 'good' and also have at least one 'Confirmed' booking.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id EXCEPT SELECT customer_id, first_name FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer ID and first name for customers who have made a booking but are not classified as 'good' customers.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT T1.product_id FROM Products_Booked AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Provisional');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a product that is not currently part of any booking with a 'Provisional' status.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.booked_count) AS Total_Booked FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING SUM(T2.booked_amount) > 200;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing products that have generated over 200 in total booking revenue, showing each product's name and the total number of times it has been booked.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.product_id) FROM Bookings AS B JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON B.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE B.customer_id = T1.customer_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have booked more than one unique product.

sql: SELECT 'Customer' AS Category, first_name FROM Customers WHERE gender_mf = '0' UNION SELECT 'Product' AS Category, product_name FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows combining two distinct lists: customers of a specific gender and all cutlery products. A 'Category' column distinguishes between the two groups, with the second column showing the corresponding customer or product name.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T2.booking_id, T2.amount_of_discount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Payments AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T3.amount_paid_in_full_yn = '0' AND T2.amount_of_discount > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the customer ID, first name, booking ID, and discount amount for customers who received a discount but have not yet fully paid for their booking.

sql: SELECT ArtistId, Name FROM Artist WHERE Name = 'AC/DC';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the artist ID and name for the artist 'AC/DC'.

sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, Address FROM Customer WHERE City = 'Stuttgart';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first name, last name, and address for all customers located in the city of Stuttgart.

sql: SELECT Name, Composer, UnitPrice FROM Track WHERE UnitPrice = 0.99;
result_summary: Returns 3290 rows listing the name, composer, and unit price for all music tracks priced at $0.99.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName, Title, HireDate FROM Employee ORDER BY HireDate DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each employee's last name, first name, job title, and hire date, ordered from the most recent hire to the oldest.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Genre ORDER BY Name;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing the name of each available music genre, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Composer, T2.Title FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.AlbumId = T2.AlbumId WHERE T2.Title = 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and composer for each track on the album 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.TrackId) FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows, listing each music genre and providing a total count of the number of tracks available within that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows, with each row providing a customer's first and last name alongside their total spending, calculated by summing all of their invoice totals.

sql: SELECT T3.Name AS TrackName, T4.Name AS MediaTypeName FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN MediaType AS T4 ON T3.MediaTypeId = T4.MediaTypeId WHERE T1.Name = 'Aerosmith';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and media type for all tracks by the artist 'Aerosmith'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.AlbumId) AS NumberOfAlbums FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY NumberOfAlbums DESC;
result_summary: Returns 204 rows listing each artist and the total count of their albums, ordered from the artist with the most albums to the one with the fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T2.FirstName AS SupportRepFirstName, T2.LastName AS SupportRepLastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.SupportRepId = T2.EmployeeId WHERE T2.FirstName = 'Jane';
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the full names of all customers assigned to the support representative named 'Jane', along with the representative's full name for each customer.

sql: SELECT T1.InvoiceId, T2.TrackId, T2.UnitPrice, T2.Quantity FROM Invoice AS T1 JOIN InvoiceLine AS T2 ON T1.InvoiceId = T2.InvoiceId WHERE T1.CustomerId = 2;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows listing the invoice ID, track ID, unit price, and quantity for all items purchased by the customer with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of the top 5 customers by their total spending, ordered from highest to lowest amount spent.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM Playlist AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.PlaylistId = T2.PlaylistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Genre AS T4 ON T3.GenreId = T4.GenreId WHERE T1.Name = 'Music' AND T4.Name = 'Rock';
result_summary: Returns 2594 rows listing the names of all 'Rock' tracks found within the 'Music' playlist.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.AlbumId) AS AlbumCount FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId GROUP BY T1.ArtistId HAVING COUNT(T2.AlbumId) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 26 rows listing artists who have released more than two albums, along with a count of their total albums.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T1.Title, COUNT(T2.CustomerId) AS CustomersSupported FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeId = T2.SupportRepId GROUP BY T1.EmployeeId HAVING COUNT(T2.CustomerId) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first name, last name, and title for each employee who supports more than one customer, along with the total count of customers they support.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSales FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY TotalSales DESC;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the total sales for each country, ordered from the country with the highest sales to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, AVG(T2.Total) AS AvgCountryInvoice FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.Country HAVING AVG(T2.Total) > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM Invoice);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying countries where the average customer invoice total is greater than the overall average invoice total across all countries.

sql: SELECT TrackId, Name, Composer FROM Track WHERE TrackId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine);
result_summary: Returns 1519 rows listing the track ID, name, and composer for all tracks that have never been sold or included on an invoice.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.TrackId) AS NumberOfTracks FROM Genre AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY NumberOfTracks DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single music genre with the most tracks, showing the genre's name and its total track count.

sql: SELECT T1.CustomerId, T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Genre AS T5 ON T4.GenreId = T5.GenreId WHERE T5.Name = 'Rock' INTERSECT SELECT T1.CustomerId, T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T2.InvoiceId = T3.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Genre AS T5 ON T4.GenreId = T5.GenreId WHERE T5.Name = 'Jazz';
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the ID, first name, and last name of customers who have purchased tracks from both the Rock and Jazz genres.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM Playlist AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.PlaylistId = T2.PlaylistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.TrackId = T3.TrackId WHERE T3.Composer = 'Angus Young, Malcolm Young, Brian Johnson';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of playlists that contain at least one track composed by 'Angus Young, Malcolm Young, Brian Johnson'.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T3.Total) AS TotalSales FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeId = T2.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice AS T3 ON T2.CustomerId = T3.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.EmployeeId ORDER BY TotalSales DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-performing sales support representative by name, along with the total sales from the customers they manage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN MediaType AS T4 ON T3.MediaTypeId = T4.MediaTypeId WHERE T4.Name = 'MPEG audio file' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN MediaType AS T4 ON T3.MediaTypeId = T4.MediaTypeId WHERE T4.Name = 'Protected AAC audio file';
result_summary: Returns 110 rows listing the names of artists who have tracks in the 'MPEG audio file' format but do not have any tracks in the 'Protected AAC audio file' format.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, MIN(T2.InvoiceDate) AS FirstInvoiceDate, MAX(T2.InvoiceDate) AS LastInvoiceDate FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows listing each customer by their first and last name, along with the dates of their first and most recent invoices.

sql: SELECT name, iata, icao FROM airlines WHERE country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 1099 rows listing the name, IATA, and ICAO codes for all airlines based in the United States.

sql: SELECT name, iata, country FROM airports WHERE city = 'Goroka';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, IATA code, and country for the airport located in the city of Goroka.

sql: SELECT src_ap, dst_ap, airline FROM routes WHERE airline = '2B';
result_summary: Returns 40 rows listing the source airport, destination airport, and airline code for all routes operated by the airline with code '2B'.

sql: SELECT name, city, elevation FROM airports WHERE elevation > 5000;
result_summary: Returns 279 rows listing the name, city, and elevation for all airports located at an elevation above 5000 feet.

sql: SELECT name, callsign, country FROM airlines WHERE active = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 1255 rows listing the name, callsign, and country for all airlines that are currently marked as active.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.rid) FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 547 rows listing each airline by name, along with a count of the total number of routes it operates.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM airports AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.apid = T2.src_apid;
result_summary: Returns 3158 rows listing the unique names of airports that serve as the origin for at least one flight route.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.country) FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T2.dst_apid = T3.apid GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 546 rows listing each airline name along with the total count of distinct countries it flies to as a destination.

sql: SELECT T1.airline, T2.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid WHERE T2.country = 'Papua New Guinea';
result_summary: Returns 161 rows listing the airline and source airport name for all routes that originate from airports in Papua New Guinea.

sql: SELECT T2.country, COUNT(T1.rid) FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid GROUP BY T2.country;
result_summary: Returns 224 rows listing each country along with the total count of flight routes that originate from airports within that country.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airlines AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid WHERE T1.dst_ap = 'KZ';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the unique names of all airlines that fly routes to the destination airport 'KZ'.

sql: SELECT src_apid FROM routes INTERSECT SELECT dst_apid FROM routes;
result_summary: Returns 3306 rows, providing a list of airport IDs that function as both a departure (source) and arrival (destination) airport across all recorded routes.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.rid) FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.rid) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 522 rows listing each airline that operates more than two routes, along with the total count of its routes.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.city, T1.country FROM airports AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.apid = T2.dst_apid GROUP BY T1.apid HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.alid) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1986 rows listing the name, city, and country of destination airports that are served by two or more distinct airlines.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.rid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the airline that operates the most routes.

sql: SELECT T1.airline, T2.name, T3.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.dst_apid = T3.apid WHERE T2.country = T3.country;
result_summary: Returns 31845 rows detailing domestic flights, listing the operating airline along with the source and destination airport names for each route.

sql: SELECT name, city, country, elevation FROM airports WHERE elevation > (SELECT AVG(elevation) FROM airports);
result_summary: Returns 2023 rows listing the name, city, country, and elevation for all airports located at a higher-than-average elevation.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T2.src_apid = T3.apid WHERE T3.country = 'Papua New Guinea' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.alid = T2.alid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T2.dst_apid = T3.apid WHERE T3.country = 'Papua New Guinea';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT country, COUNT(apid) FROM airports GROUP BY country ORDER BY COUNT(apid) DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 countries with the most airports, ordered from the highest airport count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT src_ap, dst_ap, airline FROM routes WHERE alid IN (SELECT alid FROM airlines WHERE active = 'N');
result_summary: Returns 657 rows listing the source airport, destination airport, and airline for all routes operated by airlines marked as inactive.

sql: SELECT T1.src_ap, T1.dst_ap, T2.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.dst_apid = T2.apid WHERE T2.icao LIKE 'AY%';
result_summary: Returns 158 rows listing flight routes destined for airports in Papua New Guinea (where the ICAO code starts with 'AY'), showing the source and destination airport codes along with the full name of the destination airport.

sql: SELECT country, AVG(elevation) FROM airports GROUP BY country HAVING AVG(elevation) > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows listing countries where the average airport elevation is greater than 2000, along with the calculated average elevation for each country.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM routes AS T2 JOIN airports AS T3 ON T2.dst_apid = T3.apid WHERE T2.alid = T1.alid AND T3.name LIKE '%Airport%');
result_summary: Returns 545 rows listing the names of all airlines that have at least one flight route to a destination airport with the word "Airport" in its name.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.airline, T2.airline FROM routes AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.src_apid WHERE T1.alid < T2.alid;
result_summary: Returns 29898 rows listing unique pairs of airlines that operate flights from the same departure airport.

sql: SELECT T4.name, T2.name, T3.name FROM routes AS T1 JOIN airports AS T2 ON T1.src_apid = T2.apid JOIN airports AS T3 ON T1.dst_apid = T3.apid JOIN airlines AS T4 ON T1.alid = T4.alid;
result_summary: Returns 65,612 rows listing all available flight routes, detailing the operating airline's name along with the source and destination airport names for each route.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM editor ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and age of each editor, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT Theme, Sales FROM journal WHERE Sales > 994;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the theme and sales figures for journals with high sales (exceeding 994).

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Work_Type FROM journal_committee;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique types of work performed by the journal committee.

sql: SELECT Name FROM editor WHERE Age = 34.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any editor who is 34 years old.

sql: SELECT Theme FROM journal WHERE Journal_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the theme for the specific journal with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Work_Type FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each editor's name and their corresponding work type on the journal committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T2.Work_Type FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows that pair the theme of a journal with the type of work (e.g., Photo, Article) performed by the associated journal committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each editor's name along with a count of the journal committees they are a member of.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of editors from the journal committee who are responsible for photo-related work.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an editor who serves on more than one journal committee.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T1.Sales FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID JOIN editor AS T3 ON T2.Editor_ID = T3.Editor_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Kamila Porczyk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the theme and sales figures for the journal associated with the editor named 'Kamila Porczyk'.

sql: SELECT T2.Work_Type, AVG(T1.Sales) FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T2.Work_Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each work type from the journal committee alongside the average sales for journals associated with that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID WHERE T3.Sales > (SELECT AVG(Sales) FROM journal);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of editors who serve on committees for journals with sales greater than the average sales across all journals.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo' ORDER BY T1.Age DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and age of the single oldest editor assigned to the 'Photo' work type.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Journal_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Editor_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Article';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an editor who has worked on both 'Photo' and 'Article' work types.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Journal_ID = (SELECT Journal_ID FROM journal ORDER BY Sales DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the editor who serves on the committee for the top-selling journal.

sql: SELECT Name FROM editor WHERE Editor_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Editor_ID FROM journal_committee AS T1 JOIN journal AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID WHERE T2.Theme = 'Miami Dolphins');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of editors who are not on the committee for any journal with the 'Miami Dolphins' theme.

sql: SELECT Name FROM editor WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM editor) AND Editor_ID IN (SELECT Editor_ID FROM journal_committee);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of editors who are on the journal committee and are older than the average age of all editors.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo' UNION SELECT Name FROM editor WHERE Age > 34.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of editors who either work on photos for a journal committee or are older than 34.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Photo' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Article';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the names of editors who work on photos but do not work on articles.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Editor_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Journal_ID) AS c FROM journal_committee GROUP BY Editor_ID));
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the editor who serves on the highest number of journal committees.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme, T1.Sales FROM journal AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Journal_ID = T2.Journal_ID WHERE T2.Editor_ID IN (SELECT Editor_ID FROM editor WHERE Age < 35.0);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the theme and sales figures for journals that have an editor on their committee who is younger than 35.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Sales) FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID JOIN journal AS T3 ON T2.Journal_ID = T3.Journal_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Journal_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing editors who serve on more than one journal committee, along with the total sales from all journals they manage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID = T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Work_Type) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of editors who are associated with exactly one distinct type of work on the journal committee.

sql: SELECT StuID, Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for all students located in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'dog';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all available information (Pet ID, type, age, and weight) for every pet identified as a 'dog'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing each student's first name, last name, and age, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT PetType, weight FROM Pets WHERE pet_age > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the pet type and weight for all pets older than one year.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of all female students.

sql: SELECT PetType, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfPets FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each pet type along with the total count of pets for that type.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) AS AverageAge FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major alongside the average age of students in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.PetType, T3.pet_age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing students by first and last name, along with the type and age of each pet they own.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique first and last names of students who own a pet older than two years.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, COUNT(T2.PetID) AS PetCount FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.PetID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the student ID and their total number of pets for students who own more than one pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, SUM(T3.weight) AS TotalPetWeight FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each student's first and last name along with the total combined weight of all their pets.

sql: SELECT PetType FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType ORDER BY AVG(weight) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single pet type that has the highest average weight.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight > (SELECT AVG(weight) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique first and last names of students who own pets that are heavier than the average pet weight.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'dog' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType = 'cat';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own a dog but do not own a cat.

sql: SELECT T3.* FROM Pets AS T3 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T3.PetID = T2.PetID JOIN Student AS T1 ON T2.StuID = T1.StuID WHERE T1.Age > (SELECT Age FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all details for pets that are owned by students older than 'Linda Smith'.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, AVG(T3.pet_age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city code along with the average age of pets owned by students from that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.weight = (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student who owns the heaviest pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.PetID) AS NumberOfPets FROM Student AS T1 LEFT JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the total number of pets they own.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.PetType IN (SELECT PetType FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who own a pet of a type for which more than one animal exists in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.PetType) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who own pets of only a single distinct type (e.g., only dogs, but not a dog and a cat).

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY AVG(T3.pet_age) ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student major whose students, on average, own the youngest pets.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T1.city_code = 'HKG' AND T3.weight > 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a student from the city 'HKG' who owns a pet weighing more than 10.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Major = (SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith') AND Age > (SELECT Age FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of students who are in the same major as 'Linda Smith' but are older than her.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Pet AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Pets AS T3 ON T2.PetID = T3.PetID WHERE T3.pet_age = (SELECT MIN(pet_age) FROM Pets);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student who owns the youngest pet.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname AS Fname1, T1.LName AS LName1, T2.Fname AS Fname2, T2.LName AS LName2 FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 180 rows listing all unique pairs of students who share the same major, showing the first and last name for each student in the pair.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Major = 600;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students with a Major ID of 600.

sql: SELECT dorm_name, student_capacity FROM Dorm WHERE gender = 'X';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and student capacity for all dormitories designated with a gender of 'X'.

sql: SELECT amenity_name FROM Dorm_amenity ORDER BY amenity_name;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of all available dorm amenities, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students located in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT T2.dorm_name, COUNT(T1.stuid) FROM Lives_in AS T1 JOIN Dorm AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T2.dorm_name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each dormitory by name and the total number of students residing in it.

sql: SELECT T2.amenity_name FROM Has_amenity AS T1 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T2 ON T1.amenid = T2.amenid WHERE T1.dormid = 109;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all amenities available in the specific dorm identified by ID 109.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major and the average age of students within that major.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name, COUNT(T2.amenid) FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN Has_amenity AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid WHERE T1.dormid=109 GROUP BY T1.dorm_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing the total count of amenities for the specific dorm with an ID of 109, which is 'Anonymous Donor Hall'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.room_number FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows listing the first name, last name, and assigned room number for each student found in the housing records.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid WHERE T3.dorm_name = 'Smith Hall';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of all students who reside in the 'Smith Hall' dorm.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.dorm_name FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid;
result_summary: Returns 31 rows that list each student by their first and last name, along with the name of the dormitory they live in.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major WHERE T2.Fname = 'Linda' AND T1.StuID <> T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of all students who share the same major as the student named 'Linda'.

sql: SELECT T2.dorm_name, COUNT(T1.stuid) AS num_students FROM Lives_in AS T1 JOIN Dorm AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T2.dorm_name ORDER BY num_students DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most populated dorm by name, along with the total count of students living there.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name, T1.student_capacity FROM Dorm AS T1 WHERE T1.student_capacity > (SELECT AVG(student_capacity) FROM Dorm);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing dormitories with an above-average student capacity, showing each dorm's name and its specific capacity.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID NOT IN (SELECT T2.stuid FROM Lives_in AS T2 JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid WHERE T3.dorm_name = 'Smith Hall');
result_summary: Returns 28 rows containing the first and last names of all students who do not live in 'Smith Hall'.

sql: SELECT T3.dorm_name FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid GROUP BY T3.dorm_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.city_code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of dorms that house students from more than one distinct city.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid WHERE T2.dormid IN (SELECT T3.dormid FROM Has_amenity AS T3 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T4 ON T3.amenid = T4.amenid WHERE T4.amenity_name = 'TV Lounge');
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the first and last names of all students who live in a dormitory that has a 'TV Lounge' amenity.

sql: SELECT T1.dormid FROM Has_amenity AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid GROUP BY T1.dormid HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.amenid) > 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT T2.stuid) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the IDs of dorms that are occupied by at least one student and have more than two distinct amenities.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Major = 600 UNION SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first and last names of all unique students who are either in major 600 or are from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Lives_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Dorm AS T3 ON T2.dormid = T3.dormid WHERE T3.dorm_name = 'Smith Hall' INTERSECT SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of all female students who live in 'Smith Hall'.

sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM Dorm AS T1 JOIN (SELECT dormid, COUNT(*) AS num_residents FROM Lives_in GROUP BY dormid) AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid WHERE T1.student_capacity > T2.num_residents;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of dorms that have available space, where the dorm's total capacity is greater than its current number of residents.

sql: SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major of the student with the minimum age in the table.

sql: SELECT T1.dormid FROM Has_amenity AS T1 JOIN Dorm_amenity AS T2 ON T1.amenid = T2.amenid WHERE T2.amenity_name IN ('TV Lounge', 'Study Room') GROUP BY T1.dormid HAVING COUNT(T1.amenid) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the IDs for dormitories that contain both a 'TV Lounge' and a 'Study Room'.

sql: SELECT name, Trade_Name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the generic name and trade name for all medicines that have been approved by the FDA.

sql: SELECT name, Product FROM enzyme WHERE Location = 'Cytosol';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and resulting product for all enzymes found in the Cytosol.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT interaction_type FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique types of interaction recorded between medicines and enzymes.

sql: SELECT name, Chromosome FROM enzyme ORDER BY name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each enzyme's name and its corresponding chromosome location, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medicine;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of medicines recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T2 ON T3.enzyme_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the name of each medicine alongside the name of an enzyme with which it interacts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.medicine_id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each enzyme by name along with a count of the total number of medicines it interacts with.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.enzyme_id) FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each medicine by name and the total count of enzymes it interacts with.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'ALA synthase';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of medicines that interact with the enzyme 'ALA synthase'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id WHERE T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of enzymes that are classified as having an 'inhibitor' interaction type with a medicine.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing medicines that are not FDA-approved, along with the names of the enzymes they are known to interact with.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Porphyria = 'acute intermittent porphyria';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of medicines that interact with enzymes associated with 'acute intermittent porphyria'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING COUNT(T2.medicine_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of enzymes that interact with more than one medicine.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.medicine_id FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Location = 'Mitochondrion');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of medicines that interact with enzymes located in the 'Mitochondrion'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Trade_Name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 WHERE T2.medicine_id = T1.id AND T2.interaction_type = 'inhibitor');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and trade name for all medicines that are identified as inhibitors in enzyme interactions.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' INTERSECT SELECT T3.name FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 JOIN medicine AS T3 ON T1.medicine_id = T3.id WHERE T1.interaction_type = 'inhibitor';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of medicines that are both FDA approved and are also known to be enzyme inhibitors.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T3.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of enzymes that do not interact with any non-FDA-approved medicine.

sql: SELECT name FROM medicine WHERE FDA_approved = 'No' UNION SELECT name FROM enzyme WHERE Location = 'Mitochondrion';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the combined, unique names of medicines that are not FDA-approved and enzymes located in the 'Mitochondrion'.

sql: SELECT AVG(interaction_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.medicine_id) AS interaction_count FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T1 GROUP BY T1.enzyme_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average number of medicine interactions per enzyme.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id WHERE T2.enzyme_id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM enzyme);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all medicines that interact with the enzyme having the smallest ID.

sql: SELECT T2.interaction_type FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'No' AND T3.OMIM > 125270;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the interaction types between non-FDA-approved medicines and enzymes that have an OMIM ID greater than 125270.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 WHERE T2.enzyme_id = T1.id) AS interaction_count FROM enzyme AS T1 ORDER BY interaction_count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each enzyme and the total count of its known medicine interactions, ordered from the enzyme with the most interactions to the one with the least.

sql: SELECT T3.Porphyria, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM medicine AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.medicine_id JOIN enzyme AS T3 ON T2.enzyme_id = T3.id WHERE T1.FDA_approved = 'Yes' GROUP BY T3.Porphyria;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, providing a count of unique, FDA-approved medicines for each type of porphyria.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.medicine_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the enzyme that interacts with the most medicines.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, count(T2.medicine_id) FROM enzyme AS T1 JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.enzyme_id GROUP BY T1.Location HAVING count(T2.medicine_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each cellular location where enzymes are found, along with the total count of medicine-enzyme interactions that occur in each location.

sql: SELECT name, distance FROM aircraft ORDER BY distance DESC;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each aircraft's name and its maximum flight distance, ordered from the longest to shortest distance.

sql: SELECT flno, origin, destination, price FROM flight WHERE origin = 'Los Angeles';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all flights departing from 'Los Angeles', providing the flight number, origin, destination, and price for each.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employee WHERE salary > 150000;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names and salaries of all employees who earn more than $150,000.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT destination FROM flight;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, providing a unique list of all possible flight destinations.

sql: SELECT eid, name FROM employee WHERE name LIKE 'J%';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the employee ID and name for all employees whose name begins with the letter 'J'.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T1.origin, T1.destination, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each flight's number, origin, and destination, along with the name of the specific aircraft used for that flight.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.flno) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each aircraft model's name and the total number of flights operated by that model.

sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employee WHERE eid IN (SELECT eid FROM certificate WHERE aid = 2);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and salary for all employees who hold the certificate associated with agency ID 2.

sql: SELECT destination, AVG(price) FROM flight GROUP BY destination;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each destination along with the average price for flights to that destination.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.aid) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing each employee by name along with the total number of certificates they hold.

sql: SELECT eid FROM certificate GROUP BY eid HAVING COUNT(aid) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the employee IDs for employees who have been awarded more than one certificate.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T1.price, T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.price > 200;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing flights with a price greater than 200, showing each flight's number, its price, and the name of the aircraft used.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T4.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid JOIN certificate AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid JOIN employee AS T4 ON T3.eid = T4.eid WHERE T1.origin = 'Los Angeles' AND T1.destination = 'Dallas';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique names of employees who are certified to work on aircraft that have been used for flights from Los Angeles to Dallas.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.distance FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.distance > 2000 AND T2.distance > (SELECT AVG(distance) FROM aircraft);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and maximum range of aircraft that have a range greater than the fleet-wide average and were used for long-haul flights (over 2000 miles).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 737-800' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 747-400';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of employees who are certified to fly the 'Boeing 737-800' but are not certified for the 'Boeing 747-400'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid WHERE T1.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee) GROUP BY T1.name, T1.salary HAVING count(T2.aid) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names and salaries of employees who earn above the company average salary and hold more than one certificate.

sql: SELECT T1.flno, T1.distance AS flight_distance, T2.name, T2.distance AS aircraft_max_distance FROM flight AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid WHERE T1.distance > T2.distance;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying flights where the scheduled distance exceeded the assigned aircraft's maximum flight range, showing the flight number, its distance, the aircraft's name, and the aircraft's maximum range.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM certificate AS T2 JOIN flight AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T2.eid = T1.eid AND T3.destination = 'Chicago');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of employees who are certified to operate aircraft used on flights to Chicago.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.distance FROM aircraft AS T1 WHERE T1.aid IN (SELECT aid FROM flight GROUP BY aid HAVING COUNT(flno) >= 1) AND T1.name LIKE '%Boeing%';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and travel distance for all Boeing aircraft that have at least one recorded flight.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 747-400' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Airbus A340-300';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of employees who are certified to operate either a 'Boeing 747-400' or an 'Airbus A340-300' aircraft.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.avg_price FROM aircraft AS T2 JOIN (SELECT aid, AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM flight GROUP BY aid) AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.avg_price > 200;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of aircraft whose average flight price is greater than 200, along with that calculated average price.

sql: SELECT T3.flno, T3.origin, T3.destination FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN flight AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T1.name = 'John Williams';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the flight number, origin, and destination for flights operated by aircraft that the employee 'John Williams' is certified to fly.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid = T2.eid JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.aid = T3.aid WHERE T3.name = 'Boeing 737-800' ORDER BY T1.salary DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and salary of the highest-paid employee certified to work on the 'Boeing 737-800' aircraft.

sql: SELECT name, salary, CASE WHEN salary > 150000 THEN 'High Earner' WHEN salary > 120000 THEN 'Mid Earner' ELSE 'Standard' END AS salary_category FROM employee WHERE eid IN (SELECT eid FROM certificate);
result_summary: Returns 20 rows for employees who have earned a certificate, listing their name, salary, and a corresponding salary category ('High Earner', 'Mid Earner', or 'Standard').

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.eid, T2.eid FROM certificate AS T1 JOIN certificate AS T2 ON T1.aid = T2.aid AND T1.eid < T2.eid;
result_summary: Returns 79 rows of unique pairs of employee IDs (`eid`) who have been awarded the same certificate.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details, T2.event_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(Customer_Event_ID) AS event_count FROM Customer_Events GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.organization_id, T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Things AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T2.Type_of_Thing_Code = 'Electronics' AND T2.service_type_code = 'Check';

sql: SELECT T2.organization_details, T1.Type_of_Thing_Code FROM Things AS T1 JOIN Organizations AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id JOIN Timed_Status_of_Things AS T3 ON T1.thing_id = T3.thing_id WHERE T3.Status_of_Thing_Code = 'Open';

sql: SELECT T1.organization_details FROM Organizations AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.organization_id = T2.organization_id WHERE T2.service_id = (SELECT service_id FROM Residents_Services GROUP BY service_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details of the organization that provides the single most popular service, as determined by the service most frequently used by residents.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_details, T3.property_address, T5.organization_details FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Events AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Properties AS T3 ON T2.property_id = T3.property_id JOIN Things AS T4 ON T2.thing_id = T4.thing_id JOIN Organizations AS T5 ON T4.organization_id = T5.organization_id WHERE T2.resident_id = 10;

sql: SELECT name, origin, destination FROM train;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing all available trains, showing each train's name along with its origin and destination.

sql: SELECT network_name, local_authority FROM station;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each station's network name and its associated local authority.

sql: SELECT name, time FROM train WHERE interval = 'Daily';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and scheduled time for all trains that run on a daily interval.

sql: SELECT high_temperature, low_temperature, precipitation FROM weekly_weather WHERE station_id = 1 AND day_of_week = 'Monday';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the high temperature, low temperature, and precipitation recorded on Monday for weather station ID 1.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT local_authority FROM station WHERE local_authority IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique local authority that has at least one station.

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(T2.station_id) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each train by name along with a count of the number of stations on its route.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, avg(T2.high_temperature), avg(T2.low_temperature) FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.network_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each station network name alongside the average weekly high and low temperatures for that network.

sql: SELECT T3.network_name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.name = 'Ananthapuri Express';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the station network names for all stops on the route of the 'Ananthapuri Express' train.

sql: SELECT T1.network_name, T2.high_temperature, T2.precipitation FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.local_authority = 'Chiltern' AND T2.day_of_week = 'Wednesday';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the network name, high temperature, and precipitation for weather stations located in the 'Chiltern' local authority on Wednesdays.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T3.services = 'London Overground and London Midland';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique name of the train that travels through stations serviced by both 'London Overground and London Midland'.

sql: SELECT day_of_week, max(high_temperature) FROM weekly_weather GROUP BY day_of_week;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each day of the week along with the maximum high temperature recorded for that day.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T1.origin = 'Trivandrum' AND T3.local_authority = 'Brentwood';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of a train that originates in 'Trivandrum' and also passes through a station within the 'Brentwood' local authority.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING count(T2.station_id) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of trains that have more than two stations on their route.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.station_id = (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE day_of_week = 'Monday' ORDER BY precipitation DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all trains that have a route through the station that recorded the highest precipitation on a Monday.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.train_id FROM route AS T2 WHERE T2.station_id IN (SELECT station_id FROM weekly_weather WHERE high_temperature > 60 AND day_of_week = 'Tuesday'));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of trains that have routes passing through stations where the high temperature exceeded 60 degrees on a Tuesday.

sql: SELECT T1.local_authority, avg(T2.high_temperature) FROM station AS T1 JOIN weekly_weather AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.day_of_week = 'Wednesday' GROUP BY T1.local_authority HAVING avg(T2.high_temperature) > 55;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing local authorities where the average high temperature on Wednesdays exceeds 55 degrees, showing each authority and its corresponding average temperature.

sql: SELECT location, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT origin AS location FROM train UNION ALL SELECT destination AS location FROM train) AS T1 GROUP BY location ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each unique train station and its total traffic count (as either an origin or a destination), ordered from the most to the least frequented station.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM train AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM route AS T2 JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.id WHERE T2.train_id = T1.id AND T3.local_authority = 'Watford');

result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the network whose overall average wind speed is equal to the highest average wind speed recorded at any single station.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T4.name FROM route AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.station_id = T2.station_id AND T1.train_id < T2.train_id JOIN train AS T3 ON T1.train_id = T3.id JOIN train AS T4 ON T2.train_id = T4.id;

sql: SELECT T1.name, count(DISTINCT T2.station_id), avg(T3.high_temperature), avg(T3.low_temperature) FROM train AS T1 JOIN route AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id JOIN weekly_weather AS T3 ON T2.station_id = T3.station_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name;
