sql: SELECT Railway FROM railway WHERE Railway_ID NOT IN (SELECT Railway_ID FROM train WHERE Railway_ID IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of railways that do not have any trains assigned to them.

sql: SELECT Builder, COUNT(*) FROM railway GROUP BY Builder ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the builder who has constructed the most railways, along with the total count of railways they built.

sql: SELECT Name, Age, Country FROM manager WHERE Working_year_starts = (SELECT MIN(Working_year_starts) FROM manager);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, age, and country of the manager who has the earliest working start year on record.

sql: SELECT Railway, train_count FROM (SELECT T2.Railway, COUNT(T1.Train_ID) AS train_count FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID GROUP BY T2.Railway) AS T WHERE T.train_count > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each railway that operates more than one train, along with the corresponding total count of trains.

sql: SELECT T3.Railway FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID JOIN railway AS T3 ON T2.Railway_ID = T3.Railway_ID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM manager WHERE Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the railway managed by the oldest manager from the United States.

sql: SELECT T4.Name, T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID JOIN railway_manage AS T3 ON T2.Railway_ID = T3.Railway_ID JOIN manager AS T4 ON T3.Manager_ID = T4.Manager_ID WHERE T1.Arrival > '09:00';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the manager's name and the corresponding train's name for all trains that arrive after 9:00 AM.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID GROUP BY T1.Manager_ID HAVING count(T2.Railway_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway_ID, T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID INTERSECT SELECT T1.Railway_ID, T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and name of railways that are both managed and have trains operating on them.

sql: SELECT T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Railway FROM railway AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the railway that is under management but currently has no trains assigned to it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID WHERE T2.Builder = 'GNR Doncaster' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID WHERE T2.Builder = 'MR Derby';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of trains that were built by either 'GNR Doncaster' or 'MR Derby'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM manager AS T1 JOIN railway_manage AS T2 ON T1.Manager_ID = T2.Manager_ID JOIN railway AS T3 ON T2.Railway_ID = T3.Railway_ID WHERE T3.Built = '1902';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Train_Num FROM train AS T1 JOIN railway AS T2 ON T1.Railway_ID = T2.Railway_ID JOIN railway_manage AS T3 ON T2.Railway_ID = T3.Railway_ID JOIN manager AS T4 ON T3.Manager_ID = T4.Manager_ID WHERE T4.Working_year_starts > 2005;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and number of the train managed by personnel who started working after 2005.

sql: SELECT name, Start_year FROM club WHERE Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and start year for any club located in the 'USA' region.

sql: SELECT name, Tries FROM player ORDER BY Tries DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each player's name and their total number of tries, ordered from the highest number of tries to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Competition_type, Country FROM competition WHERE Year = 2006.0 ORDER BY Country ASC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the competition type and host country for all competitions that took place in the year 2006, ordered alphabetically by country name.

sql: SELECT Position, Apps, Points FROM player WHERE name = 'Michael Platt';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the position, number of appearances (Apps), and points scored for the player named 'Michael Platt'.

sql: SELECT SUM(Gold) FROM club_rank;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total sum of gold medals won across all clubs.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.Player_ID) FROM club AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each club's name and the total number of players associated with that club.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Gold > 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the club that has won more than 10 gold medals.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T2.Points) FROM club AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each club's name along with the total points accumulated by its players.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.Rank FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID JOIN club_rank AS T3 ON T2.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each player's name, the name of the club they belong to, and the corresponding rank of that club.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM club_rank WHERE Rank = 1.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the club that holds the number one rank.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.Player_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of clubs that have more than one player.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Region = 'USA' AND T1.Position = 'Full Back';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any player whose position is 'Full Back' and who plays for a club based in the 'USA'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN competition_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID_1 OR T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID_2 JOIN competition AS T3 ON T2.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T3.Country = 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of all clubs that have participated in competitions held in Italy.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Tries FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Tries > (SELECT AVG(T2.Tries) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing players and their number of 'Tries', showing only those players who have scored more 'Tries' than the average for their respective club.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Club_ID IN (SELECT Club_ID FROM club_rank WHERE Gold >= 8);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of players who belong to clubs that have won at least 8 gold medals.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Gold > 7;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of clubs that have players but have won 7 or fewer gold medals.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 WHERE T1.Region = 'UK' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Total > 20;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of clubs that are either located in the 'UK' region or have a total rank score greater than 20.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Region = 'China' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN club_rank AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a club that is located in 'China' and also has a recorded rank.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.TotalPoints FROM club AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Club_ID, SUM(Points) AS TotalPoints FROM player GROUP BY Club_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID ORDER BY T2.TotalPoints DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the club with the highest total points, which is calculated by summing the points of all its players.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.c_id) FROM (SELECT Competition_ID, Club_ID_1 AS c_id FROM competition_result UNION SELECT Competition_ID, Club_ID_2 AS c_id FROM competition_result) AS T1 JOIN competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID GROUP BY T2.Country;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country along with the total count of unique clubs that have participated in competitions held within that country.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM player AS T1 JOIN competition_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID_1 OR T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID_2 JOIN competition AS T3 ON T2.Competition_ID = T3.Competition_ID WHERE T3.Year = 2006.0;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing the unique names of all players who participated in a competition during the year 2006.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN T1.Start_year < '2000' THEN 'Old Club' ELSE 'New Club' END AS Category, COUNT(T2.Player_ID) FROM club AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY Category;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows categorizing clubs as 'Old Club' (founded before 2000) or 'New Club' (founded in or after 2000) and provides a total count of players for each category.

sql: SELECT name FROM club WHERE Club_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Club_ID_1 FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Spain' UNION SELECT T1.Club_ID_2 FROM competition_result AS T1 JOIN competition AS T2 ON T1.Competition_ID = T2.Competition_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'Spain');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all clubs that have never participated in a competition held in Spain.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.name, T1.Points, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T1.Club_ID ORDER BY T1.Points DESC) FROM player AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each player's name, their club's name, and their total points, along with a rank for each player within their club based on who has the most points.

sql: WITH RatioCTE (Club_ID, Ratio) AS (SELECT Club_ID, CAST(Gold AS REAL) / Total FROM club_rank WHERE Total > 0) SELECT T1.name FROM club AS T1 JOIN RatioCTE AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID ORDER BY T2.Ratio DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the club with the highest ratio of gold medals to total medals.

sql: SELECT * FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'King';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing the full details for all hotel rooms that have a 'King' bed type.

sql: SELECT * FROM Reservations WHERE Room = 'RND';
result_summary: Returns 62 rows containing all reservation details, including guest information, stay dates, and rate, for bookings in the 'RND' room.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName, basePrice FROM Rooms WHERE maxOccupancy = 4;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the ID, name, and base price for all rooms that accommodate a maximum of four people.

sql: SELECT roomName, decor FROM Rooms ORDER BY roomName ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each room's name and its associated decor style, ordered alphabetically by room name.

sql: SELECT * FROM Reservations WHERE LastName = 'KLESS';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete reservation details for any customer with the last name 'KLESS'.

sql: SELECT decor, COUNT(RoomId) FROM Rooms GROUP BY decor;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each decor style along with the total number of rooms available for that style.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T2.LastName, T2.CheckIn FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room;
result_summary: Returns 600 rows listing every reservation, showing the guest's last name and check-in date for each booked room.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName FROM Rooms WHERE RoomId IN (SELECT DISTINCT Room FROM Reservations);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the ID and name for all rooms that have been reserved at least once.

sql: SELECT Code, Room, (Adults + Kids) AS TotalGuests FROM Reservations WHERE (Adults + Kids) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 384 rows for reservations with more than one guest, showing the reservation code, room code, and the total number of guests (calculated by adding adults and kids).

sql: SELECT T2.FirstName, T2.LastName, T2.CheckIn FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'modern';
result_summary: Returns 237 rows listing the first name, last name, and check-in date for all guests who have made reservations in rooms with a 'modern' decor.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, COUNT(T2.Code) AS NumberOfReservations FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each room's name along with the total count of reservations made for that room.

sql: SELECT T1.bedType, AVG(T2.Rate) AS AverageRate FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.bedType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bed type along with the average reservation rate for that type.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName FROM Rooms WHERE RoomId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Room FROM Reservations);

sql: SELECT T1.RoomId, T1.roomName, T1.maxOccupancy, T2.Code, T2.Adults, T2.Kids FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE (T2.Adults + T2.Kids) > T1.maxOccupancy;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName, SUM(Rate) AS TotalSpent FROM Reservations GROUP BY LastName, FirstName ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row for the customer with the highest total spending on reservations, listing their last name, first name, and the total amount spent.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, T1.basePrice, AVG(T2.Rate) AS AverageRate FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.RoomId, T1.roomName, T1.basePrice HAVING AVG(T2.Rate) < T1.basePrice;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, COUNT(T2.Code) AS ReservationCount FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName HAVING COUNT(T2.Code) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing rooms that have been booked more than once, along with the total count of reservations for each of those rooms.

sql: SELECT RoomId, roomName, decor FROM Rooms WHERE decor = 'modern' UNION SELECT RoomId, roomName, decor FROM Rooms WHERE basePrice = 175;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID, name, and decor for all rooms that either have a 'modern' decor or a base price of 175.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T2.LastName, T2.CheckIn FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = 'traditional' AND T1.beds > 1;
result_summary: Returns 186 rows listing the reservation code, guest's last name, and check-in date for all bookings in rooms that have a 'traditional' decor and more than one bed.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.decor) AS DistinctDecorCount FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.decor) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows identifying guests who have stayed in rooms with more than one decor style, showing each guest's name and the count of distinct decor styles they have experienced.

sql: SELECT T1.roomName, COUNT(T2.Code) FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room GROUP BY T1.roomName ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Code) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most frequently booked room and its total number of reservations.

sql: SELECT RoomId FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'King' INTERSECT SELECT Room FROM Reservations WHERE LastName = 'KLESS';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the specific room that has a 'King' bed and is also reserved by a guest with the last name 'KLESS'.

sql: SELECT RoomId FROM Rooms WHERE bedType = 'Queen' EXCEPT SELECT Room FROM Reservations;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T2.LastName, T1.roomName, T1.decor FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.decor = (SELECT decor FROM Rooms GROUP BY decor ORDER BY AVG(basePrice) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 245 rows listing guest reservations (code, last name) and room details (name, decor) for all bookings in rooms that feature the most expensive decor style, as determined by the highest average base price.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T2.Room, T2.LastName, T1.beds FROM Rooms AS T1 JOIN Reservations AS T2 ON T1.RoomId = T2.Room WHERE T1.beds = (SELECT MAX(beds) FROM Rooms);
result_summary: Returns 360 rows listing guest reservation details (code, room, and last name) for all bookings made in rooms that have the maximum available bed capacity.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and age of each visitor, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT Name, Open_Year FROM museum WHERE Open_Year = '2000';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and opening year of any museum that opened specifically in the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE Level_of_membership = 8;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the visitor who has a membership level of 8.

sql: SELECT Name, Num_of_Staff FROM museum WHERE Num_of_Staff > 25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and staff count for museums with more than 25 staff members.

sql: SELECT Num_of_Ticket, Total_spent FROM visit WHERE visitor_ID = 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows detailing the number of tickets purchased and the total amount spent for each visit made by the visitor with ID 5.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Num_of_Ticket) FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each museum's name alongside the total number of tickets recorded for all visits to that museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_Ticket > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of visitors who purchased more than 10 tickets for a single visit.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID JOIN visitor AS T3 ON T2.visitor_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Plaza Museum';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a visitor who has been to the 'Plaza Museum'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Museum_ID) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each visitor's name and the total count of museum visits they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Total_spent) FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID WHERE T1.Age = 35 GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(DISTINCT T2.visitor_ID) FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each museum by name along with the total count of its unique visitors.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Level_of_membership FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visitor AS T2 ON T1.Num_of_Staff > T2.Age;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing museum names and visitor membership levels for pairings where the number of museum staff is greater than the visitor's age.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum WHERE Num_of_Staff = (SELECT MAX(Num_of_Staff) FROM museum);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the museum with the largest number of staff.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Museum_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of visitors who have visited more than one distinct museum.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM visit AS T2 WHERE T2.Museum_ID = T1.Museum_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all museums that have never had a recorded visit.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM visitor WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM visitor);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of each visitor whose age is greater than the average age of all visitors.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID JOIN visitor AS T3 ON T2.visitor_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Plaza Museum' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID JOIN visitor AS T3 ON T2.visitor_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Capital Plaza Museum';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM museum AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T2.Total_spent > (SELECT AVG(Total_spent) FROM visit);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of museums that have had visits with spending greater than the overall average visitor spending.

sql: SELECT Name, Level_of_membership FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit WHERE Num_of_Ticket > 10);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and membership level of visitors who have purchased more than 10 tickets in a single visit.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM visitor AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM visit AS T2 JOIN museum AS T3 ON T2.Museum_ID = T3.Museum_ID WHERE T2.visitor_ID = T1.ID AND T3.Open_Year = '2012');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of visitors who have visited a museum that opened in the year 2012.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Num_of_Staff FROM museum AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Museum_ID, AVG(Num_of_Ticket) AS avg_tickets FROM visit GROUP BY Museum_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T2.avg_tickets > 5;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and number of staff for each museum that averages more than 5 tickets sold per visit.

sql: SELECT Name FROM museum WHERE Museum_ID IN (SELECT Museum_ID FROM visit GROUP BY Museum_ID HAVING SUM(Total_spent) > 100);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of museums where the total spending across all visits is greater than 100.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM visitor AS T1 JOIN visit AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.visitor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING MIN(T2.Num_of_Ticket) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any visitor who purchased more than 5 tickets on every single one of their visits.

sql: SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit WHERE Museum_ID = 1) UNION SELECT Name FROM visitor WHERE ID IN (SELECT visitor_ID FROM visit WHERE Museum_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all unique visitors who have visited either museum with ID 1 or museum with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.AvgSpent FROM museum AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Museum_ID, AVG(Total_spent) AS AvgSpent FROM visit GROUP BY Museum_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Museum_ID = T2.Museum_ID ORDER BY T2.AvgSpent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the museum with the highest average visitor spending, showing the museum's name and that average spend amount.

sql: SELECT * FROM browser;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing a complete list of all browsers, showing their ID, name, and market share.

sql: SELECT name, market_share FROM browser ORDER BY market_share DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing web browsers and their corresponding market share, ordered from the highest market share to the lowest.

sql: SELECT name, Operating_system FROM Web_client_accelerator WHERE Client = 'user';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and compatible operating systems for web client accelerators designed for end-users.

sql: SELECT name FROM Web_client_accelerator WHERE id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the web client accelerator with a specific ID of 1.

sql: SELECT compatible_since_year FROM accelerator_compatible_browser WHERE accelerator_id = 2 AND browser_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the year that a specific accelerator (ID 2) and a specific browser (ID 3) became compatible.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Operating_system FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.accelerator_id WHERE T2.browser_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and operating systems of web client accelerators that are compatible with a specific browser (browser ID 1).

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.market_share FROM browser AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.browser_id WHERE T2.accelerator_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and market share for all browsers that are compatible with a specific accelerator (ID 2).

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.browser_id) FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.accelerator_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each web client accelerator by name along with a count of its compatible browsers.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.accelerator_id) FROM browser AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.browser_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each web browser's name and the corresponding count of accelerators it is compatible with.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.compatible_since_year FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.accelerator_id JOIN browser AS T2 ON T2.id = T3.browser_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each web client accelerator, the browser it is compatible with, and the year that compatibility began.

sql: SELECT compatible_since_year FROM accelerator_compatible_browser GROUP BY compatible_since_year;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the unique years from which browsers became compatible with accelerators.

sql: SELECT AVG(market_share) FROM browser;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average market share calculated across all browsers.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM browser AS T1 WHERE T1.market_share > (SELECT AVG(T2.market_share) FROM browser AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the names of web browsers that have an above-average market share.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.accelerator_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of web client accelerators that are compatible with more than one browser.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM browser AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.browser_id FROM accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 WHERE T2.accelerator_id = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of browsers that are compatible with a specific accelerator (ID 1).

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Operating_system FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 WHERE T1.id = T2.accelerator_id AND T2.compatible_since_year = 1995);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and operating system of any web client accelerator that gained browser compatibility in the year 1995.

sql: SELECT compatible_since_year FROM accelerator_compatible_browser GROUP BY compatible_since_year HAVING COUNT(accelerator_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the year(s) in which more than one accelerator became compatible with a browser.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.accelerator_id JOIN browser AS T2 ON T2.id = T3.browser_id WHERE T1.name = 'CACHEbox';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of web browsers that are compatible with the 'CACHEbox' web client accelerator.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT accelerator_id FROM accelerator_compatible_browser WHERE browser_id = 1) INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT accelerator_id FROM accelerator_compatible_browser WHERE browser_id = 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of web client accelerators that are compatible with both browser ID 1 and browser ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM browser AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.browser_id WHERE T2.accelerator_id = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM browser AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.browser_id WHERE T2.compatible_since_year = 1996;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a browser that is compatible with accelerator ID 1, excluding any browsers that became compatible in the year 1996.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.accelerator_id WHERE T2.browser_id = (SELECT id FROM browser ORDER BY market_share DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of web client accelerators that are compatible with the browser having the highest market share.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.accelerator_id JOIN browser AS T2 ON T3.browser_id = T2.id WHERE T2.market_share < 10 AND T3.compatible_since_year > 1995;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing pairs of web client accelerators and their compatible browsers, specifically for browsers with less than 10% market share where compatibility was established after 1995.

sql: SELECT T1.Operating_system FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.accelerator_id GROUP BY T1.Operating_system HAVING avg(T2.compatible_since_year) < 1996;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the operating system for web accelerators whose compatible browsers have an average 'compatible since' year before 1996.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 WHERE T1.Operating_system = 'Windows' UNION SELECT T3.name FROM accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 JOIN Web_client_accelerator AS T3 ON T2.accelerator_id = T3.id WHERE T2.compatible_since_year = 1995;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of web client accelerators that either run on the Windows operating system or became compatible with a browser in 1995.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Web_client_accelerator AS T1 WHERE T1.id NOT IN (SELECT T2.accelerator_id FROM accelerator_compatible_browser AS T2 WHERE T2.browser_id = 3);
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the names of web client accelerators that are not compatible with the browser identified by ID 3.

sql: SELECT customer_id, customer_name, customer_details FROM Customers WHERE customer_name = 'George';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer ID, name, and details for the customer named 'George'.

sql: SELECT product_id, product_name FROM Products WHERE product_name = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product ID and name for all products with the exact name 'food'.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, date_order_placed FROM Orders WHERE order_status = 'Shipped';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all orders that have been shipped, showing the order ID, the associated customer ID, and the date the order was placed.

sql: SELECT * FROM Invoices WHERE invoice_number = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available information (invoice number, date, and details) for the invoice with an invoice number of 2.

sql: SELECT order_item_id, product_id, order_id FROM Order_Items WHERE order_item_status = 'Finish';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the item, product, and order IDs for all order items that have been marked with a 'Finish' status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_id, T2.date_order_placed FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_name = 'George';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all orders placed by the customer 'George', showing the customer's name, the order ID, and the date each order was placed.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, T1.order_item_status FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.order_id = 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product names and their corresponding item statuses for all items within a specific order (order ID 6).

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, count(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each customer by name and the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.shipment_id, T1.shipment_tracking_number, T2.invoice_date FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Invoices AS T2 ON T1.invoice_number = T2.invoice_number WHERE T2.invoice_number = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the shipment ID, tracking number, and invoice date for all shipments corresponding to invoice number 2.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T2.product_id, T2.order_item_status FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id WHERE T1.order_id = 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product ID and status for each individual item belonging to order number 6.

sql: SELECT T2.customer_name FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.order_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer who placed the order with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, count(T2.order_item_id) FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each order ID and the corresponding total count of items within that order.

sql: SELECT order_id, date_order_placed, order_status FROM Orders WHERE customer_id = (SELECT customer_id FROM Customers WHERE customer_name = 'George');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all orders placed by the customer named 'George', showing each order's ID, placement date, and current status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(T2.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have placed more than one order.

sql: SELECT T1.shipment_tracking_number, T1.shipment_date, count(T2.order_item_id) FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Shipment_Items AS T2 ON T1.shipment_id = T2.shipment_id GROUP BY T1.shipment_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, where each row provides the tracking number and date for a shipment, as well as the total number of items it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_id NOT IN (SELECT order_id FROM Shipments);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have placed orders that have not yet been shipped.

sql: SELECT T4.product_name FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Shipment_Items AS T2 ON T1.shipment_id = T2.shipment_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_item_id = T3.order_item_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.shipment_tracking_number = '0114';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the products included in the shipment with the tracking number '0114'.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.date_order_placed, count(T2.order_item_id) FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id WHERE T1.order_status = 'Shipped' GROUP BY T1.order_id HAVING count(T2.order_item_id) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T5.customer_name, T4.order_id, T3.product_name, T1.shipment_date FROM Shipments AS T1 JOIN Shipment_Items AS T2 ON T1.shipment_id = T2.shipment_id JOIN Products AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T3_1 ON T3.product_id = T3_1.product_id AND T2.order_item_id = T3_1.order_item_id JOIN Orders AS T4 ON T3_1.order_id = T4.order_id JOIN Customers AS T5 ON T4.customer_id = T5.customer_id WHERE T1.shipment_tracking_number = '3452';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_name = 'book' UNION SELECT T2.product_name FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.order_item_status = 'Finish';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with a unique list of product names, including all products from completed orders and the specific product 'book'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of customers who have purchased any product with the name 'food'.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id FROM Orders AS T1 WHERE T1.order_status = 'Shipped' INTERSECT SELECT T2.order_id FROM Shipments AS T2 WHERE T2.invoice_number = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT shipment_id FROM Shipments EXCEPT SELECT shipment_id FROM Shipment_Items WHERE order_item_id = 12;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the shipment IDs for all shipments that do not contain the specific order item with an ID of 12.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T2.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of products that have been sold in more than one distinct order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_id, T3.shipment_tracking_number FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Shipments AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T3.shipment_tracking_number IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the customer name, order ID, and shipment tracking number for every order that has been shipped.

sql: SELECT id, name FROM architect WHERE gender = 'male';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and name for all architects who are male.

sql: SELECT name, location FROM bridge WHERE location = 'Zion National Park , Utah , USA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and location of the bridge found in Zion National Park, Utah, USA.

sql: SELECT name, built_year FROM mill WHERE type = 'Grondzeiler';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and the year of construction for all mills of the 'Grondzeiler' type.

sql: SELECT name, nationality FROM architect ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and nationality of each architect, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT name, built_year FROM mill WHERE built_year > 1840;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and construction year for all mills built after 1840.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each architect's name alongside the name of a bridge they designed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.nationality, T2.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each mill's name along with the name and nationality of the corresponding architect who designed it.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each architect by name along with the total count of bridges they have designed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each architect by name along with the total number of mills they designed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.length_meters) FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each architect's name along with the average length of the bridges they designed.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.nationality FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id WHERE T2.name = 'Le Vieux Molen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and nationality of the architect who designed the mill named 'Le Vieux Molen'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id WHERE T2.built_year = 1840;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the architect and the name of the mill they designed for any mills that were built in the year 1840.

sql: SELECT name, nationality FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM bridge);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and nationality of architects who have designed at least one bridge.

sql: SELECT name, nationality FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM mill);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and nationality of architects who have designed at least one mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN mill AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of architects who have designed more than one mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T2.length_meters FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id WHERE T2.length_meters > (SELECT AVG(length_meters) FROM bridge);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the architect's name, the bridge's name, and its length for all bridges that are longer than the average length.

sql: SELECT name FROM architect WHERE nationality = 'American' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of architects who are either American or have designed a bridge.

sql: SELECT name FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM bridge) INTERSECT SELECT name FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM mill);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of architects who have designed both at least one bridge and at least one mill.

sql: SELECT name FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM bridge) EXCEPT SELECT name FROM architect WHERE id IN (SELECT architect_id FROM mill);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an architect who has designed a bridge but has not designed a mill.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.name FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id JOIN mill AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.architect_id;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing architects who have designed both bridges and mills, showing the architect's name alongside the names of the corresponding bridge and mill for every possible pairing.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.mill_count FROM architect AS T1 JOIN (SELECT architect_id, COUNT(*) AS mill_count FROM mill GROUP BY architect_id) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id ORDER BY T2.mill_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single architect who designed the most mills, showing their name and the total count of those mills.

sql: SELECT name, location, built_year FROM mill WHERE built_year < (SELECT AVG(built_year) FROM mill);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name, location, and construction year for mills that were built before the average construction year of all mills in the dataset.

sql: SELECT name, nationality FROM architect AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mill AS T2 WHERE T2.architect_id = T1.id AND T2.location = 'Coswarem');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and nationality of the architect who designed a mill in the location 'Coswarem'.

sql: SELECT name FROM architect WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT architect_id FROM bridge WHERE location = 'Guangxi , China');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of architects who have not designed a bridge in 'Guangxi, China'.

sql: SELECT T1.nationality, SUM(T2.length_meters) AS total_bridge_length FROM architect AS T1 JOIN bridge AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.architect_id GROUP BY T1.nationality;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each architect nationality alongside the total length of all bridges designed by architects of that nationality.

sql: SELECT Campus, County, Year FROM Campuses WHERE County = 'Ventura';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the campus name, county, and establishment year for the university campus located in Ventura County.

sql: SELECT Campus, CampusFee FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 1996;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the campus and its associated fee for all California State University (CSU) campuses during the year 1996.

sql: SELECT SUM(Degrees) FROM degrees WHERE Year = 1990;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of degrees awarded in the year 1990.

sql: SELECT Campus, Undergraduate, Graduate FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 AND Discipline = 5;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the undergraduate and graduate enrollment counts for each campus, specifically for discipline ID 5 during the year 2004.

sql: SELECT Campus, Faculty FROM faculty WHERE Year = 2002 ORDER BY Faculty DESC;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the campus and its faculty count for the year 2002, ordered from the campus with the most faculty to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.CampusFee FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN csu_fees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2003;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the campus name and its associated campus fee for the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T2.Year, T2.TotalEnrollment_AY FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T1.Campus = 'California State University-Bakersfield';
result_summary: Returns 49 rows listing the total student enrollment for each year at the California State University-Bakersfield campus.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN faculty AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the faculty count for each campus specifically for the year 2002.

sql: SELECT Campus, SUM(Undergraduate), SUM(Graduate) FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 GROUP BY Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows, showing the total undergraduate and graduate student enrollments for each campus during the year 2004.

sql: SELECT Year, AVG(CampusFee) FROM csu_fees GROUP BY Year HAVING AVG(CampusFee) > 1900;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the year and its average campus fee, but only for years where the average fee was greater than $1900.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Degrees) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN degrees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus GROUP BY T1.Campus ORDER BY SUM(T2.Degrees) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each university campus and the total number of degrees it has awarded, ranked from the highest total to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, SUM(T2.Graduate) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN discipline_enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.Discipline = 5 GROUP BY T1.Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each campus and the total number of graduate students for a specific discipline (ID 5) at that campus.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T2.TotalEnrollment_AY, T3.Faculty FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus JOIN faculty AS T3 ON T1.Id = T3.Campus WHERE T2.Year = 2002 AND T3.Year = 2002;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each campus's name, total academic year enrollment, and faculty count specifically for the year 2002.

sql: SELECT Campus FROM Campuses WHERE Year > 2000 AND Id IN (SELECT Campus FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 2003);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a campus that was founded after the year 2000 and also had recorded fees in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T1.Year, T1.TotalEnrollment_AY FROM enrollments AS T1 WHERE T1.TotalEnrollment_AY = (SELECT MAX(T2.TotalEnrollment_AY) FROM enrollments AS T2 WHERE T2.Campus = T1.Campus);
result_summary: Returns 23 rows, identifying for each campus the year it achieved its historical peak academic year enrollment, along with that peak enrollment figure.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus FROM degrees AS T1 WHERE T1.Year = 1992 GROUP BY T1.Campus HAVING SUM(T1.Degrees) > (SELECT AVG(Degrees) FROM degrees WHERE Year = 1992);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the campuses from 1992 whose total number of awarded degrees exceeded the overall average number of degrees awarded per program for that year.

sql: SELECT Year FROM csu_fees UNION SELECT Year FROM faculty;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing a combined, unique list of years from both the CSU fees and faculty tables.

sql: SELECT AVG(T.Undergraduate), AVG(T.Graduate) FROM (SELECT Undergraduate, Graduate FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004) AS T;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average undergraduate and graduate enrollments across all disciplines for the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus, T1.County, SUM(T2.Degrees) FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN degrees AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T1.County = 'Kern' GROUP BY T1.Campus, T1.County;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each campus located in Kern County along with the total number of degrees awarded by that campus.

sql: SELECT Campus FROM faculty WHERE Year = 2002 INTERSECT SELECT Campus FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 2003;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the campus that had faculty in the year 2002 and also had associated CSU fees in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.Campus FROM Campuses AS T1 WHERE T1.Id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Campus FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 AND Graduate > 0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of campuses that had no graduate student enrollments in the year 2004.

sql: SELECT Campus, SUM(CASE WHEN Graduate = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS no_grad_disciplines, SUM(CASE WHEN Graduate > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS grad_disciplines FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 GROUP BY Campus;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows, one for each campus, showing a count of its disciplines that offer graduate programs versus those that do not, for the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.County, T2.Year, T2.FTE_AY FROM Campuses AS T1 JOIN enrollments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.Campus WHERE T2.FTE_AY = (SELECT MAX(T3.FTE_AY) FROM enrollments AS T3 JOIN Campuses AS T4 ON T3.Campus = T4.Id WHERE T4.County = T1.County) GROUP BY T1.County;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows, identifying the single year of peak full-time equivalent (FTE) enrollment for each county, along with that peak enrollment figure.

sql: SELECT Campus FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 1996 EXCEPT SELECT Campus FROM csu_fees WHERE Year = 2003;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the campuses that had fee data in the year 1996 but did not have fee data in the year 2003.

sql: SELECT Campus, Discipline, Undergraduate FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004 AND Undergraduate > (SELECT AVG(Undergraduate) FROM discipline_enrollments WHERE Year = 2004);
result_summary: Returns 142 rows listing the campus, discipline, and number of undergraduate students for all enrollments in 2004 that had an above-average number of students for that year.

sql: SELECT player_id, name_first, name_last, birth_year FROM player WHERE birth_country = 'USA' LIMIT 20;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing a sample of players born in the USA, showing their player ID, first and last names, and birth year.

sql: SELECT player_id, game_id, team_id, league_id FROM all_star WHERE year = 1933;
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the player, game, team, and league IDs for all All-Star game participants from the year 1933.

sql: SELECT player_id, year, league_id FROM manager_award WHERE award_id = 'BBWAA Manager of the year';
result_summary: Returns 67 rows listing the player ID, year, and league for each manager who won the 'BBWAA Manager of the Year' award.

sql: SELECT player_id, salary FROM salary WHERE year = 1985 AND team_id = 'ATL';
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the player ID and salary for each player on the 'ATL' team in the year 1985.

sql: SELECT park_id, park_name, city, state FROM park WHERE country = 'US';
result_summary: Returns 242 rows listing parks located in the United States, including each park's ID, name, city, and state.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, COUNT(T2.award_id) AS number_of_awards FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_award AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 1323 rows listing each player by their first and last name, along with a total count of the awards they have received.

sql: SELECT team_id, AVG(salary) AS average_salary FROM salary WHERE year = 1985 AND league_id = 'NL' GROUP BY team_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the average player salary for each National League team during the 1985 season.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.team_id) AS number_of_teams FROM player AS T1 JOIN appearances AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 18,653 rows, listing each player by first and last name along with the total count of unique teams they have played for.

sql: SELECT T2.name_full, COUNT(T1.player_id) AS number_of_players FROM player_college AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.college_id = T2.college_id GROUP BY T2.name_full ORDER BY number_of_players DESC;
result_summary: Returns 1031 rows, listing each college and the total number of players it has produced, ordered from the college with the most players to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, SUM(T2.g) AS total_games_managed FROM player AS T1 JOIN manager AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 695 rows listing each manager's first and last name, along with the total number of games they managed throughout their career.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.yearid FROM player AS T1 JOIN hall_of_fame AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.inducted = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 312 rows listing the first name, last name, and induction year for each player who has been inducted into the Hall of Fame.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.franchise_name, T1.w FROM team AS T1 JOIN team_franchise AS T2 ON T1.franchise_id = T2.franchise_id WHERE T1.year = 1871;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name, franchise name, and number of wins (`w`) for all teams that played during the 1871 season.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.salary, T3.year FROM player AS T1 JOIN salary AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN manager AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.player_id AND T2.year = T3.year;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the name, salary, and year for players who also served as managers during that same year.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.salary FROM player AS T1 JOIN salary AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.year = 1985 AND T2.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM salary WHERE year = 1985);
result_summary: Returns 235 rows listing the first name, last name, and salary of all players who earned more than the league's average salary in 1985.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.team_id) AS number_of_teams FROM player AS T1 JOIN appearances AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.team_id) > 5;
result_summary: Returns 1151 rows listing the first name, last name, and total team count for players who have appeared for more than 5 distinct teams.

sql: SELECT T1.player_id FROM player_award AS T1 WHERE T1.award_id = 'Triple Crown' INTERSECT SELECT T2.player_id FROM hall_of_fame AS T2 WHERE T2.inducted = 'Y';

sql: SELECT T1.player_id, T1.year, T1.w, T1.l FROM manager AS T1 WHERE T1.w > (SELECT AVG(T2.w) FROM manager AS T2 WHERE T2.year = T1.year);

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last FROM player AS T1 JOIN appearances AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.team_id = 'NYA' AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM player_college WHERE college_id = 'rice');

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last FROM player AS T1 JOIN (SELECT player_id FROM all_star WHERE year = 1933 EXCEPT SELECT player_id FROM player_award WHERE year = 1933) AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id;

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.r) AS avg_runs, AVG(T1.h) AS avg_hits FROM batting AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.team_id = T2.team_id AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.year = 1871 AND T1.ab >= 100 GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each team from the 1871 season along with the average runs and hits for their players who had at least 100 at-bats.

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.player_id IN (SELECT player_id FROM hall_of_fame WHERE inducted = 'Y') AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT player_id FROM batting_postseason);
result_summary: Returns 201 rows listing the first and last names of players who have been inducted into the Hall of Fame and have also participated in postseason batting.

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.salary) AS total_salary FROM salary AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.team_id = T2.team_id AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.year = 1985 AND T1.league_id = 'NL' GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY total_salary DESC LIMIT 3;

sql: SELECT T3.name_first, T3.name_last, T3.player_id FROM player AS T3 JOIN (SELECT player_id FROM manager_award WHERE year = 1983 UNION SELECT player_id FROM player_award WHERE year = 1983) AS T4 ON T3.player_id = T4.player_id;

sql: SELECT T2.park_name, T1.year, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.team_id) AS team_count FROM home_game AS T1 JOIN park AS T2 ON T1.park_id = T2.park_id GROUP BY T1.park_id, T1.year, T2.park_name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.team_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.name_first, T1.name_last, T2.award_id, T2.year, T2.league_id FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_award AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T1.birth_country = 'USA' AND T2.league_id NOT IN ('NL', 'AL');

sql: SELECT T5.Customer_Details, T4.Policy_Type_Code, T1.Amount_Payment FROM Payments AS T1 JOIN Settlements AS T2 ON T1.Settlement_ID = T2.Settlement_ID JOIN Claims AS T3 ON T2.Claim_ID = T3.Claim_ID JOIN Customer_Policies AS T4 ON T3.Policy_ID = T4.Policy_ID JOIN Customers AS T5 ON T4.Customer_ID = T5.Customer_ID WHERE T1.Payment_Method_Code = 'MasterCard';

sql: SELECT T3.Customer_Details, T3.TotalClaimed - T3.TotalSettled AS Net_Amount FROM (SELECT T1.Customer_ID, T1.Customer_Details, SUM(T2.Amount_Claimed) AS TotalClaimed, SUM(T2.Amount_Settled) AS TotalSettled FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Policies AS CP ON T1.Customer_ID = CP.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS T2 ON CP.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Customer_ID) AS T3;
