sql: SELECT Machine_series, SUM(value_points) FROM machine GROUP BY Machine_series;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each machine series along with the total sum of its value points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.repair_ID) FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each technician by name along with the total count of repairs they have been assigned.

sql: SELECT Team, COUNT(*) FROM technician GROUP BY Team HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing teams that have more than one technician, along with the total count of technicians for each of those teams.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T3.value_points > 105.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of technicians who have been assigned to repair machines with a value point greater than 105.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID WHERE T3.Notes = 'repair Failed. Failed to achieve orbit';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and age of the technician who was assigned to the repair that specifically failed to achieve orbit.

sql: SELECT T1.Class, T1.Machine_series FROM machine AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.Machine_ID = T2.Machine_ID JOIN technician AS T3 ON T2.technician_id = T3.technician_id WHERE T3.Name = 'Joe Sewell';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the class and series for each machine that technician 'Joe Sewell' has been assigned to repair.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.value_points) FROM repair_assignment AS T1 JOIN machine AS T2 ON T1.Machine_ID = T2.Machine_ID WHERE T1.technician_id IN (SELECT technician_id FROM technician WHERE Team = 'NYY');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average value points of all machines that have been repaired by technicians from the 'NYY' team.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T1.Starting_Year > 2010 AND T3.Making_Year < 1993;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of technicians who started after 2010 and have been assigned to repair machines manufactured before 1993.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID WHERE T2.Machine_ID IN (SELECT Machine_ID FROM machine WHERE quality_rank = (SELECT MAX(quality_rank) FROM machine));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Team, count(T3.repair_ID) FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID GROUP BY T1.Team HAVING count(T3.repair_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing technician teams that have completed more than one repair, along with the total count of repairs performed by each team.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN repair AS T3 ON T2.repair_ID = T3.repair_ID WHERE T3.name = 'Discoverer' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T3.Class = '125cc';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of technicians who either worked on the 'Discoverer' repair or were assigned to repair a '125cc' class machine.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T1.Team = 'NYY' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM technician AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.technician_id = T2.technician_id JOIN machine AS T3 ON T2.Machine_ID = T3.Machine_ID WHERE T3.Team = 'Marlboro Pileri - Honda';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T1.Team FROM technician AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Team, MAX(Age) AS max_age FROM technician GROUP BY Team) AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team AND T1.Age = T2.max_age;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the oldest technician for each team, including their name, age, and team affiliation.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM repair AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.repair_ID = T2.repair_ID JOIN technician AS T3 ON T2.technician_id = T3.technician_id WHERE T3.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM technician);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of repairs that were assigned to technicians who are older than the average age of all technicians.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T1.Team FROM technician AS T1 JOIN technician AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team WHERE T1.technician_id < T2.technician_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all unique pairs of technicians who work on the same team, showing the name of each technician in the pair and their shared team.

sql: SELECT T1.Machine_series, T1.Team FROM machine AS T1 JOIN repair_assignment AS T2 ON T1.Machine_ID = T2.Machine_ID WHERE T2.repair_ID NOT IN (SELECT repair_ID FROM repair WHERE Notes = 'repair Failed. Failed to achieve orbit');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the machine series and associated team for machines involved in repairs, excluding any repairs specifically noted as having "Failed to achieve orbit".

sql: SELECT Member_Name, In_office FROM member WHERE Party_ID = '2';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and term in office for members belonging to the political party with an ID of '2'.

sql: SELECT Party_name, Minister FROM party WHERE Region_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the party name and its minister for the specific region identified by ID 1.

sql: SELECT Region_name, Date, Label FROM region WHERE Format = 'CD';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the region name, release date, and record label for all media released in the 'CD' format.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Event_Name FROM party_events;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing a unique list of all available event names.

sql: SELECT Member_Name FROM member WHERE In_office = '1996–present';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members whose term in office is specifically from 1996 to the present.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_name, T2.Region_name FROM party AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_ID = T2.Region_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each political party's name and the name of the region it belongs to.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name, T2.Party_name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each member's name and their corresponding political party affiliation.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_name, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each political party's name along with the total number of members in that party.

sql: SELECT Party_name FROM party WHERE Party_ID IN (SELECT Party_ID FROM party_events WHERE Event_Name = 'Annaual Meeting');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of parties that attended the 'Annual Meeting' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Party_name = 'Progress Party';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all members who belong to the 'Progress Party'.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Name, T2.Party_name, T3.Member_Name FROM party_events AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN member AS T3 ON T1.Member_in_charge_ID = T3.Member_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each party event, the associated political party, and the name of the member responsible for the event.

sql: SELECT T2.Region_name, COUNT(T1.Party_ID) AS NumberOfParties FROM party AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_ID = T2.Region_ID GROUP BY T2.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each region name along with the total number of parties located in that region.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party_name HAVING COUNT(T1.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of political parties that have more than one member.

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name, T2.Party_name, COUNT(T1.Event_ID) AS EventCount FROM party_events AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_ID = T3.Region_ID GROUP BY T3.Region_name, T2.Party_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total count of events held by each political party, grouped by the party's name and its corresponding region.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_name FROM party AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member AS T2 WHERE T2.Party_ID = T1.Party_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of political parties that have at least one member recorded in the system.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_name, T1.MemberCount FROM (SELECT Party_ID, COUNT(*) AS MemberCount FROM member GROUP BY Party_ID) AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.MemberCount > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of political parties that have more than one member, along with the total member count for each of those parties.

sql: SELECT T1.Event_Name, T4.Member_Name, T2.Party_name, T3.Region_name FROM party_events AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_ID = T3.Region_ID JOIN member AS T4 ON T1.Member_in_charge_ID = T4.Member_ID WHERE T3.Region_name = 'United Kingdom';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing political party events held in the 'United Kingdom', including the event name, the hosting party's name, and the member in charge.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party_events AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_in_charge_ID UNION SELECT Minister FROM party;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing a unique list of individuals who are either ministers or are members in charge of a party event.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_name FROM party AS T1 JOIN region AS T2 ON T1.Region_ID = T2.Region_ID WHERE T2.Region_name = 'United Kingdom' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Party_name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_events AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Event_Name = 'Annaual Meeting';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of political parties that are both located in the 'United Kingdom' and have held an event called 'Annual Meeting'.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID EXCEPT SELECT T2.Party_name FROM party_events AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T1.Event_Name = 'Conference';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of political parties that have members, excluding any party that held a 'Conference' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name, T2.Party_name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID WHERE T2.Region_ID IN (SELECT Region_ID FROM region WHERE Format = 'CD');
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each member's name and their party's name, for all members belonging to parties in regions with a 'CD' format.

sql: SELECT T2.Party_name FROM party_events AS T1 JOIN party AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T2.Party_name HAVING COUNT(T1.Event_ID) > (SELECT AVG(EventCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Event_ID) AS EventCount FROM party_events GROUP BY Party_ID));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of political parties that have participated in more events than the average party.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name, T3.Party_name AS MemberParty FROM member AS T1 JOIN party_events AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_in_charge_ID JOIN party AS T3 ON T1.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID WHERE T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and political party of members who are in charge of an event hosted by their own party.

sql: SELECT T4.Region_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Member_ID) AS NumberOfMembersInCharge FROM member AS T1 JOIN party_events AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_in_charge_ID JOIN party AS T3 ON T2.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID JOIN region AS T4 ON T3.Region_ID = T4.Region_ID GROUP BY T4.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each region's name alongside a count of the unique members who have been in charge of a party event within that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Member_Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN party_events AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_in_charge_ID JOIN party AS T3 ON T2.Party_ID = T3.Party_ID WHERE T3.Took_office = '1957' AND T2.Event_Name = 'Conference';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the member who was in charge of the 'Conference' event for the party that took office in 1957.

sql: SELECT Name, SSN FROM Physician WHERE Position = 'Staff Internist';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and Social Security Number (SSN) for physicians who are 'Staff Internists'.

sql: SELECT Name, Address, Phone FROM Patient WHERE PCP = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, address, and phone number for the patient whose Primary Care Physician (PCP) has an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Position FROM Nurse WHERE Registered = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and positions of all nurses who are marked as registered.

sql: SELECT Name, Cost FROM Procedures WHERE Cost > 3000.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and cost for all medical procedures with a cost greater than $3,000.

sql: SELECT Name, Description FROM Medication WHERE Brand = 'X';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and description for any medication manufactured by brand 'X'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Affiliated_With AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician JOIN Department AS T3 ON T2.Department = T3.DepartmentID;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each physician's name and the name of the department with which they are affiliated.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.AppointmentID) FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Appointment AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each physician's name along with the total count of their appointments.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Appointment AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient WHERE T2.Physician = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of patients who have an appointment with the physician identified by ID 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Trained_In AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician JOIN Procedures AS T3 ON T2.Treatment = T3.Code WHERE T3.Name = 'Reverse Rhinopodoplasty';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of physicians who are trained to perform the 'Reverse Rhinopodoplasty' procedure.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Stay AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient JOIN Room AS T3 ON T2.Room = T3.RoomNumber WHERE T3.RoomType = 'Single';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of patients who have stayed in a 'Single' room.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Physician) FROM Affiliated_With AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Department = T2.DepartmentID WHERE T1.PrimaryAffiliation = 1 GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each department's name alongside the total count of physicians who have a primary affiliation with that department.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Nurse AS T1 JOIN On_Call AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Nurse WHERE T2.BlockFloor = 1 AND T2.BlockCode = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of nurses assigned to on-call duty for Block 1 on Floor 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Appointment AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of physicians who have conducted more than one appointment.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient JOIN Procedures AS T3 ON T2.Procedures = T3.Code WHERE T3.Cost > (SELECT avg(Cost) FROM Procedures);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of patients who have undergone medical procedures costing more than the average procedure cost.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Affiliated_With AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Department = T2.DepartmentID WHERE T1.PrimaryAffiliation = 1 GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY count(T1.Physician) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single department with the most physicians who have a primary affiliation.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Trained_In AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING count(T2.Treatment) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of physicians who are trained in more than one type of treatment.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Stay AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a patient who had a hospital stay but did not undergo any procedures.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Affiliated_With AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician WHERE T2.Department = 1 AND T1.EmployeeID NOT IN (SELECT Head FROM Department WHERE Head IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of physicians who are affiliated with Department 1 but are not heads of any department.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM Affiliated_With AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Department = T2.DepartmentID JOIN Physician AS T3 ON T2.Head = T3.EmployeeID WHERE T1.Physician = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the physician who heads the department that the physician with ID 1 is affiliated with.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Nurse WHERE EmployeeID IN (SELECT Nurse FROM On_Call WHERE BlockFloor = 1 INTERSECT SELECT PrepNurse FROM Appointment);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of nurses who are simultaneously on call for Block Floor 1 and also serve as prep nurses for appointments.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Nurse AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.AssistingNurse JOIN Physician AS T3 ON T2.Physician = T3.EmployeeID WHERE T3.Position = 'Surgical Attending Physician';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of nurses who have assisted a 'Surgical Attending Physician' in a procedure.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Trained_In AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician JOIN Procedures AS T3 ON T2.Treatment = T3.Code JOIN Affiliated_With AS T4 ON T1.EmployeeID = T4.Physician WHERE T3.Cost > 3000 AND T4.Department = 1;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of physicians from Department 1 who are trained to perform procedures with a cost greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Prescribes AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient JOIN Medication AS T3 ON T2.Medication = T3.Code JOIN Department AS T4 ON T2.Physician = T4.Head WHERE T3.Name = 'Thesisin';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of patients who were prescribed the medication 'Thesisin' by a physician who is also a department head.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Patient AS T1 JOIN Undergoes AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Patient WHERE T2.Procedures = (SELECT T4.Code FROM Undergoes AS T3 JOIN Procedures AS T4 ON T3.Procedures = T4.Code ORDER BY T4.Cost DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the patient who has undergone the single most expensive procedure by cost.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Patient) FROM Physician AS T1 JOIN Prescribes AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.Physician GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each physician's name along with the total count of unique patients they have prescribed for.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names and email addresses of customers who pay with a 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_category = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all products that are categorized as 'Food'.

sql: SELECT premise_id, premise_details FROM Premises WHERE premises_type = 'Warehouse';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and details for all premises that are classified as warehouses.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_placed_datetime FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the order ID, customer ID, and placement timestamp for all orders with a 'Cancelled' status.

sql: SELECT mailshot_id, product_category, mailshot_start_date FROM Mailshot_Campaigns WHERE mailshot_name = 'US';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows detailing the mailshot ID, product category, and start date for all marketing campaigns named 'US'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name along with the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.item_order_quantity) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each product name along with the total quantity ordered for that product across all orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.mailshot_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name along with the total count of mailshots they have received.

sql: SELECT T3.premises_type, COUNT(T1.customer_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T3 ON T2.premise_id = T3.premise_id GROUP BY T3.premises_type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each premise type (e.g., 'Office', 'Residence') and the total count of customers associated with each type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.shipping_method_code = 'FedEx';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of customers who have placed an order that was shipped using FedEx.

sql: SELECT order_status_code, AVG(order_shipping_charges) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY order_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each order status code along with the average shipping charge for orders of that status.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name, T2.outcome_code, COUNT(*) FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id GROUP BY T1.mailshot_name, T2.outcome_code;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each mailshot campaign name and outcome code, along with a count of customers for each of those combinations.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_category = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of customers who have purchased a product from the 'Food' category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T4 ON T3.premise_id = T4.premise_id WHERE T4.premises_type = 'Warehouse' GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING count(T2.order_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who are associated with a warehouse address and have placed at least one order.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.item_order_quantity) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING SUM(T2.item_order_quantity) > (SELECT AVG(item_order_quantity) FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing products whose total ordered quantity exceeds the average quantity per order item, showing each product's name and its corresponding total quantity sold.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Mailshot_Customers WHERE outcome_code = 'No Response');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and email addresses for customers who did not respond to a recent mailshot campaign.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type_code = 'Billing' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.address_type_code = 'Shipping';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of customers who have a billing address but do not have a shipping address on file.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category, SUM(T3.order_shipping_charges) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.product_category;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each product category along with the total shipping charges for all orders containing items from that category.

sql: SELECT T1.mailshot_name FROM Mailshot_Campaigns AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.mailshot_id = T2.mailshot_id GROUP BY T1.mailshot_name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T2.outcome_code = 'Order' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of mailshot campaigns that have resulted in at least one customer order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_shipping_charges FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_shipping_charges = (SELECT MAX(order_shipping_charges) FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer who paid the highest shipping charge across all orders, showing their name and the specific charge amount.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Credit Card' UNION SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Mailshot_Customers AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Mailshot_Campaigns AS T3 ON T2.mailshot_id = T3.mailshot_id WHERE T3.product_category = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of customers who either paid by 'Credit Card' or were part of a mailshot campaign for the 'food' product category.

sql: SELECT T2.order_id, T1.customer_name, T4.product_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_category = 'Electronics' AND T2.shipping_method_code = 'FedEx';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT order_id, order_shipping_charges FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_shipping_charges > (SELECT AVG(order_shipping_charges) FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Cancelled');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing orders whose shipping charges are greater than the average shipping charge of all cancelled orders.

sql: SELECT customer_id FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the ID for each customer who has never had a cancelled order.

sql: SELECT T1.payment_method FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Premises AS T3 ON T2.premise_id = T3.premise_id WHERE T3.premises_type = 'Warehouse' GROUP BY T1.payment_method ORDER BY COUNT(T1.customer_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common payment method used by customers at 'Warehouse' premises, ranked by the highest number of customers.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Hometown = 'Miami';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of each person whose hometown is 'Miami'.

sql: SELECT Gymnast_ID, Floor_Exercise_Points, Pommel_Horse_Points, Rings_Points FROM gymnast WHERE Gymnast_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the Floor Exercise, Pommel Horse, and Rings points for the specific gymnast with ID 1.

sql: SELECT Name, Age, Height FROM people ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing people's name, age, and height, ordered by age from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT Gymnast_ID, Total_Points FROM gymnast WHERE Total_Points > 57.9;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and total points for gymnasts who scored above 57.9 points.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Age = 24.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all people who are exactly 24 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Hometown, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each gymnast's name, hometown, and total points.

sql: SELECT Hometown, COUNT(*) FROM people GROUP BY Hometown;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, providing a count of people for each distinct hometown.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Vault_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Vault_Points > 9.6;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and vault scores for gymnasts who scored higher than 9.6 in the vault event.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Total_Points = (SELECT MIN(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the gymnast with the lowest total points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Rings_Points > (SELECT AVG(Rings_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of gymnasts whose score in the Rings event was higher than the average for that event.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average age of all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Age > 21;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all gymnasts who are older than 21.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Santo Domingo' AND T2.Total_Points > (SELECT AVG(Total_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and ages of gymnasts from Santo Domingo whose total points are higher than the average score across all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM gymnast AS T3 WHERE T3.Total_Points >= T2.Total_Points) AS Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID ORDER BY T2.Total_Points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows ranking all gymnasts by their total points from highest to lowest, showing each gymnast's name, total points, and assigned rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast WHERE Floor_Exercise_Points > 9.7 AND Pommel_Horse_Points > 9.7);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and total points of any gymnast who scored higher than 9.7 in both the Floor Exercise and Pommel Horse events.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for each person who is not identified as a gymnast.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast WHERE Pommel_Horse_Points = (SELECT MAX(Pommel_Horse_Points) FROM gymnast) UNION SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast WHERE Rings_Points = (SELECT MIN(Rings_Points) FROM gymnast));
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of gymnasts who achieved either the maximum score on the Pommel Horse or the minimum score on the Rings.

sql: SELECT T1.Hometown, AVG(T2.Total_Points) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID GROUP BY T1.Hometown HAVING AVG(T2.Total_Points) > 58.0;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE (CASE WHEN T2.Floor_Exercise_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN T2.Pommel_Horse_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN T2.Rings_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN T2.Vault_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN T2.Parallel_Bars_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN T2.Horizontal_Bar_Points > 9.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of gymnasts who have scored higher than 9.7 in at least three different gymnastics events.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID) AND People_ID IN (SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and ages of gymnasts whose age is greater than the average age of all gymnasts.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM gymnast AS T2 WHERE T2.Gymnast_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Total_Points > 58.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any person who is also a gymnast with a total score greater than 58.0 points.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Age < 24 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T2.Total_Points FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T1.Hometown = 'Santo Domingo';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, age, and total points of gymnasts who are younger than 24 and not from 'Santo Domingo'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM people WHERE Hometown = 'Bonao' INTERSECT SELECT Gymnast_ID FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the gymnast who is from the hometown 'Bonao'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Gymnast_ID, (Floor_Exercise_Points + Pommel_Horse_Points + Rings_Points + Vault_Points + Parallel_Bars_Points + Horizontal_Bar_Points) AS Calculated_Total FROM gymnast) AS T3 ON T1.People_ID = T3.Gymnast_ID WHERE T3.Calculated_Total = (SELECT MAX(Floor_Exercise_Points + Pommel_Horse_Points + Rings_Points + Vault_Points + Parallel_Bars_Points + Horizontal_Bar_Points) FROM gymnast);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the gymnast with the highest total score, calculated by summing their points across all six apparatuses.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, 'High Vault' FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Vault_Points > 9.6 UNION SELECT T1.Name, 'High Rings' FROM people AS T1 JOIN gymnast AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Gymnast_ID WHERE T2.Rings_Points > 9.7;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing gymnasts who achieved a high score in either the vault (over 9.6 points) or rings (over 9.7 points), along with a label indicating the specific achievement.

sql: SELECT name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Ethiopia';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the name and height for each mountain located in Ethiopia.

sql: SELECT brand, name FROM camera_lens WHERE max_aperture = 1.8;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the brand and name for all camera lenses that have a maximum aperture of exactly 1.8.

sql: SELECT name, focal_length_mm FROM camera_lens WHERE brand = 'Olympus';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and focal length in millimeters for all camera lenses manufactured by 'Olympus'.

sql: SELECT name, Height FROM mountain ORDER BY Height DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the tallest mountains by height, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT name, id FROM photos WHERE color = 'Black/White';

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM photos AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.mountain_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each mountain by name and the total number of photos associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM photos AS T1 JOIN camera_lens AS T2 ON T1.camera_lens_id = T2.id WHERE T2.brand = 'Olympus';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of photos taken with an Olympus brand lens, along with the specific name of the lens used.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Height) FROM mountain AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.mountain_id FROM photos AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average height for all mountains that have at least one associated photo.

sql: SELECT brand, COUNT(*) FROM camera_lens GROUP BY brand HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing camera brands that offer more than one lens model, along with the total count of models for each brand.

sql: SELECT name, Height, Prominence FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Ethiopia' AND Prominence > 1500;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the most prominent mountains (prominence > 1500) in Ethiopia, including each mountain's name, height, and prominence.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.mountain_id WHERE T2.color = 'Black/White';

sql: SELECT color, COUNT(id) FROM photos GROUP BY color;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct photo color and the total number of photos available for that color.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS photo_count FROM photos AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.mountain_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.id ORDER BY photo_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single mountain with the most associated photos, along with the total count of those photos.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.mountain_id JOIN camera_lens AS T3 ON T2.camera_lens_id = T3.id WHERE T3.max_aperture = 1.8;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.brand, T1.name FROM camera_lens AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.camera_lens_id JOIN mountain AS T3 ON T2.mountain_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Country = 'Ethiopia';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique camera lens brand and name used to take photos of mountains in Ethiopia.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.focal_length_mm) FROM photos AS T1 JOIN camera_lens AS T2 ON T1.camera_lens_id = T2.id WHERE T1.color = 'RBG';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average focal length in millimeters for lenses used to take 'RBG' color photos.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.mountain_id GROUP BY T1.id, T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 1 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T2.color = 'Black/White' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a mountain that has multiple associated photos, including at least one that is in black and white.

sql: SELECT name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM mountain) AND id IN (SELECT DISTINCT mountain_id FROM photos);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and height for mountains that are taller than the average height and have at least one associated photo.

sql: SELECT T1.brand, T1.name FROM camera_lens AS T1 WHERE T1.id NOT IN (SELECT T2.camera_lens_id FROM photos AS T2 JOIN mountain AS T3 ON T2.mountain_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Ioll / Yoll');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the brand and name for all camera lenses that have not been used to photograph the mountain 'Ioll / Yoll'.

sql: SELECT Range, AVG(Prominence) AS avg_prominence FROM mountain GROUP BY Range HAVING COUNT(id) > 1 ORDER BY avg_prominence DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the mountain range with the highest average prominence, considering only ranges that contain more than one mountain.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM photos AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.mountain_id = T2.id WHERE T2.name = 'Abune Yosef / Guliba Amba' UNION SELECT T1.name FROM photos AS T1 JOIN camera_lens AS T2 ON T1.camera_lens_id = T2.id WHERE T2.name = 'Olympus 15mm f/8.0 Body Cap';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of photos that were either taken on the 'Abune Yosef / Guliba Amba' mountain or with the 'Olympus 15mm f/8.0 Body Cap' camera lens.

sql: SELECT T1.id, T1.name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.mountain_id JOIN camera_lens AS T3 ON T2.camera_lens_id = T3.id WHERE T3.brand = 'Olympus' EXCEPT SELECT T1.id, T1.name FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.mountain_id WHERE T2.color = 'Black/White';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the ID and name of mountains photographed with an 'Olympus' lens, excluding any that have been photographed in black and white.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, T1.name, T1.Height FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Country, MAX(Height) AS max_h FROM mountain WHERE id IN (SELECT DISTINCT mountain_id FROM photos) GROUP BY Country) AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country AND T1.Height = T2.max_h;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the country, name, and height of the tallest mountain for each country, considering only mountains that have at least one associated photo.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Range) AS range_count FROM camera_lens AS T1 JOIN photos AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.camera_lens_id JOIN mountain AS T3 ON T2.mountain_id = T3.id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Range) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each camera lens that has been used to photograph mountains in more than one distinct range, along with the count of those unique ranges.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T1.Country, T1.Range FROM mountain AS T1 JOIN mountain AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country AND T1.Range = T2.Range WHERE T1.id < T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows, with each row showing a unique pair of mountain names that are located in the same country and mountain range.

sql: SELECT note, killed, injured FROM death WHERE killed > 5;

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM ship WHERE disposition_of_ship = 'Captured';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of ships whose disposition is 'Captured'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each battle by name and the total number of ships that were lost in it.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T2.killed) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each ship's name along with the total number of deaths caused by that ship.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.ship_type, T2.note FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.note = 'Dantewada, Chhattisgarh';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and type for each ship that caused a death noted as occurring in 'Dantewada, Chhattisgarh'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.disposition_of_ship = 'Captured';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of battles in which at least one ship was captured.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING SUM(T2.killed) > 20;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of ships that are responsible for a cumulative total of more than 20 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.bulgarian_commander, T1.result FROM battle AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.lost_in_battle FROM ship AS T2);

sql: SELECT T1.ship_type, AVG(T2.killed) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.ship_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship type along with the average number of deaths caused by that type of ship.

sql: SELECT T1.name, SUM(T3.killed) AS total_killed FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each battle by name along with the total number of casualties, calculated by summing the deaths caused by ships that were lost in each respective battle.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ship AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.caused_by_ship_id FROM death AS T2 WHERE T2.killed = (SELECT MAX(killed) FROM death));
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the ship responsible for the single event with the maximum number of recorded deaths.

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of ships that were involved in incidents resulting in more than 5 deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.note = 'Dantewada, Chhattisgarh' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.killed > 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of ships that were involved in an incident in 'Dantewada, Chhattisgarh' and were also involved in an incident where more than 5 people were killed.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the single battle that resulted in the most ships being lost.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN death AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T2.killed > (SELECT AVG(killed) FROM death);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and location of ships responsible for incidents with an above-average number of casualties.

sql: SELECT T1.bulgarian_commander AS commander FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.location = 'English Channel' UNION SELECT T3.latin_commander AS commander FROM battle AS T3 JOIN ship AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.lost_in_battle WHERE T4.location = 'English Channel';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique Bulgarian and Latin commanders who were involved in a battle where a ship was lost in the 'English Channel'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.date, T1.result FROM battle AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.lost_in_battle FROM ship AS T2 JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T2.lost_in_battle HAVING SUM(T3.injured) = 0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, date, and result of battles where the ships lost in those engagements caused zero total injuries.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name FROM battle AS T1, ship AS T2 WHERE T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle AND T1.bulgarian_commander = 'Kaloyan' AND T2.id IN (SELECT caused_by_ship_id FROM death);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the battle name and ship name for ships that were lost in a battle commanded by 'Kaloyan' and were also responsible for causing a death.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.disposition_of_ship FROM ship AS T1 WHERE T1.id = (SELECT T2.caused_by_ship_id FROM death AS T2 GROUP BY T2.caused_by_ship_id ORDER BY SUM(T2.killed) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and final disposition of the ship responsible for the highest total number of deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.caused_by_ship_id FROM death AS T2 GROUP BY T2.caused_by_ship_id HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 0) AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id WHERE T1.ship_type = 'Brig';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all ships of the 'Brig' type that have been responsible for causing one or more deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.apt_number, T2.num_facilities FROM Apartments AS T1 JOIN (SELECT apt_id, count(*) AS num_facilities FROM Apartment_Facilities GROUP BY apt_id HAVING count(*) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.apt_id = T2.apt_id;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.guest_first_name, T1.guest_last_name FROM Guests AS T1 JOIN Apartment_Bookings AS T2 ON T1.guest_id = T2.guest_id JOIN Apartments AS T3 ON T2.apt_id = T3.apt_id WHERE T3.apt_type_code = 'Studio';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique first and last names of all guests who have ever booked a 'Studio' type apartment.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.building_manager FROM Apartment_Buildings AS T1 JOIN Apartments AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id WHERE T2.bedroom_count >= 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the unique building managers who oversee buildings that contain large apartments with six or more bedrooms.

sql: SELECT apt_id FROM Apartment_Facilities WHERE facility_code = 'Gym' INTERSECT SELECT apt_id FROM Apartment_Facilities WHERE facility_code = 'Boardband';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT building_full_name FROM Apartment_Buildings WHERE building_id NOT IN (SELECT building_id FROM Apartments WHERE apt_type_code = 'Flat');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the full names of apartment buildings that do not contain any apartments of the 'Flat' type.

sql: SELECT T1.guest_first_name, T2.booking_start_date, T2.booking_end_date, T3.apt_number, T4.building_short_name FROM Guests AS T1 JOIN Apartment_Bookings AS T2 ON T1.guest_id = T2.guest_id JOIN Apartments AS T3 ON T2.apt_id = T3.apt_id JOIN Apartment_Buildings AS T4 ON T3.building_id = T4.building_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing all apartment bookings, showing each guest's first name, their booking start and end dates, and the specific apartment number and building name for the reservation.

sql: SELECT apt_type_code, max(bathroom_count), max(bedroom_count) FROM Apartments GROUP BY apt_type_code HAVING max(bedroom_count) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the maximum number of bathrooms and bedrooms for apartment types that have options with more than two bedrooms.

sql: SELECT guest_first_name, guest_last_name, gender_code FROM Guests WHERE gender_code = 'Male' UNION SELECT guest_first_name, guest_last_name, gender_code FROM Guests WHERE gender_code = 'Female';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first name, last name, and gender code for all guests identified as either male or female.

sql: SELECT * FROM Investors;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows, providing a complete list of all investors along with their unique ID and associated details.

sql: SELECT * FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing all transaction details for transactions classified as a 'SALE'.

sql: SELECT transaction_type_description FROM Ref_Transaction_Types;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the description for each available transaction type.

sql: SELECT Investor_details FROM Investors WHERE investor_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the investor details for a specific investor identified by their ID of 1.

sql: SELECT lot_id, investor_id FROM Lots ORDER BY investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each lot ID and its associated investor ID, sorted in ascending order by the investor's ID.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, COUNT(T2.transaction_id) FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id GROUP BY T1.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each investor's ID along with the total count of transactions they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_type_description, SUM(T2.amount_of_transaction) FROM Ref_Transaction_Types AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.transaction_type_code = T2.transaction_type_code GROUP BY T1.transaction_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each transaction type, such as 'Purchase' or 'Sale', along with the total sum of transaction amounts for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.Investor_details FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T2.transaction_type_code = 'PUR';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the ID and details for each investor who has made a purchase transaction.

sql: SELECT AVG(amount_of_transaction) FROM Transactions WHERE date_of_transaction < '1990-01-01 00:00:00';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average transaction amount for all transactions that occurred before the year 1990.

sql: SELECT T1.lot_id, T1.lot_details FROM Lots AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.lot_id = T2.lot_id GROUP BY T1.lot_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the ID and details for each unique lot that has been involved in at least one transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, SUM(CAST(T2.share_count AS INTEGER)) FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id GROUP BY T1.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each investor's ID along with the total number of shares they have accumulated across all their transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_id, T1.date_of_transaction, T2.sales_details FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Sales AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.sales_transaction_id WHERE T1.transaction_type_code = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the transaction ID, date, and specific sales details for all transactions classified as a 'SALE'.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id GROUP BY T1.investor_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.transaction_type_code) = 1 AND MIN(T2.transaction_type_code) = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs for investors who have exclusively made 'SALE' transactions and no other type of transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.lot_id FROM Transactions_Lots AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id WHERE T2.transaction_type_code IN ('SALE', 'PUR') GROUP BY T1.lot_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.transaction_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs for lots that have been involved in both a purchase ('PUR') and a sale ('SALE') transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.Investor_details FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T2.transaction_type_code = 'PUR' ORDER BY CAST(T2.share_count AS INTEGER) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the investor (ID and details) who purchased the most shares in a single transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, SUM(T2.amount_of_transaction) FROM Investors AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T2.transaction_type_code = 'PUR' AND T1.investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE') GROUP BY T1.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying investors who have made at least one sale, showing their ID and the total value of all their purchases.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_id, COUNT(T2.lot_id) FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id GROUP BY T1.transaction_id HAVING COUNT(T2.lot_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing transaction IDs that contain more than one lot, along with a count of the number of lots in each of those transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.Investor_details FROM Investors AS T1 LEFT JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id WHERE T2.transaction_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing the ID and details of investors who have no recorded transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_type_description, AVG(CAST(T2.share_count AS INTEGER)) FROM Ref_Transaction_Types AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.transaction_type_code = T2.transaction_type_code GROUP BY T1.transaction_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each transaction type description along with the average share count for that transaction type.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Transactions AS T1 JOIN Transactions_Lots AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id JOIN Lots AS T3 ON T2.lot_id = T3.lot_id WHERE T1.transaction_type_code = 'PUR' AND T3.investor_id = 13;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing all transaction details for purchases that involved lots owned by investor ID 13.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT investor_id FROM Lots EXCEPT SELECT investor_id FROM Transactions;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the IDs of investors who hold lots but have no recorded transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, T1.sales_total, T2.purchases_total FROM (SELECT investor_id, SUM(amount_of_transaction) AS sales_total FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE' GROUP BY investor_id) AS T1 JOIN (SELECT investor_id, SUM(amount_of_transaction) AS purchases_total FROM Transactions WHERE transaction_type_code = 'PUR' GROUP BY investor_id) AS T2 ON T1.investor_id = T2.investor_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing investors who have made both sales and purchases, along with the total monetary value of their sales and purchases.

sql: SELECT T1.lot_id FROM Transactions_Lots AS T1 JOIN Transactions AS T2 ON T1.transaction_id = T2.transaction_id GROUP BY T1.lot_id HAVING MIN(T2.transaction_type_code) = 'SALE' AND MAX(T2.transaction_type_code) = 'SALE';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs for lots where all associated transactions are exclusively of the 'SALE' type.

sql: SELECT investor_id, SUM(amount_of_transaction) AS total_amount FROM Transactions GROUP BY investor_id ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the investor with the highest total transaction amount, showing their investor ID and the summed value of all their transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.investor_id, COUNT(T1.transaction_id) FROM Transactions AS T1 GROUP BY T1.investor_id HAVING AVG(T1.amount_of_transaction) > 40000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing investors whose average transaction amount exceeds 40,000, along with a count of the total number of transactions for each of those investors.

sql: SELECT employee_id, employee_name FROM Employees WHERE role_code = 'ED';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and name for all employees with the role code 'ED'.

sql: SELECT document_id, receipt_date, receipt_number FROM Documents ORDER BY receipt_date DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the document ID, receipt date, and receipt number for all documents, ordered with the most recently received documents first.

sql: SELECT T1.document_id, T1.receipt_number, T2.document_status_description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Status AS T2 ON T1.document_status_code = T2.document_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document's ID and receipt number, along with its corresponding human-readable status description.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Documents WHERE document_status_code = 'done';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of documents that have a status of 'done'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT role_code FROM Employees;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each of the unique employee role codes found in the company.

sql: SELECT T2.shipping_agent_name, count(T1.document_id) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Shipping_Agents AS T2 ON T1.shipping_agent_code = T2.shipping_agent_code GROUP BY T2.shipping_agent_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each shipping agent by name and the total count of documents they have handled.

sql: SELECT document_id, count(draft_number) FROM Document_Drafts GROUP BY document_id HAVING count(draft_number) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying documents that have more than one draft, listing each document's ID alongside the total count of its drafts.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id JOIN Documents AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id WHERE T3.document_status_code = 'working';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of employees who have handled a document that is currently in a 'working' state.

sql: SELECT document_id, draft_number, draft_details FROM Document_Drafts WHERE document_id IN (SELECT document_id FROM Documents WHERE document_type_code = 'Paper');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the document ID, draft number, and draft details for all drafts associated with documents of type 'Paper'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_id, T2.mailing_date FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_Mailed AS T2 ON T1.document_id = T2.document_id WHERE T1.document_type_code = 'Paper';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the document ID and mailing date for all documents that are of the 'Paper' type.

sql: SELECT T1.role_description FROM Roles AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Employees AS T2 WHERE T2.role_code = T1.role_code);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the descriptions of roles that are currently assigned to at least one employee.

sql: SELECT T1.document_id, COUNT(T2.copy_number) FROM Document_Drafts AS T1 JOIN Draft_Copies AS T2 ON T1.document_id = T2.document_id GROUP BY T1.document_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each document's ID along with the total count of its draft copies.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name, T2.role_description, T4.document_id, T5.document_status_description FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T1.employee_id = T3.employee_id JOIN Documents AS T4 ON T3.document_id = T4.document_id JOIN Ref_Document_Status AS T5 ON T4.document_status_code = T5.document_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows that link employees to documents they have handled, providing the employee's name, their role, the document ID, and the document's current status.

sql: SELECT T2.employee_name FROM (SELECT employee_id, count(document_id) AS doc_count FROM Circulation_History GROUP BY employee_id HAVING count(document_id) > 0) AS T1 JOIN Employees AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all employees who have circulated at least one document.

sql: WITH DraftCounts AS (SELECT document_id, COUNT(*) as NumDrafts FROM Document_Drafts GROUP BY document_id) SELECT T1.document_id, T1.NumDrafts FROM DraftCounts AS T1 WHERE T1.NumDrafts > (SELECT AVG(NumDrafts) FROM DraftCounts);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying documents with an above-average number of drafts, showing each document's ID and its total draft count.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.role_code = 'ED' UNION SELECT T2.employee_name FROM Employees AS T2 JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T2.employee_id = T3.employee_id WHERE T3.document_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of all employees who either have the 'ED' role code or have a circulation history associated with document ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.role_code = 'ED' INTERSECT SELECT T2.employee_name FROM Employees AS T2 JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T2.employee_id = T3.employee_id WHERE T3.document_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the employee who both holds the 'ED' role and has a circulation history associated with document ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.employee_name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.role_code = 'ED' EXCEPT SELECT T2.employee_name FROM Employees AS T2 JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T2.employee_id = T3.employee_id WHERE T3.document_id = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an employee with the role code 'ED' who has not been involved in the circulation of document 2.

sql: SELECT T1.document_id, T1.draft_number FROM Document_Drafts AS T1 LEFT JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.document_id = T2.document_id AND T1.draft_number = T2.draft_number WHERE T2.employee_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the document ID and draft number for all document drafts that have not yet been circulated to any employee.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.employee_name, T4.role_description FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Circulation_History AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id JOIN Documents_Mailed AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id JOIN Roles AS T4 ON T1.role_code = T4.role_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique employees and their role descriptions for those who have been involved in the circulation of mailed documents.

sql: SELECT T1.document_type_description, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.employee_id) AS number_of_employees FROM Ref_Document_Types AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.document_type_code = T2.document_type_code JOIN Circulation_History AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id JOIN Employees AS T4 ON T3.employee_id = T4.employee_id GROUP BY T1.document_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each document type description along with the total count of unique employees who have circulated documents of that type.

sql: SELECT document_id, mailing_date FROM Documents_Mailed WHERE document_id NOT IN (SELECT document_id FROM Circulation_History);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the ID and mailing date for documents that were mailed but have no corresponding circulation history record.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN document_status_code = 'working' THEN 'Current' ELSE 'Archived' END AS category, COUNT(document_id) FROM Documents GROUP BY category;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total number of documents, grouped into 'Current' and 'Archived' categories based on their status.

sql: SELECT document_id, receipt_date FROM Documents WHERE shipping_agent_code = (SELECT shipping_agent_code FROM Documents WHERE document_id = 3) AND document_id != 3;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the document ID and receipt date for all other documents handled by the same shipping agent as document ID 3.

sql: SELECT T2.role_description, count(T1.employee_id) FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T2.role_description HAVING count(T1.employee_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing job roles shared by more than one employee, along with the total count of employees assigned to each of those popular roles.

sql: SELECT student_id, first_name, last_name, email_address FROM Students WHERE first_name = 'Timmothy';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the student ID, first name, last name, and email address for the student with the first name 'Timmothy'.

sql: SELECT course_name, course_description FROM Courses WHERE course_name = 'math';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the course name and description for the course specifically named 'math'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Semesters WHERE semester_name = 'spring 2010';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the semester named 'spring 2010'.

sql: SELECT line_1, city, country FROM Addresses WHERE country = 'Iceland';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the street address, city, and country for all known addresses located in Iceland.

sql: SELECT department_name, department_description FROM Departments ORDER BY department_name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each department's name and description, sorted by department name in reverse alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT T1.semester_name, count(T2.student_id) FROM Semesters AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.semester_id = T2.semester_id GROUP BY T1.semester_name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each semester name along with the total count of student enrollments for that semester.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.department_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Degree_Programs AS T3 ON T2.degree_program_id = T3.degree_program_id JOIN Departments AS T4 ON T3.department_id = T4.department_id WHERE T4.department_name = 'history';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who are enrolled in the 'history' department.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Enrolment_Courses AS T3 ON T2.student_enrolment_id = T3.student_enrolment_id WHERE T3.course_id = 6;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all students enrolled in the course with an ID of 6.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, count(T2.degree_program_id) FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Degree_Programs AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each department name along with the total number of degree programs offered by that department.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.city, T2.country FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id = T2.address_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the city and country of their current address.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, T2.section_name, T2.section_description FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Sections AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.course_name = 'math';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the section name and description for the 'math' course.

sql: SELECT T1.student_enrolment_id, T1.student_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS T1 JOIN Semesters AS T2 ON T1.semester_id = T2.semester_id WHERE T2.semester_name = 'summer 2010';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the student enrolment ID and student ID for all students who were enrolled in the 'summer 2010' semester.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Degree_Programs AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_name HAVING count(T2.degree_program_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all departments that offer more than one degree program.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 WHERE T1.student_id IN (SELECT T2.student_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS T2 JOIN Degree_Programs AS T3 ON T2.degree_program_id = T3.degree_program_id JOIN Departments AS T4 ON T3.department_id = T4.department_id WHERE T4.department_name = 'history');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of the student enrolled in the 'history' department.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment_Courses AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_id HAVING count(T2.student_enrolment_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of courses that have more than one student enrolled.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T4.course_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Enrolment_Courses AS T3 ON T2.student_enrolment_id = T3.student_enrolment_id JOIN Courses AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id WHERE T1.date_first_registered > '2000-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first name, last name, and an enrolled course name for each student who first registered after January 1st, 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.country = 'Iceland' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id = T2.address_id WHERE T2.country = 'Burundi';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students whose current address is in either 'Iceland' or 'Burundi'.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS T1 WHERE T1.degree_program_id = 4 EXCEPT SELECT T1.student_id FROM Student_Enrolment AS T1 WHERE T1.semester_id = 13;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the student ID for any student in degree program 4, excluding any students who are also enrolled in semester 13.

sql: SELECT T1.semester_name FROM Semesters AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.semester_id = T2.semester_id GROUP BY T1.semester_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the semester with the highest number of student enrollments.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Student_Enrolment_Courses AS T3 ON T2.student_enrolment_id = T3.student_enrolment_id JOIN Transcript_Contents AS T4 ON T3.student_course_id = T4.student_course_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique first and last names of students who have at least one entry on their academic transcript.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name FROM Departments AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Degree_Programs AS T2 WHERE T2.department_id = T1.department_id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of departments that do not offer any degree programs.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.permanent_address_id = T2.permanent_address_id WHERE T1.student_id < T2.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows identifying pairs of different students who share the same permanent address, listing the first and last name for each student.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, COUNT(T2.section_id) FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Sections AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T1.course_name HAVING COUNT(T2.section_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each course name along with the total number of sections offered for that course.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.degree_summary_name, T4.department_name, T5.semester_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Degree_Programs AS T3 ON T2.degree_program_id = T3.degree_program_id JOIN Departments AS T4 ON T3.department_id = T4.department_id JOIN Semesters AS T5 ON T2.semester_id = T5.semester_id WHERE T4.department_name = 'history';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying all student enrollments in the 'history' department, listing each student's name, their degree program, and the specific semester of their enrollment.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.student_enrolment_id) AS enrolment_count FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(T2.student_enrolment_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of students with at least one course enrolment, along with a count of their total enrolments.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Cargo ship';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and tonnage for all ships that are classified as 'Cargo ship'.

sql: SELECT Code, Location, Fate FROM mission WHERE Launched_Year = 1930;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the code, location, and fate for all missions that were launched in the year 1930.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship ORDER BY Tonnage DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all ships and their tonnage, ordered from highest to lowest tonnage.

sql: SELECT Location, Fate FROM mission WHERE Speed_knots = 25;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the location and fate of all missions that were conducted at a speed of exactly 25 knots.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM ship;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of all ships.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name of each ship and the location of the mission it was assigned to.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, count(T2.Mission_ID) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each ship's name and the total count of missions it has undertaken.

sql: SELECT Nationality, AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each nationality and the corresponding average ship tonnage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Launched_Year = 1930;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of ships that participated in missions launched in the year 1930.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, MAX(T2.Speed_knots) FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each ship nationality alongside the maximum speed in knots recorded for a ship of that nationality during a mission.

sql: SELECT Type, count(*) FROM ship GROUP BY Type HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the types of ships that have more than one vessel, along with the total count for each of those types.

sql: SELECT Code, Launched_Year, Location FROM mission WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM ship WHERE Nationality = 'United Kingdom');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the mission code, launch year, and location for all missions conducted by ships with a 'United Kingdom' nationality.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Speed_knots = (SELECT MAX(Speed_knots) FROM mission);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the name and type of the ship or ships that achieved the fastest recorded mission speed.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Nationality != 'United States' ORDER BY Tonnage DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and tonnage for the single largest non-US ship, identified by having the highest tonnage.

sql: SELECT Nationality FROM ship GROUP BY Nationality HAVING AVG(Tonnage) > (SELECT AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the nationality whose ships have an average tonnage greater than the overall average tonnage of all ships.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Location = 'Germany' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM ship WHERE Type = 'Cargo ship';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the cargo ship that has also been on a mission in Germany.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Location = 'Germany';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of ships that have been on a mission, but have never had a mission located in Germany.

sql: SELECT Launched_Year, count(*), AVG(Speed_knots) FROM mission GROUP BY Launched_Year HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying launch years with more than one mission, showing the total mission count and the average mission speed for each of those years.

sql: SELECT Name, Tonnage FROM ship WHERE Nationality = 'United Kingdom' AND Tonnage > (SELECT MAX(Tonnage) FROM ship WHERE Nationality = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and tonnage of any British ship whose tonnage is greater than that of the largest ship from the United States.

sql: SELECT T2.Location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Location HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Type) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each location where missions have been conducted by two or more distinct types of ships.

sql: SELECT T2.Code, T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Tonnage IN (SELECT Tonnage FROM ship ORDER BY Tonnage DESC LIMIT 2);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the mission code and ship name for missions carried out by the ships with the two highest tonnage values.

sql: SELECT T2.Location FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Location HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Nationality) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing mission locations that have been visited by ships from at least two different nationalities.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Fate = 'Decommissioned 1950' AND T1.Tonnage > (SELECT AVG(Tonnage) FROM ship WHERE Nationality = 'United Kingdom');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.
