sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mission AS T2 WHERE T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID AND T2.Location = 'Germany') UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United Kingdom';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of ships that are either from the United Kingdom or have participated in a mission located in Germany.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM ship AS T1 JOIN mission AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T1.Ship_ID, T1.Name HAVING count(T2.Mission_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT * FROM Projects;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all projects, showing the project ID and a description of the project details for each.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all documents that are classified with the document type code 'CV'.

sql: SELECT Account_Details FROM Accounts WHERE Statement_ID = 57;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the account details for all entries associated with statement ID 57.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Budget_Type_Code FROM Documents_with_Expenses;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique budget type codes found across all documents with expenses.

sql: SELECT Project_Details FROM Projects WHERE Project_ID = 30;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the project details for the specific project with an ID of 30.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document's name and its corresponding document type name, as defined by the reference table.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Project_Details FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document name alongside the details of the project it belongs to.

sql: SELECT T1.Account_Details, T2.Statement_Details FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Statements AS T2 ON T1.Statement_ID = T2.Statement_ID WHERE T2.Statement_ID = 57;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the account details and statement details for all accounts associated with the specific statement ID 57.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each document type name alongside the total count of documents for that specific type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all documents that have associated expenses.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Details, T2.Budget_Type_Description FROM Documents_with_Expenses AS T1 JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T2 ON T1.Budget_Type_Code = T2.Budget_Type_Code WHERE T1.Budget_Type_Code = 'GV';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing document details and the budget type description for all documents associated with the 'Government' budget type (code 'GV').

sql: SELECT T2.Project_Details, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID GROUP BY T2.Project_Details;
error_message: Discarded: SQL Runtime Error - no such column: T2.Project_Details

sql: SELECT T1.Project_ID, T1.Project_Details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Documents AS T2 WHERE T2.Project_ID = T1.Project_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the ID and details for projects that have at least one associated document.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Document_Description FROM Documents WHERE Project_ID NOT IN (SELECT Project_ID FROM Projects WHERE Project_Details = 'Society Research project');
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the name and description of documents that are not associated with the 'Society Research project'.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Document_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of document types that are associated with more than one document.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM Documents UNION SELECT Project_Details FROM Projects;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows presenting a combined, unique list of document names from the 'Documents' table and project details from the 'Projects' table.

sql: SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents INTERSECT SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_with_Expenses;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique IDs for all documents that have associated expenses.

sql: SELECT AVG(DocCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Document_ID) AS DocCount FROM Documents GROUP BY Project_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the average number of documents per project.

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details, T2.Document_Name, T2.Document_Date FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID WHERE T2.Document_Date = (SELECT MAX(T3.Document_Date) FROM Documents AS T3 WHERE T3.Project_ID = T1.Project_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, with each row listing the project details alongside the name and date of the most recent document associated with that project.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents EXCEPT SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_with_Expenses);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of documents that have no associated expenses recorded against them.

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details FROM Projects AS T1 WHERE T1.Project_ID IN (SELECT T2.Project_ID FROM Documents AS T2 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T3 ON T2.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the project details for all projects that have at least one associated document with expenses.

sql: SELECT T3.Budget_Type_Description, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T3 ON T2.Budget_Type_Code = T3.Budget_Type_Code WHERE T3.Budget_Type_Code = 'GV' GROUP BY T3.Budget_Type_Description;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the 'Government' budget type description and the total number of documents associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details, COUNT(T2.Document_ID) FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID WHERE T1.Project_ID IN (SELECT Project_ID FROM Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV') GROUP BY T1.Project_Details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the project details and the total number of associated documents for projects that include a document with a 'CV' type code.

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details, T2.Document_Name, T4.Budget_Type_Description FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T3 ON T2.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T4 ON T3.Budget_Type_Code = T4.Budget_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows that link project details and their associated document names to the descriptive budget category for any related expenses.

sql: SELECT T1.Project_Details, T2.Document_Name, T3.Document_Type_Name, T5.Budget_Type_Description FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Project_ID = T2.Project_ID JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T3 ON T2.Document_Type_Code = T3.Document_Type_Code JOIN Documents_with_Expenses AS T4 ON T2.Document_ID = T4.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Budget_Codes AS T5 ON T4.Budget_Type_Code = T5.Budget_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing documents associated with projects, showing each project's details, the document's name, its type, and the budget description for its related expenses.

sql: SELECT name, Industry FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and industry for all companies that are headquartered in the USA.

sql: SELECT name, Height, Stories FROM buildings WHERE City = 'Mexico City';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name, height, and number of stories for all buildings located in Mexico City.

sql: SELECT name, Sales_billion FROM Companies ORDER BY Sales_billion DESC;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing all company names and their sales in billions, ordered from the highest sales to the lowest.

sql: SELECT building_id, company_id FROM Office_locations WHERE move_in_year = 2021;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the building and the company for an office location that was established in the year 2021.

sql: SELECT name, Status FROM buildings WHERE Status = 'proposed';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and status for any building currently in the 'proposed' stage.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.move_in_year FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each company's name and the corresponding year they moved into their office location.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.company_id FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each building's name and the ID of a company that has an office located within it.

sql: SELECT Industry, COUNT(*) FROM Companies GROUP BY Industry;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each industry along with the total number of companies within that industry.

sql: SELECT City, AVG(Height) FROM buildings GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each city along with the average height of its buildings.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.name, T3.move_in_year FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.company_id JOIN buildings AS T2 ON T3.building_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing each company's name, the name of the building where its office is located, and the year the company moved into that building.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Headquarters FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.move_in_year > 2021;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the name and headquarters of companies that have opened a new office location after the year 2021.

sql: SELECT T1.City, COUNT(T2.company_id) FROM buildings AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.building_id GROUP BY T1.City;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city along with the total number of company offices located in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Status = 'under construction';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of companies with offices in buildings that are currently under construction.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Sales_billion = (SELECT MAX(Sales_billion) FROM Companies);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the company that has the highest sales in billions.

sql: SELECT name, Stories FROM buildings WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM buildings);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and number of stories for all buildings that are taller than the average height.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT company_id FROM Office_locations);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of companies that do not have any registered office locations.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.City FROM buildings AS T1 WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T2.building_id FROM Office_locations AS T2 JOIN Companies AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and cities of buildings that house companies in the 'Banking' industry.

sql: SELECT Headquarters, COUNT(*) FROM Companies GROUP BY Headquarters HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each headquarters location that is home to more than one company, along with the total count of companies for that location.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Headquarters FROM Companies AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Office_locations AS T2 JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T2.company_id = T1.id AND T3.City = 'Mexico City');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and headquarters of companies that have an office location in Mexico City.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Sales_billion, T1.Profits_billion FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN (SELECT AVG(Sales_billion) AS avg_sales, AVG(Profits_billion) AS avg_profits FROM Companies) AS T2 ON T1.Sales_billion > T2.avg_sales AND T1.Profits_billion < T2.avg_profits;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, sales, and profits for companies that have above-average sales but below-average profits.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' UNION SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the names of companies that are either headquartered in the 'USA' or operate in the 'Banking' industry.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' INTERSECT SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of banking companies that are headquartered in the USA.

sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of companies headquartered in the USA, excluding any that are in the banking industry.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.name, T1.Height FROM buildings AS T1 WHERE T1.Height = (SELECT MAX(T2.Height) FROM buildings AS T2 WHERE T2.City = T1.City);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, identifying the name and height of the tallest building within each city listed in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, T3.Height FROM Companies AS T1 JOIN Office_locations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN buildings AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Height > 210;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing companies with offices in buildings taller than 210, showing the company name, the building's name, and its height.

sql: SELECT staff_first_name, staff_last_name FROM Staff;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first and last name of every staff member.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_details FROM Product ORDER BY product_name;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and details for each product, ordered alphabetically by product name.

sql: SELECT problem_log_id, problem_id, log_entry_date FROM Problem_Log WHERE problem_status_code = 'Solved';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing log entries for problems marked as 'Solved', showing the log ID, problem ID, and the date the entry was made.

sql: SELECT problem_id, product_id, date_problem_reported FROM Problems WHERE reported_by_staff_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the problem ID, associated product ID, and the date reported for all issues logged by the staff member with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT problem_category_description FROM Problem_Category_Codes;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions for all available problem categories.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T2.product_name FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each problem's description alongside the name of the associated product.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_log_id, T2.staff_first_name, T2.staff_last_name FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.assigned_to_staff_id = T2.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, listing each problem log ID along with the first and last name of the staff member assigned to it.

sql: SELECT problem_status_code, COUNT(*) FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_status_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each distinct problem status code and the total count of problems associated with that status.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T2.staff_first_name AS reporter_first_name, T3.staff_first_name AS authorizer_first_name FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.reported_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T1.closure_authorised_by_staff_id = T3.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each problem's description along with the first names of the staff members who reported the problem and who authorized its closure.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, COUNT(T1.problem_id) FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each product name and the total number of problems associated with that product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name, COUNT(T2.problem_log_id) AS assignments_count FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, with each row listing a staff member's first and last name and the total count of problem logs assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_log_id, T1.log_entry_description, T2.problem_category_description FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T2 ON T1.problem_category_code = T2.problem_category_code WHERE T1.problem_category_code = 'GUI';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all problem log entries that are categorized as 'GUI' (User Interface), showing each entry's ID, description, and the full category description.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have been assigned to more than one problem.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Product AS T1 JOIN Problems AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.problem_id IN (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log WHERE problem_category_code = 'Middleware');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products that have had a reported problem associated with the 'Middleware' problem category.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN (SELECT reported_by_staff_id AS id FROM Problems INTERSECT SELECT assigned_to_staff_id AS id FROM Problem_Log) AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have both reported a problem and have also been assigned to one.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.assigned_to_staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.problem_status_code = 'Solved' THEN 1 END) = COUNT(*);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of staff members who have successfully resolved every single problem assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description, T4.problem_category_description, T5.problem_status_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Problem_Log AS T3 ON T1.problem_id = T3.problem_id JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T4 ON T3.problem_category_code = T4.problem_category_code JOIN Problem_Status_Codes AS T5 ON T3.problem_status_code = T5.problem_status_code WHERE T2.product_name = 'rose';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows detailing the description, category, and status for problems associated with the 'rose' product.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE T1.staff_id IN (SELECT reported_by_staff_id FROM Problems EXCEPT SELECT closure_authorised_by_staff_id FROM Problems);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first names of staff members who have reported problems but have never authorized a problem closure.

sql: SELECT T2.problem_category_description, COUNT(T1.problem_id), COUNT(DISTINCT T1.assigned_to_staff_id) FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Problem_Category_Codes AS T2 ON T1.problem_category_code = T2.problem_category_code GROUP BY T2.problem_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows summarizing logged issues by category, showing the total count of problems and the number of unique staff members assigned per category.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, COUNT(T1.problem_id) AS problem_count FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name ORDER BY problem_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the product with the most reported problems, showing its name and the total count of those problems.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_first_name, T1.staff_last_name, COALESCE(T2.reported_count, 0), COALESCE(T3.authorized_count, 0), COALESCE(T4.assigned_count, 0) FROM Staff AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT reported_by_staff_id, COUNT(*) AS reported_count FROM Problems GROUP BY reported_by_staff_id) AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.reported_by_staff_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT closure_authorised_by_staff_id, COUNT(*) AS authorized_count FROM Problems GROUP BY closure_authorised_by_staff_id) AS T3 ON T1.staff_id = T3.closure_authorised_by_staff_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT assigned_to_staff_id, COUNT(*) AS assigned_count FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY assigned_to_staff_id) AS T4 ON T1.staff_id = T4.assigned_to_staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each staff member's full name, along with a total count of the problems they have reported, authorized for closure, and been assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.problem_description FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN (SELECT problem_id FROM Problem_Log GROUP BY problem_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT assigned_to_staff_id) > 1) AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions of problems that have been assigned to more than one distinct staff member.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Product WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT T1.product_id FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Problem_Log AS T2 ON T1.problem_id = T2.problem_id WHERE T2.problem_category_code = 'GUI');
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of products that have not had any logged problems related to the GUI category.

sql: SELECT 'Reporter' FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.reported_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.staff_first_name = 'Lacey' AND T2.staff_last_name = 'Bosco' UNION SELECT 'Authorizer' FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.closure_authorised_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.staff_first_name = 'Lacey' AND T2.staff_last_name = 'Bosco' UNION SELECT 'Assignee' FROM Problem_Log AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.assigned_to_staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.staff_first_name = 'Lacey' AND T2.staff_last_name = 'Bosco';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the distinct roles ('Reporter', 'Authorizer', 'Assignee') held by the staff member 'Lacey Bosco' in the problem and problem log systems.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, AVG(julianday(T1.date_problem_closed) - julianday(T1.date_problem_reported)) FROM Problems AS T1 JOIN Product AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.date_problem_closed IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each product name along with the average time in days taken to resolve a reported problem for that product, considering only problems that have been closed.

sql: SELECT characteristic_type_code, characteristic_type_description FROM Ref_Characteristic_Types;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available characteristic types, providing the code and description for each.

sql: SELECT product_name, typical_selling_price FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each product name and its corresponding typical selling price.

sql: SELECT characteristic_name, characteristic_data_type FROM Characteristics WHERE characteristic_type_code = 'Grade';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and data type for each characteristic that is classified as a 'Grade'.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Herbs';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all products that are classified within the 'Herbs' product category.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT product_characteristic_value FROM Product_Characteristics;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all the unique characteristic values that can be assigned to a product.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.color_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each product's name along with its corresponding color description, created by matching products to a color reference table.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_description, COUNT(T1.product_id) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code GROUP BY T2.product_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category description along with the total count of products within that category.

sql: SELECT T1.characteristic_name, T2.characteristic_type_description FROM Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_type_code = T2.characteristic_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each characteristic name along with its corresponding type description, mapping characteristics to their defined categories.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.product_characteristic_value = 'low';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of products that have a characteristic value of 'low'.

sql: SELECT T2.characteristic_name, T3.product_characteristic_value FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T2 ON T3.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id WHERE T1.product_name = 'cumin';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the characteristic name and its corresponding value for the product 'cumin'.

sql: SELECT T2.color_description, AVG(CAST(T1.typical_selling_price AS REAL)) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code GROUP BY T2.color_description;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each product color description and the corresponding average typical selling price for products of that color.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.characteristic_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T2 ON T3.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Spices';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique characteristic name associated with products in the 'Spices' category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.color_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code WHERE CAST(T1.typical_selling_price AS REAL) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(typical_selling_price AS REAL)) FROM Products WHERE typical_selling_price IS NOT NULL AND typical_selling_price != '');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and color description for products with a selling price that is higher than the average selling price across all products.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_description FROM Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 WHERE T2.product_category_code IN (SELECT T1.product_category_code FROM Products AS T1 GROUP BY T1.product_category_code HAVING COUNT(T1.product_id) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the descriptions for product categories that contain more than one distinct product.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Product_Characteristics AS T2 JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T2.product_id = T1.product_id AND T3.characteristic_type_code = 'Grade');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all products that have an associated characteristic of type 'Grade'.

sql: SELECT T2.characteristic_name, T3.characteristic_type_description FROM Product_Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.characteristic_id = T2.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_type_code = T3.characteristic_type_code GROUP BY T2.characteristic_name, T3.characteristic_type_description HAVING COUNT(T1.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing characteristic names and their corresponding type descriptions for characteristics that are shared by more than one product.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.color_description, T3.product_category_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Colors AS T2 ON T1.color_code = T2.color_code JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T3 ON T1.product_category_code = T3.product_category_code WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Spices' AND T1.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Product_Characteristics WHERE product_characteristic_value = 'low');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing spices that possess a 'low' characteristic value, showing each product's name, color, and category description.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING COUNT(T2.characteristic_id) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products that have two or more associated characteristics.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Spices' UNION SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE color_code = '9';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique names of products that are either in the 'Spices' category or have a color code of '9'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Herbs' EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_type_code = 'Grade';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all products categorized as 'Herbs' that do not have an associated 'Grade' characteristic.

sql: SELECT T2.characteristic_type_description FROM Product_Characteristics AS T1 JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T1.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id JOIN Ref_Characteristic_Types AS T2 ON T3.characteristic_type_code = T2.characteristic_type_code GROUP BY T2.characteristic_type_description ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the description of the product characteristic type that appears most frequently across all products.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Product_Characteristics AS T2 JOIN Characteristics AS T3 ON T2.characteristic_id = T3.characteristic_id WHERE T3.characteristic_name = 'slow') AS T4 ON T1.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T1.product_category_code = 'Herbs';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE color_code = (SELECT color_code FROM Products WHERE product_name = 'cumin') AND product_name != 'cumin';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products that share the same color code as the product 'cumin'.

sql: SELECT T2.characteristic_name FROM Characteristics AS T2 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T1 ON T2.characteristic_id = T1.characteristic_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_category_code = 'Spices' INTERSECT SELECT T2.characteristic_name FROM Characteristics AS T2 JOIN Product_Characteristics AS T1 ON T2.characteristic_id = T1.characteristic_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T1.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_category_code = 'Herbs';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_description, MAX(CAST(T1.typical_selling_price AS REAL)) FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Ref_Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.product_category_code = T2.product_category_code WHERE T1.typical_selling_price IS NOT NULL AND T1.typical_selling_price != '' GROUP BY T2.product_category_description;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category description alongside the maximum typical selling price for a product within that category.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Occupation FROM player WHERE Occupation = 'Researcher';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the player whose occupation is 'Researcher'.

sql: SELECT Club_name FROM club WHERE Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all clubs located in the USA region.

sql: SELECT Player_name, Votes FROM player ORDER BY Votes DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each player's name and their total votes, ordered from the highest number of votes to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Coach_name, Gender FROM coach;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and gender for every coach in the database.

sql: SELECT Club_name, Start_year FROM club WHERE Start_year > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and founding year for all clubs established after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name, T2.Club_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each coach's name and the name of the club they belong to.

sql: SELECT Residence, COUNT(*) FROM player GROUP BY Residence;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique player residence along with the total count of players from that location.

sql: SELECT Gender, AVG(Votes) FROM player GROUP BY Gender;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each gender alongside the average number of votes received by players of that gender.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, T2.Points FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, each listing a club's name along with its corresponding points from match results.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Starting_year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all players who began a coaching relationship in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name, SUM(T2.Gold), SUM(T2.Bronze) FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Club_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each club's name alongside the total count of their gold and bronze medals.

sql: SELECT Gender, COUNT(*) FROM coach GROUP BY Gender;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each gender ('F' and 'M') and the total count of coaches for each respective gender.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T3.Coach_name, T4.Club_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T4 ON T3.Club_ID = T4.Club_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each player, their assigned coach, and the name of the club that coach belongs to.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T3.Coach_name = 'Jameson Tomas';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all players who are coached by 'Jameson Tomas'.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Club_name HAVING SUM(T2.Points) > 130;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs that have accumulated more than 130 total points from their matches.

sql: SELECT T1.Coach_name FROM coach AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Coach_ID = T2.Coach_ID GROUP BY T1.Coach_ID, T1.Coach_name HAVING COUNT(T2.Player_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of coaches who are assigned to more than one player.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T1.Votes, T1.Residence FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Votes > (SELECT AVG(T2.Votes) FROM player AS T2 WHERE T2.Residence = T1.Residence);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying players who received more votes than the average for their specific residence, showing the player's name, their vote count, and their residence.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Residence = 'Winnipeg' UNION SELECT T2.Coach_name FROM coach AS T2 JOIN club AS T3 ON T2.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID WHERE T3.Region = 'USA';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the combined names of players who live in Winnipeg and coaches of clubs in the USA region.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Gold > 10 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM coach AS T3 WHERE T3.Club_ID = T1.Club_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of clubs that have an assigned coach and have also won more than 10 gold medals in a match.

sql: SELECT T3.Club_name, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) FROM player_coach AS T1 JOIN coach AS T2 ON T1.Coach_ID = T2.Coach_ID JOIN club AS T3 ON T2.Club_ID = T3.Club_ID GROUP BY T3.Club_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each club's name along with the total number of players associated with that club.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID WHERE T2.Coach_ID NOT IN (SELECT Coach_ID FROM coach WHERE Rank = 3);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of players who are not coached by a coach with a rank of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Region, SUM(T2.Points) FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID GROUP BY T1.Region;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each region along with the total points accumulated by all clubs from that region.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T1.Gender = 'F' AND T3.Gender = 'M';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of female players who are coached by a male coach.

sql: SELECT T1.Player_name, T1.Occupation, T3.Coach_name FROM player AS T1 JOIN player_coach AS T2 ON T1.Player_ID = T2.Player_ID JOIN coach AS T3 ON T2.Coach_ID = T3.Coach_ID WHERE T2.Starting_year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the player's name, their occupation, and their coach's name for all player-coach relationships that began in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.Club_name FROM club AS T1 JOIN match_result AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID = T2.Club_ID WHERE T2.Points = (SELECT MAX(Points) FROM match_result);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the club that achieved the highest points in a match.

sql: SELECT Shop_ID, Address, Num_of_staff FROM shop WHERE Open_Year = '2010';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the shop ID, address, and number of staff for all shops that opened in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM member WHERE Membership_card = 'Black';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and age of all members who hold a 'Black' membership card.

sql: SELECT Name, Level_of_membership FROM member WHERE Address = 'Hartford';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and level of membership for all members located in Hartford.

sql: SELECT Address, Score FROM shop ORDER BY Score DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing shop addresses and their scores, ordered from the highest score to the lowest.

sql: SELECT HH_ID, Shop_ID, Num_of_shaff_in_charge FROM happy_hour WHERE Month = 'May';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the happy hour ID, shop ID, and the number of staff in charge for all happy hour events that occurred in May.

sql: SELECT T1.Address, T2.Month, T2.Num_of_shaff_in_charge FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Month = 'May';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the address, month, and number of staff in charge for all shop happy hours that occurred in May.

sql: SELECT Address, COUNT(Member_ID) FROM member GROUP BY Address;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each unique address along with the total number of members residing at that address.

sql: SELECT Open_Year, AVG(Score) FROM shop GROUP BY Open_Year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each opening year for shops along with the average score for all shops opened in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_amount FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each happy hour member's name along with the total amount for each of their individual happy hour transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.Address, COUNT(T2.HH_ID) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN happy_hour AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each shop's address and the total count of happy hours available at that location.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T3 ON T2.HH_ID = T3.HH_ID WHERE T3.Num_of_shaff_in_charge = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of members who have attended happy hour events that were managed by exactly 10 staff members.

sql: SELECT Open_Year, SUM(Num_of_staff) FROM shop GROUP BY Open_Year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, showing the total number of staff for all shops, grouped by their opening year.

sql: SELECT Shop_ID, Address, Score FROM shop WHERE Score > (SELECT AVG(Score) FROM shop);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying shops with an above-average score, listing the ID, address, and specific score for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Member_ID HAVING count(T2.HH_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of members who have attended more than one happy hour event.

sql: SELECT Address, Num_of_staff FROM shop WHERE Num_of_staff = (SELECT MAX(Num_of_staff) FROM shop);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the shop with the largest staff, showing its address and the total number of staff.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total_amount FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Membership_card = 'Black' AND T2.Total_amount > (SELECT AVG(Total_amount) FROM happy_hour_member);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and transaction amounts for 'Black' card members whose spending during a happy hour event was greater than the average of all happy hour members.

sql: SELECT Month FROM happy_hour GROUP BY Month ORDER BY SUM(Num_of_shaff_in_charge) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single month with the highest total number of staff in charge for happy hours.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T3 ON T2.HH_ID = T3.HH_ID JOIN shop AS T4 ON T3.Shop_ID = T4.Shop_ID WHERE T1.Address = 'Hartford' AND T4.Open_Year = '2010';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a member from Hartford who is part of a happy hour hosted at a shop that opened in 2010.

sql: SELECT Shop_ID, Address FROM shop AS T1 WHERE T1.Score > 38.0 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM happy_hour AS T2 WHERE T2.Shop_ID = T1.Shop_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ID and address for highly-rated shops (score > 38) that also offer a happy hour.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age, T2.Total_amount FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Total_amount > 20 AND T1.Age < (SELECT avg(Age) FROM member);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying members younger than the average age who spent more than $20 during happy hour, listing their name, age, and the total amount spent.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_ID, T1.Address, T1.Num_of_staff FROM shop AS T1 WHERE T1.Num_of_staff > (SELECT AVG(T2.Num_of_staff) FROM shop AS T2 WHERE T2.Open_Year = T1.Open_Year);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing shops with a higher-than-average number of staff compared to other shops that opened in the same year, showing each shop's ID, address, and staff count.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Address = 'Waterbury' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Level_of_membership = 4;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who both live in 'Waterbury' and have a membership level of 4.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Membership_card = 'Black' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM member AS T2 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T3 ON T2.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T3.HH_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of members with a 'Black' membership card, excluding those who attended happy hour event #1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN happy_hour_member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN happy_hour AS T3 ON T2.HH_ID = T3.HH_ID WHERE T3.Shop_ID = (SELECT Shop_ID FROM shop ORDER BY Score DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of members who attended a happy hour at the single highest-scoring shop.

sql: SELECT Name, Address FROM member WHERE Address = 'Waterbury' UNION SELECT Name, Address FROM member WHERE Membership_card = 'Black';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and address for all members who either live in 'Waterbury' or hold a 'Black' membership card.

sql: SELECT Name, Population, Location FROM county_public_safety WHERE Police_force = 'RCMP';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, population, and location of counties that are policed by the RCMP.

sql: SELECT Name, White, Black, Asian FROM city WHERE County_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing cities within the county with ID 1, showing each city's name and the corresponding demographic breakdown for its White, Black, and Asian populations.

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM county_public_safety ORDER BY Population DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and population of the most populous county.

sql: SELECT Name FROM city WHERE Asian > 0.1;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of cities where the proportion of the Asian population is greater than 10%.

sql: SELECT Name, Crime_rate FROM county_public_safety ORDER BY Crime_rate DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each county's name and its crime rate, ordered from the highest crime rate to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows that list each city and its corresponding county name based on their shared county ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.City_ID) FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each county by name along with the total count of cities within that county.

sql: SELECT Location, AVG(Population) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each location along with the average population calculated for that specific area.

sql: SELECT Police_force, SUM(Police_officers) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Police_force;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each distinct police force and the total number of police officers associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.City_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of county public safety organizations that are associated with more than one city.

sql: SELECT Name, Hispanic FROM city WHERE County_ID = 1 ORDER BY Hispanic DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city with the highest Hispanic population percentage within the county with ID 1, showing the city's name and that percentage.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Population), SUM(Police_officers) FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, providing a summary for each location that includes the total population and the total number of police officers.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Crime_rate, T1.Population FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Aguadilla';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name, crime rate, and population of the county where the city 'Aguadilla' is located.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Asian) > 0.2;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of counties where the summed 'Asian' population metric across all of their constituent cities is greater than 0.2.

sql: SELECT Name, Crime_rate FROM county_public_safety WHERE Crime_rate > (SELECT AVG(Crime_rate) FROM county_public_safety);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and crime rates for all counties that have an above-average crime rate.

sql: SELECT Name FROM city WHERE County_ID IN (SELECT County_ID FROM county_public_safety WHERE Population > 200000);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of cities located in counties where the public safety population exceeds 200,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.City_ID) AS NumberOfCities FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID GROUP BY T1.County_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.City_ID) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing counties that contain more than two cities, along with the total count of cities within each of those counties.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'East' AND T1.Case_burden > 90;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of cities located within eastern counties that have a public safety case burden greater than 90.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.White) FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T1.Police_force = 'RCMP';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average 'White' population percentage for cities within counties that are policed by the RCMP.

sql: SELECT Name FROM city WHERE County_ID IN (SELECT County_ID FROM county_public_safety WHERE Crime_rate > 120) UNION SELECT Name FROM city WHERE Hispanic > 99.5;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of cities that are either located in counties with a crime rate above 120 or have a Hispanic population percentage greater than 99.5%.

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM county_public_safety AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.County_ID = T1.County_ID AND T2.Black > 7);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and population for counties that contain at least one city with a Black population percentage greater than 7.

sql: SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Population > 100000 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM county_public_safety WHERE Police_force = 'RCMP';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a county with a population over 100,000 that is not policed by the RCMP.

sql: SELECT Name, Residents_per_officer FROM county_public_safety WHERE Location = 'East' AND Residents_per_officer = (SELECT MAX(Residents_per_officer) FROM county_public_safety WHERE Location = 'East');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the county in the 'East' region that has the highest number of residents per police officer.

sql: WITH LocationAvgCrime AS (SELECT Location, AVG(Crime_rate) as AvgRate FROM county_public_safety GROUP BY Location) SELECT T1.Name, T1.Crime_rate, T2.AvgRate FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN LocationAvgCrime AS T2 ON T1.Location = T2.Location WHERE T1.Crime_rate > T2.AvgRate;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying counties with a crime rate higher than the average for their location, showing each county's name, its specific crime rate, and the location's average rate.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Name, T2.Multiracial FROM county_public_safety AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.County_ID = T2.County_ID WHERE T2.Multiracial = (SELECT MAX(Multiracial) FROM city);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city with the highest multiracial percentage, along with its corresponding county name and that percentage value.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE customer_first_name = 'Aniyah';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete profile details for the customer with the first name 'Aniyah'.

sql: SELECT card_number, card_type_code FROM Customers_Cards WHERE card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the card number and type for all customer cards that are identified as credit cards.

sql: SELECT transaction_id, transaction_amount FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_type = 'Refund';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the transaction ID and amount for all financial transactions that have been classified as a refund.

sql: SELECT account_id, customer_id, account_name FROM Accounts ORDER BY customer_id DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing account details, including account ID, customer ID, and account name, ordered by the customer ID from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_amount = 1701.23;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for any financial transaction with an exact amount of 1701.23.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T2.card_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows showing each customer's first and last name along with a count of the total number of cards they possess.

sql: SELECT T2.card_type_code, SUM(T1.transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id GROUP BY T2.card_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each card type along with the total sum of all transaction amounts processed for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Debit';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have a debit card.

sql: SELECT T1.account_id, T1.account_name, COUNT(T2.transaction_id) FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id GROUP BY T1.account_id;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each account's ID and name, along with a count of the total number of financial transactions for that account.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM Accounts AS T1 GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T1.account_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying customers who hold more than one account, showing each customer's ID and their total account count.

sql: SELECT T1.* FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 WHERE T1.account_id IN (SELECT T2.account_id FROM Accounts AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = (SELECT T3.customer_id FROM Customers AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_first_name = 'Aniyah'));
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT transaction_type, AVG(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions GROUP BY transaction_type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each financial transaction type alongside the average transaction amount for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer's first and last name along with the total amount they have spent across all their financial transactions.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T3.transaction_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.card_id = T3.card_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(T3.transaction_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first name, last name, and total transaction count for each customer who has made more than one transaction.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T3.transaction_amount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.transaction_amount > (SELECT AVG(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the customer's first name and the transaction amount for every transaction that is larger than the overall average transaction amount.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Accounts AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T2.other_account_details = 'VIP');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the customer ID, first name, and last name for all customers who hold a 'VIP' account.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP' UNION SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the customer ID and first name for all customers who are either designated as 'VIP' in their account details or who hold a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'VIP' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the customer ID, first name, and last name for customers who are both 'VIP' account holders and also have a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.other_account_details = 'Regular' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.card_type_code = 'Credit';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer ID and first name for customers who have a 'Regular' account but do not have a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.account_name, T3.transaction_amount, T4.card_type_code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN Customers_Cards AS T4 ON T3.card_id = T4.card_id WHERE T2.customer_id = T4.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing a financial transaction, including the customer's first name, account name, transaction amount, and the type of card used, ensuring the card belongs to the account holder.

sql: SELECT AVG(T3.transaction_amount) FROM (SELECT customer_id FROM Accounts GROUP BY customer_id HAVING count(account_id) > 1) AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id;
result_summary: Returns a single row with the average transaction amount across all transactions belonging to customers who hold more than one account.

sql: SELECT T1.card_id, T2.card_number FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Customers_Cards AS T2 ON T1.card_id = T2.card_id GROUP BY T1.card_id HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.transaction_type) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying cards by their ID and number that have been used for at least two distinct types of financial transactions.

sql: SELECT transaction_id, account_id, transaction_date, transaction_amount FROM (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY transaction_date DESC) as rn FROM Financial_Transactions) WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, with each row representing the single most recent financial transaction for each unique account.

sql: SELECT T1.transaction_id, T1.transaction_amount, T2.transaction_id, T2.transaction_amount FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON date(T1.transaction_date) = date(T2.transaction_date) AND T1.transaction_id < T2.transaction_id;
result_summary: Returns 79 rows, each representing a unique pair of financial transactions that occurred on the same day, showing the transaction ID and amount for both transactions in the pair.

sql: WITH CustomerTotals AS (SELECT T2.customer_id, SUM(T3.transaction_amount) AS TotalAmount FROM Accounts AS T2 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id GROUP BY T2.customer_id) SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, T2.TotalAmount FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN CustomerTotals AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.TotalAmount > (SELECT AVG(TotalAmount) FROM CustomerTotals);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers whose total transaction amount is greater than the overall average, listing each customer's first name, last name, and their total amount spent.

sql: SELECT * FROM member WHERE Nationality = 'Argentina';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows providing a list of all members from Argentina, including their member ID, name, and role.

sql: SELECT Location, Attendance FROM performance WHERE Host = 'New York Rangers';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the location and attendance for the performance hosted by the 'New York Rangers'.

sql: SELECT Name, Role FROM member WHERE Member_ID = '1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and role for the specific member identified by Member_ID '1'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM member;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total count of all members.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Host FROM performance ORDER BY Host DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique hosts of performances, ordered in reverse alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Num_of_Pieces FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_Pieces = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of members who had exactly 2 pieces associated with their attendance.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Attendance FROM performance AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Performance_ID = T2.Performance_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Madison Square Garden';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the date and attendance for a performance held at 'Madison Square Garden' where members were in attendance.

sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(Member_ID) FROM member GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each nationality along with the total number of members from that nation.

sql: SELECT T1.Host, AVG(T1.Attendance) FROM performance AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Host;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each host along with their average performance attendance.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Num_of_Pieces) FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each member's name along with the total number of pieces recorded for them across all attendance records.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Performance_ID = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all members who attended the performance with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Location FROM performance AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Performance_ID = T2.Performance_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_Pieces > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the locations of performances where a member played more than two pieces.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Location FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Argentina';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of members from Argentina and the locations of the performances they have attended.

sql: SELECT Location, Attendance FROM performance WHERE Attendance > (SELECT AVG(Attendance) FROM performance);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the location and attendance for performances that had an attendance greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Member_ID HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_Pieces) > 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who have contributed a total of more than 3 pieces across all their attendance records.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Nationality FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance WHERE Num_of_Pieces = 4);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and nationality of members who have an attendance record corresponding to exactly 4 pieces.

sql: SELECT T3.Location, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T1.Role = 'Violin' GROUP BY T3.Location;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each performance location and the total count of attending members with the role 'Violin'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Role = 'Prime Violin' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T3.Host = 'New York Rangers';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Nationality = 'Argentina' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN member_attendance AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Performance_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all members from Argentina who did not attend performance number 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Host, T1.Date FROM performance AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member_attendance AS T2 WHERE T2.Performance_ID = T1.Performance_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the host and date of the performance for which no member attendance was recorded.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Member_ID HAVING COUNT(Performance_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the member who has attended more than one performance.

sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Nationality = 'Uruguay' UNION SELECT Host FROM performance WHERE Attendance > 1800;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing a combined list of names, including all members from Uruguay and the hosts of any performance with an attendance greater than 1800.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Role FROM member AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Member_ID, SUM(Num_of_Pieces) AS TotalPieces FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Member_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID ORDER BY T2.TotalPieces DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and role of the top 5 members who have participated in the most musical pieces, ordered from highest to lowest total pieces.

sql: SELECT Host, Date FROM performance WHERE Performance_ID = (SELECT Performance_ID FROM member_attendance GROUP BY Performance_ID ORDER BY COUNT(Member_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the host and date of the single performance that had the highest member attendance.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member_attendance AS T2 JOIN performance AS T3 ON T2.Performance_ID = T3.Performance_ID WHERE T2.Member_ID = T1.Member_ID AND T3.Location = 'Verizon Center');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all members who have attended a performance at the 'Verizon Center'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE Major = 600;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows containing the complete records for all students belonging to major 600.

sql: SELECT ClubName, ClubLocation FROM Club ORDER BY ClubName;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each club's name and its location, ordered alphabetically by club name.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of all female students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Position FROM Member_of_club;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique positions that members hold within a club.

sql: SELECT ClubName FROM Club WHERE ClubLocation = 'AKW';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all clubs with the location code 'AKW'.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T2.ClubName FROM Member_of_club AS T1 JOIN Club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each student's ID and the corresponding name of the club they are a member of.

sql: SELECT T2.ClubName, COUNT(T1.StuID) FROM Member_of_club AS T1 JOIN Club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID GROUP BY T2.ClubName;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each club by name along with the total count of its student members.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Member_of_club WHERE ClubID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who are members of the club with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each student major along with the average age of students in that major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Position = 'President';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of any student who is a club president.

sql: SELECT ClubName FROM Club WHERE ClubID IN (SELECT DISTINCT ClubID FROM Member_of_club);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all clubs that have at least one member.

sql: SELECT city_code, COUNT(StuID) FROM Student GROUP BY city_code;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows, listing each city code along with the total count of students for that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.ClubName, T3.Position FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID JOIN Club AS T2 ON T3.ClubID = T2.ClubID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows detailing student club memberships, providing each student's first and last name, the name of their club, and their specific position within that club.

sql: SELECT T2.ClubName, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID JOIN Club AS T2 ON T3.ClubID = T2.ClubID GROUP BY T2.ClubName;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each club name along with the average age of its student members.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Member_of_club);
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the first and last names of students who are not members of any club.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(T3.Age) FROM Student AS T3 JOIN Member_of_club AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.StuID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, and age of the oldest student who is a member of a club.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName, COUNT(T2.StuID) FROM Club AS T1 LEFT JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.ClubID = T2.ClubID GROUP BY T1.ClubName;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each club's name and the corresponding total count of its student members, including clubs with zero members.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID WHERE T3.ClubName = 'Bootup Baltimore' AND T1.Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students in the 'Bootup Baltimore' club who are younger than the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.ClubID = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.ClubID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who are members of the club with ID 1 but are not members of the club with ID 2.

sql: SELECT T1.ClubName FROM Club AS T1 WHERE T1.ClubLocation = 'AKW' INTERSECT SELECT T2.ClubName FROM Club AS T2 JOIN Member_of_club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of clubs at the 'AKW' location that also have at least one member.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Position = 'President';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who are either from Baltimore (city code 'BAL') or are the president of a club.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY COUNT(T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the major with the most students who are members of a club.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID WHERE T1.Advisor = 1121 AND T3.ClubLocation = 'AKW';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of students who are advised by advisor #1121 and are members of a club located at 'AKW'.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.ClubID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Club AS T3 ON T2.ClubID = T3.ClubID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows counting the number of unique clubs that students belong to, grouped by the student's city code.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Member_of_club AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.ClubID IN (SELECT ClubID FROM Member_of_club WHERE Position = 'President');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all students who are members of any club that has a 'President'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Ref_Template_Types;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all available template types, showing each template's type code and its corresponding full description.

sql: SELECT * FROM Documents WHERE Document_Name = 'Summer Show';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available information for the document named 'Summer Show'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Paragraphs WHERE Paragraph_Text = 'Korea';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with all available details for paragraphs where the entire text is exactly 'Korea'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Documents;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing one value: the total count of all documents.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Template_Type_Code FROM Templates;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique template type codes available from the templates table.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Version_Number FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T1.Document_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the document with ID 1 and the version number of the template it uses.

sql: SELECT T1.Template_ID, T2.Template_Type_Description FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code WHERE T1.Template_Type_Code = 'PPT';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and type description for all templates specifically classified as presentations (type code 'PPT').

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, COUNT(T2.Paragraph_ID) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T1.Document_Name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each document by name, along with a count of the total number of paragraphs it contains.

sql: SELECT avg(Version_Number) FROM Templates;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average version number calculated across all templates.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Date_Effective_From > '2010-01-01';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a document that is based on a template which became effective after January 1, 2010.

sql: SELECT T2.Template_Type_Description, COUNT(T1.Template_ID) FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Template_Type_Description;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each template type description and the total count of templates available for that type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the unique names of all documents that have at least one associated paragraph.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code WHERE T3.Template_Type_Description = 'Presentation';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all documents that use a template classified as a 'Presentation'.

sql: SELECT T1.Paragraph_Text FROM Paragraphs AS T1 JOIN Documents AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Document_Name = 'Summer Show';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the paragraph text from the document named 'Summer Show'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Paragraphs AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID GROUP BY T1.Document_Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Paragraph_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of all documents that contain at least one paragraph.

sql: SELECT T2.Template_Type_Description FROM Templates AS T1 JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T2 ON T1.Template_Type_Code = T2.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Template_Type_Description HAVING count(T1.Template_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions of template types that have more than one template associated with them.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Template_ID FROM Documents WHERE Template_ID IN (SELECT Template_ID FROM Templates WHERE Version_Number > (SELECT AVG(Version_Number) FROM Templates));
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and ID for documents that are based on templates with a version number greater than the average version number of all templates.

sql: SELECT T1.Template_Type_Description FROM Ref_Template_Types AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Templates AS T2 WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code = T1.Template_Type_Code);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the descriptions for each template type that has at least one corresponding template instance.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID WHERE T2.Template_Type_Code IN ('CV', 'AD');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of documents that were created using either a 'CV' (Curriculum Vitae) or 'AD' (Advertisement) template.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Document_Description FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID = 3 UNION SELECT Document_Name, Document_Description FROM Documents WHERE Template_ID = 7;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and description for documents that either have a specific ID of 3 or use template ID 7.

sql: SELECT Template_ID FROM Documents EXCEPT SELECT Template_ID FROM Templates WHERE Template_Type_Code = 'PP';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing the template IDs for all documents, excluding those with a 'PP' template type code.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.ParaCount FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Document_ID, COUNT(*) AS ParaCount FROM Paragraphs GROUP BY Document_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each document's name along with the total count of paragraphs it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.Template_ID, T1.Version_Number, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Documents AS T2 WHERE T2.Template_ID = T1.Template_ID) AS Document_Count FROM Templates AS T1 WHERE T1.Template_Type_Code = 'PP';
