result_summary: Returns 6 rows for templates with the type code 'PP', showing each template's ID, version number, and the total count of documents that use it.

sql: SELECT T3.Template_Type_Description FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Templates AS T2 ON T1.Template_ID = T2.Template_ID JOIN Ref_Template_Types AS T3 ON T2.Template_Type_Code = T3.Template_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Template_Type_Description ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Document_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the description of the template type that is associated with the highest number of documents.

sql: SELECT Document_Name FROM Documents WHERE Document_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Document_ID FROM Paragraphs);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of documents that have no associated paragraphs or content.

sql: SELECT Name FROM musical WHERE Year = 1986;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all musicals that premiered in the year 1986.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM actor;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and age of every actor.

sql: SELECT Name, Category FROM musical WHERE Nominee = 'Bob Fosse';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and nomination category for all musicals where 'Bob Fosse' was the nominee.

sql: SELECT Name, Character FROM actor WHERE age = 22;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and character played by any actor who is 22 years old.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Award FROM musical;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct types of awards received by musicals.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each actor's name alongside the name of the musical they performed in.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Actor_ID) FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each musical by name along with the total number of actors in its cast.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.age) FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each musical's name alongside the average age of the actors performing in that musical.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID WHERE T2.Award = 'Tony Award';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing actors and the name of the corresponding Tony Award-winning musical they performed in.

sql: SELECT Nominee, COUNT(*) FROM musical GROUP BY Nominee;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each nominee and the total count of their nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Character FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'The Phantom of the Opera';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the actors and the characters they played in the musical 'The Phantom of the Opera'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID WHERE T1.age > 25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an actor and the musical they are in, for all actors older than 25.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM actor WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM actor);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for all actors who are older than the average age of all actors in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Actor_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of musicals that have more than one actor in their cast.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID WHERE T1.age = (SELECT MIN(age) FROM actor);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of musicals in which the youngest actor or actors have performed.

sql: SELECT Nominee FROM musical GROUP BY Nominee HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of nominees who have received more than one musical nomination.

sql: SELECT Name FROM actor WHERE Musical_ID IN (SELECT Musical_ID FROM musical WHERE Nominee = 'Bob Fosse');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of actors who have performed in a musical for which 'Bob Fosse' was a nominee.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID GROUP BY T2.Musical_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Actor_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the musical that has the largest cast (the most actors).

sql: SELECT Name FROM actor WHERE Musical_ID = (SELECT Musical_ID FROM actor WHERE Name = 'Ray Meagher') AND Name != 'Ray Meagher';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the actor who performed in the same musical as 'Ray Meagher'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING AVG(T1.age) < 25;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of musicals where the average age of the cast is less than 25.

sql: SELECT Name FROM actor UNION SELECT Nominee FROM musical;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the unique names of all individuals who are either an actor or a musical nominee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM actor AS T1 WHERE T1.Musical_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM actor AS T2 WHERE T2.age > 25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an actor who is both in the musical with ID 1 and is older than 25.

sql: SELECT Name FROM musical WHERE Year = 1986 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM musical WHERE Nominee = 'Cleavant Derricks';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of musicals from the year 1986, excluding any that had 'Cleavant Derricks' as a nominee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM musical AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM actor AS T2 WHERE T2.Musical_ID = T1.Musical_ID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of musicals that have at least one actor associated with them.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Year FROM actor AS T1 JOIN musical AS T2 ON T1.Musical_ID = T2.Musical_ID WHERE T1.age < (SELECT MAX(age) FROM actor);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each actor's name and the year of the musical they appeared in, excluding the oldest actor(s) from the results.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Year FROM singer WHERE Citizenship = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and birth year for each singer with United States citizenship.

sql: SELECT Title FROM song WHERE Highest_Position = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the titles of all songs that have reached the number one chart position.

sql: SELECT Name, Citizenship FROM singer WHERE Birth_Year = 1944.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and citizenship of any singer who was born in the year 1944.

sql: SELECT Name, Net_Worth_Millions FROM singer ORDER BY Net_Worth_Millions DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each singer's name and their net worth in millions, ordered from the highest net worth to the lowest.

sql: SELECT Title, Sales FROM song ORDER BY Sales ASC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title and sales for each song, ordered from the lowest sales to the highest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Title FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Citizenship = 'France';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of singers from France and the titles of their songs.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Song_ID) FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each singer by name and the total number of songs they have.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Sales) FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each singer's name along with their total sales, calculated by summing the sales from all of their songs.

sql: SELECT Citizenship, AVG(Net_Worth_Millions) FROM singer GROUP BY Citizenship;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each citizenship and the average net worth in millions for singers from that country.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Highest_Position = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of singers who have had at least one song reach the number one chart position.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Title FROM song AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Sales > 500000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the singer's name and song title for all songs with sales exceeding 500,000.

sql: SELECT AVG(Sales) FROM song WHERE Singer_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns a single row showing the average sales for all songs by the singer with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Song_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of singers who have recorded more than one song.

sql: SELECT Title, Sales FROM song WHERE Sales > (SELECT AVG(Sales) FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles and sales for all songs with sales figures greater than the overall average sales.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Singer_ID IN (SELECT Singer_ID FROM song WHERE Highest_Position = 1.0);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all singers who have had a song reach the number one position.

sql: SELECT Name, Net_Worth_Millions FROM singer WHERE Net_Worth_Millions < (SELECT AVG(Net_Worth_Millions) FROM singer);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and net worth (in millions) of singers whose net worth is below the average for all singers in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Net_Worth_Millions FROM singer AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID AND T2.Sales > 1000000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and net worth of singers who have at least one song with sales greater than 1,000,000.

sql: SELECT Citizenship FROM singer GROUP BY Citizenship HAVING AVG(Birth_Year) < 1947.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the citizenships for which the average birth year of its singers is before 1947.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 WHERE T1.Citizenship = 'United States' AND (SELECT count(*) FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID) = 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of singers from the United States who have no recorded songs.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T3.Title FROM (SELECT Singer_ID, MAX(Sales) AS max_s FROM song GROUP BY Singer_ID) AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID JOIN song AS T3 ON T1.Singer_ID = T3.Singer_ID AND T1.max_s = T3.Sales;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each singer's name alongside the title of their single best-selling song based on sales.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Citizenship = 'France' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Highest_Position = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing a unique list of singer names, combining all singers with French citizenship and all singers who have had a #1 hit song.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Singer_ID NOT IN (SELECT Singer_ID FROM song);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of singers who are not associated with any songs.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM song AS T1 WHERE T1.Sales > (SELECT AVG(T2.Sales) FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.Singer_ID = T1.Singer_ID);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the titles of songs that have sold more than the average for their respective artist.

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Citizenship = 'United States' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T1.Highest_Position = 1.0;
error_message: Discarded: SQL Runtime Error - no such column: T1.Highest_Position

sql: SELECT Name FROM singer WHERE Citizenship = 'United States' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.Singer_ID = T2.Singer_ID WHERE T2.Highest_Position = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of singers from the United States who have never had a song reach the number one position.

sql: SELECT Title, Director FROM movie WHERE Year = 1999;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the title and director for all movies released in the year 1999.

sql: SELECT Book_Title, Author_or_Editor FROM book_club WHERE Category = 'Gay M/SF';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the book titles and their authors or editors for all books within the 'Gay M/SF' category.

sql: SELECT Title, Director FROM movie WHERE Budget_million = 5.0;

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T2.Title FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id;

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T2.Book_Title FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN book_club AS T2 ON T1.book_club_id = T2.book_club_id;

sql: SELECT Incorporated_in, COUNT(*) FROM culture_company GROUP BY Incorporated_in;

sql: SELECT AVG(Budget_million) FROM movie WHERE Year = 1999;

sql: SELECT Publisher, COUNT(*) FROM book_club WHERE Result = 'Nom' GROUP BY Publisher;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each publisher and the total count of their book club nominations.

sql: SELECT Director, COUNT(*) FROM movie GROUP BY Director HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T2.Title, T3.Book_Title FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id JOIN book_club AS T3 ON T1.book_club_id = T3.book_club_id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'China';

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T2.Budget_million > (SELECT AVG(Budget_million) FROM movie);

sql: SELECT T1.Type FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN book_club AS T2 ON T1.book_club_id = T2.book_club_id WHERE T2.Category = 'Gay M/SF' GROUP BY T1.Type HAVING count(T1.Company_name) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique types of companies that are associated with book clubs in the 'Gay M/SF' category.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Director FROM movie AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM culture_company AS T2 WHERE T2.movie_id = T1.movie_id);

sql: SELECT Company_name FROM culture_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China' UNION SELECT Company_name FROM culture_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'Hong Kong';

sql: SELECT Publisher FROM book_club WHERE Year = 1989 INTERSECT SELECT Publisher FROM book_club WHERE Category = 'Gay M/SF';

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM movie AS T1 WHERE T1.Year = 1999 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Title FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'China';

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T2.Title, T3.Book_Title FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id JOIN book_club AS T3 ON T1.book_club_id = T3.book_club_id WHERE T2.Gross_worldwide > 3000000 AND T3.Result = 'Nom';

sql: SELECT T1.Type, SUM(T1.Group_Equity_Shareholding) FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN book_club AS T2 ON T1.book_club_id = T2.book_club_id WHERE T2.Year = 1989 GROUP BY T1.Type;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Director FROM movie WHERE Director NOT IN (SELECT T2.Director FROM culture_company AS T1 JOIN movie AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id WHERE T1.Type = 'Joint Venture');

sql: SELECT Director FROM movie GROUP BY Director HAVING SUM(Gross_worldwide) > 100000;

sql: SELECT T1.project_id, T1.project_details FROM Projects AS T1 JOIN Project_Outcomes AS T2 ON T1.project_id = T2.project_id JOIN Research_Outcomes AS T3 ON T2.outcome_code = T3.outcome_code WHERE T3.outcome_description = 'Research Patent';

sql: SELECT T1.organisation_details, T3.Project_Count, T4.Total_Grant FROM Organisations AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT organisation_id, COUNT(*) AS Project_Count FROM Projects GROUP BY organisation_id) AS T3 ON T1.organisation_id = T3.organisation_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT organisation_id, SUM(grant_amount) AS Total_Grant FROM Grants GROUP BY organisation_id) AS T4 ON T1.organisation_id = T4.organisation_id;

sql: SELECT ID, Name, District, Population FROM city WHERE CountryCode = 'AFG';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID, name, district, and population for each city located in Afghanistan.

sql: SELECT Name, Population, LifeExpectancy FROM country WHERE Continent = 'Africa' ORDER BY Population DESC;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Population, T1.CountryCode FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.Population = (SELECT MAX(T2.Population) FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.CountryCode = T1.CountryCode) ORDER BY T1.Population DESC;

sql: SELECT Name, GNP, Continent FROM country WHERE Continent = 'Africa' AND Population > 12000000 UNION SELECT Name, GNP, Continent FROM country WHERE Continent = 'Asia' AND Population > 22000000;
result_summary: Returns 37 rows listing the name, Gross National Product (GNP), and continent for countries in Africa with a population over 12 million, combined with countries in Asia having a population over 22 million.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 WHERE T1.Continent = 'North America' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM country AS T2 JOIN countrylanguage AS T3 ON T2.Code = T3.CountryCode WHERE T3.Language = 'English' AND T2.Continent = 'North America';
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the names of countries in North America that do not have English listed as a spoken language.

sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'North America' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent = 'Africa' AND T2.IsOfficial = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the official languages that are common to countries in both North America and Africa.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS CityName, T2.Name AS CountryName, T3.Language FROM city AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code JOIN countrylanguage AS T3 ON T2.Code = T3.CountryCode WHERE T1.CountryCode = 'AFG';
result_summary: Returns 20 rows pairing each city in Afghanistan with every language spoken in the country, providing the city name, country name, and the language.

sql: SELECT AVG(MaxPop) FROM (SELECT MAX(T1.Population) AS MaxPop FROM city AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode = T2.Code WHERE T2.Continent = 'Asia' GROUP BY T2.Code);
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average population of the largest city from each country on the continent of Asia.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Continent FROM country AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM country AS T2 WHERE T2.Continent = T1.Continent AND T2.Population > 20000000);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique continents that contain at least one country with a population greater than 20 million.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.GNP > 5000 GROUP BY T1.Code, T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Population) > 2000000;
result_summary: Returns 71 rows listing the names of countries with a GNP over 5000 and a total population across their major cities greater than 2,000,000.

sql: SELECT T1.Language, COUNT(T1.CountryCode) AS CountryCount FROM countrylanguage AS T1 WHERE T1.IsOfficial = 'F' GROUP BY T1.Language ORDER BY CountryCount DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 most widespread unofficial languages, showing the number of countries where each is spoken, ordered from the highest country count to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Region, T1.Population FROM country AS T1 WHERE T1.IndepYear = 1919 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Region, T1.Population FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = 'Dutch';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, region, and population for any country that gained independence in 1919, excluding those where Dutch is a spoken language.

sql: SELECT Name, Continent, GNP FROM country WHERE GNP > (SELECT AVG(GNP) FROM country WHERE Continent = 'North America' AND GNP IS NOT NULL) AND Continent = 'North America';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the North American countries with a Gross National Product (GNP) that is above the average for the continent.

sql: SELECT * FROM shop WHERE Open_Year = 2006;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for shops that opened during the year 2006.

sql: SELECT Device, Carrier FROM device WHERE Software_Platform = 'Android';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the device model and its associated carrier for all devices that use the 'Android' software platform.

sql: SELECT Shop_Name, Location FROM shop ORDER BY Open_Year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing all shops and their locations, sorted to show the most recently opened shops first.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Carrier FROM device;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows providing a unique list of all mobile carriers associated with the devices on record.

sql: SELECT Device, Software_Platform FROM device WHERE Device_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the device name and software platform for the specific device with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, SUM(T2.Quantity) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each shop name along with the total quantity of items in stock for that shop.

sql: SELECT T1.Device, SUM(T2.Quantity) FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID GROUP BY T1.Device;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, with each row showing a device name and the corresponding total quantity available in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, COUNT(T2.Device_ID) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each shop's name along with the total count of devices it has in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Device, COUNT(T2.Shop_ID) FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID GROUP BY T1.Device;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each device and a count of the number of different shops that have that device in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, T3.Device, T2.Quantity FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN device AS T3 ON T2.Device_ID = T3.Device_ID WHERE T1.Shop_Name = 'Best Buy';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the device name and its corresponding stock quantity for the shop named 'Best Buy'.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_Name HAVING SUM(T2.Quantity) > 100;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of shops that have a total stock quantity of more than 100 items.

sql: SELECT T1.Device FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID WHERE T2.Quantity > 100;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all devices that have more than 100 units currently in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, T1.Location FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Device_ID IN (SELECT Device_ID FROM device WHERE Software_Platform = 'Android');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and location of shops that stock devices running on the 'Android' software platform.

sql: SELECT T1.Device, T1.Carrier FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T3.Open_Year = 2006;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the device and its carrier for any device stocked in a shop that opened in the year 2006.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN device AS T3 ON T2.Device_ID = T3.Device_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Software_Platform) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Shop_Name FROM shop AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM stock AS T2 WHERE T2.Shop_ID = T1.Shop_ID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of shops that currently have no items in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Device FROM device AS T1 WHERE T1.Device_ID IN (SELECT Device_ID FROM stock GROUP BY Device_ID HAVING COUNT(Shop_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of devices that are available in more than one shop.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Quantity > (SELECT AVG(Quantity) FROM stock);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of shops that hold any item in a quantity greater than the average stock quantity across all shops.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, T1.Location FROM shop AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Shop_Name, T2.Location FROM shop AS T2 JOIN stock AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID JOIN device AS T4 ON T3.Device_ID = T4.Device_ID WHERE T4.Software_Platform = 'iOS';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names and locations of shops that do not stock any devices running the 'iOS' software platform.

sql: SELECT Shop_Name FROM shop UNION ALL SELECT Device FROM device;
result_summary: Returns 18 rows, creating a single combined list containing all shop names and all device names.

sql: SELECT T1.Software_Platform, AVG(T2.Quantity) FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID GROUP BY T1.Software_Platform;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each software platform and the corresponding average stock quantity for devices on that platform.

sql: SELECT Device_ID FROM stock WHERE Shop_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT Device_ID FROM stock WHERE Shop_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the device ID for a device that is available in stock at both Shop 1 and Shop 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Shop_Name, T2.TotalQuantity FROM shop AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Shop_ID, SUM(Quantity) AS TotalQuantity FROM stock GROUP BY Shop_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID ORDER BY T2.TotalQuantity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the shop with the highest total stock quantity, listing the shop's name and its total quantity.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Shop_Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN device AS T3 ON T2.Device_ID = T3.Device_ID WHERE T1.Open_Year < 2010 AND T3.Software_Platform = 'Android';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of shops that opened before 2010 and have Android devices in stock.

sql: SELECT T1.Carrier FROM device AS T1 JOIN stock AS T2 ON T1.Device_ID = T2.Device_ID GROUP BY T1.Carrier HAVING count(*) > (SELECT CAST(count(*) AS REAL) / count(DISTINCT T3.Carrier) FROM stock AS T4 JOIN device AS T3 ON T4.Device_ID = T3.Device_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the carrier whose total number of devices in stock is greater than the average stock count across all carriers.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM employee WHERE City = 'Bristol';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of all employees who are located in the city of Bristol.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM shop WHERE Number_products > 6000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and location of shops that carry more than 6000 products.

sql: SELECT Employee_ID, Bonus FROM evaluation WHERE Year_awarded = '2011';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the employee ID and bonus amount for all evaluations awarded in the year 2011.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM employee ORDER BY Age ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each employee's name and age, ordered from the youngest employee to the oldest.

sql: SELECT Employee_ID, Shop_ID FROM hiring WHERE Is_full_time = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the employee and shop IDs for all hiring records of part-time employees.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Start_from FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each employee's name alongside their hiring start year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Employee_ID) FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each shop's name along with the total number of employees working in that shop.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Bonus) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each employee's name along with the total sum of their bonuses.

sql: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM employee GROUP BY City;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each city along with the total number of employees working in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Bonus = 3200.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age of employees who received a bonus of exactly $3200.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the employee who has received more than one performance evaluation.

sql: SELECT Name, Number_products FROM shop WHERE Number_products > (SELECT AVG(Number_products) FROM shop);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and product counts for shops that have a higher-than-average number of products.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID WHERE T3.Location = 'Valkeakoski' AND T1.City = 'Bristol';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the employee who lives in 'Bristol' and works at a shop located in 'Valkeakoski'.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T2.Bonus) FROM hiring AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T1.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each shop's name along with the average bonus received by its employees.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hiring AS T2 WHERE T2.Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID AND T2.Start_from > '2003') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM evaluation AS T3 WHERE T3.Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID AND T3.Bonus > 2900);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who were hired after 2003 and received a bonus greater than 2900.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T1.Age) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID JOIN shop AS T3 ON T2.Shop_ID = T3.Shop_ID GROUP BY T3.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Employee_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the shop name and the average age of its employees, but only for shops that have hired more than one person.

sql: SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE Employee_ID NOT IN (SELECT Employee_ID FROM evaluation);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of employees who have not yet received an evaluation.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.City = 'Bristol' UNION SELECT T3.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.Employee_ID = T3.Employee_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Valkeakoski';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of employees who either live in the city of Bristol or work for a shop located in Valkeakoski.

sql: SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE Age > 40 EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Is_full_time = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of an employee who is over 40 years old and is not a full-time employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Bonus FROM employee AS T1 JOIN evaluation AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.Bonus > (SELECT AVG(Bonus) FROM evaluation);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and bonus amount for each employee whose bonus is greater than the company-wide average.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Is_full_time = 'T' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM shop AS T1 JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID WHERE T2.Start_from > '2005';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of shops that have both full-time employees and have hired staff after 2005.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM employee AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Employee_ID, MAX(Year_awarded) AS latest_year FROM evaluation GROUP BY Employee_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Employee_ID = T2.Employee_ID WHERE T2.latest_year = '2016';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of employees whose most recent evaluation award was received in the year 2016.

sql: SELECT Name FROM employee WHERE City = (SELECT City FROM employee WHERE Name = 'George Chuter') AND Name <> 'George Chuter';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all employees who live in the same city as 'George Chuter', excluding 'George Chuter' himself.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Number_products FROM shop AS T1 WHERE T1.Shop_ID IN (SELECT Shop_ID FROM hiring GROUP BY Shop_ID HAVING count(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and number of products for shops that have more than one hiring record.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location, COUNT(T2.Employee_ID) FROM shop AS T1 LEFT JOIN hiring AS T2 ON T1.Shop_ID = T2.Shop_ID GROUP BY T1.Shop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each shop's name and location along with the total count of employees hired for that shop.

sql: SELECT * FROM Movie WHERE year = 1939;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details (movie ID, title, year, and director) for movies released in the year 1939.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer WHERE rID = 201;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the reviewer with the specific ID of 201.

sql: SELECT title, director FROM Movie ORDER BY year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title and director of each movie, ordered from the most recent to the oldest release year.

sql: SELECT * FROM Rating WHERE stars = 4;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all recorded details for movie ratings that were exactly 4 stars.

sql: SELECT title FROM Movie WHERE director = 'George Lucas';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of the movie directed by 'George Lucas'.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.stars FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T2.stars = 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of all movies that have received a 2-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.ratingDate = '2011-01-22';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of reviewers who submitted a rating on January 22, 2011.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Rating WHERE mID = 101;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of ratings for the movie with an ID of 101.

sql: SELECT T1.title, AVG(T2.stars) FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each movie title along with its average star rating, calculated from all available ratings for that movie.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.mID) FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each reviewer's name and the total number of movie ratings they have submitted.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T1.title, T3.stars FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T3 ON T1.mID = T3.mID JOIN Reviewer AS T2 ON T3.rID = T2.rID WHERE T1.title = 'Gone with the Wind';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the individual reviews for the movie 'Gone with the Wind', showing the reviewer's name and the star rating they provided for each review.

sql: SELECT mID, MIN(stars), MAX(stars) FROM Rating GROUP BY mID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the movie ID, minimum star rating, and maximum star rating for movies that have been reviewed more than once.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 WHERE T1.mID IN (SELECT T2.mID FROM Rating AS T2 WHERE T2.stars > 2);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the titles of movies that have received at least one rating greater than 2 stars.

sql: SELECT name FROM Reviewer WHERE rID NOT IN (SELECT rID FROM Rating);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID WHERE T2.stars = (SELECT MAX(stars) FROM Rating);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of all movies that have received the highest possible star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID GROUP BY T1.rID HAVING COUNT(T2.mID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of reviewers who have provided more than one movie rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T1.director, T2.AvgStars FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN (SELECT mID, AVG(stars) AS AvgStars FROM Rating GROUP BY mID) AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each movie's title and director, along with its corresponding average star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Rating AS T2 JOIN Reviewer AS T3 ON T2.rID = T3.rID WHERE T2.mID = T1.mID AND T3.name = 'Sarah Martinez');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of the movie that was rated by the reviewer 'Sarah Martinez'.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.mID, T2.rID HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the titles of movies that have been rated more than once by the same reviewer.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.title FROM Reviewer AS T1, Movie AS T2 EXCEPT SELECT T3.name, T4.title FROM Reviewer AS T3 JOIN Rating AS T5 ON T3.rID = T5.rID JOIN Movie AS T4 ON T5.mID = T4.mID;
result_summary: Returns 52 rows listing all combinations of reviewers and movies for which the reviewer has not yet submitted a rating.

sql: SELECT T1.director FROM Movie AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.mID = T2.mID GROUP BY T1.director ORDER BY COUNT(T1.mID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the director with the highest number of rated movies, determined by counting their entries in the rating table.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.ratingDate = (SELECT MAX(ratingDate) FROM Rating);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the reviewer who submitted the most recent rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T1.year FROM Movie AS T1 WHERE T1.mID IN (SELECT mID FROM Rating WHERE stars=2) AND T1.mID IN (SELECT mID FROM Rating WHERE stars=4);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the title and year of movies that have received both a 2-star rating and a 4-star rating.

sql: SELECT T1.title, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Rating AS T2 WHERE T2.mID = T1.mID) AS RatingCount FROM Movie AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title of each movie along with the total number of ratings it has received.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM Reviewer AS T1 JOIN Rating AS T2 ON T1.rID = T2.rID WHERE T2.stars > (SELECT AVG(T3.stars) FROM Rating AS T3 WHERE T3.mID = T2.mID);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of reviewers who have given at least one movie a rating that is higher than that movie's average rating.

sql: SELECT * FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'Russia';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all available details (ID, nationality, name, birth date, and height) for every person whose nationality is 'Russia'.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and height of each person, ordered from the tallest to the shortest.

sql: SELECT Poker_Player_ID, Earnings FROM poker_player WHERE Best_Finish = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and earnings for each poker player who has achieved a first-place finish.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 185;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of people who are taller than 185.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM poker_player;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the total count of all poker players.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Earnings FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and total earnings for each person who is a poker player.

sql: SELECT T2.Nationality, COUNT(T1.Poker_Player_ID) FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each nationality and the total number of poker players from that nationality.

sql: SELECT T2.Nationality, AVG(T1.Earnings) AS Average_Earnings FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each nationality along with the average earnings for poker players from that country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Earnings FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Nationality = 'Russia' ORDER BY T1.Earnings DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and earnings of poker players from Russia, ordered from highest to lowest earnings.

sql: SELECT T2.Nationality, SUM(T1.Final_Table_Made) AS Total_Final_Tables FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality HAVING COUNT(T1.Poker_Player_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the total number of final tables made by players, filtered to show only nationalities that have more than one poker player.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Money_Rank FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Best_Finish = 2.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the name and money rank of poker players whose best tournament finish was second place.

sql: SELECT Name, Nationality FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'Russia' UNION SELECT Name, Nationality FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'Bulgaria';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names and nationalities of all people who are from either Russia or Bulgaria.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Earnings FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Earnings > (SELECT AVG(Earnings) FROM poker_player);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the names and total earnings for poker players with above-average earnings.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID IN (SELECT T1.People_ID FROM poker_player AS T1 WHERE T1.Best_Finish = 1.0 AND T1.Final_Table_Made > 20);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of poker players who have achieved a first-place finish and have made more than 20 final tables.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM poker_player AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID AND T2.Money_Rank < 100);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the nationality and name for each poker player who is ranked within the top 99 by money.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Best_Finish = 1.0 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Earnings > 100000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of poker players who have both won a tournament (achieved a 1st place finish) and earned more than $100,000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Nationality = 'Russia' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Best_Finish != 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of Russian poker players who have achieved a first-place finish in a tournament.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(T2.Height) FROM people AS T2 WHERE T2.Nationality = T1.Nationality);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and height of each person who is taller than the average height for their respective nationality.

sql: WITH Nat_Avg AS (SELECT T2.Nationality, AVG(T1.Earnings) as avg_earn FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality) SELECT T3.Name, T4.Earnings, T5.avg_earn FROM people AS T3 JOIN poker_player AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID JOIN Nat_Avg AS T5 ON T3.Nationality = T5.Nationality WHERE T4.Earnings > T5.avg_earn;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing poker players whose earnings are greater than the average for their nationality, showing each player's name, their individual earnings, and their country's average earnings.

sql: SELECT T2.Nationality, AVG(T1.Final_Table_Made) FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality ORDER BY AVG(T1.Earnings) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the nationality and average number of final tables made for players from the country with the single highest average earnings.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Earnings FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Nationality = 'Russia' AND T1.Earnings = (SELECT MAX(T3.Earnings) FROM poker_player AS T3 JOIN people AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.People_ID WHERE T4.Nationality = 'Russia');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the top-earning poker player from Russia along with their total earnings.

sql: SELECT T2.Nationality FROM poker_player AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Nationality HAVING AVG(T1.Final_Table_Made) > (SELECT AVG(Final_Table_Made) FROM poker_player);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the nationality of poker players who, on average, have made more final tables than the average across all players.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T_FILTERED.Earnings FROM (SELECT People_ID, Earnings FROM poker_player WHERE Final_Table_Made > 40) AS T_FILTERED JOIN people AS T2 ON T_FILTERED.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T_FILTERED.Earnings;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and earnings of the poker player who has made more than 40 final tables, ordered from lowest to highest earnings.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Earnings, (SELECT count(*) FROM poker_player) FROM people AS T1 JOIN poker_player AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Bulgaria';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and earnings for a poker player from Bulgaria, also including a total count of all poker players in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Nationality, T2.Final_Table_Made FROM people AS T1 JOIN poker_player AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Final_Table_Made = (SELECT MIN(Final_Table_Made) FROM poker_player WHERE Best_Finish = 2.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, nationality, and number of final tables made by the poker player who has the minimum number of final table appearances among all players with a best finish of 2nd place.

sql: SELECT * FROM Highschooler WHERE name = 'Gabriel';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the ID, name, and grade for all high school students named 'Gabriel'.

sql: SELECT ID, name FROM Highschooler WHERE grade = 9;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the ID and name of all 9th-grade high schoolers.

sql: SELECT * FROM Friend WHERE student_id = 1510;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that list the friend IDs for the student with an ID of 1510.

sql: SELECT liked_id FROM Likes WHERE student_id = 1689;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of the student that the student with ID 1689 has liked.

sql: SELECT ID, name, grade FROM Highschooler ORDER BY name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the ID, name, and grade for each high school student, ordered alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.grade FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.friend_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.student_id = 1510;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and grade for each friend of the high school student with ID 1510.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.liked_id = 1709;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the high schoolers who have liked the student with ID 1709.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.friend_id) FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows, listing each high schooler by name and providing a total count of their friends.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.liked_id) FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID GROUP BY T1.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each high schooler's name alongside the total count of other students they have liked.

sql: SELECT T1.name AS student_name, T3.name AS friend_name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T2.friend_id = T3.ID WHERE T1.grade = 9;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each 9th-grade student's name alongside the name of one of their friends.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.liked_id = T2.ID WHERE T1.student_id = 1689;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the high schooler who is liked by the student with ID 1689.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T1.grade FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the unique name and grade of each high schooler who has at least one friend.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T3.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.liked_id = T3.ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Likes AS T4 WHERE T4.student_id = T1.liked_id AND T4.liked_id = T1.student_id) AND T1.student_id < T1.liked_id;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, with each row listing the names of a pair of high school students who have a mutual "like" for one another.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.grade FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING COUNT(T1.friend_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and grade for each high school student who has more than one friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.liked_id GROUP BY T2.liked_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the single most popular high schooler, determined by who received the most "likes" from other students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name, T4.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Friend AS T3 ON T2.friend_id = T3.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T4 ON T3.friend_id = T4.ID WHERE T1.ID != T4.ID;
result_summary: Returns 23 unique rows, each containing a pair of highschooler names where the second student is a "friend of a friend" of the first.

sql: SELECT friend_id FROM Friend WHERE student_id = 1510 INTERSECT SELECT student_id FROM Likes WHERE liked_id = 1709;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of a student who is both a friend of student 1510 and also liked the student/item with ID 1709.

sql: SELECT friend_id FROM Friend WHERE student_id = 1689 UNION SELECT liked_id FROM Likes WHERE student_id = 1689;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique student IDs of everyone who is either a friend of or has been liked by the student with ID 1689.

sql: SELECT friend_id FROM Friend WHERE student_id = 1510 EXCEPT SELECT student_id FROM Likes WHERE liked_id = 1709;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID of a friend of student 1510, specifically excluding any friends who also liked student 1709.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Friend AS T3 WHERE T3.student_id = T1.student_id AND T3.friend_id = T1.liked_id);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have liked another student they are not currently friends with.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Likes AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.ID WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Likes AS T3 WHERE T3.liked_id = T1.student_id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of high schoolers who have liked another student but have never received a like in return.

sql: SELECT T1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Friend AS T2 INNER JOIN Likes AS T3 ON T2.friend_id = T3.student_id AND T2.student_id = T3.liked_id WHERE T2.student_id = T1.ID) FROM Highschooler AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows, listing each highschooler's name and the corresponding count of their friends who have also "liked" them.

sql: SELECT T3.grade FROM (SELECT T1.grade, count(*) AS c FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.grade) AS T3 ORDER BY T3.c DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the high school grade with the highest total count of friendships.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Likes AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.liked_id AND T1.friend_id = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of high schoolers who are liked by one of their friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM Highschooler AS T1 JOIN Friend AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.student_id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T2.friend_id = T3.ID WHERE T1.grade = T3.grade;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing pairs of high school students who are friends and are in the same grade, showing the names of both students in each pair.

sql: SELECT Name, Origin FROM program;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and origin of each program.

sql: SELECT Name, Rating_in_percent FROM channel WHERE Rating_in_percent > 0.5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and rating percentage for channels with a rating greater than 50%.

sql: SELECT Owner FROM program WHERE Launch = 2005.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the owners of programs that were launched in the year 2005.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Time_of_day FROM broadcast;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each distinct time of day that a broadcast occurs.

sql: SELECT Name FROM channel WHERE Owner = 'CCTV';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of channels that are owned by 'CCTV'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Program_ID) FROM broadcast AS T1 JOIN channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel_ID = T2.Channel_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each TV channel's name along with a count of the total number of programs broadcast by that channel.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, AVG(T1.Share_in_percent) FROM broadcast_share AS T1 JOIN program AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each program by name along with its average broadcast share percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T2.Time_of_day = 'Morning';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all programs that are broadcast during the 'Morning' time slot.

sql: SELECT Launch, COUNT(Program_ID) FROM program GROUP BY Launch;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each launch year along with the total number of programs launched in that year.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T3 ON T1.Program_ID = T3.Program_ID JOIN channel AS T2 ON T3.Channel_ID = T2.Channel_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Aniworld';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of the channels that broadcast the program 'Aniworld'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Share_in_percent FROM broadcast_share AS T1 JOIN program AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T1.Date = '01,May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and broadcast share percentage for TV programs that aired on May 1st.

sql: SELECT Name FROM channel ORDER BY Rating_in_percent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the channel with the highest rating percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID GROUP BY T1.Program_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Channel_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of TV programs that are broadcast on more than one channel.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM broadcast AS T1 JOIN channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel_ID = T2.Channel_ID WHERE T1.Program_ID IN (SELECT Program_ID FROM program WHERE Owner = 'Hunan Broadcasting System (HBS)');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of channels that have broadcasted programs owned by the 'Hunan Broadcasting System (HBS)'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM broadcast_share AS T1 JOIN program AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID GROUP BY T2.Name ORDER BY AVG(T1.Share_in_percent) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the program with the highest average broadcast share percentage.

sql: SELECT Name FROM channel WHERE Owner = (SELECT Owner FROM channel GROUP BY Owner ORDER BY COUNT(Channel_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all channels that belong to the single owner with the most channels.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T2.Time_of_day = 'Morning' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T2.Time_of_day = 'Night';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of television programs that are broadcast during both the 'Morning' and 'Night' time slots.

sql: SELECT T1.Owner, SUM(T2.Share_in_percent) FROM channel AS T1 JOIN broadcast_share AS T2 ON T1.Channel_ID = T2.Channel_ID GROUP BY T1.Owner;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each channel owner and the sum of their corresponding broadcast share percentages.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 WHERE T1.Program_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Program_ID FROM broadcast_share) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T2.Time_of_day = 'Night';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a program that has been shared, excluding any programs that were broadcast at night.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM channel AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Channel_ID = T2.Channel_ID JOIN program AS T3 ON T2.Program_ID = T3.Program_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Kaku' AND T1.Rating_in_percent > 0.4;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of channels that have a rating greater than 40% and have broadcast the program 'Kaku'.

sql: SELECT T1.Origin, MAX(T3.Share_in_percent) FROM program AS T1 JOIN broadcast AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID JOIN broadcast_share AS T3 ON T2.Program_ID = T3.Program_ID AND T2.Channel_ID = T3.Channel_ID GROUP BY T1.Origin;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each program's origin and the peak broadcast share percentage achieved by any program from that origin.

sql: SELECT Name FROM program WHERE Launch = (SELECT Launch FROM program WHERE Name = 'Aniworld') AND Name != 'Aniworld';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any program that launched in the same year as the 'Aniworld' program.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM channel AS T1 WHERE T1.Share_in_percent > 3 AND T1.Channel_ID IN (SELECT T2.Channel_ID FROM broadcast AS T2 JOIN program AS T3 ON T2.Program_ID = T3.Program_ID WHERE T3.Launch = 2004.0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of channels with a market share over 3% that broadcast programs launched in the year 2004.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM channel AS T1 JOIN channel AS T2 ON T1.Owner = T2.Owner AND T1.Channel_ID < T2.Channel_ID;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing unique pairs of different channels that share the same owner.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM broadcast AS T1 JOIN program AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID JOIN channel AS T3 ON T1.Channel_ID = T3.Channel_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'CCTV-1' UNION SELECT T2.Name FROM broadcast_share AS T1 JOIN program AS T2 ON T1.Program_ID = T2.Program_ID WHERE T1.Date = '02,May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of programs that were either broadcast on the 'CCTV-1' channel or shared on May 2nd.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT ResName FROM Restaurant WHERE Rating = 4;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a restaurant with a rating of exactly 4.

sql: SELECT ResTypeName, ResTypeDescription FROM Restaurant_Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each available restaurant type along with its corresponding description.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of all female students.

sql: SELECT ResName, Rating FROM Restaurant ORDER BY Rating DESC;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and rating for each restaurant, ordered from highest to lowest rating.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Time, T2.Spent FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.StuID = 1001;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, time, and amount spent for a restaurant visit made by the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, SUM(T2.Spent) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each student by name along with the total amount they have spent across all restaurant visits.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName, T3.ResTypeName FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Type_Of_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID JOIN Restaurant_Type AS T3 ON T2.ResTypeID = T3.ResTypeID WHERE T1.ResName = 'Subway';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the restaurant type for the specific restaurant named 'Subway'.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(StuID) FROM Student WHERE Major = 600 GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total number of students for the major identified by the code '600'.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName, AVG(T2.Spent) FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID GROUP BY T1.ResName;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each restaurant's name along with the average amount spent per visit at that restaurant.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.Age = 18;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of a student who is 18 years old and has visited a restaurant.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ResName FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of restaurants that have had at least one visit.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Type_Of_Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID JOIN Restaurant_Type AS T4 ON T3.ResTypeID = T4.ResTypeID WHERE T4.ResTypeName = 'Sandwich';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the unique first and last name of a student who has visited a sandwich restaurant.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID WHERE T3.Rating = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age of students who have visited restaurants with a rating of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Visits_Restaurant AS T1 WHERE T1.Spent > (SELECT AVG(Spent) FROM Visits_Restaurant);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the student ID for a restaurant visit where the amount spent exceeded the average amount spent across all visits.

sql: SELECT T4.ResTypeName, SUM(T2.Spent) AS TotalSpent FROM Visits_Restaurant AS T2 JOIN Type_Of_Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID JOIN Restaurant_Type AS T4 ON T3.ResTypeID = T4.ResTypeID GROUP BY T4.ResTypeName;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each restaurant type along with the total amount spent on visits to that type of restaurant.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID GROUP BY T1.ResName HAVING count(*) = (SELECT count(*) FROM Visits_Restaurant GROUP BY ResID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the restaurants that are tied for the highest number of visits.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID WHERE T1.Sex = 'F' AND T3.Rating >= 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique first and last name of a female student who has visited a restaurant with a rating of 3 or higher.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID WHERE T3.ResName = 'Honeygrow';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of a student who has visited at least one restaurant but has not visited 'Honeygrow'.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY SUM(T2.Spent) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student major whose students collectively spent the most money at restaurants.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Type_Of_Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID JOIN Restaurant_Type AS T4 ON T3.ResTypeID = T4.ResTypeID WHERE T4.ResTypeName = 'Stir-fry' UNION SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Age < 20;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the unique first and last names of students who have either visited a 'Stir-fry' restaurant or are younger than 20 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName, T1.Address FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID WHERE T2.Spent = (SELECT MAX(Spent) FROM Visits_Restaurant);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and address of the restaurant that corresponds to the single highest expenditure recorded across all visits.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, MAX(T3.Rating) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Restaurant AS T3 ON T2.ResID = T3.ResID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, listing each student's first and last name along with the maximum rating of any restaurant they have visited.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code, AVG(T2.Spent) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each student city code along with the average amount spent by students from that city on restaurant visits.

sql: SELECT T1.ResName FROM Restaurant AS T1 JOIN Visits_Restaurant AS T2 ON T1.ResID = T2.ResID WHERE T1.Rating >= (SELECT AVG(Rating) FROM Restaurant) GROUP BY T1.ResName;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a visited restaurant whose rating is at or above the average for all restaurants.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Age = 23;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of all pilots who are exactly 23 years old.

sql: SELECT Airport_Name, Total_Passengers, International_Passengers FROM airport WHERE Airport_Name = 'London Gatwick';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total and international passenger counts for London Gatwick airport.

sql: SELECT Aircraft, Max_Gross_Weight FROM aircraft WHERE Description = 'Light utility helicopter';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the aircraft model and its maximum gross weight for aircraft specifically classified as a 'Light utility helicopter'.

sql: SELECT Location, Date, Fastest_Qualifying FROM match WHERE Country = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the location, date, and fastest qualifying person for the match that took place in Australia.

sql: SELECT Pilot_Id, Name, Age FROM pilot ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each pilot's ID, name, and age, ordered from the oldest pilot to the youngest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Round) FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each pilot by name and the total count of matches they have won.

sql: SELECT T1.Airport_Name, T3.Aircraft FROM airport AS T1 JOIN airport_aircraft AS T2 ON T1.Airport_ID = T2.Airport_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each airport and the specific aircraft associated with it.

sql: SELECT Airport_Name, Total_Passengers FROM airport WHERE Total_Passengers > (SELECT AVG(Total_Passengers) FROM airport);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and total passenger counts for airports with an above-average number of passengers.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot WHERE T2.Country = 'Brazil';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the pilot who won a match that took place in Brazil.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfMatches FROM match GROUP BY Country ORDER BY NumberOfMatches DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each country alongside a count of the total matches held there, ordered from the country with the most matches to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Aircraft FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Winning_Aircraft = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the winning pilot of Round 1 and the specific aircraft they used to win the match.

sql: SELECT T1.Airport_Name, COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) FROM airport AS T1 JOIN airport_aircraft AS T2 ON T1.Airport_ID = T2.Airport_ID GROUP BY T1.Airport_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each airport's name along with the total count of aircraft associated with that airport.

sql: SELECT T1.Airport_Name, COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) FROM airport AS T1 JOIN airport_aircraft AS T2 ON T1.Airport_ID = T2.Airport_ID GROUP BY T1.Airport_Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each airport and the total number of aircraft it contains, ordered from the airport with the most aircraft to the fewest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM match AS T2 WHERE T2.Winning_Pilot = T1.Pilot_Id) AS Wins FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Pilot_Id IN (SELECT Winning_Pilot FROM match);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each pilot who has won at least one match, along with their total number of wins.

sql: SELECT Country FROM (SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS MatchCount FROM match GROUP BY Country) ORDER BY MatchCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the country that has hosted the most matches.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Age = 23 UNION SELECT T2.Name FROM pilot AS T2 JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_Id = T3.Winning_Pilot WHERE T3.Country = 'Brazil';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of pilots who are either 23 years old or have won a match held in Brazil.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Winning_Aircraft = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Description = 'Turboshaft utility helicopter';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the pilot who has won a match while flying a 'Turboshaft utility helicopter'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM pilot WHERE Age = 23 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of a pilot who is 23 years old and has won at least one match.

sql: SELECT Name FROM pilot EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN match AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_Id = T2.Winning_Pilot;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all pilots who have never won a match.

result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and age of the pilot who won the final round of the competition.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, AVG(T2.Age) FROM match AS T1 JOIN pilot AS T2 ON T1.Winning_Pilot = T2.Pilot_Id GROUP BY T1.Country;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each country where a match was held and the average age of the winning pilots for those matches.

sql: SELECT Aircraft FROM aircraft WHERE Aircraft_ID NOT IN (SELECT Winning_Aircraft FROM match);
