sql: SELECT T1.Airport_Name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN airport_aircraft AS T2 ON T1.Airport_ID = T2.Airport_ID WHERE T1.Total_Passengers > 30000000 GROUP BY T1.Airport_Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of major airports (with over 30 million total passengers) that have at least one associated aircraft.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM pilot WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM pilot) AND Pilot_Id IN (SELECT Winning_Pilot FROM match);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age for pilots who are older than the average pilot age and have won at least one match.

sql: SELECT T1.Aircraft FROM aircraft AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM airport_aircraft AS T2 JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.Airport_ID = T3.Airport_ID WHERE T2.Aircraft_ID = T1.Aircraft_ID AND T3.Airport_Name = 'London Heathrow');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an aircraft that is associated with 'London Heathrow' airport.

sql: SELECT customer_first_name, customer_last_name, town_city FROM Customers WHERE state_county_province = 'WestVirginia';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, and town/city for the customer located in West Virginia.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE product_color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name and unit price for all products colored 'Red'.

sql: SELECT production_type_code, product_type_description FROM Product_Categories;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each available product category, including its production type code and a corresponding description.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, date_order_placed FROM Orders ORDER BY date_order_placed DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the order ID, customer ID, and placement date for the 5 most recent orders, sorted from newest to oldest.

sql: SELECT product_name, unit_price FROM Products WHERE unit_price > 600;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the product name and unit price for all products costing more than 600.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) AS number_of_orders FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each customer by first and last name, along with the total count of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.product_quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each product name alongside the total number of units sold for that product across all orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T2.date_order_placed, T3.invoice_number, T3.invoice_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T1.customer_id = 8;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing the order and invoice history for a specific customer (ID 8), including their first name, the order placement date, the invoice number, and the invoice date.

sql: SELECT T1.account_name, SUM(T2.transaction_amount) AS total_transactions FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id GROUP BY T1.account_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, showing each account name alongside the total sum of all their transaction amounts.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_description, COUNT(T2.product_id) AS number_of_products FROM Product_Categories AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code GROUP BY T1.product_type_description HAVING COUNT(T2.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing product categories that contain more than one product, along with a count of the number of products for each of those categories.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.product_name, T2.product_quantity FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.product_quantity >= 6;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing distinct products from orders with a quantity of 6 or more, showing the product's name and the specific quantity ordered.

sql: SELECT T1.account_name, T2.transaction_id, T2.transaction_date, T2.transaction_amount FROM Accounts AS T1 JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.transaction_type = 'Payment';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing all transactions of the type 'Payment', showing the associated account name, transaction ID, date, and amount for each.

sql: SELECT T5.product_type_description, SUM(T1.transaction_amount) AS total_revenue FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Invoices AS T2 ON T1.invoice_number = T2.invoice_number JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Product_Categories AS T5 ON T4.production_type_code = T5.production_type_code WHERE T1.transaction_type = 'Payment' GROUP BY T5.product_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each product category and the corresponding total revenue generated from customer payments.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T4.production_type_code) AS distinct_categories_ordered FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.production_type_code) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying customers who have ordered from two or more distinct product categories, listing their full name and the total count of unique categories purchased from.

sql: SELECT T3.customer_first_name, T3.customer_last_name, SUM(T1.transaction_amount) AS total_spent FROM Financial_Transactions AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T1.transaction_type = 'Payment' GROUP BY T3.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top 3 customers by their total spending on 'Payment' transactions, ordered from highest to lowest amount spent.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id FROM Order_Items AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_id FROM Invoice_Line_Items AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the IDs of products that have been ordered but have not yet appeared on an invoice.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, AVG(julianday(T3.invoice_date) - julianday(T2.date_order_placed)) AS avg_days_to_invoice FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Invoices AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer by ID and first name, along with the average time in days from when an order is placed to when it is invoiced.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.country = 'USA' AND T1.customer_id IN (SELECT T2.customer_id FROM Orders AS T2 JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.production_type_code = 'Books');
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the customer ID, first name, and last name of a customer from the USA who has purchased a book.

sql: SELECT T2.account_id, AVG(T3.transaction_amount) AS avg_transaction FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.state_county_province = 'Arkansas' AND T2.other_account_details = 'Regular' GROUP BY T2.account_id;

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.product_type_description FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Categories AS T2 ON T1.production_type_code = T2.production_type_code UNION SELECT T3.customer_first_name || ' ' || T3.customer_last_name, 'Never Ordered' FROM Customers AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Orders);
result_summary: Returns 19 rows that combine a list of all products and their category descriptions with a list of customers who have never placed an order, identified by the description 'Never Ordered'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name, MAX(T2.date_order_placed) AS most_recent_order, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Accounts AS T3 WHERE T3.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T3.other_account_details = 'VIP') AS vip_account_count FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each customer by name, along with the date of their most recent order and a count of how many 'VIP' accounts they have.

sql: SELECT invoice_number, COUNT(product_id) AS num_products, SUM(derived_product_cost) AS total_cost FROM Invoice_Line_Items GROUP BY invoice_number HAVING COUNT(product_id) > 1 AND SUM(derived_product_cost) > 300;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for high-value, multi-item invoices, listing each invoice number, the count of products, and the total cost for orders with more than one product and a value greater than 300.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Accounts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Financial_Transactions AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN Invoices AS T4 ON T3.invoice_number = T4.invoice_number JOIN Orders AS T5 ON T4.order_id = T5.order_id WHERE T3.transaction_type = 'Payment' AND strftime('%Y', T5.date_order_placed) = '2012';

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Accounts WHERE other_account_details = 'Regular') AND T1.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Accounts WHERE other_account_details = 'VIP');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the customer ID, first name, and last name for customers who have a 'Regular' account but do not also have a 'VIP' account.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, T3.product_type_description, SUM(T1.product_quantity) AS total_quantity FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Product_Categories AS T3 ON T2.production_type_code = T3.production_type_code GROUP BY T2.product_id, T2.product_name, T3.product_type_description ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.EMP_LNAME, T2.EMP_FNAME, COUNT(T1.CLASS_CODE) AS ClassesTaught FROM CLASS AS T1 JOIN EMPLOYEE AS T2 ON T1.PROF_NUM = T2.EMP_NUM GROUP BY T2.EMP_LNAME, T2.EMP_FNAME;

sql: SELECT T1.DEPT_CODE, T1.StudentCount FROM (SELECT DEPT_CODE, COUNT(*) AS StudentCount FROM STUDENT GROUP BY DEPT_CODE) AS T1 JOIN (SELECT MAX(StudentCount) AS MaxC FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS StudentCount FROM STUDENT GROUP BY DEPT_CODE)) AS T2 ON T1.StudentCount = T2.MaxC;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the largest number of students, showing its department code and the total student count.

sql: SELECT T2.SCHOOL_CODE, AVG(T1.STU_GPA) AS AVG_GPA FROM STUDENT AS T1 JOIN DEPARTMENT AS T2 ON T1.DEPT_CODE = T2.DEPT_CODE GROUP BY T2.SCHOOL_CODE;

sql: SELECT Staff_ID, Staff_Details FROM Staff ORDER BY Staff_Details DESC;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'Medical';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique IDs for claims that have an associated 'Medical' type document.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, T2.Policy_ID, T2.Policy_Type_Code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T1.Customer_ID = 334;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the customer's details, policy ID, and policy type code for all policies held by a single, specific customer.

sql: SELECT T1.Policy_ID, COUNT(T2.Claim_Header_ID) FROM Policies AS T1 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID GROUP BY T1.Policy_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each policy ID along with the total number of claims filed against it.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, COUNT(T2.Claim_ID) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID GROUP BY T1.Staff_Details;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each staff member alongside the total count of claims they have processed.

sql: SELECT Claim_Type_Code, AVG(Amount_Claimed), MAX(Amount_Piad) FROM Claim_Headers GROUP BY Claim_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each claim type, along with the average amount claimed and the maximum amount paid for that specific type.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.Created_Date FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID WHERE T2.Document_Type_Code = 'Medical';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the staff members who created 'Medical' type documents, along with the date each document was created.

sql: SELECT T1.Claim_Status_Name, COUNT(T2.Claim_ID) FROM Claims_Processing_Stages AS T1 JOIN Claims_Processing AS T2 ON T1.Claim_Stage_ID = T2.Claim_Stage_ID GROUP BY T1.Claim_Status_Name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each claim processing status (e.g., 'Open', 'Close') along with the total number of claims in that status.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID, COUNT(Document_Type_Code) FROM Claims_Documents GROUP BY Claim_ID HAVING COUNT(Document_Type_Code) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each claim ID alongside the total number of documents associated with it for claims that have at least one document.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details, T3.Claim_Header_ID, T3.Claim_Status_Code FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Policies AS T2 ON T1.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T1.Customer_Details = 'Mrs. Hanna Willms';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the claim header ID and claim status code for all claims associated with the customer 'Mrs. Hanna Willms'.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Claims_Documents AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Created_by_Staff_ID WHERE T2.Claim_ID IN (SELECT Claim_Header_ID FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing the details of staff members who have created documents for claims currently marked as 'Disputed'.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Amount_Claimed FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Amount_Claimed > (SELECT AVG(Amount_Claimed) FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Policy_ID = 518);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the claim ID and amount for all claims whose value is greater than the average amount claimed under policy ID 518.

sql: SELECT T2.Customer_Details, T1.Claim_ID, T1.Claim_Outcome_Code FROM Claims_Processing AS T1 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T1.Claim_ID = T3.Claim_Header_ID JOIN Policies AS T4 ON T3.Policy_ID = T4.Policy_ID JOIN Customers AS T2 ON T4.Customer_ID = T2.Customer_ID WHERE T1.Staff_ID = 589;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the customer details, claim ID, and claim outcome code for all claims processed by staff member 589.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_ID, T1.Staff_Details FROM Staff AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Claims_Processing AS T2 WHERE T2.Staff_ID = T1.Staff_ID AND T2.Claim_Outcome_Code = 'In progress');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the staff ID and details for all staff members who are currently assigned to at least one claim with an 'In progress' status.

sql: SELECT Claim_Header_ID, Date_of_Claim, Amount_Claimed FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Policy_ID IN (SELECT Policy_ID FROM Policies WHERE Policy_Type_Code = 'Jurisdiction') AND Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the claim ID, date, and amount for any disputed claim filed under a 'Jurisdiction' type policy.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID FROM Policies EXCEPT SELECT Policy_ID FROM Claim_Headers;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the policy IDs for all policies that have no associated claims filed against them.

sql: SELECT Claim_Type_Code, COUNT(*) FROM Claim_Headers GROUP BY Claim_Type_Code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 UNION SELECT 'Disputed', COUNT(*) FROM Claim_Headers WHERE Claim_Status_Code = 'Disputed';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing claim types with more than one claim and their corresponding counts, along with a separate row showing the total count of all disputed claims.

sql: SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'Document' INTERSECT SELECT Claim_ID FROM Claims_Processing WHERE Claim_Outcome_Code = 'In progress';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs for claims that are currently 'In progress' and also have an associated document of type 'Document'.

sql: SELECT T1.Staff_Details, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Claims_Processing AS T2 WHERE T2.Staff_ID = T1.Staff_ID) AS Processed_Count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Claims_Documents AS T3 WHERE T3.Created_by_Staff_ID = T1.Staff_ID) AS Document_Count FROM Staff AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, one for each staff member, showing their details along with a count of the total claims processed and total documents created by them.

sql: WITH StaffClaimCounts AS (SELECT Staff_ID, COUNT(Claim_ID) AS claim_count FROM Claims_Processing GROUP BY Staff_ID) SELECT T1.Staff_Details, T2.claim_count FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN StaffClaimCounts AS T2 ON T1.Staff_ID = T2.Staff_ID WHERE T2.claim_count > (SELECT AVG(claim_count) FROM StaffClaimCounts);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying staff members who have processed an above-average number of claims, showing their details and total claim count.

sql: SELECT T1.Customer_Details FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.Customer_ID IN (SELECT T2.Customer_ID FROM Policies AS T2 JOIN Claim_Headers AS T3 ON T2.Policy_ID = T3.Policy_ID WHERE T3.Claim_Type_Code = 'Child Birth' AND T3.Amount_Claimed > 300);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the details of customers who have filed a 'Child Birth' claim for an amount greater than 300.

result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the claim ID, status code, and type code for claims under Policy ID 518 that have associated documents created by staff member 427.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM customer AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rental AS T2 JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film_category AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN category AS T5 ON T4.category_id = T5.category_id WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T5.name = 'Action') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM rental AS T2 JOIN inventory AS T3 ON T2.inventory_id = T3.inventory_id JOIN film_category AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id JOIN category AS T5 ON T4.category_id = T5.category_id WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T5.name = 'Animation');
result_summary: Returns 426 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have rented films from both the 'Action' and 'Animation' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.payment_id, T1.customer_id, T1.amount FROM payment AS T1 WHERE T1.amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM payment);
result_summary: Returns 7746 rows listing the payment ID, customer ID, and transaction amount for all payments that are greater than the overall average payment amount.

sql: SELECT film_id, title FROM film WHERE film_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.film_id FROM rental AS T1 JOIN inventory AS T2 ON T1.inventory_id = T2.inventory_id);
result_summary: Returns 42 rows listing the film ID and title for all films that have never been rented out.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.rental_id) AS rental_count FROM customer AS T1 JOIN rental AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id ORDER BY rental_count DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the customers with the most rentals, showing each customer's ID, full name, and their total rental count, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.actor_id, T3.first_name, T3.last_name FROM film_actor AS T1 JOIN film_actor AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id JOIN actor AS T3 ON T2.actor_id = T3.actor_id WHERE T1.actor_id = 1 AND T2.actor_id != 1;
result_summary: Returns 79 rows listing the unique actor ID, first name, and last name for all actors who have appeared in the same film as the actor with ID 1.

sql: SELECT AVG(film_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T1.film_id) AS film_count FROM film_category AS T1 GROUP BY T1.category_id) AS T2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average number of films per category.

sql: SELECT first_name FROM actor WHERE first_name = 'PENELOPE' UNION SELECT first_name FROM customer WHERE first_name = 'PATRICIA';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the first names from a combined search for any actor named 'PENELOPE' and any customer named 'PATRICIA'.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T1.replacement_cost, T2.category_id FROM film AS T1 JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id WHERE T1.replacement_cost > (SELECT AVG(T3.replacement_cost) FROM film AS T3 JOIN film_category AS T4 ON T3.film_id = T4.film_id WHERE T4.category_id = T2.category_id);
result_summary: Returns 509 rows listing films with a replacement cost that is higher than the average for their specific category, showing each film's title, its replacement cost, and its category ID.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN film_actor AS T2 ON T1.actor_id = T2.actor_id JOIN film_category AS T3 ON T2.film_id = T3.film_id JOIN category AS T4 ON T3.category_id = T4.category_id WHERE T4.name = 'Action' EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM actor AS T1 JOIN film_actor AS T2 ON T1.actor_id = T2.actor_id JOIN film_category AS T3 ON T2.film_id = T3.film_id JOIN category AS T4 ON T3.category_id = T4.category_id WHERE T4.name = 'Children';
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the first and last names of actors who have appeared in 'Action' films but have not appeared in any 'Children' films.

sql: SELECT T3.city, COUNT(T1.customer_id) AS customer_count FROM customer AS T1 JOIN address AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN city AS T3 ON T2.city_id = T3.city_id GROUP BY T3.city_id ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city with the most customers, along with the total customer count for that city.

result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each property's name along with its corresponding property type description.

sql: SELECT T2.property_type_description, COUNT(T1.property_id) FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Ref_Property_Types AS T2 ON T1.property_type_code = T2.property_type_code GROUP BY T2.property_type_description;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each property type description along with the total number of properties for each category.

sql: SELECT T1.property_name, T3.feature_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows that list each property's name alongside its corresponding available feature name, creating a complete inventory of features for each property.

sql: SELECT T1.property_name, T1.property_address FROM Properties AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Other_Property_Features AS T2 WHERE T1.property_id = T2.property_id);
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names and addresses of properties that have at least one associated feature recorded.

sql: SELECT property_name, buyer_offered_price, agreed_selling_price FROM Properties WHERE buyer_offered_price > agreed_selling_price;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing properties where the buyer's offered price was greater than the final agreed selling price, showing the property name alongside both price points.

sql: SELECT T1.feature_name, T2.feature_type_name FROM Other_Available_Features AS T1 JOIN Ref_Feature_Types AS T2 ON T1.feature_type_code = T2.feature_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each available feature and its corresponding type name, derived by matching the feature code to the reference feature types table.

sql: SELECT property_name, room_count FROM Properties WHERE room_count > 1;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the name and room count for all properties that have more than one room.

sql: SELECT property_name, property_address FROM Properties WHERE property_id IN (SELECT property_id FROM Other_Property_Features WHERE feature_id = 2);

sql: SELECT T2.property_type_description FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Ref_Property_Types AS T2 ON T1.property_type_code = T2.property_type_code GROUP BY T1.property_type_code HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows that list the descriptions of property types which appear more than once in the properties table.

sql: SELECT T3.feature_name, T4.feature_type_name, T2.property_feature_description FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id JOIN Ref_Feature_Types AS T4 ON T3.feature_type_code = T4.feature_type_code WHERE T1.property_id = 15;
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing a feature for the property with ID 15, including the feature's name, its type, and a specific description.

result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the property types that average more than 5 rooms, showing each property type's description and its calculated average room count.

sql: SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_id IN (SELECT property_id FROM Other_Property_Features) AND property_type_code != 'House';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of non-house properties that have additional features listed in a separate table.

sql: SELECT T2.feature_name FROM Other_Property_Features AS T1 JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T2 ON T1.feature_id = T2.feature_id GROUP BY T2.feature_name HAVING count(T1.property_id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.feature_id = 3 EXCEPT SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.feature_id = 4;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of properties that have feature ID 3 but explicitly do not have feature ID 4.

sql: SELECT T1.property_name, T1.agreed_selling_price FROM Properties AS T1 WHERE T1.agreed_selling_price > (SELECT avg(T2.agreed_selling_price) FROM Properties AS T2 WHERE T2.property_type_code = T1.property_type_code);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying properties that sold for a price higher than the average for their respective property type, showing each property's name and its agreed selling price.

sql: SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code = 'House' UNION SELECT T1.property_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id WHERE T2.feature_id = 3;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of properties that are either classified as a 'House' or have a specific feature (feature ID 3).

sql: WITH FeatureCounts AS (SELECT feature_id, COUNT(property_id) AS num_properties FROM Other_Property_Features GROUP BY feature_id), FeatureRanks AS (SELECT T2.feature_type_code, T2.feature_name, T1.num_properties, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.feature_type_code ORDER BY T1.num_properties DESC) AS rk FROM FeatureCounts AS T1 JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T2 ON T1.feature_id = T2.feature_id) SELECT T1.feature_type_name, T2.feature_name FROM Ref_Feature_Types AS T1 JOIN FeatureRanks AS T2 ON T1.feature_type_code = T2.feature_type_code WHERE T2.rk = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the single most common feature within each property feature category (e.g., 'Amenity'), determined by counting which feature appears in the most properties for that category.

sql: SELECT property_name, date_on_market, date_sold FROM Properties WHERE date_sold IS NOT NULL ORDER BY JULIANDAY(date_sold) - JULIANDAY(date_on_market) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single property that took the longest to sell, showing its name, the date it was listed on the market, and the date it was sold.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.feature_name FROM Properties AS T1 JOIN Other_Property_Features AS T2 ON T1.property_id = T2.property_id JOIN Other_Available_Features AS T3 ON T2.feature_id = T3.feature_id JOIN Ref_Feature_Types AS T4 ON T3.feature_type_code = T4.feature_type_code WHERE T1.property_type_code = 'House' AND T4.feature_type_code = 'Amenity';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique amenity feature available for properties classified as 'House'.

sql: SELECT T1.CName, T2.DName FROM Course AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.DNO = T2.DNO WHERE T1.DNO = 600;

sql: SELECT T1.LName, T1.Fname, COUNT(T2.CID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.CID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first and last names of students enrolled in more than one course, along with the total count of courses each student is taking.

sql: SELECT T1.Lname, T1.Fname, T3.DName FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Member_of AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID JOIN Department AS T3 ON T2.DNO = T3.DNO WHERE T2.Appt_Type = 'Primary';
result_summary: Returns 58 rows listing the last name, first name, and department name for each faculty member based on their primary department appointment.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.DName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.DNO;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing each student's first and last name, along with the name of their corresponding major department.

sql: SELECT LName, FName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the last name, first name, and age for all students who are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.Lname, T1.Fname FROM Faculty AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Student AS T2 WHERE T2.Advisor = T1.FacID);
result_summary: Returns 18 rows containing the first and last names of all faculty members who are currently advising at least one student.

sql: SELECT FacID FROM Faculty WHERE Rank = 'Professor' INTERSECT SELECT Advisor FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the faculty IDs for all professors who also serve as student advisors.

sql: SELECT StuID FROM Student WHERE LName IS NOT NULL EXCEPT SELECT StuID FROM Minor_in;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the student IDs for all students who do not have a declared minor.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.gradepoint) AS GPA FROM Enrolled_in AS T1 JOIN Gradeconversion AS T2 ON T1.Grade = T2.lettergrade WHERE T1.StuID = 1001;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the Grade Point Average (GPA) for the student with an ID of 1001.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.CName, T4.DName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Enrolled_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Course AS T3 ON T2.CID = T3.CID JOIN Department AS T4 ON T3.DNO = T4.DNO WHERE T1.StuID = 1001;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows for a single student (ID 1001), listing their name, each course they are enrolled in, and the name of the department that offers the course.

sql: SELECT T2.DName FROM Course AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.DNO = T2.DNO WHERE T1.Credits = 3 GROUP BY T2.DName HAVING COUNT(T1.CID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of departments that offer more than one 3-credit course.

sql: SELECT Lname, Fname FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F' UNION SELECT Lname, Fname FROM Faculty WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of all individuals identified as female, combining both students and faculty into a single list.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Advisor = T2.FacID JOIN Member_of AS T3 ON T2.FacID = T3.FacID WHERE T2.Rank = 'Professor' AND T3.Appt_Type = 'Primary';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of students whose advisor is a full 'Professor' with a 'Primary' appointment type.

sql: SELECT T2.DName, COUNT(T1.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.DNO GROUP BY T2.DName HAVING COUNT(T1.StuID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each department with more than one student, showing the department name and the total count of students for each.

sql: SELECT StuID, LName, Fname FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT T1.StuID FROM Enrolled_in AS T1 JOIN Course AS T2 ON T1.CID = T2.CID WHERE T2.DNO = 600);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the student ID, last name, and first name for the student who is not enrolled in any courses from department number 600.

sql: SELECT T2.DName, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.DNO GROUP BY T2.DName;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each department name along with the average age of students majoring in that department.

sql: SELECT T3.DName, T3.FacultyCount FROM (SELECT T2.DName, COUNT(T1.FacID) AS FacultyCount FROM Member_of AS T1 JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.DNO = T2.DNO GROUP BY T2.DName) AS T3 ORDER BY T3.FacultyCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the most faculty members, showing the department's name and its total faculty count.

sql: SELECT Name, Park, Height FROM roller_coaster WHERE Park = 'Kemah Boardwalk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and height of the roller coaster located at the 'Kemah Boardwalk' park.

sql: SELECT Name, Population FROM country WHERE Population > 9000000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and population for any country with more than 9 million people.

sql: SELECT Name, Speed FROM roller_coaster ORDER BY CAST(Speed AS INTEGER) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each roller coaster's name and speed, ordered numerically from fastest to slowest.

sql: SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE Status = 'Operating';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all roller coasters with a current status of 'Operating'.

sql: SELECT Name, Area FROM country WHERE Area < 100000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and area for each country with a land area of less than 100,000 square units.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Park FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Austria';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and park for each roller coaster located in Austria.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country's name along with the total count of roller coasters located in that country.

sql: SELECT Park, AVG(Length), MAX(Height) FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Park;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each park along with the average length of its roller coasters and the height of its tallest roller coaster.

sql: SELECT Name, Speed FROM roller_coaster WHERE Country_ID IN (SELECT Country_ID FROM country WHERE Population > 5000000);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and speed of roller coasters located in countries with a population greater than 5 million.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of countries that have at least one roller coaster in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Park FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE T2.Languages = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and park for each roller coaster located in a German-speaking country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID ORDER BY T2.Name ASC, T1.Length DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing roller coasters and their corresponding countries, ordered alphabetically by country and then by roller coaster length from longest to shortest.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM roller_coaster);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and height for all roller coasters that are taller than the average height of all coasters in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name FROM country WHERE Country_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Country_ID FROM roller_coaster);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM roller_coaster AS T2 WHERE T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID AND T2.Status = 'Operating');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of countries that contain at least one currently operating roller coaster.

sql: SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height > 100 INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE Length > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of roller coasters that are both taller than 100 units and longer than 4000 units.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.AvgLength FROM country AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Country_ID, AVG(Length) AS AvgLength FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Country_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Country_ID = T3.Country_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each country's name along with the average length of its roller coasters.

sql: SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height > 90 EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM roller_coaster WHERE CAST(Speed AS INTEGER) > 60;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of roller coasters with a height greater than 90 units, excluding any that also have a speed over 60 units.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 WHERE T1.Country_ID = (SELECT Country_ID FROM country ORDER BY Area ASC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of all roller coasters located in the country with the smallest geographical area.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name AS CountryName, CASE WHEN CAST(T1.Speed AS INTEGER) >= 70 THEN 'Fast' WHEN CAST(T1.Speed AS INTEGER) > 50 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Slow' END AS Speed_Category FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each roller coaster, its country, and a calculated speed category ('Fast', 'Medium', or 'Slow').

sql: SELECT Name FROM country WHERE Population > (SELECT SUM(Length) FROM roller_coaster);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of countries whose population is greater than the total combined length of all roller coasters.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T2 WHERE T2.Country_ID = T1.Country_ID ORDER BY T2.Length DESC LIMIT 1) AS Longest_Coaster FROM country AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, with each row listing a country's name alongside the name of the longest roller coaster found in that country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM roller_coaster AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country_ID = T2.Country_ID WHERE CAST(T1.Speed AS INTEGER) > 55 GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Roller_Coaster_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of countries that have at least one roller coaster with a speed greater than 55.

sql: SELECT AVG(CoasterCount) FROM (SELECT Park, COUNT(Roller_Coaster_ID) AS CoasterCount FROM roller_coaster GROUP BY Park);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value that represents the average number of roller coasters per park.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Speed FROM roller_coaster WHERE Height > 130 UNION SELECT Name, Height, Speed FROM roller_coaster WHERE CAST(Speed AS INTEGER) > 65;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name, height, and speed of all roller coasters that are either taller than 130 feet or faster than 65 mph.

sql: SELECT name, Home_Games, Average_Attendance FROM stadium ORDER BY Average_Attendance DESC;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing each stadium's name, number of home games, and average attendance, ordered from the highest to the lowest average attendance.

sql: SELECT * FROM game WHERE Season = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with complete details for all games played during the 2007 season, including the date, teams, score, and competition type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Player FROM injury_accident;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the unique names of all players who have had a recorded injury.

sql: SELECT name, Capacity_Percentage FROM stadium WHERE Capacity_Percentage > 100;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying stadiums that have exceeded 100% capacity, listing each stadium's name and its specific capacity percentage.

sql: SELECT Home_team, Away_team, Score FROM game WHERE Competition = 'League';
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the home team, away team, and final score for all matches played in the 'League' competition.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.stadium_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each stadium by name along with the total count of games played in that stadium.

sql: SELECT Player, SUM(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Player ORDER BY SUM(Number_of_matches) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each player and the total number of matches associated with their injuries, ordered from the highest number of matches to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.Player, T2.Injury, T2.Number_of_matches FROM game AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id WHERE T1.Competition = 'League';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing players who were injured during league matches, along with their specific injury and the number of matches affected by it.

sql: SELECT Injury, AVG(Number_of_matches) FROM injury_accident GROUP BY Injury;

sql: SELECT Source, COUNT(*) FROM injury_accident WHERE Source = 'inter.it' GROUP BY Source;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total count of injury accidents originating from the source 'inter.it'.

sql: SELECT Season, COUNT(*) FROM game GROUP BY Season;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each season and the corresponding total count of games played in that season.

sql: SELECT T2.Player, T2.Injury FROM game AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id WHERE T1.Home_team = 'Pakhtakor' OR T1.Away_team = 'Pakhtakor';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the player and their specific injury for all games in which the team 'Pakhtakor' participated, either as the home or away team.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.stadium_id JOIN injury_accident AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.game_id WHERE T3.Player = 'Davide Santon';

sql: SELECT T2.Player, SUM(T2.Number_of_matches) FROM game AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id WHERE T1.Competition = 'League' GROUP BY T2.Player HAVING SUM(T2.Number_of_matches) > 5;

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the home team, away team, and final score for games where a player's injury resulted in an above-average number of missed matches.

sql: SELECT T3.Player, T3.Injury FROM stadium AS T1 JOIN game AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.stadium_id JOIN injury_accident AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.game_id WHERE T1.name = 'Cowboys Stadium';

sql: SELECT T2.Player, T2.Injury FROM game AS T1 JOIN injury_accident AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.game_id WHERE T1.id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM game WHERE Season = 2007);

result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of schools whose total budgeted funds are greater than their total endowment funds.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name, T1.Enrollment, T2.TotalDonation FROM School AS T1 JOIN (SELECT School_id, SUM(amount) AS TotalDonation FROM endowment GROUP BY School_id) AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T2.TotalDonation > 9.5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing schools that have received total donations exceeding 9.5, showing each school's name, current enrollment, and the total donation amount.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 WHERE T1.Enrollment > 600 UNION SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T2.Year = 2000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of schools that either have an enrollment greater than 600 or had a budget in the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 WHERE T1.County = '50 Marshall' INTERSECT SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN endowment AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of schools that are both located in '50 Marshall' county and have a recorded endowment.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN endowment AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id EXCEPT SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN endowment AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T2.donator_name = 'San José de Ocoa';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of schools that have received an endowment from any donator except for 'San José de Ocoa'.

sql: SELECT School_name, Location FROM School AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM budget AS T2 WHERE T2.School_id = T1.School_id AND T2.Year = 1999);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and location of the school that had a budget record for the year 1999.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Budgeted > 5000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS High_Budget_Years, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Budgeted <= 5000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Low_Budget_Years FROM School AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id GROUP BY T1.School_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, providing a breakdown for each school by counting the number of years its budget was greater than 5000 versus the number of years it was 5000 or less.

sql: SELECT School_name, Enrollment FROM School WHERE Enrollment = (SELECT MAX(Enrollment) FROM School);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the school with the maximum student enrollment, showing the school's name and that enrollment number.

sql: SELECT T1.donator_name FROM endowment AS T1 WHERE T1.School_id = (SELECT School_id FROM School ORDER BY Enrollment DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the donator for the school with the largest student enrollment.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id GROUP BY T1.School_name HAVING AVG(T2.Budgeted) < (SELECT AVG(Invested) FROM budget);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of schools whose average budgeted amount is less than the overall average invested amount across all budgets.

sql: SELECT T2.donator_name FROM School AS T1 JOIN endowment AS T2 ON T1.School_id = T2.School_id WHERE T1.County = '71 St. Joseph';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of donators who have contributed to schools located in '71 St. Joseph' county.

sql: SELECT T1.School_name, T1.TotalAmount, (SELECT COUNT(T2.School_id) FROM (SELECT School_id, SUM(amount) AS TotalAmount FROM endowment GROUP BY School_id) AS T2 WHERE T2.TotalAmount > T1.TotalAmount) + 1 AS Rank FROM (SELECT T3.School_id, T3.School_name, SUM(T4.amount) AS TotalAmount FROM School AS T3 JOIN endowment AS T4 ON T3.School_id = T4.School_id GROUP BY T3.School_id) AS T1 ORDER BY Rank;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows ranking schools by their total endowment, listing each school's name, the calculated total endowment amount, and its corresponding rank from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of every contestant.

sql: SELECT * FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'NY';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all vote details for votes cast from the state of New York.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM VOTES;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of votes recorded in the system.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT state FROM AREA_CODE_STATE;
result_summary: Returns 51 rows, providing a distinct list of all states and territories for which area code information is available.

sql: SELECT * FROM CONTESTANTS WHERE contestant_number = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available information for the contestant specifically identified by contestant number 1.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name, COUNT(T1.vote_id) FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T2.contestant_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each contestant's name along with the total count of votes they received.

sql: SELECT state, COUNT(*) FROM VOTES GROUP BY state;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each state along with the total count of votes cast in that state.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.state = 'CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received a vote from the state of California.

sql: SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the contestant number for any contestant who received more than one vote.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T2 WHERE T2.contestant_number IN (SELECT T1.contestant_number FROM VOTES AS T1 WHERE T1.state = 'NY');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of contestants who received votes from the state of New York.

sql: SELECT T1.phone_number, T1.state AS registered_state, T2.state AS area_code_state FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN AREA_CODE_STATE AS T2 ON CAST(T1.phone_number / 10000000 AS INTEGER) = T2.area_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each voter's phone number, their registered voting state, and the state corresponding to their phone number's area code.

sql: SELECT T1.vote_id, T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number ORDER BY T1.created DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the vote ID and the corresponding contestant's name for the most recent votes, ordered from newest to oldest.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T2.contestant_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.state) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received votes from more than one state.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name, COUNT(T1.vote_id) AS vote_count FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number GROUP BY T2.contestant_name HAVING COUNT(T1.vote_id) = (SELECT MAX(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number));
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the contestant who received the maximum number of votes, showing their name and total vote count.

sql: SELECT contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS WHERE contestant_number NOT IN (SELECT contestant_number FROM VOTES);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of all contestants who have not yet received any votes.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.state = 'NY' INTERSECT SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.state = 'CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the contestant who received votes from voters in both New York and California.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.state = 'NY' EXCEPT SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.state = 'CA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a contestant who received votes in New York ('NY') but did not receive any votes in California ('CA').

sql: SELECT T1.state, COUNT(T1.vote_id) FROM VOTES AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number = (SELECT contestant_number FROM CONTESTANTS WHERE contestant_name = 'Kelly Clauss') GROUP BY T1.state;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each state from which the contestant 'Kelly Clauss' received votes, along with the total vote count for that state.

sql: SELECT state FROM VOTES GROUP BY state ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the state with the highest total number of votes.

sql: SELECT T1.state, T1.c FROM (SELECT state, COUNT(*) as c FROM VOTES GROUP BY state) AS T1 WHERE T1.c > (SELECT AVG(c) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM VOTES GROUP BY state) AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the state and its corresponding vote count, for any state whose vote total is greater than the average vote total across all states.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name FROM CONTESTANTS AS T2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM VOTES AS T1 WHERE T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number AND T1.state = 'NY');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of contestants who received at least one vote from the state of 'NY'.

sql: SELECT T3.state, T2.contestant_name, COUNT(*) as vote_count FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number JOIN AREA_CODE_STATE AS T3 ON CAST(T1.phone_number / 10000000 AS INTEGER) = T3.area_code WHERE T1.state != T3.state GROUP BY T3.state, T2.contestant_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows tallying the number of votes each contestant received from voters who cast their vote in a state different from their phone number's home state, grouped by the voter's state.

sql: SELECT A.contestant_name, B.state, B.num_votes FROM CONTESTANTS AS A JOIN (SELECT contestant_number, state, count(*) as num_votes FROM VOTES GROUP BY contestant_number, state) AS B ON A.contestant_number = B.contestant_number;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the total number of votes each contestant received per state, listing the contestant's name, the state, and the vote count.

sql: SELECT T2.contestant_name, T1.created FROM VOTES AS T1 JOIN CONTESTANTS AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.vote_id IN (SELECT vote_id FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'NY' UNION SELECT vote_id FROM VOTES WHERE state = 'CA');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the contestant's name and the timestamp for each individual vote cast from either New York ('NY') or California ('CA').

sql: SELECT T1.state, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM VOTES AS T2 WHERE T2.state = T1.state) AS total_votes FROM AREA_CODE_STATE AS T1 WHERE T1.state IN ('NJ', 'CT');
result_summary: Returns 13 rows for area code records in New Jersey ('NJ') and Connecticut ('CT'), listing the state alongside the total vote count for that state.

sql: SELECT * FROM country WHERE Capital = 'Jakarta';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the country whose capital city is 'Jakarta'.

sql: SELECT Name, Team_id FROM team;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and unique ID for every team in the database.

sql: SELECT Player, College FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Class = '1996 MLS College Draft';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player from the '1996 MLS College Draft' and the college they attended.

sql: SELECT Player, Years_Played FROM player WHERE Team = 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and years played for each player on Team 1.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Position FROM match_season;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows providing a distinct list of all available player positions in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.Country_name, COUNT(T1.Player) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN country AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.Country_id GROUP BY T2.Country_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each country's name alongside the total number of players from that country recorded in the match season.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each player's name and their corresponding team name.

sql: SELECT Position, AVG(Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season GROUP BY Position;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each player position along with the average draft pick number for that position.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.College FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T2.Name = 'Columbus Crew';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player and their attended college for the team 'Columbus Crew'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each team's name alongside the total count of players on that team.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.Season = 1996;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the player and their corresponding team name for matches that occurred during the 1996 season.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Position FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T2.Name = 'Anais VonRueden';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the player's name and their position for the team named 'Anais VonRueden'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(T1.Player_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any team that has more than one player.

sql: SELECT Player, Years_Played FROM player WHERE Team IN (SELECT Team_id FROM team WHERE Name = 'Columbus Crew');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the players who have played for the 'Columbus Crew' team, along with the specific years they played.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, count(DISTINCT T1.College) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.College) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing teams that have played in more than one distinct college, showing each team's name and the corresponding count of colleges.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Position, T1.Draft_Pick_Number FROM match_season AS T1 WHERE T1.Draft_Pick_Number > (SELECT AVG(T2.Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season AS T2 WHERE T2.Position = T1.Position);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing players whose draft pick number is higher (i.e., they were selected later) than the average draft pick number for their specific position.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of teams that have participated in a match season but have no players associated with them.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM player AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of teams that have both participated in a match season and have players assigned to them.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Player, T1.Draft_Pick_Number FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id JOIN (SELECT Team, MIN(Draft_Pick_Number) AS min_pick FROM match_season GROUP BY Team) AS T3 ON T1.Team = T3.Team AND T1.Draft_Pick_Number = T3.min_pick;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying the player with the earliest (lowest number) draft pick for each team, showing the team name, player name, and the draft pick number itself.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Team IN (SELECT Team FROM match_season WHERE Position = 'Midfielder' AND College = 'Virginia');
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Player FROM match_season WHERE Draft_Class = '1996 MLS College Draft' UNION SELECT Player FROM player WHERE Singles_WL = '1–0';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who were either part of the '1996 MLS College Draft' or have a singles win-loss record of '1–0'.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Team NOT IN (SELECT T2.Team FROM match_season AS T2 WHERE T2.Draft_Class = 'Project-40');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the players whose teams were not part of the 'Project-40' draft class.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Player), AVG(T1.Draft_Pick_Number) FROM match_season AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.Team = T2.Team_id WHERE T1.Team IN (SELECT DISTINCT Team FROM player) GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows for teams that have players, listing each team's name, their total player count from the season, and the average draft pick number for those players.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM player AS T1 WHERE T1.Team IN (SELECT T2.Team FROM match_season AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Team HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Draft_Class) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who are on teams that have fielded players from more than one distinct draft class.

sql: SELECT Position FROM match_season GROUP BY Position HAVING AVG(Draft_Pick_Number) < 10;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the player position where the average draft pick number for players in that role is less than 10.

sql: SELECT custid, name FROM ACCOUNTS;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the customer ID and name for all customer accounts.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the account holder for customer ID 2.

sql: SELECT balance FROM CHECKING WHERE custid = 3;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the checking account balance for the customer with ID 3.

sql: SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance = 200000.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the customer ID for any savings account with a balance of exactly $200,000.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ACCOUNTS;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total count of all accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name along with their corresponding savings account balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T1.name = 'Brown';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and checking account balance for the customer named 'Brown'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and the combined balance from their savings and checking accounts.

sql: SELECT AVG(balance) FROM SAVINGS;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the calculated average balance across all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT SUM(balance) FROM CHECKING;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total sum of the balances from all checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid ORDER BY T2.balance DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing each customer's name and their corresponding savings account balance, ordered from the highest balance to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance > 2000.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of customers who have a balance greater than $2000 in their checking account.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance > (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM SAVINGS);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the customer whose savings account balance is greater than the average balance of all savings accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid WHERE T2.balance < (SELECT AVG(balance) FROM CHECKING);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers whose checking account balance is below the average balance across all checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM CHECKING AS T2 WHERE T2.custid = T1.custid AND T2.balance = 2000.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any customer who has a checking account with a balance of exactly $2000.0.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance, T3.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance > T3.balance;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing customers whose savings account balance is greater than their checking account balance, showing the customer's name alongside both account balances.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance > 200000.0) UNION SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM CHECKING WHERE balance > 2500.0);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of customers who either have a savings account balance over $200,000 or a checking account balance over $2,500.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance > 200000.0) INTERSECT SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM CHECKING WHERE balance > 2500.0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has both a savings balance over $200,000 and a checking balance over $2,500.

sql: SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM SAVINGS WHERE balance > 200000.0) EXCEPT SELECT name FROM ACCOUNTS WHERE custid IN (SELECT custid FROM CHECKING WHERE balance = 10000.0);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers who have a savings account balance greater than $200,000, excluding any who also have a checking account with a balance of exactly $10,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid GROUP BY T1.name HAVING T2.balance + T3.balance > 220000.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of customers whose combined balance across their savings and checking accounts is greater than $220,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.custid, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_bal FROM SAVINGS AS T2 JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T2.custid = T3.custid) AS T4 ON T1.custid = T4.custid ORDER BY T4.total_bal DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the customer with the highest combined balance across their savings and checking accounts.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid ORDER BY T2.balance DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and savings balance for the customer with the second-highest balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, CASE WHEN T2.balance > 5000 THEN 'High' WHEN T2.balance > 2500 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS category FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN CHECKING AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's name alongside a category ('High', 'Medium', or 'Low') based on their checking account balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.balance + T3.balance AS total_balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN SAVINGS AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN CHECKING AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T2.balance + T3.balance > (SELECT AVG(S.balance + C.balance) FROM SAVINGS AS S JOIN CHECKING AS C ON S.custid = C.custid);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying customers whose combined savings and checking balance exceeds the overall average, showing their name and that total balance.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.total_balance FROM ACCOUNTS AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.custid, SUM(T2.balance) AS total_balance FROM (SELECT custid, balance FROM SAVINGS UNION ALL SELECT custid, balance FROM CHECKING) AS T2 GROUP BY T2.custid) AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T3.total_balance < 300000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows showing the name and total combined balance for customers whose sum of balances across their savings and checking accounts is less than $300,000.

sql: SELECT * FROM list WHERE Classroom = 101;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows with the last name, first name, grade, and classroom number for all students assigned to classroom 101.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName FROM teachers;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the last and first name for every teacher in the dataset.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName FROM list WHERE Grade = 6;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the last and first names of all students in the 6th grade.

sql: SELECT * FROM teachers WHERE LastName = 'MACROSTIE';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the last name, first name, and classroom number for any teacher whose last name is 'MACROSTIE'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM list;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of records in the `list` table.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.LastName = 'CAR';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the last and first name of the teacher who instructs the student with the last name 'CAR'.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, count(T1.LastName) FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each teacher by first and last name, along with the total count of students in their assigned classroom.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, AVG(T1.Grade) FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName ORDER BY AVG(T1.Grade) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each teacher's last name alongside the average grade of their students, ordered from the highest average grade to the lowest.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName FROM teachers WHERE Classroom IN (SELECT Classroom FROM list WHERE Grade = 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the last and first name of a teacher who teaches 2nd grade.

sql: SELECT Classroom FROM list GROUP BY Classroom HAVING count(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, providing a distinct list of all classroom numbers present in the data.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, T1.Grade FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, T1.Grade;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, providing a unique list of teachers by their first and last names along with the specific grade level they teach.

sql: SELECT MAX(Grade), MIN(Grade) FROM list;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the highest and lowest grade found across all records in the list.

sql: SELECT LastName, FirstName FROM list WHERE Classroom = (SELECT Classroom FROM list WHERE LastName = 'CAR') AND LastName <> 'CAR';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the last and first names of all students who are in the same classroom as the student with the last name 'CAR'.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, COUNT(T1.LastName) FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.Grade > 0 GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName HAVING COUNT(T1.LastName) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each teacher's full name and the total number of students they teach in grades 1 and higher.

sql: SELECT T1.LastName, T1.FirstName FROM teachers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM list AS T2 WHERE T2.Classroom = T1.Classroom AND T2.LastName = 'CAR');
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the last and first names of teachers who have a student with the last name 'CAR' in their classroom.

sql: SELECT LastName FROM list UNION SELECT LastName FROM teachers;
result_summary: Returns 72 rows containing a combined and unique list of last names from both the 'list' and 'teachers' tables.

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom WHERE T1.Grade = (SELECT MIN(Grade) FROM list);

sql: SELECT T2.LastName, T2.FirstName, CASE WHEN AVG(T1.Grade) >= 4 THEN 'Teaches Upper Grades' ELSE 'Teaches Lower Grades' END FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.Classroom = T2.Classroom GROUP BY T2.LastName, T2.FirstName;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows, listing each teacher's name and categorizing them as teaching 'Upper Grades' or 'Lower Grades' based on whether the average grade level of their classroom is 4 or higher.

sql: SELECT LastName, COUNT(*) AS NameCount FROM (SELECT LastName FROM list UNION ALL SELECT LastName FROM teachers) GROUP BY LastName ORDER BY NameCount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 72 rows listing each unique last name found across both the 'list' and 'teachers' tables, along with a count of its total occurrences, ordered from most frequent to least frequent.
