sql: SELECT player_id, first_name, last_name, birth_date FROM players WHERE country_code = 'SUI';
result_summary: Returns 243 rows listing players from Switzerland, showing their player ID, first and last names, and birth date.

sql: SELECT tourney_name, winner_name, loser_name, score FROM matches WHERE surface = 'Hard';
result_summary: Returns 205 rows for tennis matches played on a hard court, detailing the tournament name, the winning and losing players, and the final score for each match.

sql: SELECT ranking_date, ranking, ranking_points FROM rankings WHERE player_id = 200033;
result_summary: Returns 943 rows detailing the ranking history for a specific player, listing their rank and ranking points for each recorded date.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, country_code FROM players WHERE hand = 'R';
result_summary: Returns 5028 rows listing the first name, last name, and country code for all players who are right-handed.

sql: SELECT winner_name, loser_name, minutes FROM matches WHERE minutes > 80;
result_summary: Returns 184 rows listing extended tennis matches that lasted over 80 minutes, showing the names of the winner and loser along with the match duration in minutes.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.match_num) AS wins FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.winner_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 38 rows listing each player by their first and last name, along with a total count of the matches they have won.

sql: SELECT surface, AVG(winner_age) AS avg_winner_age FROM matches GROUP BY surface;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each court surface type along with the average age of the match winners on that surface.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, MIN(T2.ranking) AS best_rank FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 2775 rows, listing each player's first and last name along with their best-ever (lowest) achieved ranking.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.match_num) AS losses FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.loser_id WHERE T1.country_code = 'USA' GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first name, last name, and total number of losses for each player from the USA.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.ranking_points) AS total_points FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.ranking <= 10 GROUP BY T1.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 42 rows, providing each player's first and last name along with the total sum of their ranking points accumulated from their top 10 ranking performances.

sql: SELECT tourney_name, COUNT(*) AS num_matches FROM matches GROUP BY tourney_name;
result_summary: Returns 53 rows listing each tournament name along with the total number of matches played in that tournament.

sql: SELECT T1.country_code, AVG(T2.ranking) AS avg_rank FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.country_code;
result_summary: Returns 95 rows, listing the average player ranking for each country, grouped by country code.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM players WHERE player_id IN (SELECT winner_id FROM matches WHERE tourney_name = 'WTA Championships');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of players who have won a match in the 'WTA Championships' tournament.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.avg_rank FROM players AS T1 JOIN (SELECT player_id, AVG(ranking) AS avg_rank FROM rankings GROUP BY player_id) AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.avg_rank < (SELECT AVG(ranking) FROM rankings);
result_summary: Returns 636 rows identifying players whose average ranking is better than the overall average, showing each player's first name, last name, and their calculated average rank.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id JOIN matches AS T3 ON T1.player_id = T3.winner_id GROUP BY T1.player_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(T3.match_num) > 5 AND AVG(T2.ranking) < 50;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of players who have won more than five matches while maintaining an average ranking of better than 50.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.winner_id EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id WHERE T2.ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows listing the first and last names of all players who have won a match, excluding any player who has ever held the number 1 ranking.

sql: SELECT T3.country_code, MIN(T3.best_rank) AS country_best_rank FROM (SELECT T1.country_code, MIN(T2.ranking) AS best_rank FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id, T1.country_code) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.country_code;
result_summary: Returns 95 rows, with each row listing a country's code and the single best ranking ever achieved by any player from that country.

sql: SELECT winner_name, COUNT(*) AS upset_wins FROM matches WHERE winner_rank > loser_rank AND loser_rank IS NOT NULL GROUP BY winner_name ORDER BY upset_wins DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top players with the most upset victories (wins against a higher-ranked opponent), ordered from the highest number of upsets to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.winner_id WHERE T2.surface = 'Hard' INTERSECT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN matches AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.loser_id WHERE T2.surface = 'Hard';
result_summary: Returns 32 rows listing the first and last names of players who have both won at least one match and lost at least one match on a hard court surface.

sql: SELECT T1.ranking_date, T1.ranking, (SELECT T2.ranking FROM rankings AS T2 WHERE T2.player_id = T1.player_id AND T2.ranking_date < T1.ranking_date ORDER BY T2.ranking_date DESC LIMIT 1) AS previous_rank FROM rankings AS T1 WHERE T1.player_id = 200033 ORDER BY T1.ranking_date;
result_summary: Returns 943 rows detailing the complete ranking history for player 200033, showing their rank on a given date alongside their rank from the previous period, ordered chronologically.

sql: SELECT tourney_name, AVG(minutes) FROM matches WHERE minutes IS NOT NULL GROUP BY tourney_name HAVING AVG(minutes) > (SELECT AVG(minutes) FROM matches WHERE minutes IS NOT NULL);
result_summary: Returns 27 rows listing tournaments where the average match duration is greater than the overall average, showing each tournament's name and its specific average match length in minutes.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 WHERE T1.player_id IN (SELECT winner_id FROM matches WHERE loser_name = 'Agnieszka Radwanska') AND T1.player_id IN (SELECT loser_id FROM matches WHERE winner_name = 'Serena Williams');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of players who have won a match against 'Agnieszka Radwanska' and also lost a match to 'Serena Williams'.

sql: SELECT T3.first_name, T3.last_name, SUM(T2.wins) AS total_wins, SUM(T2.losses) AS total_losses FROM (SELECT winner_id AS player_id, 1 AS wins, 0 AS losses FROM matches UNION ALL SELECT loser_id, 0, 1 FROM matches) AS T2 JOIN players AS T3 ON T2.player_id = T3.player_id GROUP BY T3.player_id;
result_summary: Returns 50 rows listing each player's career win-loss record, showing their first name, last name, and the total number of wins and losses aggregated from all matches.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM players AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM matches AS T2 WHERE (T2.winner_id = T1.player_id OR T2.loser_id = T1.player_id) AND T2.year = 2013);
result_summary: Returns 8 rows with the first and last names of players who participated in at least one match during the year 2013.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, MAX(T2.ranking) - MIN(T2.ranking) AS rank_swing FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.player_id HAVING COUNT(T2.ranking) > 1 ORDER BY rank_swing DESC LIMIT 10;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows identifying the players with the most volatile ranking history, ordered from the largest rank swing to the smallest. For each player with at least two ranking records, the list shows their name and the calculated difference between their highest and lowest rank.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Chevrolet House';

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Price_in_Dollar FROM furniture AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Furniture_ID = T2.Furniture_ID WHERE T2.Price_in_Dollar > (SELECT AVG(Price_in_Dollar) FROM furniture_manufacte);

result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of furniture produced by the manufacturer with the largest number of shops.

sql: SELECT Name, Open_Year FROM manufacturer AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM furniture_manufacte AS T2 WHERE T2.Manufacturer_ID = T1.Manufacturer_ID);

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T3.avg_price FROM (SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Price_in_Dollar) AS avg_price FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID GROUP BY T1.Name) AS T3 WHERE T3.avg_price > 100;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM manufacturer AS T1 JOIN furniture_manufacte AS T2 ON T1.Manufacturer_ID = T2.Manufacturer_ID JOIN furniture AS T3 ON T2.Furniture_ID = T3.Furniture_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Chabudai';

result_summary: Returns 59 rows, listing the first and last name of each customer alongside the first and last name of their assigned employee support representative.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM artists AS T1 JOIN albums AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.artist_id GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.total) FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows, with each row listing a customer's first and last name and the total sum of their spending across all invoices.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.composer FROM tracks AS T1 JOIN albums AS T2 ON T1.album_id = T2.id WHERE T2.title = 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and composer for each track found on the album 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM genres AS T1 JOIN tracks AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.genre_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing each music genre by name alongside the total count of tracks belonging to that genre.

sql: SELECT T1.country, SUM(T2.total) FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.country;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing the total invoice sales for all customers, grouped by their country.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM employees AS T1 JOIN employees AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.reports_to GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each manager by their first and last name and providing a count of their direct reports.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T2.total) AS total_spent FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the top-spending customers, showing each customer's first and last name alongside their total calculated spending, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM genres AS T1 JOIN tracks AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.genre_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 100;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each music genre that contains more than 100 tracks, along with the total count of tracks for each of those genres.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T4.name, T2.invoice_date FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id JOIN invoice_lines AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.invoice_id JOIN tracks AS T4 ON T3.track_id = T4.id WHERE T1.first_name = 'Leonie';

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM artists AS T1 JOIN albums AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.artist_id JOIN tracks AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.album_id JOIN genres AS T4 ON T3.genre_id = T4.id WHERE T4.name IN ('Rock', 'Metal') GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T4.name) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of artists who have produced music in both the 'Rock' and 'Metal' genres.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.total) FROM employees AS T1 JOIN customers AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.support_rep_id JOIN invoices AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.customer_id WHERE T1.title = 'Sales Support Agent' GROUP BY T1.id;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each 'Sales Support Agent' by their first and last name, along with the total sum of sales from the customers they support.

sql: SELECT T1.id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, AVG(T2.total) FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING AVG(T2.total) > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM invoices);
result_summary: Returns 23 rows identifying high-value customers whose average invoice total is greater than the overall average for all invoices, showing each customer's ID, name, and their specific average invoice amount.

sql: WITH TrackSales AS (SELECT T1.track_id, SUM(T1.quantity) as total_sold FROM invoice_lines AS T1 GROUP BY T1.track_id), RankedTracks AS (SELECT T2.name AS track_name, T3.name AS genre_name, T1.total_sold, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.genre_id ORDER BY T1.total_sold DESC) as sales_rank FROM TrackSales AS T1 JOIN tracks AS T2 ON T1.track_id = T2.id JOIN genres AS T3 ON T2.genre_id = T3.id) SELECT track_name, genre_name, total_sold FROM RankedTracks WHERE sales_rank = 1;
result_summary: Returns 331 rows identifying the top-selling track(s) for each music genre, showing the track's name, its genre, and the total units sold to achieve that rank.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.quantity) AS rock_tracks_bought FROM customers AS T1 JOIN invoices AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id JOIN invoice_lines AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.invoice_id JOIN tracks AS T4 ON T3.track_id = T4.id JOIN genres AS T5 ON T4.genre_id = T5.id WHERE T5.name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T1.id ORDER BY rock_tracks_bought DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single customer who has purchased the most 'Rock' genre tracks, showing their first name, last name, and the total quantity bought.

result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and number of platforms for all train stations that have more than 10 platforms.

result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each train's name, service, and scheduled time, ordered from the latest time of day to the earliest.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID  =  T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID  =  T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Name  =  'Ananthapuri Express';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all stations serviced by the 'Ananthapuri Express' train.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Train_ID) FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each station name and the total number of trains that service that station.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Time FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'London Waterloo';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and associated time for all trains that pass through the 'London Waterloo' station.

sql: SELECT SUM(Total_Passengers) FROM station WHERE Location = 'London';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the total number of passengers across all stations located in London.

sql: SELECT Name, Total_Passengers, Number_of_Platforms FROM station WHERE Number_of_Platforms > 15;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows for large stations with more than 15 platforms, listing each station's name, total passenger count, and platform count.

sql: SELECT T3.Location, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Service) FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID GROUP BY T3.Location;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each station location and the corresponding count of distinct train services available there.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T1.Service = 'Daily' AND T2.Station_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of trains that operate on a 'Daily' service and stop at a specific station (Station ID 1).

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Train_ID) FROM station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID  =  T2.Station_ID GROUP BY T1.Station_ID HAVING count(T2.Train_ID)  >  (SELECT avg(cnt) FROM (SELECT count(Train_ID) AS cnt FROM train_station GROUP BY Station_ID));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the stations with an above-average number of passing trains, showing each station's name and its corresponding train count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID  =  T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID IN (SELECT Station_ID FROM station WHERE Location  =  'London');
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of trains that stop at any station located in London.

sql: SELECT Name, Total_Passengers FROM station WHERE Total_Passengers = (SELECT MAX(Total_Passengers) FROM station);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the station with the highest number of total passengers, listing its name and that passenger count.

sql: SELECT T1.Train_ID FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'London Waterloo' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Train_ID FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'London Bridge';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the IDs of trains that stop at 'London Waterloo' but do not stop at 'London Bridge'.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T4.Name FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID AND T1.Train_ID < T2.Train_ID JOIN train AS T3 ON T1.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID JOIN train AS T4 ON T2.Train_ID = T4.Train_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing pairs of train names for trains that share a common station.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Service = 'Daily' GROUP BY T3.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Train_ID) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of stations that are served by two or more distinct daily train services.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Total_Passengers) FROM station GROUP BY Location ORDER BY SUM(Total_Passengers) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the station location with the highest total number of passengers.

sql: SELECT Name FROM train WHERE Train_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Train_ID FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.Station_ID = T2.Station_ID WHERE T2.Number_of_Platforms < 15);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of trains that exclusively stop at large stations (those with 15 or more platforms).

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T2.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID WHERE T1.Name IN ('Ananthapuri Express', 'Guruvayur Express') GROUP BY T3.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Name) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the train station where both the 'Ananthapuri Express' and 'Guruvayur Express' trains stop.

sql: SELECT Name FROM train WHERE Service = 'Daily' UNION SELECT Name FROM station WHERE Location = 'London';
result_summary: Returns 17 rows containing a combined list of train names that have daily service and station names that are located in London.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM train AS T1 JOIN train_station AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID WHERE T2.Station_ID = (SELECT Station_ID FROM station ORDER BY Number_of_Platforms DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of trains that stop at the station with the largest number of platforms.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Annual_entry_exit) FROM station AS T1 WHERE T1.Station_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.Station_ID FROM train_station AS T2 JOIN train AS T3 ON T2.Train_ID = T3.Train_ID WHERE T3.Service = 'Daily');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average annual passenger entry and exit count for all stations that provide 'Daily' train service.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, MAX(T2.Time) FROM train_station AS T1 JOIN train AS T2 ON T1.Train_ID = T2.Train_ID JOIN station AS T3 ON T1.Station_ID = T3.Station_ID GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each station by name, along with the latest train time associated with that station.

sql: SELECT user_name, user_login FROM Users WHERE role_code = 'DBA';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the user name and login information for all users who have the role of Database Administrator (DBA).

sql: SELECT document_name, access_count FROM Documents WHERE document_type_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and access count for all documents that are classified as a 'Book'.

sql: SELECT image_url FROM Images WHERE image_name = 'top1';
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the image URL for the image specifically named 'top1'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Functional_Areas WHERE functional_area_description = 'Signature';
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing all details for the functional area specifically described as 'Signature'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Documents;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of documents in the database.

sql: SELECT T2.role_description, COUNT(T1.user_id) FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T2.role_description;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each user role description along with the total count of users assigned to that role.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T1.access_count FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code WHERE T2.functional_area_code = '1';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and access count for each document associated with functional area code '1'.

sql: SELECT document_type_code, AVG(access_count) FROM Documents GROUP BY document_type_code;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the average access count for each document type.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T2.section_title FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code WHERE T1.document_code = '217';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the document name and its associated section title for the specific document identified by code '217'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, COUNT(T2.functional_area_code) FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code GROUP BY T1.document_name HAVING COUNT(T2.functional_area_code) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each document's name and the total count of functional areas associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.user_name FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code WHERE T2.role_description = 'Project Manager';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the user names of all users who are assigned the 'Project Manager' role.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Structures AS T2 ON T1.document_structure_code = T2.document_structure_code WHERE T2.document_structure_code = T2.parent_document_structure_code;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that are classified as top-level or parent documents.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name, T4.image_url FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T3 ON T2.section_id = T3.section_id JOIN Images AS T4 ON T3.image_id = T4.image_id;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows, pairing each document's name with the URL of an image contained within that document.

sql: SELECT T2.role_description FROM Users AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.role_code = T2.role_code GROUP BY T2.role_description ORDER BY COUNT(T1.user_id) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the description of the most common user role, determined by the highest number of assigned users.

sql: SELECT document_name, document_code FROM Documents WHERE document_code NOT IN (SELECT T1.document_code FROM Document_Sections AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T2 ON T1.section_id = T2.section_id);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows with the names and codes of documents that do not contain any images.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Functional_Areas AS T3 ON T2.functional_area_code = T3.functional_area_code WHERE T3.parent_functional_area_code = '8';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the names of all documents that belong to any functional area under the parent functional area with code '8'.

sql: SELECT T4.image_name, T4.image_url FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections AS T2 ON T1.document_code = T2.document_code JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T3 ON T2.section_id = T3.section_id JOIN Images AS T4 ON T3.image_id = T4.image_id WHERE T1.document_type_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and URL for all images found within documents that are classified as a 'Book'.

sql: SELECT T1.document_type_code, T1.document_name, T1.access_count FROM Documents AS T1 JOIN (SELECT document_type_code, MAX(access_count) AS max_access FROM Documents GROUP BY document_type_code) AS T2 ON T1.document_type_code = T2.document_type_code AND T1.access_count = T2.max_access;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, identifying the name and access count of the single most-accessed document within each document type.

sql: SELECT T3.section_title FROM Images AS T1 JOIN Document_Sections_Images AS T2 ON T1.image_id = T2.image_id JOIN Document_Sections AS T3 ON T2.section_id = T3.section_id WHERE T1.image_url = 'http://www.bernierconnelly.com/1.jpg';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of the document section where a specific image, identified by its URL, is located.

sql: SELECT role_description FROM Roles UNION SELECT document_structure_description FROM Document_Structures;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows providing a consolidated, unique list of descriptions by combining role descriptions and document structure descriptions into a single column.

sql: SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.document_code IN (SELECT document_code FROM Document_Functional_Areas WHERE functional_area_code = '1') INTERSECT SELECT T1.document_name FROM Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.document_code IN (SELECT document_code FROM Document_Sections WHERE section_title = 'after');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of documents that belong to functional area '1' and also contain a section titled 'after'.

sql: SELECT document_code FROM Document_Functional_Areas WHERE functional_area_code = '1' EXCEPT SELECT document_code FROM Document_Functional_Areas WHERE functional_area_code = '9';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the document codes that are in functional area '1' but not in functional area '9'.

sql: SELECT AVG(SectionCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(section_id) AS SectionCount FROM Document_Sections GROUP BY document_code);
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the calculated average number of sections per document.

sql: SELECT T1.functional_area_description FROM Functional_Areas AS T1 LEFT JOIN Document_Functional_Areas AS T2 ON T1.functional_area_code = T2.functional_area_code WHERE T2.document_code IS NULL;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.user_name FROM Users AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Roles AS T2 WHERE T1.role_code = T2.role_code AND T2.role_description = 'Database Administrator');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the user names for all users who have the 'Database Administrator' role.

sql: SELECT forename, surname, nationality FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 49 rows listing the forename, surname, and nationality for all drivers of German nationality.

sql: SELECT name, location FROM circuits WHERE country = 'Australia';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location for all race circuits located in Australia.

sql: SELECT name, date FROM races WHERE year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the name and date of each race that took place during the 2009 season.

sql: SELECT name FROM constructors WHERE nationality = 'British';
result_summary: Returns 85 rows listing the names of all constructors with a British nationality.

sql: SELECT status FROM status ORDER BY statusId;
result_summary: Returns 134 rows listing every possible status, sorted in a predefined order based on their unique identifier.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.raceId) FROM races AS T1 JOIN circuits AS T2 ON T1.circuitId = T2.circuitId GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 72 rows listing each circuit by name along with the total count of races held at that location.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.points) AS total_points FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T2.driverId ORDER BY total_points DESC;
result_summary: Returns 842 rows listing each driver's full name and their total career points, ordered from the highest-scoring driver to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T1.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Finished';
result_summary: Returns 6009 rows listing the first and last names of drivers for every race they successfully finished.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.wins) AS total_wins FROM driverStandings AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T1.driverId HAVING SUM(T1.wins) > 0 ORDER BY total_wins DESC;
result_summary: Returns 107 rows listing the first name, last name, and total career wins for each driver with at least one win, ordered from the highest number of wins to the lowest.

sql: SELECT name, date FROM races WHERE circuitId = (SELECT circuitId FROM circuits WHERE name = 'Albert Park Grand Prix Circuit');
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing the name and date for each race held at the 'Albert Park Grand Prix Circuit'.

sql: SELECT T1.position, T1.q1, T1.q2, T1.q3 FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId WHERE T2.forename = 'Lewis' AND T2.surname = 'Hamilton' AND T3.name = 'Australian Grand Prix';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing Lewis Hamilton's qualifying results from the Australian Grand Prix, showing his final position and lap times for the Q1, Q2, and Q3 sessions for each race he participated in.

sql: SELECT T2.name, SUM(T1.points) FROM constructorResults AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T1.raceId, T2.name HAVING SUM(T1.points) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 890 rows listing constructor names and their total points from individual races, filtered to only include performances where the constructor scored more than 10 points.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, COUNT(*) AS pole_positions FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.position = 1 GROUP BY T2.driverId HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing drivers who have achieved more than one pole position, along with the total count of their pole positions.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T3.year = 2009;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of each unique driver who won at least one race during the 2009 season.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, SUM(T1.points) AS total_points FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId GROUP BY T2.driverId HAVING SUM(T1.points) > (SELECT AVG(total_points) FROM (SELECT SUM(points) AS total_points FROM results GROUP BY driverId));
result_summary: Returns 109 rows listing the first name, last name, and total career points for each driver who has accumulated more points than the overall average across all drivers.

sql: SELECT forename, 'Driver' FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'German' UNION SELECT name, 'Constructor' FROM constructors WHERE nationality = 'German';
result_summary: Returns 50 rows listing the names of all German drivers and constructors, with a second column identifying their role as either 'Driver' or 'Constructor'.

sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T2.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Finished' INTERSECT SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN status AS T3 ON T2.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.status = 'Accident';
result_summary: Returns 224 rows listing the first and last names of all drivers who have, at some point, both successfully finished a race and also had a race result classified as an 'Accident'.

sql: SELECT name FROM constructors EXCEPT SELECT T2.name FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1;
result_summary: Returns 164 rows listing the names of all constructors that have never won a race (achieved a first-place finish).

sql: SELECT T1.year, T3.forename, T3.surname FROM races AS T1 JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T2.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T1.circuitId = (SELECT circuitId FROM circuits WHERE name = 'Albert Park Grand Prix Circuit') AND T2.positionOrder = 1;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the year and the first and last name of the winning driver for each race held at the 'Albert Park Grand Prix Circuit'.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, T3.name, T1.fastestLapTime FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T4.name = 'Australian Grand Prix' AND T4.year = 2009 AND T1.positionOrder = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the winner of the 2009 Australian Grand Prix, showing their full name, constructor (team) name, and their fastest lap time for the race.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name, T3.location FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId JOIN races AS T4 ON T1.raceId = T4.raceId JOIN circuits AS T3 ON T4.circuitId = T3.circuitId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T2.nationality = 'British';
result_summary: Returns 54 rows listing the unique names and locations of circuits where a British driver has won a race (achieved a first-place finish).

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM races AS T1 JOIN qualifying AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN results AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId AND T2.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T2.position = 1 AND T3.positionOrder != 1;
result_summary: Returns 174 rows listing the names of races where the driver who qualified in pole position (first place) did not go on to win the final race.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.statusId) AS status_count FROM results AS T1 JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId GROUP BY T2.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.statusId) > 3;
result_summary: Returns 137 rows listing race car constructors that have experienced more than three distinct race result statuses, along with the specific count of unique statuses for each.

sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, COUNT(*) AS wins FROM results AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.positionOrder = 1 AND T2.driverRef != 'hamilton' GROUP BY T1.driverId ORDER BY wins DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the driver with the most all-time race wins, excluding 'hamilton', and shows their first name, surname, and total win count.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM races AS T1 JOIN constructorResults AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T2.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'McLaren' AND T2.points > 0 INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM races AS T1 JOIN constructorResults AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId JOIN constructors AS T3 ON T2.constructorId = T3.constructorId WHERE T3.name = 'Williams' AND T2.points > 0;
result_summary: Returns 36 rows listing the names of races where both the 'McLaren' and 'Williams' constructor teams scored points.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE LName = 'Smith';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows with the complete records for all students whose last name is 'Smith'.

sql: SELECT GName FROM Video_Games WHERE GType = 'Role-playing game';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the video game that is classified as a 'Role-playing game'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 18;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than 18.

sql: SELECT SportName, HoursPerWeek FROM SportsInfo WHERE OnScholarship = 'Y';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the sport and the number of hours per week for each athlete on a scholarship.

sql: SELECT LName, Fname FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the last and first names of all female students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.SportName = 'Football';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all students who play football.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, SUM(T2.Hours_Played) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Plays_Games AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each student's first and last name along with the total hours they have played across all games.

sql: SELECT T1.GName, COUNT(T2.StuID) AS NumberOfPlayers FROM Video_Games AS T1 JOIN Plays_Games AS T2 ON T1.GameID = T2.GameID GROUP BY T1.GName;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each video game's name along with the total number of students who play it.

sql: SELECT OnScholarship, AVG(HoursPerWeek) FROM SportsInfo GROUP BY OnScholarship;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows, showing the average hours per week for students on a scholarship compared to those who are not.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Plays_Games AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Video_Games AS T3 ON T2.GameID = T3.GameID WHERE T3.GName = 'RNG Stone';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all students who play the video game 'RNG Stone'.

sql: SELECT SportName FROM SportsInfo GROUP BY SportName HAVING SUM(GamesPlayed) > 20;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of sports for which the total number of games played is greater than 20.

sql: SELECT StuID, GameID, Hours_Played FROM Plays_Games WHERE Hours_Played > (SELECT AVG(Hours_Played) FROM Plays_Games);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the student ID, game ID, and hours played for gaming sessions that lasted longer than the overall average play time.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, SUM(T2.Hours_Played) AS TotalGameHours FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Plays_Games AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN SportsInfo AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing a student's first name, last name, and the total hours they've played games, for each student who is also involved in sports.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Age FROM Student AS T1 JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.SportName = 'Football' AND T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(T3.Age) FROM Student AS T3 JOIN SportsInfo AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.StuID WHERE T4.SportName = 'Football');
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name and age for the oldest student who plays football.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.OnScholarship = 'Y' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Plays_Games AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Hours_Played > 20;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows with the first and last names of students who are either on a sports scholarship or have played more than 20 hours in games.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.SportName = 'Athletics';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of any student who is from Baltimore and also participates in Athletics.

sql: SELECT T1.GName FROM Video_Games AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Plays_Games AS T2 JOIN Student AS T3 ON T2.StuID = T3.StuID WHERE T3.city_code = 'BAL' AND T2.GameID = T1.GameID);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of video games that are played by students residing in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT AVG(TotalHours) FROM (SELECT T1.StuID, SUM(T1.Hours_Played) AS TotalHours FROM Plays_Games AS T1 GROUP BY T1.StuID) AS StudentTotals;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing a single value representing the average of total hours played across all students.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 LEFT JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID LEFT JOIN Plays_Games AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID LEFT JOIN Video_Games AS T4 ON T3.GameID = T4.GameID WHERE T2.OnScholarship = 'Y' OR T4.GType = 'Collectible card game';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique first and last names of students who are either on a sports scholarship or play collectible card games.

sql: SELECT T2.SportName, COUNT(T1.StuID) as NumPlayers, AVG(T1.Age) as AvgAge FROM Student AS T1 JOIN SportsInfo AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T2.SportName HAVING COUNT(T1.StuID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each sport with more than one player, along with the total number of players and their average age.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM SportsInfo AS T1 WHERE T1.SportName = 'Football' EXCEPT SELECT T2.StuID FROM SportsInfo AS T2 WHERE T2.SportName = 'Athletics';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the student IDs of students who play Football but do not also participate in Athletics.

sql: SELECT * FROM customer WHERE state = 'Utah';

sql: SELECT * FROM bank WHERE city = 'New York City';

sql: SELECT loan_ID, amount FROM loan WHERE amount > 2500;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and amount for all loans with a value greater than 2,500.

sql: SELECT cust_name, acc_bal FROM customer ORDER BY acc_bal DESC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each customer's name and account balance, sorted from the highest account balance to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT loan_type FROM loan;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique types of loans available.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, SUM(T2.amount) FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID GROUP BY T1.cust_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each customer's name alongside the total sum of their loan amounts.

sql: SELECT T1.bname, COUNT(T2.cust_ID) FROM bank AS T1 JOIN customer AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T1.bname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each bank branch name along with the total number of customers associated with that branch.

sql: SELECT loan_type FROM loan GROUP BY loan_type HAVING SUM(amount) > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the loan type for which the total amount of all associated loans is greater than 4000.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.loan_type = 'Mortgages';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who has a mortgage loan.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T1.acc_bal FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID WHERE T2.bname = 'downtown';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and account balance for the customer associated with the 'downtown' bank branch.

sql: SELECT cust_name, credit_score FROM customer WHERE credit_score > (SELECT AVG(credit_score) FROM customer);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and credit score of customers who have an above-average credit score.

sql: SELECT T2.city, SUM(T1.amount) FROM loan AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.city;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each city along with the total sum of loan amounts from bank branches in that city.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, SUM(T3.amount) FROM customer AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.cust_ID = T3.cust_ID WHERE T2.city = 'New York City' GROUP BY T1.cust_name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer name and their total loan amount for customers who bank at a branch in 'New York City'.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE cust_ID IN (SELECT cust_ID FROM loan GROUP BY cust_ID HAVING COUNT(loan_ID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who has taken out more than one loan.

sql: SELECT T1.bname FROM bank AS T1 JOIN customer AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T1.bname HAVING AVG(T2.credit_score) < (SELECT AVG(credit_score) FROM customer);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of bank branches where the average credit score of their customers is lower than the overall average credit score across all customers.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE state = 'New York' UNION SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE acc_type = 'checking';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing a unique list of customer names who are either located in 'New York' or have a 'checking' account.

sql: SELECT T2.city FROM loan AS T1 JOIN bank AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T2.city HAVING SUM(T1.amount) > 4000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing cities where the total value of all loans from branches within that city is greater than 4000.

sql: SELECT T2.state, AVG(T2.acc_bal) FROM (SELECT * FROM customer WHERE credit_score > 25) AS T2 GROUP BY T2.state;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the average account balance for customers with a credit score above 25, grouped by their state.

sql: SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE state = 'Utah' INTERSECT SELECT cust_name FROM customer WHERE acc_type = 'saving';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the customer who both resides in 'Utah' and has a 'saving' account.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT loan_type FROM loan WHERE cust_ID NOT IN (SELECT cust_ID FROM customer WHERE state = 'New York');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique types of loans held by customers who do not live in the state of New York.

sql: SELECT bname, CASE WHEN no_of_customers > 300 THEN 'Large' WHEN no_of_customers >= 150 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Small' END FROM bank;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each bank branch name and a corresponding size category ('Large', 'Medium', or 'Small') based on its number of customers.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 WHERE T1.acc_type = 'saving' EXCEPT SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_ID = T2.cust_ID WHERE T2.loan_type = 'Business';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has a 'saving' account but does not have a 'Business' loan.

sql: SELECT T1.bname FROM bank AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID GROUP BY T1.bname HAVING MAX(T2.amount) > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM loan);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the bank branch name for branches where the maximum loan amount is greater than the average loan amount across all loans.

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name, T3.loan_type FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.cust_ID = T3.cust_ID JOIN bank AS T2 ON T3.branch_ID = T2.branch_ID WHERE T2.bname = 'morningside' AND T3.amount > 2000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer's name and their loan type for all loans greater than 2000 issued by the 'morningside' branch.

sql: SELECT T1.bname FROM bank AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM customer AS T2 WHERE T2.branch_ID = T1.branch_ID AND T2.credit_score > 200);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the bank branch that has at least one customer with a credit score greater than 200.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.bikes_available FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T1.city = 'San Jose';

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.bikes_available) FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.name;

sql: SELECT date, max_temperature_f FROM weather WHERE zip_code = 94107 ORDER BY max_temperature_f DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS trip_count FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY trip_count DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the top 5 most popular trip starting stations, ordered from highest to lowest by the total count of trips that began at each one.

sql: SELECT T2.cloud_cover, AVG(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN weather AS T2 ON SUBSTR(T1.start_date, 1, INSTR(T1.start_date, ' ') - 1) = T2.date AND T1.zip_code = T2.zip_code WHERE T2.cloud_cover = 4 GROUP BY T2.cloud_cover;

sql: SELECT T1.name, AVG(T2.bikes_available) AS avg_bikes FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY avg_bikes DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.bike_id, AVG(T1.duration) AS avg_dur FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id WHERE T2.city = 'San Jose' GROUP BY T1.bike_id ORDER BY avg_dur DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single bike with the highest average trip duration for trips that originated in the city of 'San Jose'.

sql: SELECT SUBSTR(start_date, 1, INSTR(start_date, ' ') - 1) AS trip_date FROM trip GROUP BY trip_date HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT SUBSTR(start_date, 1, INSTR(start_date, ' ') - 1)) FROM trip);

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM trip AS T1 JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.start_station_id = T2.id WHERE T1.start_station_id = T1.end_station_id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING COUNT(T1.id) > 0;

result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, height, and company of entrepreneurs who are taller than the average height of all people.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Company, T2.Money_Requested FROM people AS T1 JOIN entrepreneur AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Money_Requested = (SELECT MAX(Money_Requested) FROM entrepreneur);

sql: SELECT Investor, SUM(Money_Requested) FROM entrepreneur GROUP BY Investor HAVING SUM(Money_Requested) > (SELECT AVG(Money_Requested) FROM entrepreneur);

sql: SELECT T1.Investor, T1.Total_Request FROM (SELECT Investor, SUM(Money_Requested) AS Total_Request FROM entrepreneur GROUP BY Investor) AS T1 WHERE T1.Total_Request > 100000;

sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN Money_Requested >= 100000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Large_Requests, SUM(CASE WHEN Money_Requested < 100000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Small_Requests FROM entrepreneur;
result_summary: Returns 1 row that counts the total number of entrepreneur funding requests, separating them into two columns: 'Large_Requests' for those at or above $100,000 and 'Small_Requests' for those below $100,000.

sql: SELECT Investor FROM entrepreneur GROUP BY Investor ORDER BY SUM(Money_Requested) DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Company FROM entrepreneur AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID) AS Company_Name FROM people AS T1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Money_Requested FROM people AS T1 JOIN entrepreneur AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY abs(T2.Money_Requested - (SELECT AVG(Money_Requested) FROM entrepreneur)) LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T2.Maker, COUNT(T1.ModelId) FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN car_makers AS T2 ON T1.Maker = T2.Id GROUP BY T2.Maker;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows, listing each car maker alongside the total number of car models they manufacture.

sql: SELECT T1.Make, T3.FullName, T4.CountryName FROM car_names AS T1 JOIN cars_data AS T2 ON T1.MakeId = T2.Id JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T1.Model = T3.Maker JOIN countries AS T4 ON T3.Country = T4.CountryId WHERE T2.Cylinders = 8 AND T4.CountryName = 'usa';

sql: SELECT T1.FullName FROM car_makers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM car_names AS T2 JOIN cars_data AS T3 ON T2.MakeId = T3.Id WHERE T2.Model = T1.Maker AND T3.Year = 1970);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the full names of car manufacturers that produced at least one car model in the year 1970.

sql: SELECT T3.CountryName FROM cars_data AS T1 JOIN car_names AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.MakeId JOIN car_makers AS T4 ON T2.Model = T4.Maker JOIN countries AS T3 ON T4.Country = T3.CountryId GROUP BY T3.CountryName HAVING AVG(T1.Accelerate) > (SELECT AVG(Accelerate) FROM cars_data);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing countries where the average car acceleration is higher than the overall average across all cars.

sql: SELECT T1.Maker FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN countries AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.CountryId WHERE T2.CountryName = 'usa' UNION SELECT T1.Maker FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN countries AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.CountryId WHERE T2.CountryName = 'germany';

sql: SELECT CountryName, Number_of_Makers FROM (SELECT T2.CountryName, COUNT(T1.Id) AS Number_of_Makers FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN countries AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.CountryId GROUP BY T2.CountryName) WHERE Number_of_Makers > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each country that has more than one car maker, along with the total count of makers in that country.

sql: SELECT T4.Continent, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.Id), AVG(T5.Weight) FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN countries AS T2 ON T1.Country = T2.CountryId JOIN car_names AS T3 ON T1.Maker = T3.Model JOIN continents AS T4 ON T2.Continent = T4.ContId JOIN cars_data AS T5 ON T3.MakeId = T5.Id GROUP BY T4.Continent;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, one for each continent, showing the total count of distinct car makers and the average weight of cars associated with that continent.

sql: SELECT T2.CountryName, count(T1.ModelId) FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T1.Maker = T3.Id JOIN countries AS T2 ON T3.Country = T2.CountryId GROUP BY T2.CountryName ORDER BY count(T1.ModelId) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns the single country that produces the most car models, along with the total count of models for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T2.Make, T1.Horsepower FROM cars_data AS T1 JOIN car_names AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.MakeId WHERE T1.Horsepower = (SELECT MAX(T3.Horsepower) FROM cars_data AS T3 WHERE T3.Year = T1.Year);
result_summary: Returns 19 rows identifying the most powerful car for each year in the dataset, listing the car's production year, make, and its maximum horsepower value.

sql: SELECT T1.Cylinders, count(*) FROM cars_data AS T1 JOIN car_names AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.MakeId JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T2.Model = T3.Maker JOIN countries AS T4 ON T3.Country = T4.CountryId WHERE T4.CountryName = 'usa' GROUP BY T1.Cylinders;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the total count of cars from the USA, grouped by the number of cylinders.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T5.Continent FROM cars_data AS T1 JOIN car_names AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.MakeId JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T2.Model = T3.Maker JOIN countries AS T4 ON T3.Country = T4.CountryId JOIN continents AS T5 ON T4.Continent = T5.ContId WHERE T1.Cylinders = 8;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T2.city, T3.monthly_rental FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Student_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.student_id WHERE T3.other_details = 'house';

sql: SELECT T1.incident_type_description, COUNT(T2.incident_id) AS number_of_incidents FROM Ref_Incident_Type AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.incident_type_code = T2.incident_type_code GROUP BY T1.incident_type_description;

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T2.monthly_rental > (SELECT AVG(T4.monthly_rental) FROM Student_Addresses AS T4 JOIN Addresses AS T5 ON T4.address_id = T5.address_id WHERE T5.city = T3.city);

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T1.student_id NOT IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students_in_Detention);
