result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common incident type that resulted in a student receiving a detention, determined by counting the detentions associated with each type.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' AND T2.incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' AND T2.incident_type_code = 'NOISE';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first and last names of students residing in the USA who have been involved in either a 'VIOLENCE' or 'NOISE' related behavior incident.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(*) AS num_detentions FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Detention AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id = T2.teacher_id GROUP BY T1.teacher_id ORDER BY num_detentions DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the teacher who has assigned the most detentions, showing their first name, last name, and the total count.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.date_incident_start, T3.incident_summary, T4.datetime_detention_start, T4.detention_summary FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Students_in_Detention AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T3 ON T2.incident_id = T3.incident_id JOIN Detention AS T4 ON T2.detention_id = T4.detention_id;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows, providing a detailed record for each student's detention by linking their name to the original incident's summary and start date, as well as the detention's summary and start time.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.incident_type_code) = 1;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows containing the first and last names of students who have had behavior incidents of only a single, unique type.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Assessment_Notes AS T3 ON T1.student_id = T3.student_id WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', T2.date_incident_start) = strftime('%Y-%m', T3.date_of_notes);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code IN ('DISTURB', 'NOISE') GROUP BY T1.student_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.incident_type_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who have been involved in both disturbance-related and noise-related behavior incidents.

sql: SELECT T3.state_province_county, SUM(T2.monthly_rental) AS total_rental FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id GROUP BY T3.state_province_county ORDER BY total_rental DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top three states/provinces with the highest total monthly rental paid by students, ordered from highest to lowest total rental.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'VIOLENCE' EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Behavior_Incident AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.incident_type_code = 'NOISE';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of students who have had a violence-related incident, excluding any who have also had a noise-related incident.

sql: SELECT Document_Name, Document_Description FROM All_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and description for all documents identified as a Curriculum Vitae (CV).

sql: SELECT Employee_Name, Date_of_Birth FROM Employees ORDER BY Date_of_Birth ASC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing employee names and their dates of birth, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Roles WHERE Role_Code = 'ED';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of roles where the role code is 'ED'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each document name alongside its corresponding document type name.

sql: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE Gender_MFU = '1';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing all available details for employees identified with gender code '1'.

sql: SELECT T2.Document_Type_Name, count(T1.Document_ID) FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code GROUP BY T2.Document_Type_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each document type name along with the total number of documents belonging to that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID WHERE T2.Location_Code = 'e';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all documents found at the location with code 'e'.

sql: SELECT T1.Role_Code, count(*) FROM Employees AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Role_Code HAVING count(*) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each role code present in the employees table along with the total count of employees assigned to that role.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T3.Location_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Document_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Document_ID = T2.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Locations AS T3 ON T2.Location_Code = T3.Location_Code WHERE T1.Document_ID = 7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and storage location for a specific document (ID 7).

sql: SELECT Employee_Name FROM Employees WHERE Role_Code IN (SELECT Role_Code FROM Roles WHERE Role_Name = 'Editor');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing the names of all employees who have the role of 'Editor'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Role_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code WHERE T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique job roles of employees who have authorized the destruction of documents.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T1.Date_Stored FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Calendar AS T2 ON T1.Date_Stored = T2.Calendar_Date;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and storage date for documents whose storage date is validated against a reference calendar.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed) AND T1.Employee_ID IN (SELECT Destroyed_by_Employee_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who have both authorized a document for destruction and also physically carried out a document destruction.

sql: SELECT Document_ID FROM All_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'CV' EXCEPT SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed WHERE Actual_Destruction_Date IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the Document IDs for all CVs (Curriculum Vitae) that have not yet been marked as destroyed.

sql: SELECT T2.Location_Name, COUNT(T1.Document_ID) AS DocumentCount FROM Document_Locations AS T1 JOIN Ref_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_Code = T2.Location_Code GROUP BY T2.Location_Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Document_ID) > 0 ORDER BY DocumentCount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing locations and their corresponding total document counts, ordered from the location with the most documents to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 WHERE T2.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID AND T2.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM All_Documents WHERE Document_Type_Code = 'BK'));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of employees who have authorized the destruction of at least one document with the type code 'BK'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name, T2.Document_Type_Name, T4.Location_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 JOIN Ref_Document_Types AS T2 ON T1.Document_Type_Code = T2.Document_Type_Code JOIN Document_Locations AS T3 ON T1.Document_ID = T3.Document_ID JOIN Ref_Locations AS T4 ON T3.Location_Code = T4.Location_Code WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing documents scheduled for destruction, showing each document's name, type, and physical location.

sql: SELECT T1.Role_Name, T1.Role_Description FROM Roles AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.Role_Code FROM Employees AS T2);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT 'Authorizers' AS Category, COUNT(DISTINCT Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed UNION SELECT 'Destroyers', COUNT(DISTINCT Destroyed_by_Employee_ID) FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows that categorize employees as either 'Authorizers' or 'Destroyers' and provides the count of unique employees for each role in the document destruction process.

sql: WITH DocumentLocationCounts AS (SELECT Location_Code, COUNT(Document_ID) as DocCount FROM Document_Locations GROUP BY Location_Code) SELECT T2.Location_Name, T1.DocCount FROM DocumentLocationCounts AS T1 JOIN Ref_Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_Code = T2.Location_Code WHERE T1.DocCount > (SELECT AVG(DocCount) FROM DocumentLocationCounts);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying locations with an above-average number of documents, showing each location's name and its total document count.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name, T2.Role_Name, T4.Document_Name FROM Employees AS T1 JOIN Roles AS T2 ON T1.Role_Code = T2.Role_Code JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T3 ON T1.Employee_ID = T3.Destroyed_by_Employee_ID JOIN All_Documents AS T4 ON T3.Document_ID = T4.Document_ID;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name and role of each employee responsible for destroying a document, along with the name of the specific document they destroyed.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Date_of_Birth < (SELECT T2.Planned_Destruction_Date FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 JOIN All_documents AS T3 ON T2.document_id = T3.document_id WHERE T3.Document_Name = 'Robin CV');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of employees who were born before the planned destruction date for the document 'Robin CV'.

sql: SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Document_Locations WHERE Location_Code = 'e') INTERSECT SELECT T1.Document_Name FROM All_Documents AS T1 WHERE T1.Document_ID IN (SELECT Document_ID FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of documents that are located at location 'e' and are also scheduled to be destroyed.

sql: SELECT T2.Role_Name, count(T3.Document_ID) FROM Roles AS T2 LEFT JOIN Employees AS T1 ON T2.Role_Code = T1.Role_Code LEFT JOIN Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T3 ON T1.Employee_ID = T3.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID GROUP BY T2.Role_Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each employee role and the total number of documents that employees in that role have authorized for destruction.

sql: SELECT T1.Employee_Name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Documents_to_be_Destroyed AS T2 WHERE T2.Destruction_Authorised_by_Employee_ID = T1.Employee_ID) AS Authorized_Count FROM Employees AS T1 WHERE T1.Role_Code = 'MG';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each employee with the role code 'MG' alongside a count of the total number of document destructions they have authorized.

sql: SELECT * FROM university WHERE School = 'University of Delaware';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details, such as location, founding year, and enrollment, for the University of Delaware.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, ACC_Regular_Season FROM basketball_match WHERE All_Games_Percent = 0.946;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the basketball team with an overall game winning percentage of 94.6%, showing the team's name and its ACC regular season record.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the school name and location for all universities with a 'Public' affiliation.

sql: SELECT School, Founded FROM university ORDER BY Founded ASC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing universities and their founding years, ordered chronologically from the oldest to the newest.

sql: SELECT Team_Name, All_Home, All_Road FROM basketball_match ORDER BY Team_Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each basketball team's name along with their overall home and road records, ordered alphabetically by team name.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the university school name alongside a team name from each of its associated basketball matches.

sql: SELECT T1.Affiliation, COUNT(T2.Team_ID) FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T1.Affiliation;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each university affiliation along with the total count of basketball matches played by schools with that affiliation.

sql: SELECT Affiliation FROM university GROUP BY Affiliation HAVING COUNT(School_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the affiliation that is associated with more than one university.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Enrollment FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.ACC_Regular_Season = '14–2';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the university that achieved a '14–2' ACC regular season record, along with its student enrollment.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM university WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_ID FROM basketball_match);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and locations of all universities that have participated in a basketball match.

sql: SELECT AVG(Enrollment) FROM university WHERE Enrollment IS NOT NULL;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with a single value representing the average enrollment for all universities where enrollment data is available.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID ORDER BY T2.All_Games_Percent DESC;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each university's name and its overall basketball game win percentage, ordered from the highest percentage to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Enrollment FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Enrollment > (SELECT AVG(T3.Enrollment) FROM university AS T3);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the university that has a basketball team and an enrollment size greater than the average of all universities, showing the school's name and its enrollment number.

sql: SELECT School FROM university AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM basketball_match AS T2 WHERE T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID AND T2.All_Games_Percent > 0.85);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a university whose basketball team has won more than 85% of its games.

sql: SELECT Location FROM university GROUP BY Location HAVING AVG(Enrollment) > 19000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing university locations where the average student enrollment across all universities in that location is greater than 19,000.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Location FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.All_Games_Percent > (SELECT AVG(All_Games_Percent) FROM basketball_match);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and locations of universities whose basketball teams have a win percentage that is above the average win percentage across all games.

sql: SELECT School_ID FROM university INTERSECT SELECT School_ID FROM basketball_match;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the school IDs for universities that have also participated in a basketball match.

sql: SELECT School FROM university WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_ID FROM university EXCEPT SELECT School_ID FROM basketball_match);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the school that has not participated in any basketball matches.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name, T2.All_Games_Percent FROM (SELECT School_ID, School FROM university WHERE Affiliation = 'Public') AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the school name, team name, and all-games win percentage for basketball matches involving public universities.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN All_Games_Percent > 0.9 THEN 'Elite' WHEN All_Games_Percent > 0.8 THEN 'Great' ELSE 'Good' END AS Tier, COUNT(*) FROM basketball_match GROUP BY Tier;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows categorizing basketball matches into performance tiers ('Elite', 'Great', 'Good') based on their game win percentage, along with a count of how many matches fall into each tier.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.Team_Name FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.All_Games_Percent = (SELECT MAX(All_Games_Percent) FROM basketball_match);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the school and team name for the basketball team with the highest all-games win percentage.

sql: SELECT School AS Name, 'University' AS Type FROM university UNION ALL SELECT Team_Name AS Name, 'Team' AS Type FROM basketball_match;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows that combine all university and basketball team names into a single list, with a 'Type' column indicating whether the entry is a 'University' or a 'Team'.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Location FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.Affiliation = 'Public' AND T1.Founded < 1800;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of public universities founded before 1800 that have participated in a basketball match.

sql: SELECT T1.School FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Team_Name <> 'Duke';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of universities that participated in basketball matches, excluding any matches involving the 'Duke' team.

sql: SELECT T1.Location FROM university AS T1 JOIN basketball_match AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.All_Games_Percent > 0.7 GROUP BY T1.Location HAVING COUNT(T1.School_ID) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique locations of universities whose basketball teams have a winning percentage greater than 70%.

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player WHERE yCard = 'no';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and high score for each player who has not received a yellow card.

sql: SELECT cName, enr FROM College WHERE state = 'LA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and enrollment number for the college located in the state of Louisiana ('LA').

sql: SELECT pID, cName FROM Tryout WHERE decision = 'yes';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the player ID and college name for all tryouts that resulted in a successful ('yes') decision.

sql: SELECT pName, yCard, HS FROM Player ORDER BY HS DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each player's name, yellow card status, and high score (HS), ordered from the highest score to the lowest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT pPos FROM Tryout;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique player positions available in the tryouts.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T2.pPos FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'ASU';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of players who tried out for 'ASU' and the position they played during that tryout.

sql: SELECT T1.cName, COUNT(T2.pID) FROM College AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.cName = T2.cName GROUP BY T1.cName;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each college name along with a count of the number of players who participated in tryouts for that college.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, COUNT(T2.cName) FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID GROUP BY T1.pName;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each player by name along with a total count of the tryouts they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T1.HS, T3.cName, T3.enr FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName WHERE T3.enr > 15000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing players who tried out for large colleges (with an enrollment over 15,000), showing the player's name, high school score, the college name, and its enrollment number.

sql: SELECT cName, pPos FROM Tryout WHERE pID IN (SELECT pID FROM Player WHERE pName = 'Andrew');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the college name and position for all tryouts associated with any player named 'Andrew'.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.enr) FROM College AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.cName = T2.cName WHERE T2.pPos = 'goalie';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average enrollment size for colleges where a player tried out for the 'goalie' position.

sql: SELECT T1.cName FROM College AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.cName = T2.cName GROUP BY T1.cName HAVING count(T2.pID) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of colleges that have had at least one player tryout.

sql: SELECT pName, HS FROM Player WHERE HS > (SELECT avg(HS) FROM Player);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and high scores for all players whose high score is greater than the overall average high score.

sql: SELECT cName, enr FROM College WHERE enr > (SELECT avg(enr) FROM College);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and enrollment numbers for all colleges that have an enrollment greater than the overall average.

sql: SELECT pName FROM Player WHERE pID IN (SELECT pID FROM Tryout GROUP BY pID HAVING count(cName) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a player who has tried out for more than one college.

sql: SELECT pName FROM Player WHERE pID NOT IN (SELECT pID FROM Tryout);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the player who has not participated in any tryouts.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'FSU' EXCEPT SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'LSU';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a player who tried out for 'FSU' but did not also try out for 'LSU'.

sql: SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'LSU' INTERSECT SELECT T1.pName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'ASU';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the player who has tried out for both the 'LSU' and 'ASU' college teams.

sql: SELECT T1.cName, T1.state FROM College AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tryout AS T2 WHERE T2.cName = T1.cName AND T2.decision = 'yes');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and states of colleges that have had at least one successful tryout (i.e., a 'yes' decision).

sql: SELECT T3.state, avg(T1.HS) FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID JOIN College AS T3 ON T2.cName = T3.cName GROUP BY T3.state;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each state alongside the average HS score for players who tried out at colleges within that state.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, count(T2.pID), sum(CASE WHEN T2.decision = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID GROUP BY T1.pName;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each player's name along with the total count of their tryouts and the number of those tryouts that were successful.

sql: SELECT state, sum(enr) FROM College GROUP BY state ORDER BY sum(enr) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the state with the highest total college enrollment, listed along with its corresponding summed enrollment total.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T1.HS FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.cName = 'ASU' AND T1.HS >= (SELECT avg(T4.HS) FROM Player AS T4 JOIN Tryout AS T5 ON T4.pID = T5.pID WHERE T5.cName = 'LSU');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and high school scores of players who tried out for 'ASU' and have a score at or above the average score of players who tried out for 'LSU'.

sql: SELECT T1.pName, T2.cName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T1.pID = 10001 UNION ALL SELECT T1.pName, T2.cName FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Tryout AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T1.pID = 20002;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of two specific players (IDs 10001 and 20002) and the corresponding college names for which they completed a tryout.

sql: SELECT T2.pName, T1.cName, T1.pPos, T2.HS FROM Tryout AS T1 JOIN Player AS T2 ON T1.pID = T2.pID WHERE T2.HS >= (SELECT avg(T4.HS) FROM Tryout AS T3 JOIN Player AS T4 ON T3.pID = T4.pID WHERE T3.pPos = T1.pPos);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying players whose high school score is at or above the average for their specific tryout position, showing their name, the college name, their position, and their score.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students located in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM Faculty WHERE Rank = 'Professor';
result_summary: Returns 27 rows listing the first and last names of all faculty members with the rank of 'Professor'.

sql: SELECT activity_name FROM Activity ORDER BY activity_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows listing the names of all available activities, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 20;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of all students who are older than 20.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Room FROM Faculty WHERE Building = 'NEB';
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing the first name, last name, and room number for all faculty members located in the 'NEB' building.

sql: SELECT T2.activity_name, COUNT(T1.stuid) FROM Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Activity AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid GROUP BY T2.activity_name;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing each activity by name along with the total count of students participating in that activity.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T3.activity_name FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Activity AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid;
result_summary: Returns 70 rows, listing each student by their first and last name alongside every specific activity they participate in.

sql: SELECT Sex, COUNT(StuID) FROM Student GROUP BY Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total number of students for each sex.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T3.activity_name FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID JOIN Activity AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid WHERE T1.FacID = 1082;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name of a specific faculty member (ID 1082) and each activity they participate in.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName AS Student_LName, T2.Lname AS Advisor_LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.Advisor = T2.FacID;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing each student's first and last name alongside the last name of their assigned faculty advisor.

sql: SELECT Major, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each student major along with the average age of students within that major.

sql: SELECT T1.activity_name FROM Activity AS T1 WHERE T1.actid IN (SELECT actid FROM Faculty_Participates_in);
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of all activities that have at least one faculty member participating in them.

sql: SELECT T1.activity_name FROM Activity AS T1 JOIN (SELECT actid FROM Participates_in INTERSECT SELECT actid FROM Faculty_Participates_in) AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names of activities in which both students and faculty members participate.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Activity AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid WHERE T3.activity_name = 'Mountain Climbing';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of students who participate in the 'Mountain Climbing' activity.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.Advisor WHERE T2.city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of faculty members who advise any student from the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName HAVING COUNT(T2.actid) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the first and last names of students who participate in more than two activities.

sql: SELECT T2.activity_name FROM Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Activity AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid GROUP BY T1.actid ORDER BY COUNT(T1.stuid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the most popular activity, determined by the activity with the highest count of student participants.

sql: SELECT T1.activity_name FROM Activity AS T1 JOIN (SELECT actid FROM Participates_in EXCEPT SELECT actid FROM Faculty_Participates_in) AS T2 ON T1.actid = T2.actid;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of an activity that has participants, but where none of the participants are faculty members.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Advisor NOT IN (SELECT FacID FROM Faculty WHERE Rank = 'Professor');
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the first and last names of students whose advisor is not a faculty member with the rank of 'Professor'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than the average student age.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Major, MIN(Age) AS min_age FROM Student GROUP BY Major) AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.Age = T2.min_age;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying the youngest student within each major, listing their first name, last name, and major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName, T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.city_code = T2.city_code WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing unique pairs of different students who live in the same city, showing the first and last names for each student in the pair along with their shared city code.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM Faculty AS T1 JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID WHERE T2.actid IN (SELECT T3.actid FROM Participates_in AS T3 JOIN Student AS T4 ON T3.stuid = T4.StuID WHERE T4.Fname = 'Linda' AND T4.LName = 'Smith');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique first and last names of faculty members who participate in any of the same activities as the student 'Linda Smith'.

sql: SELECT T2.Rank FROM Faculty_Participates_in AS T1 JOIN Faculty AS T2 ON T1.FacID = T2.FacID GROUP BY T1.FacID ORDER BY COUNT(T1.actid) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the academic rank of the single faculty member who participates in the highest number of activities.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Participates_in AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.stuid JOIN Faculty_Participates_in AS T3 ON T2.actid = T3.actid WHERE T1.Advisor = T3.FacID;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of students who participate in the same activities as their academic advisor.

sql: SELECT * FROM user_profiles WHERE name = 'Mary';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all profile details for the user whose name is 'Mary'.

sql: SELECT text, createdate FROM tweets WHERE uid = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the content and creation date for all tweets posted by the user with ID 1.

sql: SELECT name, followers FROM user_profiles WHERE followers > 30000000;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and follower counts of highly popular users with more than 30 million followers.

sql: SELECT f2 FROM follows WHERE f1 = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the user IDs of the accounts followed by the user with ID 1.

sql: SELECT uid, text FROM tweets WHERE id = 4;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the author's user ID and the text for the specific tweet with an ID of 4.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.text FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the text of each tweet alongside the name of the user who posted it.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each user's name and the total number of tweets they have posted.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM follows AS T1 JOIN user_profiles AS T2 ON T1.f1 = T2.uid WHERE T1.f2 = 3;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all users who follow the specific user with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM follows AS T1 JOIN user_profiles AS T2 ON T1.f2 = T2.uid WHERE T1.f1 = 7;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name of the user profile that the user with ID 7 is following.

sql: SELECT f1, COUNT(f2) FROM follows GROUP BY f1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each user and the total count of other users they follow.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.email FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN follows AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.f2 GROUP BY T1.uid;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and email for each unique user who is followed by at least one other user.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.followers FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid WHERE T1.partitionid = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT name, followers FROM user_profiles WHERE followers > (SELECT followers FROM user_profiles WHERE name = 'Mary');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names and follower counts for all users who have more followers than the user 'Mary'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of users who have posted more than one tweet.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM user_profiles AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM follows AS T2 JOIN user_profiles AS T3 ON T2.f2 = T3.uid WHERE T2.f1 = T1.uid AND T3.followers > 5000000);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of users who follow at least one account that has more than 5 million followers.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.followers) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM tweets) AS T1 JOIN user_profiles AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average number of followers for all distinct users who have posted a tweet.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM user_profiles AS T1 WHERE T1.followers > 6000000 UNION SELECT T3.name FROM tweets AS T2 JOIN user_profiles AS T3 ON T2.uid = T3.uid WHERE T2.text LIKE '%company%';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of users who either have more than 6 million followers or have posted a tweet containing the word 'company'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM user_profiles AS T1 WHERE T1.followers > 6000000 INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM user_profiles AS T2 JOIN tweets AS T3 ON T2.uid = T3.uid;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of users who have both more than 6 million followers and have posted at least one tweet.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM follows AS T1 JOIN user_profiles AS T2 ON T1.f1 = T2.uid WHERE T1.f2 = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T4.name FROM follows AS T3 JOIN user_profiles AS T4 ON T3.f2 = T4.uid WHERE T3.f1 = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a user who follows user ID 1, but is not followed back by that user.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.followers FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN (SELECT uid FROM tweets GROUP BY uid ORDER BY COUNT(id) DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and follower count for the user who has posted the most tweets.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM user_profiles AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM follows AS T2 WHERE T2.f1 = T1.uid) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM follows AS T3 WHERE T3.f2 = T1.uid);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of users who both follow other users and are also followed by other users.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.id) AS tweet_count, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.f2) AS following_count FROM user_profiles AS T1 LEFT JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid LEFT JOIN follows AS T3 ON T1.uid = T3.f1 WHERE T1.uid IN (1, 3) GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for a specific set of users, providing each user's name along with a count of their total tweets and the number of other users they follow.

sql: SELECT T2.text, T2.createdate FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN tweets AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.uid WHERE T1.followers > (SELECT AVG(followers) FROM user_profiles);
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the text and creation date of tweets from users who have an above-average number of followers.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.followers FROM user_profiles AS T1 JOIN follows AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.f2 WHERE T1.followers > 1000000 GROUP BY T1.uid HAVING COUNT(T2.f1) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and follower count for popular users who have more than 1 million followers and are followed by at least one other person.

sql: SELECT T4.name FROM follows AS T1 JOIN follows AS T2 ON T1.f2 = T2.f1 JOIN user_profiles AS T4 ON T2.f2 = T4.uid WHERE T1.f1 = 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of users who are followed by the people that user ID 1 also follows, representing second-degree connections.

sql: SELECT Tourist_Details FROM Visitors WHERE Tourist_ID = 164;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the tourist details for the specific visitor with an ID of 164.

sql: SELECT Theme_Park_Details FROM Theme_Parks WHERE Theme_Park_Details = 'Disney';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the theme park entry where the details are specifically 'Disney'.

sql: SELECT Name, Other_Details FROM Staff WHERE Name = 'Whitney';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name and other details for the staff member named 'Whitney'.

sql: SELECT Location_Name, Address FROM Locations WHERE Location_ID = 333;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and address for the specific location with an ID of 333.

sql: SELECT hotel_id, price_range FROM Hotels WHERE pets_allowed_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing hotels that allow pets, showing each hotel's ID and its corresponding price range.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location_Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Locations AS T2 ON T1.Location_ID = T2.Location_ID WHERE T1.How_to_Get_There = 'walk';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of tourist attractions accessible by walking, along with their corresponding location name.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Visit_ID) FROM Visits AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each tourist attraction's name along with the total number of visits it has received.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Theme_Parks AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Theme_Park_ID WHERE T2.Theme_Park_Details = 'Sea World';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of staff members who work at the 'Sea World' theme park.

sql: SELECT T1.hotel_id, T2.star_rating_description FROM Hotels AS T1 JOIN Ref_Hotel_Star_Ratings AS T2 ON T1.star_rating_code = T2.star_rating_code WHERE T1.star_rating_code = '5';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the ID for each 5-star hotel along with its corresponding star rating description.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Feature_Details FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID JOIN Features AS T3 ON T2.Feature_ID = T3.Feature_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'flying elephant';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the feature details for the tourist attraction named 'flying elephant'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Staff_ID) AS StaffCount FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING COUNT(T1.Staff_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of tourist attractions with more than one staff member, along with the total count of staff for each location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Ref_Attraction_Types AS T2 ON T1.Attraction_Type_Code = T2.Attraction_Type_Code WHERE T2.Attraction_Type_Description = 'park';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of all tourist attractions that are classified as parks.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 WHERE T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID IN (SELECT T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID FROM Visits AS T2 JOIN Visitors AS T3 ON T2.Tourist_ID = T3.Tourist_ID WHERE T3.Tourist_Details = 'Vincent');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of tourist attractions visited by the tourist named 'Vincent'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID GROUP BY T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID, T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Feature_ID) > (SELECT AVG(FeatureCount) FROM (SELECT COUNT(Feature_ID) AS FeatureCount FROM Tourist_Attraction_Features GROUP BY Tourist_Attraction_ID));
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the names of tourist attractions that have a higher-than-average number of associated features.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Location_Name FROM Photos AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID JOIN Locations AS T3 ON T2.Location_ID = T3.Location_ID WHERE T2.Attraction_Type_Code = '2';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the photo name, the associated tourist attraction name, and its location, filtered for attractions of a specific type (code '2').

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID WHERE T2.Name IS NOT NULL EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM Visits AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of tourist attractions that have staff but have no recorded visits.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Tourist_Attractions AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Tourist_Attraction_ID, COUNT(*) AS StaffCount FROM Staff GROUP BY Tourist_Attraction_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID ORDER BY T2.StaffCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the tourist attraction that employs the most staff members.

sql: SELECT T3.Attraction_Type_Description, COUNT(T1.Visit_ID) FROM Visits AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID JOIN Ref_Attraction_Types AS T3 ON T2.Attraction_Type_Code = T3.Attraction_Type_Code GROUP BY T3.Attraction_Type_Description ORDER BY COUNT(T1.Visit_ID) DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each tourist attraction type and the total number of visits for that type, ordered from most popular to least popular.

sql: SELECT T1.Tourist_Details FROM Visitors AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Visits AS T2 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T3 ON T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T3.Tourist_Attraction_ID WHERE T2.Tourist_ID = T1.Tourist_ID AND T3.Attraction_Type_Code = '2');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the details for a visitor who has visited at least one tourist attraction with an 'Attraction_Type_Code' of '2'.

sql: SELECT T2.Museum_Details, COUNT(T1.Feature_ID) FROM Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T1 JOIN Museums AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Museum_ID GROUP BY T2.Museum_ID, T2.Museum_Details;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details for a museum along with the total count of its associated tourist attraction features.

sql: SELECT T1.hotel_id, T1.star_rating_code, T1.price_range FROM Hotels AS T1 WHERE T1.price_range = (SELECT MAX(T2.price_range) FROM Hotels AS T2 WHERE T2.star_rating_code = T1.star_rating_code);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows identifying the most expensive hotels within each star rating category, listing their ID, star rating, and price range.

sql: SELECT T1.Tourist_Details, T3.Visit_Date FROM Visitors AS T1 JOIN Visits AS T3 ON T1.Tourist_ID = T3.Tourist_ID JOIN Museums AS T2 ON T3.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Museum_ID WHERE T2.Museum_Details = 'MoMA';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the details of a tourist and the date of their visit to the 'MoMA' museum.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Photos AS T1 WHERE T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID IN (SELECT T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID FROM Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 JOIN Features AS T3 ON T2.Feature_ID = T3.Feature_ID WHERE T3.Feature_Details = 'park');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of photos taken at tourist attractions that are classified as a 'park'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T2 ON T1.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'flying elephant' UNION SELECT T3.Tourist_Details FROM Visits AS T4 JOIN Tourist_Attractions AS T5 ON T4.Tourist_Attraction_ID = T5.Tourist_Attraction_ID JOIN Visitors AS T3 ON T4.Tourist_ID = T3.Tourist_ID WHERE T5.Name = 'flying elephant';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the combined names of staff who work at and visitors who have visited the 'flying elephant' tourist attraction.

sql: SELECT T1.Theme_Park_Details, COUNT(T2.Feature_ID) AS NumberOfFeatures FROM Theme_Parks AS T1 JOIN Tourist_Attraction_Features AS T2 ON T1.Theme_Park_ID = T2.Tourist_Attraction_ID GROUP BY T1.Theme_Park_ID, T1.Theme_Park_Details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing the details for each theme park alongside a count of its associated tourist attraction features.

sql: SELECT Name, Party FROM representative WHERE State = 'Indiana';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and party of the representative from the state of Indiana.

sql: SELECT * FROM election WHERE Seats = 10.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all available details for elections that resulted in exactly 10 seats.

sql: SELECT Name, Party, State FROM representative ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each representative's name, party, and state, sorted alphabetically by name.

sql: SELECT Date, Votes FROM election WHERE Votes > 10000;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the date and vote count for elections that received more than 10,000 votes.

sql: SELECT Representative_ID, State, Party FROM representative WHERE Name = 'John Quayle';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the Representative ID, State, and Party for the representative named 'John Quayle'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Date FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each representative along with their corresponding election date.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Election_ID) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each representative's name along with a count of the total number of elections they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, SUM(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each political party along with the total sum of votes they received across all elections.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Seats) AS Total_Seats FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Seats) >= 10.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing representatives who have won a total of 10 or more seats, showing each representative's name and their total seat count.

sql: SELECT T2.Date, T2.Votes, T2.Seats FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing the election date, votes received, and seats won for all representatives belonging to the 'Democratic' party.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, AVG(T2.Vote_Percent) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each political party along with the average vote percentage received by its representatives across elections.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.State FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names and states of representatives who have participated in an election.

sql: SELECT T1.Party FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Place = 1.0 GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing a distinct list of political parties whose representatives have won an election by finishing in first place.

sql: SELECT Party FROM representative GROUP BY Party HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT State) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the political parties that have representatives in more than one state.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Votes = (SELECT MAX(Votes) FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the representative who received the highest number of votes in an election.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Representative_ID, T1.Name HAVING AVG(T2.Place) < (SELECT AVG(Place) FROM election);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives whose average election finishing place is better than the overall average place across all elections.

sql: SELECT Name, State FROM representative WHERE State = 'Indiana' UNION SELECT Name, State FROM representative WHERE Party = 'Republican';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and state of representatives who are either from the state of Indiana or are part of the Republican party.

sql: SELECT Name FROM representative WHERE Representative_ID IN (SELECT Representative_ID FROM election GROUP BY Representative_ID HAVING MIN(Votes) > 9500);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of representatives who have received more than 9500 votes in every one of their recorded elections.

sql: SELECT T1.State FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.State ORDER BY SUM(T2.Votes) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the state that has the highest cumulative vote total across all its representatives.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Election_ID) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT Name FROM representative WHERE Party = 'Republican' AND Representative_ID NOT IN (SELECT Representative_ID FROM election WHERE Place = 1.0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of Republican representatives who have never won an election by finishing in 1st place.

sql: SELECT Name, State FROM representative WHERE Party = 'Republican' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.State FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T2.Place = 2.0;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and state of Republican representatives who have never finished second in an election.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Votes) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING SUM(T2.Votes) > (SELECT AVG(TotalVotes) FROM (SELECT SUM(Votes) AS TotalVotes FROM election GROUP BY Representative_ID));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of representatives who received more votes than the overall average, along with the total sum of their votes.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, T1.Name, T2.Vote_Percent FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE (T1.Party, T2.Vote_Percent) IN (SELECT T3.Party, MAX(T4.Vote_Percent) FROM representative AS T3 JOIN election AS T4 ON T3.Representative_ID = T4.Representative_ID GROUP BY T3.Party);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying the representative from each political party who achieved the highest vote percentage in an election, listing the party, name, and that specific vote percentage.

sql: SELECT T1.Party, AVG(T2.Seats) FROM representative AS T1 JOIN election AS T2 ON T1.Representative_ID = T2.Representative_ID WHERE T1.Party IN (SELECT T3.Party FROM representative AS T3 JOIN election AS T4 ON T3.Representative_ID = T4.Representative_ID GROUP BY T3.Party HAVING AVG(T4.Votes) > 10000) GROUP BY T1.Party;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing political parties whose candidates average over 10,000 votes, along with the average number of seats held by representatives from those parties.

sql: SELECT player_name, height, weight FROM Player WHERE player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye';
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the name, height, and weight for the player named 'Aaron Appindangoye'.

sql: SELECT team_long_name, team_short_name FROM Team WHERE team_short_name = 'GEN';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the long and short names for all teams with the short name 'GEN'.

sql: SELECT name FROM League WHERE country_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the football league associated with country ID 1.

sql: SELECT name FROM Country ORDER BY name DESC;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the names of all countries, ordered in reverse alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT player_api_id) FROM Player_Attributes;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of unique players in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS num_leagues FROM League AS T1 JOIN Country AS T2 ON T1.country_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each country along with a count of the number of soccer leagues associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name, T2.overall_rating, T2.date FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T1.player_api_id = 505942;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows detailing the historical overall ratings for a specific player (ID 505942), showing their name, the rating, and the date each rating was recorded.

sql: SELECT preferred_foot, AVG(overall_rating) AS avg_rating, MAX(potential) AS max_potential FROM Player_Attributes GROUP BY preferred_foot;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the average overall rating and maximum potential for players, broken down by their preferred foot (left, right, or unspecified).

sql: SELECT T1.team_long_name, T2.buildUpPlaySpeed, T2.date FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T1.team_api_id = 9930 AND T2.buildUpPlaySpeedClass = 'Balanced';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the name, build-up play speed, and attribute date for the team 'FC Aarau' specifically for periods when its play style was classified as 'Balanced'.

sql: SELECT defenceAggressionClass, AVG(defenceTeamWidth) FROM Team_Attributes WHERE defenceAggressionClass = 'Press' GROUP BY defenceAggressionClass;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average defensive team width for teams specifically using a 'Press' style of defense.

sql: SELECT player_api_id, COUNT(id) AS num_entries FROM Player_Attributes GROUP BY player_api_id HAVING COUNT(id) > 10;
result_summary: Returns 7439 rows identifying players who have more than 10 historical attribute entries, listing each player's ID alongside the total count of their entries.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name, T2.overall_rating, T2.potential FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T1.player_name = 'Aaron Cresswell';
result_summary: Returns 33 historical attribute records for the player 'Aaron Cresswell', listing their name, overall rating, and potential at different points in time.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T2.date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM Player_Attributes WHERE player_api_id = T1.player_api_id) AND T2.overall_rating < (SELECT AVG(overall_rating) FROM Player_Attributes WHERE player_api_id = T1.player_api_id);
result_summary: Returns 3607 rows listing the names of players whose most recent overall rating is lower than their career average rating.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name, MIN(T2.overall_rating) AS min_rating, MAX(T2.overall_rating) AS max_rating FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id GROUP BY T1.player_name HAVING MAX(T2.overall_rating) - MIN(T2.overall_rating) >= 5;
result_summary: Returns 8200 rows identifying players whose overall rating has fluctuated by at least 5 points, showing each player's name alongside their minimum and maximum recorded ratings.

sql: SELECT T1.team_long_name FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T2.buildUpPlayDribblingClass = 'Little' AND T1.team_api_id IN (SELECT team_api_id FROM Team_Attributes WHERE buildUpPlayDribblingClass = 'Normal');
result_summary: Returns 888 rows listing the long names of teams that have exhibited both 'Little' and 'Normal' styles of build-up play dribbling.

sql: SELECT T1.team_long_name, AVG(T2.defenceAggression) AS avg_aggression FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T1.team_api_id IN (SELECT team_api_id FROM Team_Attributes WHERE defenceDefenderLineClass = 'Cover') GROUP BY T1.team_long_name ORDER BY avg_aggression DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top teams that use a 'Cover' defensive line strategy, showing each team's average defensive aggression, and ordered from most to least aggressive.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T2.preferred_foot = 'right' AND T2.potential > 70 EXCEPT SELECT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T2.overall_rating > 65;
result_summary: Returns 653 rows listing the names of high-potential, right-footed players who are not yet considered top-rated (i.e., their overall rating is 65 or lower).

sql: SELECT T1.player_name, T2.reactions FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T1.height < (SELECT AVG(height) FROM Player) ORDER BY T2.reactions DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the shorter-than-average player with the highest 'reactions' score, showing their name and reactions value.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name, 'left' AS foot FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T2.preferred_foot = 'left' AND T2.shot_power > 75 UNION SELECT T1.player_name, 'right' AS foot FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T2.preferred_foot = 'right' AND T2.shot_power = 55;
result_summary: Returns 1228 rows listing player names and their preferred foot, combining two groups: left-footed players with a shot power greater than 75, and right-footed players with a shot power of exactly 55.

sql: SELECT T1.team_long_name, T2.buildUpPlaySpeed FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T2.buildUpPlaySpeedClass = 'Balanced' AND T2.buildUpPlaySpeed > (SELECT AVG(buildUpPlaySpeed) FROM Team_Attributes WHERE buildUpPlaySpeedClass = 'Balanced');
result_summary: Returns 616 rows identifying teams with a 'Balanced' build-up play style whose speed is above the average for that style, showing each team's full name and their specific play speed value.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE substr(T2.date, 1, 4) = '2016' AND T1.player_api_id IN (SELECT player_api_id FROM Player_Attributes WHERE substr(date, 1, 4) = '2015');
result_summary: Returns 5368 rows listing the unique names of players who were active in both the 2015 and 2016 seasons.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 JOIN (SELECT player_api_id, MAX(date) AS max_date FROM Player_Attributes GROUP BY player_api_id) AS T3 ON T1.player_api_id = T3.player_api_id JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id AND T2.date = T3.max_date WHERE T2.balance < T2.agility AND T2.strength > T2.stamina;
result_summary: Returns 2859 rows, providing the names of players whose most recent attributes show they are more agile than balanced and have greater strength than stamina.

sql: SELECT T1.player_name FROM Player AS T1 WHERE T1.player_api_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT player_api_id FROM Player_Attributes);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.team_long_name FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T2.buildUpPlayDribblingClass = 'Normal' INTERSECT SELECT T1.team_long_name FROM Team AS T1 JOIN Team_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.team_api_id = T2.team_api_id WHERE T2.chanceCreationPassingClass = 'Normal';
result_summary: Returns 246 rows listing the long names of football teams whose build-up play dribbling style and chance creation passing style are both classified as 'Normal'.

sql: SELECT T3.player_name, T3.overall_rating FROM (SELECT T1.player_name, T2.overall_rating, T2.preferred_foot FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id) AS T3 JOIN (SELECT preferred_foot, AVG(overall_rating) AS avg_rating FROM Player_Attributes GROUP BY preferred_foot) AS T4 ON T3.preferred_foot = T4.preferred_foot WHERE T3.overall_rating > T4.avg_rating LIMIT 20;
result_summary: Returns 20 rows listing the name and overall rating for a sample of players whose rating is higher than the average rating for all players with the same preferred foot.

result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the male participant in a wedding that took place at a church organized by 'Wycam Clark'.

sql: SELECT T2.Country, T3.Country FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Female_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Year = 2014;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the respective home countries of the male and female partners for weddings that occurred in the year 2014.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM people AS T1 JOIN wedding AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.Male_ID OR T1.People_ID = T2.Female_ID ORDER BY T1.Age;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and age of people who have participated in a wedding, ordered by age from youngest to oldest.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Year) FROM church AS T1 JOIN wedding AS T2 ON T1.Church_ID = T2.Church_ID WHERE T1.Open_Date > 1830 GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM people);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM church AS T1 JOIN wedding AS T2 ON T1.Church_ID = T2.Church_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Male_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the church that has hosted the most weddings.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T3.Name FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Female_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = T3.Country;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of married couples where both individuals are from the same country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM church AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM wedding AS T2 JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Male_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T2.Church_ID = T1.Church_ID AND T3.Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of churches that have hosted a wedding where the groom is from the United States.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT Male_ID FROM wedding UNION SELECT Female_ID FROM wedding);

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID OR T1.Female_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Age = (SELECT MAX(T3.Age) FROM people AS T3 JOIN wedding AS T4 ON T3.People_ID = T4.Male_ID OR T3.People_ID = T4.Female_ID);

sql: SELECT T2.Name AS Male_Name, T3.Name AS Female_Name FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Female_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T2.Age > T3.Age;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of married couples where the male partner is older than the female partner.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM people WHERE Country = 'Canada' AND People_ID IN (SELECT Male_ID FROM wedding UNION SELECT Female_ID FROM wedding) UNION SELECT Name, Country FROM people WHERE Country = 'Sweden' AND People_ID IN (SELECT Male_ID FROM wedding UNION SELECT Female_ID FROM wedding);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and countries of people from either Canada or Sweden who have participated in a wedding.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'United States' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM wedding AS T4 JOIN people AS T3 ON T4.Male_ID = T3.People_ID JOIN church AS T5 ON T4.Church_ID = T5.Church_ID WHERE T5.Organized_by = 'Ezra Booth';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of American men who have had a wedding, excluding those whose wedding was held at a church organized by 'Ezra Booth'.

sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Age), AVG(T3.Age) FROM wedding AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Male_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Female_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Year > 2014;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the average age of male and female participants for all weddings that occurred after the year 2014.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM church AS T1 JOIN wedding AS T2 ON T1.Church_ID = T2.Church_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Male_ID = T3.People_ID OR T2.Female_ID = T3.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING AVG(T3.Age) > 32;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of each church where the average age of people married there is greater than 32.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Year, T3.Name FROM church AS T1 JOIN wedding AS T2 ON T1.Church_ID = T2.Church_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.Male_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T3.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM people WHERE Is_Male = 'T');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the church name, wedding year, and groom's name for weddings where the groom's age was greater than the average age of all males.

sql: SELECT StuID, Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for all students located in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all female students.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 20;

sql: SELECT Registration_Date FROM Voting_record WHERE StuID = 1001;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the voting registration date for the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student ORDER BY LName ASC;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows with the first and last names of all students, ordered alphabetically by their last name.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major and the total number of students enrolled in that major.

sql: SELECT Sex, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the average age of students, broken down by gender.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Registration_Date = '08/30/2015';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of students who registered to vote on the specific date of August 30, 2015.

sql: SELECT President_Vote, COUNT(*) FROM Voting_record GROUP BY President_Vote;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, tallying the total number of votes received for each distinct presidential vote option.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.President_Vote = 1004;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of students who voted for the presidential candidate with ID 1004.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Registration_Date FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows containing the first name, last name, and registration date for each student who has a corresponding voting record.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Voting_record WHERE Election_Cycle = 'Spring';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of voting records from the 'Spring' election cycle.

sql: SELECT T2.Fname, T2.LName FROM Voting_record AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.StuID = T1.Class_President_Vote;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the student who voted for themself in the class president election.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.President_Vote WHERE T2.President_Vote IN (SELECT President_Vote FROM Voting_record GROUP BY President_Vote HAVING count(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of students who received more than one vote for president, with each row representing a single vote cast for that popular candidate.

sql: SELECT T1.Major FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.Major ORDER BY COUNT(T2.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the student major with the highest number of voters, determined by counting voting records for each major and selecting the top one.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Vice_President_Vote = 1007 AND T2.President_Vote != 1007;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows with the first and last names of students who voted for candidate 1007 for Vice President but a different candidate for President.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.President_Vote = 1004;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age of students who cast their vote for presidential candidate 1004.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Fname, T2.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.Major = T2.Major AND T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 180 rows, with each row containing the first and last names for a unique pair of students who are enrolled in the same major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Class_Senator_Vote = 1028 AND T2.President_Vote != 1028;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first and last names of students who voted for candidate 1028 for Class Senator but voted for a different candidate for President.

sql: SELECT T1.city_code FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.city_code ORDER BY AVG(T1.Age) ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the city code with the lowest average age of students who have a voting record.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN T1.Age < 20 THEN 'Under 20' ELSE '20 and Over' END, COUNT(T2.StuID) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Voting_record AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY CASE WHEN T1.Age < 20 THEN 'Under 20' ELSE '20 and Over' END;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of student voting records, categorized into two age groups: 'Under 20' and '20 and Over'.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Major = (SELECT Major FROM Student WHERE Fname = 'Linda' AND LName = 'Smith') AND StuID != 1001;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the first and last names of all students who share the same major as 'Linda Smith', excluding the student with ID 1001.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT StuID FROM Voting_record WHERE Registration_Date = '08/30/2015' EXCEPT SELECT StuID FROM Voting_record WHERE Registration_Date = '08/30/2013') AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of a student who registered to vote on August 30, 2015, but was not registered on the same date in 2013.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT StuID FROM Voting_record WHERE President_Vote = 1004 INTERSECT SELECT StuID FROM Voting_record WHERE Class_Senator_Vote = 1028) AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of students who voted for both presidential candidate 1004 and class senator candidate 1028.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT President_Vote FROM Voting_record GROUP BY President_Vote ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.President_Vote;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of the student who won the presidential election by receiving the most votes.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM journalist ORDER BY Age DESC;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Event_ID) FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each journalist's name and the total number of news reports they have filed.

sql: SELECT Nationality, AVG(Age) FROM journalist GROUP BY Nationality;
