sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.journalist_ID) FROM event AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each event by name and the total count of news reports associated with that event.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID WHERE T2.Work_Type = 'Screening';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and age for each journalist who has completed 'Screening' type work.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID GROUP BY T1.journalist_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.Event_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a journalist who has written more than one news report.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN journalist AS T3 ON T2.journalist_ID = T3.journalist_ID WHERE T3.Age > 30;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of events that were reported on by journalists older than 30.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Event_Attendance) FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each journalist's name along with the total attendance of all the events they have reported on.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Attendance > (SELECT AVG(Event_Attendance) FROM event);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of journalists who have reported on events with an attendance greater than the average for all events.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Nationality FROM journalist AS T1 WHERE T1.journalist_ID IN (SELECT T2.journalist_ID FROM news_report AS T2 JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'Marathon');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and nationality of journalists who reported on any event held at the 'Marathon' venue.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Attendance > 1000 AND T1.Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM journalist);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of journalists who are older than the average age and have reported on events with an attendance greater than 1000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID = (SELECT Event_ID FROM event ORDER BY Event_Attendance DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of journalists who filed a report on the event with the highest attendance.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM journalist WHERE journalist_ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT journalist_ID FROM news_report);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and age of journalists who have not authored any news reports.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID JOIN journalist AS T3 ON T2.journalist_ID = T3.journalist_ID WHERE T3.Nationality = 'England';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of events that have been covered in news reports by journalists from England.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID GROUP BY T1.journalist_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Work_Type) = 1 AND MIN(T2.Work_Type) = 'Screening';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of journalists whose only work type for news reports has been 'Screening'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM event AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.journalist_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the event that was covered by the most journalists.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Olympia Games Openning';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of each journalist who reported on the 'Olympia Games Openning' event.

sql: SELECT Name FROM journalist WHERE Nationality = 'England' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'Victoria';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of English journalists who have not reported on any event held at the 'Victoria' venue.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Years_working) FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Event_Attendance > 500;
result_summary: Returns a single row calculating the average years of experience for journalists who have reported on large events with an attendance of over 500 people.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Venue IN ('Marathon', 'Victoria') GROUP BY T1.journalist_ID HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Venue) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a journalist who has reported on events at both the 'Marathon' and 'Victoria' venues.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM journalist WHERE Nationality = 'Wales' UNION SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM journalist AS T1 JOIN news_report AS T2 ON T1.journalist_ID = T2.journalist_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.Event_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'Victoria';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of journalists who are either from Wales or have reported on an event held at the 'Victoria' venue.

sql: SELECT * FROM wine WHERE Winery = 'Robert Biale';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing all available details for wines produced by the 'Robert Biale' winery.

sql: SELECT Grape FROM grapes WHERE Color = 'Red';
result_summary: Returns 13 rows listing the names of all grape varieties that are classified as red in color.

sql: SELECT Appelation, County FROM appellations WHERE State = 'California' ORDER BY County;
result_summary: Returns 57 rows listing wine appellations and their corresponding counties within California, ordered alphabetically by county name.

sql: SELECT Name, Score FROM wine WHERE Score = 93;
result_summary: Returns 55 rows listing the name and score for all wines that received a score of exactly 93.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Winery FROM wine;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows, providing a distinct list of all wineries represented in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Winery, COUNT(*) FROM wine GROUP BY Winery;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows listing each winery and the corresponding count of wines it produces.

sql: SELECT Year, AVG(Score) FROM wine GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each year alongside the average score for all wines produced in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery, T2.Color FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T1.Grape = 'Zinfandel';
result_summary: Returns 78 rows listing the name, winery, and grape color for all wines specifically made from Zinfandel grapes.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T2.County = 'Sonoma';
result_summary: Returns 186 rows listing the names and wineries of wines that originate from appellations within Sonoma county.

sql: SELECT Winery FROM wine GROUP BY Winery HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 104 rows listing the names of wineries that have more than one wine listed in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.Grape, AVG(T2.Price) FROM grapes AS T1 JOIN wine AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape GROUP BY T1.Grape;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows, listing each grape type along with the average price for wines made from that grape.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(Cases) FROM wine WHERE Score >= 93 GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the total number of cases for highly-rated wines (score of 93 or higher), grouped by year.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape JOIN appellations AS T3 ON T1.Appelation = T3.Appelation WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' AND T3.isAVA = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 276 rows listing the names of red wines that originate from an official American Viticultural Area (AVA).

sql: SELECT Appelation FROM wine GROUP BY Appelation HAVING AVG(Score) > (SELECT AVG(Score) FROM wine);
result_summary: Returns 22 rows listing the wine appelations whose average score is higher than the overall average score of all wines in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Winery FROM wine WHERE Appelation IN ('Napa Valley', 'St. Helena') GROUP BY Winery HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT Appelation) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the wineries that produce wine in both the 'Napa Valley' and 'St. Helena' appellations.

sql: SELECT T1.Grape, T1.Name, T1.Price FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' AND T1.Price = (SELECT MAX(T3.Price) FROM wine AS T3 WHERE T3.Grape = T1.Grape);
result_summary: Returns 12 rows identifying the single most expensive wine for each type of red grape, showing the grape variety, wine name, and its price.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM wine WHERE Grape = 'Zinfandel' AND Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM wine WHERE State = 'California');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and price of Zinfandel wines that are more expensive than the average price of a wine from California.

sql: SELECT Grape FROM wine WHERE State = 'California' EXCEPT SELECT Grape FROM wine WHERE State != 'California';
result_summary: Returns 17 rows listing the grape varietals that are found exclusively in California and not in any other state.

sql: SELECT Winery, SUM(Cases) AS TotalCases FROM wine WHERE Score > 90 GROUP BY Winery ORDER BY TotalCases DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the top wineries by total cases of high-scoring (score > 90) wine, ordered from the highest number of cases to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.Appelation FROM appellations AS T1 WHERE T1.County = 'Sonoma' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM wine AS T2 WHERE T2.Appelation = T1.Appelation AND T2.Score = 93);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of appellations within Sonoma county that have produced at least one wine with a score of exactly 93.

sql: SELECT T1.Grape, T1.Name, T1.Price, T1.Year FROM wine AS T1 WHERE T1.Price > (SELECT AVG(T2.Price) FROM wine AS T2 WHERE T2.Year = T1.Year);
result_summary: Returns 189 rows listing the grape, name, price, and year for wines that are priced higher than the average for their respective vintage year.

sql: SELECT T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape WHERE T2.Color = 'Red' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Winery FROM wine AS T1 JOIN appellations AS T2 ON T1.Appelation = T2.Appelation WHERE T2.Area = 'North Coast';
result_summary: Returns 146 rows listing the wineries that both produce wine from red grapes and are located in the 'North Coast' area.

sql: SELECT Winery, MAX(Score) - MIN(Score) AS ScoreRange FROM wine GROUP BY Winery HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 104 rows, listing the score range (the difference between the maximum and minimum score) for each winery that has more than one reviewed wine.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Color, T3.isAVA FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape JOIN appellations AS T3 ON T1.Appelation = T3.Appelation WHERE T1.Winery = 'Robert Biale';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, grape color, and AVA status for each wine from the 'Robert Biale' winery.

sql: SELECT T1.Winery, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Color = 'Red' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS RedGrapeCount, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Color <> 'Red' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NonRedGrapeCount FROM wine AS T1 JOIN grapes AS T2 ON T1.Grape = T2.Grape GROUP BY T1.Winery;
result_summary: Returns 281 rows, listing each winery along with a count of its wines made from red grapes and a separate count for wines made from non-red grapes.

sql: SELECT Name, Height_feet, Floors FROM building ORDER BY Height_feet DESC;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each building's name, height in feet, and number of floors, ordered from the tallest building to the shortest.

sql: SELECT Institution, Enrollment FROM Institution WHERE Type = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and enrollment number for each institution classified as 'Public'.

sql: SELECT common_name, protein_name FROM protein WHERE accession_number = 'XP_002942331.1';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the common name of the organism and the name of the protein that corresponds to the specific accession number 'XP_002942331.1'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM Institution;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing all the unique geographical locations of the institutions.

sql: SELECT Name, Floors FROM building WHERE Height_feet > 150;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and number of floors for all buildings that are taller than 150 feet.

sql: SELECT T1.Institution, T2.Name FROM Institution AS T1 JOIN building AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each institution and the name of the building it is associated with.

sql: SELECT Location, COUNT(Institution_id) FROM Institution GROUP BY Location;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each unique location along with the total count of institutions situated in that location.

sql: SELECT Type, AVG(Enrollment) FROM Institution GROUP BY Type;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each type of institution and the average enrollment for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.protein_name, T2.Institution FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id WHERE T2.Type = 'Public';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of proteins and the corresponding names of the public institutions they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T2.Institution, COUNT(T1.protein_name) FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id GROUP BY T2.Institution;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each institution along with the total count of proteins associated with it.

sql: SELECT T1.Institution, T2.Height_feet FROM Institution AS T1 JOIN building AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id WHERE T1.Founded > 1900;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of institutions founded after 1900 and the height in feet of their associated buildings.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Street_address FROM building AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id WHERE T2.Primary_Conference = 'none';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and street address for buildings that house an institution with no primary conference affiliation.

sql: SELECT T1.common_name, T2.Institution, T3.Name FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id JOIN building AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.building_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, matching each protein's common name to its associated institution and the name of that institution's building.

sql: SELECT Location, COUNT(Institution_id) FROM Institution GROUP BY Location HAVING COUNT(Institution_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing each location and the corresponding total count of institutions within that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM building AS T1 WHERE T1.building_id IN (SELECT T2.building_id FROM Institution AS T2 WHERE T2.Type = 'Public');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of buildings that are classified as public institutions.

sql: SELECT protein_name, sequence_length FROM protein WHERE sequence_length > (SELECT AVG(sequence_length) FROM protein);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing proteins with a sequence length greater than the average across all proteins, showing each protein's name and its specific length.

sql: SELECT T1.Institution, T1.Location FROM Institution AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM protein AS T2 WHERE T2.Institution_id = T1.Institution_id);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and location for each institution that has at least one associated protein record.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, AVG(T1.sequence_length) FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id JOIN building AS T3 ON T2.building_id = T3.building_id GROUP BY T3.Name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each building's name alongside the average sequence length of proteins from the institutions housed within that building.

sql: SELECT Institution, Founded FROM Institution WHERE Founded < 1900 UNION SELECT Institution, Founded FROM Institution WHERE Enrollment > 3000;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and founding year for institutions that were either founded before 1900 or have an enrollment greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT T1.Institution, T1.Enrollment FROM (SELECT Institution, Enrollment FROM Institution WHERE Type = 'Private') AS T1 ORDER BY T1.Enrollment DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and enrollment number for the private institution with the largest student body.

sql: SELECT T2.Institution FROM protein AS T1 JOIN Institution AS T2 ON T1.Institution_id = T2.Institution_id GROUP BY T2.Institution HAVING count(T1.protein_name) > 0 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Institution FROM Institution AS T1 JOIN building AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id WHERE T2.Floors > 5;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of institutions that both have at least one associated protein and are located in a building with more than 5 floors.

sql: SELECT Institution FROM Institution WHERE Type = 'Private' INTERSECT SELECT Institution FROM Institution WHERE Team = 'Tigers';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the institution that is both a private school and has a team named 'Tigers'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM building WHERE building_id NOT IN (SELECT building_id FROM Institution WHERE Type = 'Public');
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of buildings that are not classified as 'Public' institutions.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, T1.Institution, T1.Enrollment FROM Institution AS T1 WHERE T1.Enrollment = (SELECT MAX(T2.Enrollment) FROM Institution AS T2 WHERE T2.Type = T1.Type);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, with each row identifying the institution that has the highest enrollment for each distinct institution type, along with its name and enrollment number.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM Institution AS T1 JOIN building AS T2 ON T1.building_id = T2.building_id GROUP BY T2.Name HAVING SUM(T1.Enrollment) > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of buildings where the total enrollment of all institutions located within that building exceeds 1000.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE customer_name = 'Tillman Ernser';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available details for the specific customer named 'Tillman Ernser'.

sql: SELECT product_details FROM Products;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details for every product available in the database.

sql: SELECT address_content, zip_postcode FROM Addresses WHERE city = 'Lucasville';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the street address and zip code for any addresses found within the city of 'Lucasville'.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the order and customer IDs for all orders that have been cancelled.

sql: SELECT customer_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers WHERE payment_method = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who pay with cash and the date each one became a customer.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer by name and the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T3.product_details, T2.order_quantity FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.order_id = 14;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the product details and ordered quantity for each item included in customer order 14.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T3.address_content FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T2.address_type = 'Billing';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's name and their corresponding billing address.

sql: SELECT T2.product_details, SUM(T1.order_quantity) FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_details;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product's details along with the total quantity ordered for that product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(T2.address_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have more than one registered address.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.contact_number FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Contact_Channels AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.channel_code = 'Email';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing customer names and their contact numbers for all records where the communication channel is 'Email'.

sql: SELECT order_status, COUNT(*) FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY order_status;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of customer orders for each unique order status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T3.order_quantity) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_details = 'Dove Chocolate' GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each customer who purchased 'Dove Chocolate' along with the total quantity of that product they ordered across all their transactions.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.order_id, T2.customer_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T2 JOIN Order_Items AS T1 ON T2.order_id = T1.order_id WHERE T1.order_quantity > (SELECT AVG(order_quantity) FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the unique order and customer IDs for any order that contains at least one item with a quantity greater than the overall average item quantity.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customer_Addresses AS T2 JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T3.state_province_county = 'Colorado') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customer_Orders AS T4 WHERE T4.customer_id = T1.customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has both an address in Colorado and has placed at least one order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing customers who have placed more than one order, showing each customer's name and their total order count.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Credit Card' UNION SELECT T2.customer_name FROM Customers AS T2 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.address_type = 'Billing';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing a unique list of customer names who either pay by credit card or have a registered billing address.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'USA' INTERSECT SELECT T4.customer_name FROM Customers AS T4 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T5 ON T4.customer_id = T5.customer_id WHERE T5.order_status = 'Cancelled';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of customers who are located in the USA and have also had at least one order with a 'Cancelled' status.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id EXCEPT SELECT T3.customer_name FROM Customers AS T3 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T4 ON T3.customer_id = T4.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T5 ON T4.order_id = T5.order_id JOIN Products AS T6 ON T5.product_id = T6.product_id WHERE T6.product_details = 'Latte';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of customers who have placed an order but have never ordered a 'Latte'.

sql: SELECT T3.customer_name, T1.total_quantity FROM (SELECT order_id, SUM(order_quantity) AS total_quantity FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id) AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T1.total_quantity > 5;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of customers who placed large orders (containing more than 5 items) and the total item quantity for each of those orders.

sql: SELECT T3.state_province_county, COUNT(T5.product_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T4 ON T1.customer_id = T4.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T5 ON T4.order_id = T5.order_id GROUP BY T3.state_province_county;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each state, province, or county alongside the total number of product items ordered by customers from that location.

sql: SELECT product_details FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items GROUP BY product_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the details for products that have been included in more than one order.

sql: SELECT T3.city, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.customer_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T1.payment_method = 'Cash' GROUP BY T3.city;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each city alongside the total count of unique customers in that city who use 'Cash' as their payment method.

sql: SELECT T3.customer_name, T4.customer_name, T1.address_id FROM Customer_Addresses AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id AND T1.customer_id < T2.customer_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T1.customer_id = T3.customer_id JOIN Customers AS T4 ON T2.customer_id = T4.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows identifying pairs of different customers who share an address, listing the names of both customers and their shared address ID.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.order_status) = 1 AND MIN(T2.order_status) = 'Delivered';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the customer who only has orders with a 'Delivered' status.

sql: SELECT Player_ID, Player, Age, Position FROM player WHERE Team = 'Tampa Bay Devil Rays';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the Player ID, name, age, and position for the player on the 'Tampa Bay Devil Rays' team.

sql: SELECT School, Location, Enrollment FROM school WHERE Founded = 1890.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name, location, and enrollment for the school that was founded in the year 1890.

sql: SELECT Player, Team, Position FROM player WHERE Age = 15 ORDER BY Player;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the name, team, and position of all 15-year-old players, sorted alphabetically by player name.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Position FROM player;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing all the unique player positions available in the dataset.

sql: SELECT School, Location FROM school WHERE Denomination = 'Catholic';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of schools with a 'Catholic' denomination.

sql: SELECT T2.School, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T2.School;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each school name along with the total number of players from that school.

sql: SELECT Denomination, AVG(Enrollment) FROM school GROUP BY Denomination;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each school denomination alongside the average student enrollment for schools within that denomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Age, T2.Nickname FROM player AS T1 JOIN school_details AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 23 rows listing each player's name and age, along with the nickname of the school they attend.

sql: SELECT T2.School FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T2.School HAVING COUNT(T1.Player_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the schools that have more than one registered player.

sql: SELECT Position, MAX(Age) FROM player GROUP BY Position;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each unique player position along with the maximum age of any player in that position.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.School_Year, T2.Class_A, T2.Class_AA FROM school AS T1 JOIN school_performance AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_Id;
result_summary: Returns 22 rows, listing each school's name along with its historical performance classifications ('Class A' and 'Class AA') for each school year on record.

sql: SELECT Day_or_Boarding, SUM(Enrollment) FROM school GROUP BY Day_or_Boarding;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the total student enrollment for each school type (Day or Day & Boarding).

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Age, T2.School FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Enrollment > (SELECT AVG(Enrollment) FROM school);
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name, age, and school for players who attend schools with an above-average student enrollment.

sql: SELECT School_ID, School FROM school WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_ID FROM player) INTERSECT SELECT School_ID, School FROM school WHERE School_ID IN (SELECT School_Id FROM school_performance);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the ID and name of schools that have both registered players and recorded school performances.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T1.Age FROM player AS T1 JOIN school_details AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Division = 'Division 1' AND T1.Age > 14;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the names and ages of players who are older than 14 and attend a 'Division 1' school.

sql: SELECT T1.School, T1.Location FROM school AS T1 JOIN school_details AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Division = 'Division 1' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM player AS T3 WHERE T3.School_ID = T1.School_ID AND T3.Position = 'Third baseman');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location of 'Division 1' schools that do not have a player in the 'Third baseman' position.

sql: SELECT T1.Denomination FROM school AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Denomination ORDER BY SUM(T1.Enrollment) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the school denomination with the highest total student enrollment across all its schools.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.School, T3.Nickname, T3.Class FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID JOIN school_details AS T3 ON T1.School_ID = T3.School_ID WHERE T2.Founded < 1900;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing players who attended schools founded before the year 1900, showing their name, school, school nickname, and class.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.Player AS Player_2, T3.School FROM player AS T1 JOIN player AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID AND T1.Player_ID < T2.Player_ID JOIN school AS T3 ON T1.School_ID = T3.School_ID;
result_summary: Returns 67 rows listing all unique pairs of players who attended the same school, showing both player names and the name of their shared school.

sql: SELECT T2.School, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) AS PlayerCount FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T2.School HAVING COUNT(T1.Player_ID) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM player WHERE School_ID = 2);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the school name and its player count for schools that have more players than the school with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Player FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Denomination = 'Anglican' UNION SELECT Player FROM player WHERE Position = 'Shortstop';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of players who either attended an 'Anglican' school or play the 'Shortstop' position.

sql: SELECT T2.School, COUNT(T1.Player_ID) AS NumberOfPlayers FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T2.School_ID ORDER BY NumberOfPlayers DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the school with the most players, showing the school's name and the total count of its players.

sql: SELECT T1.Player, T2.School FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T1.School_ID IN (SELECT School_Id FROM school_performance WHERE School_Year = '1987-88');
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing players and their schools, for all schools that had a performance record in the 1987-88 school year.

sql: SELECT T1.School FROM school AS T1 JOIN school_details AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID WHERE T2.Class = 'Flight A' AND T1.School_ID IN (SELECT School_Id FROM school_performance WHERE Class_AA = 'Blanco');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the school that has a 'Flight A' class and also has a 'Blanco' performance in Class_AA.

sql: SELECT T2.Denomination, AVG(T1.Age) FROM player AS T1 JOIN school AS T2 ON T1.School_ID = T2.School_ID GROUP BY T2.Denomination;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each school denomination along with the average age of players attending schools of that denomination.

sql: SELECT T1.School_ID, T1.School FROM school AS T1 WHERE T1.Denomination = 'Anglican' EXCEPT SELECT T1.School_ID, T1.School FROM school AS T1 WHERE T1.Denomination = 'Catholic';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the School ID and name for all schools that have an 'Anglican' denomination but not a 'Catholic' one.

sql: SELECT Hardware_Model_name, Company_name FROM phone WHERE Company_name = 'LG Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the hardware model name for a phone manufactured by LG Electronics.

sql: SELECT Model_name, RAM_MiB, ROM_MiB FROM chip_model WHERE Launch_year = 2002.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the model name, RAM, and ROM for all chip models launched in the year 2002.

sql: SELECT * FROM screen_mode WHERE Type = 'Graphics';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all screen modes classified as 'Graphics' type, showing all of their technical specifications like pixel resolution, hardware colours, and memory usage.

sql: SELECT Model_name, Launch_year FROM chip_model ORDER BY Launch_year DESC;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each chip model name and its corresponding launch year, ordered from most recent to oldest.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Company_name FROM phone;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the unique names of all companies that manufacture phones.

sql: SELECT T1.Hardware_Model_name, T2.Launch_year, T2.RAM_MiB FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name WHERE T1.Company_name = 'Nokia Corporation';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing Nokia phone models along with the launch year and RAM (in MiB) of their internal chipsets.

sql: SELECT T1.Hardware_Model_name, T2.Pixels, T2.Hardware_colours FROM phone AS T1 JOIN screen_mode AS T2 ON T1.screen_mode = T2.Graphics_mode WHERE T1.Accreditation_type = 'Full';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the model name, screen pixel resolution, and number of supported hardware colours for all phones that have received 'Full' accreditation.

sql: SELECT Company_name, COUNT(*) FROM phone GROUP BY Company_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each company name alongside the total count of phone models they have in the database.

sql: SELECT T2.Model_name, COUNT(T1.Hardware_Model_name) FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name GROUP BY T2.Model_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each chip model and the number of phone models that are equipped with it.

sql: SELECT Launch_year, AVG(RAM_MiB), AVG(ROM_MiB) FROM chip_model GROUP BY Launch_year;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each launch year alongside the average RAM and ROM (in MiB) for all chip models released in that year.

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T1.Hardware_Model_name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name WHERE T2.WiFi = 'No';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the company name and hardware model of phones whose internal chip model does not support WiFi.

sql: SELECT T1.Hardware_Model_name, T2.Char_cells FROM phone AS T1 JOIN screen_mode AS T2 ON T1.screen_mode = T2.Graphics_mode ORDER BY T1.Company_name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each phone's hardware model name and its corresponding screen's character cell dimensions, ordered alphabetically by company name.

sql: SELECT T1.Hardware_Model_name, T2.RAM_MiB, T3.Pixels FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name JOIN screen_mode AS T3 ON T1.screen_mode = T3.Graphics_mode WHERE T1.Company_name = 'Sony Mobile Communications';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the hardware model name, RAM size in MiB, and screen resolution in pixels for all phones manufactured by 'Sony Mobile Communications'.

sql: SELECT Model_name, RAM_MiB FROM chip_model WHERE RAM_MiB > (SELECT AVG(RAM_MiB) FROM chip_model);
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the model names and RAM for all chip models that have an above-average amount of RAM.

sql: SELECT T1.Model_name, T1.Launch_year FROM chip_model AS T1 WHERE T1.Model_name IN (SELECT T2.chip_model FROM phone AS T2 WHERE T2.Accreditation_type = 'Full');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the model name and launch year for chip models that are used in phones with 'Full' accreditation.

sql: SELECT Company_name, COUNT(*) FROM phone GROUP BY Company_name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each company name alongside the total count of their associated phone models.

sql: SELECT T2.Model_name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name GROUP BY T2.Model_name HAVING COUNT(T1.Hardware_Model_name) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a chip model that is used in more than one phone hardware model.

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name, T1.Hardware_Model_name FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name WHERE T2.RAM_MiB = (SELECT MAX(T4.RAM_MiB) FROM phone AS T3 JOIN chip_model AS T4 ON T3.chip_model = T4.Model_name);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the company and hardware model of phones that are equipped with the highest amount of RAM available.

sql: SELECT T1.Company_name FROM phone AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM chip_model AS T2 WHERE T2.Model_name = T1.chip_model AND T2.Launch_year = 2002.0);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of phone companies that manufactured phones using a chip model launched in 2002.

sql: SELECT Company_name FROM phone WHERE chip_model = 'X5' UNION SELECT Company_name FROM phone WHERE Accreditation_level = 'joyn Hot Fixes';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of companies whose phones either use the 'X5' chip model or have a 'joyn Hot Fixes' accreditation level.

sql: SELECT Company_name FROM phone WHERE chip_model = 'X5' INTERSECT SELECT Company_name FROM phone WHERE Accreditation_type = 'Full';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of phone companies that both use the 'X5' chip model and have 'Full' accreditation.

sql: SELECT chip_model FROM phone WHERE Company_name = 'Nokia Corporation' EXCEPT SELECT chip_model FROM phone WHERE Company_name = 'LG Electronics';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the chip model used in phones by 'Nokia Corporation' but not by 'LG Electronics'.

sql: SELECT T3.Type, AVG(T2.RAM_MiB) FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name JOIN screen_mode AS T3 ON T1.screen_mode = T3.Graphics_mode GROUP BY T3.Type;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each screen mode type and the average RAM (in MiB) for phone chips associated with that type.

sql: SELECT Model_name, Launch_year, RAM_MiB, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Launch_year ORDER BY RAM_MiB DESC) AS ram_rank FROM chip_model;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing each chip model's name, launch year, and RAM, while also assigning a rank to each model based on its RAM size (highest to lowest) within each launch year.

sql: SELECT T.Company, T.avg_ram FROM (SELECT T1.Company_name AS Company, AVG(T2.RAM_MiB) AS avg_ram FROM phone AS T1 JOIN chip_model AS T2 ON T1.chip_model = T2.Model_name GROUP BY T1.Company_name) AS T WHERE T.avg_ram > 30;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing phone companies whose phones, on average, use chips with more than 30 MiB of RAM, along with the calculated average RAM for each company.

sql: SELECT name, born_state, age FROM head ORDER BY age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name, birth state, and age of each head, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT * FROM department WHERE Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the complete details for the department with a ranking of 1.

sql: SELECT name FROM head WHERE born_state = 'Alabama';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of heads of state who were born in Alabama.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Creation FROM department;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the unique creation years for all departments.

sql: SELECT Name, Budget_in_Billions FROM department WHERE Creation = '1789';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and budget (in billions) for all departments founded in the year 1789.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM management AS T1 JOIN head AS T2 ON T1.head_ID = T2.head_ID WHERE T1.temporary_acting = 'Yes';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of department heads who are designated as serving in a temporary acting capacity.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID WHERE T2.head_ID = 2;

sql: SELECT born_state, COUNT(*) FROM head GROUP BY born_state;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each state of birth along with the total count of heads born in that state.

sql: SELECT born_state, AVG(age) FROM head GROUP BY born_state;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each birth state along with the average age of the heads from that state.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.name FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_ID = T3.head_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name of each department and the name of its corresponding head.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.department_ID) FROM management AS T1 JOIN head AS T2 ON T1.head_ID = T2.head_ID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each head's name along with the total number of departments they manage.

sql: SELECT SUM(Num_Employees) FROM department WHERE Ranking > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total number of employees from all departments with a ranking greater than 2.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM head WHERE head_ID NOT IN (SELECT head_ID FROM management);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and age of heads who are not part of the management team.

sql: SELECT Name FROM department WHERE Department_ID IN (SELECT department_ID FROM management GROUP BY department_ID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any department that has more than one manager.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM head WHERE age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM head);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of the oldest head(s) in the dataset.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_ID = T3.head_ID WHERE T3.age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM head);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique departments that are managed by a head whose age is greater than the average age of all heads.

sql: SELECT born_state FROM head GROUP BY born_state HAVING AVG(age) > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM head);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the states of birth where the average age of heads from that state is greater than the overall average age of all heads.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_ID = T3.head_ID WHERE T1.Budget_in_Billions = (SELECT MAX(Budget_in_Billions) FROM department);

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM management AS T1 JOIN head AS T2 ON T1.head_ID = T2.head_ID WHERE T1.temporary_acting = 'Yes' UNION SELECT name FROM head WHERE born_state = 'California';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of heads who are either serving in a temporary acting capacity or were born in California.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM management AS T1 JOIN head AS T2 ON T1.head_ID = T2.head_ID EXCEPT SELECT name FROM head WHERE born_state = 'Alabama';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of heads of management, excluding any who were born in Alabama.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Num_Employees FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_ID = T3.head_ID WHERE T3.age = (SELECT MIN(age) FROM head);
