sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.head_ID) FROM department AS T1 LEFT JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID WHERE T1.Budget_in_Billions > 10 GROUP BY T1.Department_ID;
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing each department with a budget over 10 billion and the corresponding number of assigned heads.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Budget_in_Billions) FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.Department_ID = T2.department_ID JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_ID = T3.head_ID WHERE T3.born_state = 'Alabama';
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average budget, in billions, for departments where the department head was born in Alabama.

sql: SELECT Wrestler_ID, Name, Reign FROM wrestler WHERE Location = 'Tijuana , Mexico';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the ID, name, and reign for all wrestlers located in Tijuana, Mexico.

sql: SELECT Name, Days_held FROM wrestler WHERE Days_held > 1000;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of wrestlers who have held a title for over 1000 days, along with the exact number of days for their reign.

sql: SELECT * FROM Elimination ORDER BY Time ASC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing all wrestler eliminations in chronological order from earliest to latest by time.

sql: SELECT Wrestler_ID, Eliminated_By, Elimination_Move FROM Elimination WHERE Team = 'Team Batista';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows detailing the eliminations for members of 'Team Batista', listing each wrestler's ID, the opponent who eliminated them, and the specific move used.

sql: SELECT Name, Event FROM wrestler WHERE Event = 'Live event';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the names of all wrestlers who participated in a 'Live event'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Team, T2.Elimination_Move FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each wrestler by name, their team affiliation, and the specific move that led to their elimination.

sql: SELECT Team, COUNT(*) AS Number_of_Eliminations FROM Elimination GROUP BY Team;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total number of eliminations for each team.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Eliminated_By = 'Orton';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the wrestler who was eliminated by 'Orton'.

sql: SELECT Elimination_Move, COUNT(*) AS Move_Count FROM Elimination GROUP BY Elimination_Move ORDER BY Move_Count DESC;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each unique elimination move and its total count, ordered from the most frequent to the least frequent move.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Eliminated_By FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Tijuana , Mexico';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of wrestlers from 'Tijuana , Mexico' and the opponent who eliminated them.

sql: SELECT Event, AVG(Days_held) FROM wrestler GROUP BY Event;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each wrestling event along with the average number of days it was held.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Time FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Eliminated_By = 'Punk';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the wrestler eliminated by 'Punk' and the time of that elimination.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Eliminated_By FROM wrestler AS T1 LEFT JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each wrestler's name and the name of the person who eliminated them; this includes all wrestlers, even those who were not eliminated.

sql: SELECT Name, Days_held FROM wrestler WHERE Days_held > (SELECT AVG(Days_held) FROM wrestler);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name and title-holding duration for wrestlers who have held a title longer than the overall average.

sql: SELECT Team, COUNT(*) AS Elimination_Count FROM Elimination GROUP BY Team HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each team with more than one elimination, along with the total count of their eliminations.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Team = 'Team Batista' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Elimination_Move = 'Paydirt';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of wrestlers from 'Team Batista', excluding any who were eliminated by the 'Paydirt' move.

sql: SELECT Name FROM wrestler WHERE Wrestler_ID NOT IN (SELECT Wrestler_ID FROM Elimination);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of wrestlers who have not been eliminated from the competition.

sql: SELECT Name, Days_held, Event FROM wrestler WHERE Days_held = (SELECT MAX(Days_held) FROM wrestler);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the wrestler with the longest title reign, showing their name, the number of days they held the title, and the associated event.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Team = 'Team Orton' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Team = 'Team Batista';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all wrestlers who were members of either 'Team Orton' or 'Team Batista'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Team FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Elimination AS T3 WHERE T3.Team = T2.Team AND T3.Eliminated_By = 'Orton');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and team of every wrestler who is on a team where at least one teammate was eliminated by 'Orton'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Time = (SELECT MIN(Time) FROM Elimination);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the wrestler who had the fastest elimination time.

sql: SELECT Reign_Category, count(*) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN Days_held > 365 THEN 'Long Reign' WHEN Days_held > 100 THEN 'Medium Reign' ELSE 'Short Reign' END AS Reign_Category FROM wrestler) AS T1 GROUP BY Reign_Category;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows categorizing wrestler reigns into 'Long', 'Medium', and 'Short' based on days held, and provides the total count of wrestlers within each category.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Team = 'Team Batista' INTERSECT SELECT Name FROM wrestler WHERE Location = 'Tijuana , Mexico';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the wrestler who is both a member of 'Team Batista' and from 'Tijuana, Mexico'.

sql: SELECT T2.Elimination_Move, COUNT(*) FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T1.Days_held > 100 GROUP BY T2.Elimination_Move;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each elimination move and a count of how many times it was used by wrestlers who have held a title for over 100 days.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Team FROM wrestler AS T1 JOIN Elimination AS T2 ON T1.Wrestler_ID = T2.Wrestler_ID WHERE T2.Team = (SELECT Team FROM Elimination WHERE Wrestler_ID = 1);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names and team for all wrestlers who are on the same team as the wrestler with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT series_name, Language FROM TV_Channel WHERE Country = 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the series name and language for TV channels available in Italy.

sql: SELECT Title, Directed_by FROM Cartoon;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the title of each cartoon and the name of its director.

sql: SELECT Episode, Rating FROM TV_series WHERE Rating > 5;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the name and rating of TV series episodes that have a rating greater than 5.

sql: SELECT series_name, Package_Option FROM TV_Channel WHERE Pay_per_view_PPV = 'no';
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing TV series that are not pay-per-view, along with the subscription package option they belong to.

sql: SELECT Title, Production_code FROM Cartoon WHERE Production_code > 101.0;
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the title and production code for all cartoons with a production code greater than 101.

sql: SELECT T1.Episode, T2.series_name FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each TV episode alongside the name of the TV channel on which it is broadcast.

sql: SELECT T2.series_name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM Cartoon AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id GROUP BY T2.series_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each TV channel series name along with the total count of cartoons associated with it.

sql: SELECT T2.series_name, AVG(T1.Rating) FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id GROUP BY T2.series_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, listing each TV series name along with the calculated average rating for all shows associated with that series.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T1.Original_air_date FROM Cartoon AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id WHERE T2.Country = 'Italy';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the title and original air date for all cartoons that were aired on TV channels in Italy.

sql: SELECT T2.series_name, SUM(T1.Viewers_m) FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id GROUP BY T2.series_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows, calculating the total viewership (in millions) for each TV series name by summing the viewers from all associated shows.

sql: SELECT T1.Episode FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id WHERE T2.Content = 'music';
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the episode names of TV series that are broadcast on channels with 'music' content.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Directed_by FROM Cartoon AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id WHERE T2.Package_Option = 'Sky Famiglia';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique directors of cartoons that air on TV channels available in the 'Sky Famiglia' package.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel FROM TV_series INTERSECT SELECT Channel FROM Cartoon) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of TV channels that broadcast both regular TV series and cartoons.

sql: SELECT T2.series_name FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id GROUP BY T2.id ORDER BY AVG(T1.Rating) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the TV channel that has the highest average rating across all of its TV series.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM Cartoon AS T1 WHERE T1.Channel NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Channel FROM TV_series);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the titles of cartoons that air on channels which do not broadcast any TV series.

sql: SELECT Directed_by, COUNT(*) FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Directed_by ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the director who has directed the most cartoons, along with the total count of cartoons they directed.

sql: SELECT Episode, Rating FROM TV_series WHERE Rating > (SELECT AVG(Rating) FROM TV_series);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the episode name and rating for all TV series episodes with a rating higher than the overall average.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T1.Language FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel FROM TV_series EXCEPT SELECT Channel FROM Cartoon) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the series name and language for TV series that are broadcast on channels which do not air cartoons.

sql: SELECT T1.series_name, T2.series_count, T3.cartoon_count FROM TV_Channel AS T1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS series_count FROM TV_series GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel LEFT JOIN (SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS cartoon_count FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Channel) AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.Channel;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each TV channel's name, along with a total count of its associated TV series and cartoons.

sql: SELECT T1.Episode, T1.Viewers_m FROM TV_series AS T1 WHERE T1.Viewers_m > (SELECT AVG(T2.Viewers_m) FROM TV_series AS T2 WHERE T2.Channel = T1.Channel) AND T1.Channel IN (SELECT id FROM TV_Channel WHERE Country = 'Italy');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing TV series episodes from Italian channels that have viewership numbers higher than the average for their respective channel, showing the episode name and its viewership in millions.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Directed_by FROM Cartoon AS T1 WHERE T1.Channel IN (SELECT T2.Channel FROM TV_series AS T2 WHERE T2.Rating > 5);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique directors of cartoons that are broadcast on channels featuring TV series with a rating greater than 5.

sql: SELECT T1.Episode FROM TV_series AS T1 JOIN TV_Channel AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.id WHERE T2.Pixel_aspect_ratio_PAR = '4:3' UNION ALL SELECT T3.Title FROM Cartoon AS T3 JOIN TV_Channel AS T4 ON T3.Channel = T4.id WHERE T4.Pixel_aspect_ratio_PAR = '4:3';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the titles of all TV series episodes and cartoon titles that are broadcast on channels with a '4:3' pixel aspect ratio.

sql: SELECT T3.series_name FROM (SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS num_series FROM TV_series GROUP BY Channel) AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Channel, COUNT(*) AS num_cartoons FROM Cartoon GROUP BY Channel) AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.Channel JOIN TV_Channel AS T3 ON T1.Channel = T3.id WHERE T1.num_series >= T2.num_cartoons;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of TV channels that broadcast at least as many TV series as they do cartoons.

sql: SELECT T1.Written_by, AVG(T2.Rating) FROM Cartoon AS T1 JOIN TV_series AS T2 ON T1.Channel = T2.Channel GROUP BY T1.Written_by;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows, listing each cartoon writer and the average rating of TV series that air on the same channel as that writer's cartoons.

sql: SELECT T1.Episode, T1.Viewers_m FROM TV_series AS T1 WHERE T1.Channel = (SELECT Channel FROM Cartoon WHERE Directed_by = 'Ben Jones') ORDER BY T1.Viewers_m DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the most popular episode by viewership from a TV series that aired on the same channel as a cartoon directed by 'Ben Jones'.

sql: SELECT customer_id, email_address, town_city FROM Customers WHERE state = 'Hawaii';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer ID, email address, and town/city for customers located in the state of Hawaii.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_category_code FROM Products WHERE product_price = 35;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name and category code for any product priced at exactly 35.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, email_address FROM Staff ORDER BY last_name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first name, last name, and email address for all staff members, ordered alphabetically by last name.

sql: SELECT complaint_id, product_id, customer_id, date_complaint_raised FROM Complaints WHERE complaint_status_code = 'New';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows detailing all complaints with a 'New' status, providing the complaint, product, and customer IDs, along with the date each complaint was raised.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_category_code = 'Book';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price for all products categorized as books.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address, COUNT(T2.complaint_id) FROM Customers AS T1 LEFT JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each customer's ID and email address, along with the total number of complaints filed by that customer.

sql: SELECT product_category_code, AVG(product_price) FROM Products GROUP BY product_category_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product category along with the average product price for that category.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.complaint_id) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each staff member's first and last name along with the total count of complaints they have closed.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address, T1.town_city FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.complaint_status_code = 'New';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the distinct customers who have filed a complaint that is currently in 'New' status, providing each customer's ID, email address, and city.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T3.town_city, T3.state FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the product name that received a complaint, along with the town/city and state of the customer who made the complaint.

sql: SELECT T2.product_category_code, COUNT(T1.complaint_id) FROM Complaints AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_category_code;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total number of complaints for each product category.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Complaints AS T2 WHERE T2.complaint_outcome_code = 'OK');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and prices for products that have had a complaint resolved with an 'OK' outcome.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_price > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique first and last name of a staff member who handled a complaint related to a product priced higher than the average.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.product_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying customers who have complained about more than one distinct product, listing their ID, email address, and the total count of unique products involved in their complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.product_category_code, COUNT(T2.complaint_id) AS complaint_count FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_category_code ORDER BY complaint_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single product category with the highest number of complaints, along with the total complaint count for that category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T2.staff_id = T3.staff_id WHERE T1.state = 'Hawaii' AND T3.first_name = 'Lucie';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the customer ID and email address for any customer from Hawaii whose complaint was handled by the staff member named Lucie.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id, T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.customer_type_code = 'Good Credit Rating' EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_id, T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Failure';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the ID and name of products complained about by customers with a 'Good Credit Rating', excluding any products that have ever had a 'Product Failure' complaint.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.product_price) AS total_value_of_complaints FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_status_code = 'New' GROUP BY T1.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each staff member assigned to 'New' complaints and the total monetary value of the products associated with those complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.email_address, T4.first_name, T4.last_name, T3.date_complaint_raised FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(date_complaint_raised) AS max_date FROM Complaints GROUP BY customer_id) AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Complaints AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id AND T2.max_date = T3.date_complaint_raised JOIN Staff AS T4 ON T3.staff_id = T4.staff_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying each customer's most recent complaint, listing the customer's email, the date the complaint was raised, and the name of the staff member who handled it.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.product_category_code) FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.product_category_code) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each staff member's ID and name who has handled complaints for two or more distinct product categories, along with the count of those categories.

sql: SELECT T1.product_id, T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 LEFT JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_id IS NULL;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.email_address FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_category_code IN ('Book', 'Hardware') GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.product_category_code) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the customer ID and email address for any customer who has filed complaints about products from both the 'Book' and 'Hardware' categories.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.complaint_id) AS closed_complaints FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.complaint_status_code = 'Closed' GROUP BY T1.staff_id ORDER BY closed_complaints DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single staff member with the most closed complaints, showing their first name, last name, and their total count of closed complaints.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.complaint_type_code = 'Product Unusable' ORDER BY T1.product_price DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the most expensive products that have been reported as 'Product Unusable', ordered from highest to lowest price.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Customers AS T3 ON T2.customer_id = T3.customer_id WHERE T3.state = 'Tennessee' INTERSECT SELECT T1.staff_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Complaints AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_category_code = 'Food';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the ID and full name of staff members who have handled complaints from customers in Tennessee and also complaints about products in the 'Food' category.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Salary FROM employee WHERE Salary = 30000;
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first name, last name, and salary for any employee earning exactly 30000.

sql: SELECT Pname, Plocation FROM project WHERE Plocation = 'Houston';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location for all projects located in Houston.

sql: SELECT Dname, Dnumber FROM department ORDER BY Dname ASC;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and number for each department, sorted alphabetically by department name.

sql: SELECT Dependent_name, Relationship FROM dependent WHERE Essn = 333445555;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names and relationships of all dependents for the employee with an ESSN of '333445555'.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Sex FROM employee;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first name, last name, and sex for every employee in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname, T2.Dname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first name, last name, and department name for each employee.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname, SUM(T2.Hours) FROM project AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Pnumber = T2.Pno GROUP BY T1.Pname;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each project's name along with the total number of hours worked on that project.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the first and last names of all employees who work in the 'Research' department.

sql: SELECT T2.Dname, COUNT(T1.Ssn) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each department name along with the total number of employees in that department.

sql: SELECT T2.Fname, T2.Lname, T1.Dname FROM department AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.Mgr_ssn = T2.Ssn;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last name of each department's manager, along with the name of the department they manage.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN dependent AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn WHERE T2.Relationship = 'Spouse';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of employees who have a spouse listed as a dependent.

sql: SELECT T2.Dlocation FROM department AS T1 JOIN dept_locations AS T2 ON T1.Dnumber = T2.Dnumber WHERE T1.Dname = 'Administration';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the location of the 'Administration' department.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN project AS T3 ON T2.Pno = T3.Pnumber WHERE T3.Pname = 'ProductX';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of all employees assigned to the 'ProductX' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.Super_ssn = T2.Ssn WHERE T2.Fname = 'Franklin' AND T2.Lname = 'Wong';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of employees who report directly to the manager 'Franklin Wong'.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Ssn IN (SELECT Essn FROM works_on WHERE Pno IN (SELECT Pnumber FROM project WHERE Plocation != 'Houston'));
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing the first and last names of all employees who are assigned to at least one project not located in Houston.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Mgr_ssn WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dependent AS T3 WHERE T3.Essn = T1.Ssn);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of department managers who have at least one dependent.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname FROM project AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Pnumber = T2.Pno GROUP BY T1.Pname HAVING SUM(T2.Hours) >= 40.0;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of projects where the sum of hours worked on them is 40 or greater.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname, Salary FROM employee WHERE Salary > (SELECT AVG(T1.Salary) FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first name, last name, and salary of employees whose salary is greater than the average salary of the 'Research' department.

sql: SELECT Fname, Lname FROM employee WHERE Ssn NOT IN (SELECT Essn FROM works_on);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber JOIN dept_locations AS T3 ON T2.Dnumber = T3.Dnumber WHERE T3.Dlocation = 'Bellaire';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all employees whose department has a location in 'Bellaire'.

sql: SELECT T1.Pname, T2.Dname, T3.Fname AS Manager_Fname, T3.Lname AS Manager_Lname FROM project AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dnum = T2.Dnumber JOIN employee AS T3 ON T2.Mgr_ssn = T3.Ssn WHERE T1.Plocation = 'Sugarland' OR T1.Plocation = 'Bellaire';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for projects located in 'Sugarland' or 'Bellaire', showing each project's name, its associated department, and the first and last name of that department's manager.

sql: SELECT T1.Dname, COUNT(T2.Dlocation) FROM department AS T1 JOIN dept_locations AS T2 ON T1.Dnumber = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T1.Dname HAVING COUNT(T2.Dlocation) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the total count of locations for each department, filtered to include only departments with at least one location.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber WHERE T2.Dname = 'Research' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn JOIN project AS T3 ON T2.Pno = T3.Pnumber WHERE T3.Pname = 'ProductY';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last names of employees in the 'Research' department, excluding those who are assigned to the 'ProductY' project.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.Lname FROM employee AS T1 JOIN works_on AS T2 ON T1.Ssn = T2.Essn GROUP BY T1.Ssn, T1.Fname, T1.Lname HAVING COUNT(T2.Pno) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of employees who are assigned to more than one project.

sql: SELECT T3.Dname, T3.AvgSalary FROM (SELECT T2.Dname, AVG(T1.Salary) AS AvgSalary FROM employee AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.Dno = T2.Dnumber GROUP BY T2.Dname) AS T3 ORDER BY T3.AvgSalary DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the highest average employee salary, displaying the department's name and that average salary.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and email for all customers who use a 'Credit Card' as their payment method.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_type_code = 'Hardware';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and price for all products classified as 'Hardware'.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, contact_phone FROM Contacts WHERE gender = 'female';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the first name, last name, and contact phone number for all contacts identified as female.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the order ID, customer ID, and order date for all customer orders that have a 'Completed' status.

sql: SELECT line_1_number_building, city FROM Addresses WHERE state_province_county = 'Nevada';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the street address and city for all addresses located in the state of Nevada.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T2.order_id, T2.order_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_name = 'Sterling';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the order ID and order date for all orders placed by the customer named 'Sterling'.

sql: SELECT T3.product_name, T3.product_price, T2.order_quantity FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T1.order_id = 9;
result_summary: Returns 1 row detailing the items within a specific customer order (order_id 9), showing each product's name, its price, and the quantity ordered.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing each customer's name along with the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T3.city, COUNT(T1.customer_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Address_History AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id GROUP BY T3.city;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each city along with the total count of customers who have an address history in that city.

sql: SELECT T2.product_name, SUM(T1.order_quantity) FROM Order_Items AS T1 JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T2.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each product name along with the total quantity ordered for that product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_phone FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Part';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names and phone numbers of customers who have an order with a 'Part' status.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Contacts AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the first and last names of contacts for all customers whose payment method is 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items WHERE order_quantity = '7');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of products that have been included in an order with a quantity of exactly 7.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T3.order_quantity * T4.product_price) AS total_spent FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows, listing each customer's name alongside the total calculated amount they have spent across all of their orders.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing customers who have placed more than one order, along with the total count of orders for each of those customers.

sql: SELECT AVG(order_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(order_id) AS order_count FROM Customer_Orders GROUP BY customer_id);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of orders placed per customer.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Contacts AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id AND T2.gender = 'male');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and email of customers who have at least one male contact on file.

sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers UNION SELECT first_name FROM Contacts;
result_summary: Returns 30 rows containing a single, unique list of names created by combining the names from the customer table with the first names from the contacts table.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'Apple';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of customers who have ordered the product 'Apple'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, (SELECT MAX(T2.order_date) FROM Customer_Orders AS T2 WHERE T2.customer_id = T1.customer_id) AS most_recent_order FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and most recent order date for each customer who has a purchase history.

sql: SELECT T2.city, T2.state_province_county, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.customer_id) FROM Customer_Address_History AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id GROUP BY T2.city, T2.state_province_county HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.customer_id) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each city and state combination, along with the total count of unique customers associated with that location.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Completed');
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the names of products that have never been included in any completed customer order.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_type_code = 'Hardware' INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_type_code = 'Clothes';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of customers who have purchased items from both the 'Hardware' and 'Clothes' product categories.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Customer_Orders) EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Contacts);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of customers who have placed an order but for whom there is no associated contact information.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.order_quantity * T1.product_price) AS total_revenue FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Customer_Orders AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed' GROUP BY T1.product_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top revenue-generating products from completed orders, showing the product name and its calculated total revenue, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT Name, Date FROM workshop WHERE Venue = 'London UK';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and date for all workshops held in London, UK.

sql: SELECT Author, College FROM submission WHERE Scores = 79.0;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the author and college for each submission that received a score of exactly 79.0.

sql: SELECT Submission_ID FROM Acceptance WHERE Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the submission IDs for all submissions that have been marked as 'Accepted'.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM workshop;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all workshops.

sql: SELECT Author, Scores FROM submission ORDER BY Scores DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing submission authors and their corresponding scores, ranked from highest to lowest score.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the authors whose submissions were accepted.

sql: SELECT College, AVG(Scores) FROM submission GROUP BY College;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each college along with the average submission score for that college.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Submission_ID) FROM workshop AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each workshop by name along with the total count of its accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT Venue FROM workshop GROUP BY Venue HAVING count(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the venues that have hosted more than one workshop.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T3.Name FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the authors of accepted submissions along with the name of the workshop where each submission was accepted.

sql: SELECT Result, COUNT(*) FROM Acceptance GROUP BY Result;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows summarizing acceptance outcomes, listing each result type (e.g., 'Accepted', 'Rejected') and the total count of records for each.

sql: SELECT T1.Author, T1.College FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Rejected';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the author and college for each submission that was ultimately rejected.

sql: SELECT Author, Scores FROM submission WHERE Scores > (SELECT AVG(Scores) FROM submission);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the authors and scores for submissions that performed better than the overall average score.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Venue FROM workshop AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Acceptance AS T2 JOIN submission AS T3 ON T2.Submission_ID = T3.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Workshop_ID = T1.Workshop_ID AND T3.College = 'Florida');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and venue of workshops that have accepted at least one submission from the 'Florida' college.

sql: SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE Author NOT IN (SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted');
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the names of authors who do not have any accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID GROUP BY T1.Author HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Result) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the author whose submissions have received more than one type of acceptance result (e.g., both 'Accepted' and 'Rejected').

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Rejected' EXCEPT SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the authors who have had submissions rejected but have never had a submission accepted.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T3.Venue = 'London UK' INTERSECT SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE College = 'Miami (OH)';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the author from 'Miami (OH)' college who also had a submission accepted into a workshop held in 'London UK'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM workshop AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID WHERE T2.Submission_ID = (SELECT Submission_ID FROM submission ORDER BY Scores DESC LIMIT 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Venue, AVG(T3.Scores) FROM workshop AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID JOIN submission AS T3 ON T2.Submission_ID = T3.Submission_ID GROUP BY T1.Venue;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each workshop venue alongside the average score of its accepted submissions.

sql: SELECT T1.College FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted' GROUP BY T1.College ORDER BY AVG(T1.Scores) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the college with the highest average submission score among all of its accepted applicants.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name FROM workshop AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Rejected' AND T1.Workshop_ID NOT IN (SELECT Workshop_ID FROM Acceptance WHERE Result = 'Accepted');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of workshops where all submitted papers were rejected.

sql: SELECT Author FROM submission WHERE College = 'Florida' UNION SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Accepted';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing a unique list of authors who are either from 'Florida' college or have had a submission accepted.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T1.Author, T1.Scores FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID JOIN workshop AS T3 ON T2.Workshop_ID = T3.Workshop_ID WHERE T1.Scores = (SELECT MAX(T5.Scores) FROM Acceptance AS T4 JOIN submission AS T5 ON T4.Submission_ID = T5.Submission_ID WHERE T4.Workshop_ID = T2.Workshop_ID);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows identifying the workshop, author, and score for the submission(s) that achieved the highest score within each respective workshop.

sql: SELECT T1.Author FROM submission AS T1 JOIN Acceptance AS T2 ON T1.Submission_ID = T2.Submission_ID GROUP BY T1.Author HAVING count(T2.Workshop_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the authors who have had submissions accepted into more than one workshop.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the name and age for all hosts whose nationality is the United States.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, Location FROM party WHERE First_year = '2000';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the theme and location for all parties that were established in the year 2000.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and age of each host, ordered from oldest to youngest.

sql: SELECT * FROM party WHERE Party_Theme = 'Club Q-BASE';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all details for the party specifically themed 'Club Q-BASE'.

sql: SELECT Host_ID, Name, Age FROM host WHERE Age > 35;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the ID, name, and age for each host who is older than 35.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Age FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID WHERE T2.Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of each host designated as the main person in charge.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T2.Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each party's theme along with the name of the host who is the main person in charge.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Party_ID) FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID GROUP BY T1.Host_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each host by name and the total number of parties they have hosted.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, T1.Location FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Austin Daye';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the theme and location for any parties hosted by the individual named 'Austin Daye'.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID GROUP BY T1.Party_ID, T1.Party_Theme HAVING count(T2.Host_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the theme of any party that has more than one host.

sql: SELECT Nationality, AVG(Age) FROM host GROUP BY Nationality;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each host nationality along with the average age of hosts from that nationality.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Nationality FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID WHERE T2.Party_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and nationality of the host for the party with ID 1.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Hemkade 48 Zaandam' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Various locations';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of hosts who have hosted a party at 'Hemkade 48 Zaandam' but not at 'Various locations'.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM host WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM host) AND Host_ID IN (SELECT Host_ID FROM party_host WHERE Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and age of hosts who are designated as the main person in charge of a party and are older than the average age of all hosts.

sql: SELECT Party_Theme, Number_of_hosts FROM party WHERE Number_of_hosts > (SELECT AVG(Number_of_hosts) FROM party);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing parties with an above-average number of hosts, showing each party's theme and its host count.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Host_ID = T2.Host_ID GROUP BY T1.Host_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Party_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the host who has hosted the most parties.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, T1.Location FROM party AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM party_host AS T2 JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T2.Party_ID = T1.Party_ID AND T3.Nationality = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the theme and location for each party hosted by at least one person from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM host AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T3 ON T1.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID JOIN party_host AS T4 ON T3.Party_ID = T4.Party_ID JOIN host AS T2 ON T2.Host_ID = T4.Host_ID WHERE T1.Host_ID < T2.Host_ID;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing pairs of hosts' names who have co-hosted at least one party together.

sql: SELECT Nationality FROM host GROUP BY Nationality ORDER BY AVG(Age) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the nationality of the host group with the highest average age.

sql: SELECT Name FROM host WHERE Host_ID NOT IN (SELECT Host_ID FROM party_host WHERE Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all hosts who are not designated as the main person in charge for any party.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID GROUP BY T1.Party_ID, T1.Party_Theme HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN T3.Nationality = 'United States' THEN 1 END) = COUNT(T3.Host_ID);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the themes of parties that are hosted exclusively by individuals from the United States.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM party_host AS T1 JOIN host AS T3 ON T1.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.Is_Main_in_Charge = 'T' AND T1.Party_ID = (SELECT Party_ID FROM party ORDER BY Number_of_hosts DESC LIMIT 1);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.First_year = '2000' INTERSECT SELECT T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID WHERE T1.First_year = '2002';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Party_Theme, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Host_ID) AS NumberOfUniqueHosts FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID GROUP BY T1.Party_Theme;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each party theme and the corresponding count of unique hosts that have organized parties with that theme.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM party AS T1 JOIN party_host AS T2 ON T1.Party_ID = T2.Party_ID JOIN host AS T3 ON T2.Host_ID = T3.Host_ID GROUP BY T3.Host_ID, T3.Name HAVING MIN(T1.First_year) = '2000' AND MAX(T1.First_year) = '2000';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the host who exclusively hosted parties in the year 2000.

sql: SELECT * FROM useracct;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing all user accounts, showing the user ID and name for each account.

sql: SELECT i_id, title FROM item WHERE title = 'apple';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the ID and title for the specific item named 'apple'.

sql: SELECT * FROM review WHERE rating = 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing all details for reviews that received a perfect rating of 10.

sql: SELECT u_id, name FROM useracct WHERE name = 'Helen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the user ID and name for the user account named 'Helen'.

sql: SELECT * FROM trust WHERE source_u_id = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing all users trusted by the user with ID 1, showing the specific user being trusted and the corresponding trust level.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.rating FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each review's rating along with the name of the user who submitted it.

sql: SELECT T1.title, T2.rating FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the title of each item alongside the rating from an individual review.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.a_id) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each user's name alongside the total number of reviews they have written.

sql: SELECT T1.title, AVG(T2.rating) FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each item's title along with its calculated average rating based on all associated reviews.

sql: SELECT name FROM useracct WHERE u_id IN (SELECT u_id FROM review WHERE rating = 10);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have given a perfect rating of 10 on a review.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.target_u_id) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.source_u_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each user by name along with the total number of other users they trust.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.source_u_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.target_u_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have established a trust relationship with more than one other user.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id JOIN item AS T3 ON T2.i_id = T3.i_id WHERE T3.title = 'orange';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have written a review for the item titled 'orange'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT u_id FROM review) INTERSECT SELECT T2.name FROM useracct AS T2 WHERE T2.u_id IN (SELECT source_u_id FROM trust);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have both written a review and trusted another user.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 WHERE T1.u_id IN (SELECT target_u_id FROM trust) AND T1.u_id NOT IN (SELECT source_u_id FROM trust);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who are trusted by at least one other user, but do not themselves trust any other users.

sql: SELECT T1.title, AVG(T2.rating) FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id WHERE T2.u_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT source_u_id FROM trust) GROUP BY T1.title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each item's title and its average rating, calculated only from reviews made by trusted source users.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.a_id) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.a_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows identifying users who have submitted more than one review, showing each user's name and their total review count.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.source_u_id GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY AVG(T2.trust) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the user who gives the highest average trust rating to others.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id GROUP BY T1.title HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.u_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of an item that has received reviews from more than one distinct user.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN trust AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.source_u_id JOIN useracct AS T3 ON T2.target_u_id = T3.u_id WHERE T2.target_u_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT u_id FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows showing the names of users who trust other users, filtered to only include cases where the trusted user has also written a review.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id WHERE T2.rating = (SELECT MAX(rating) FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of users who have submitted a review with the highest possible rating.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.a_id), AVG(T2.rating) FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id GROUP BY T1.name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each user's name, the total count of reviews they have submitted, and their average given rating.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id = T2.i_id WHERE T2.rating > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM review);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique titles of items that have received at least one review with a rating higher than the overall average rating across all reviews.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM useracct AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.u_id = T2.u_id WHERE T2.i_id IN (SELECT T3.i_id FROM review AS T3 JOIN useracct AS T4 ON T3.u_id = T4.u_id WHERE T4.name = 'Helen') AND T1.name <> 'Helen';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a user who has reviewed at least one of the same items as the user 'Helen', excluding 'Helen' herself from the result.

sql: SELECT T3.name, T5.title FROM trust AS T1 JOIN useracct AS T2 ON T1.source_u_id = T2.u_id JOIN useracct AS T3 ON T1.target_u_id = T3.u_id JOIN review AS T4 ON T3.u_id = T4.u_id JOIN item AS T5 ON T4.i_id = T5.i_id WHERE T2.name = 'Helen';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows showing the names of users trusted by 'Helen' and the titles of the items those users have reviewed.

sql: SELECT * FROM Catalogs;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing a complete list of all available product catalogs, detailing their ID, name, publisher, publication date, and latest revision date.

sql: SELECT attribute_name, attribute_data_type FROM Attribute_Definitions;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each defined attribute's name and its corresponding data type.

sql: SELECT catalog_entry_name, price_in_dollars FROM Catalog_Contents WHERE price_in_dollars = 200.78;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and price of the catalog item that costs exactly $200.78.

sql: SELECT catalog_name, date_of_publication FROM Catalogs WHERE catalog_publisher = 'Koepp-Rutherford handmade chocolate store';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the catalog name and publication date for catalogs published by 'Koepp-Rutherford handmade chocolate store'.

sql: SELECT catalog_entry_name, price_in_dollars FROM Catalog_Contents ORDER BY price_in_dollars DESC;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and price for each catalog item, ordered from most to least expensive.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, T1.catalog_publisher FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id WHERE T2.catalog_level_name = 'Category';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and publisher for catalogs that are specifically defined at the 'Category' level.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_level_name, COUNT(T2.catalog_entry_id) FROM Catalog_Structure AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number GROUP BY T1.catalog_level_name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each catalog level name (e.g., 'Category', 'Product') along with the total count of catalog entries for that level.

sql: SELECT catalog_entry_name, AVG(price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents GROUP BY catalog_entry_name;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing each catalog entry name along with its average price in dollars.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id AND T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T3 ON T2.attribute_id = T3.attribute_id WHERE T3.attribute_name = 'Black';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all catalog entries that have the 'Black' attribute.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, T2.catalog_level_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id WHERE T1.catalog_publisher = 'Murray Coffee shop';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the catalog name and its structural level for catalogs published by 'Murray Coffee shop'.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, COUNT(T2.attribute_id) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id GROUP BY T1.catalog_entry_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, listing each catalog entry name alongside the total count of its associated additional attributes.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T1.price_in_euros FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.catalog_entry_id IN (SELECT T2.catalog_entry_id FROM Catalog_Contents AS T2 WHERE T2.price_in_euros > 150);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name and price for all catalog entries that cost more than 150 euros.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T5.attribute_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T4 ON T3.catalog_entry_id = T4.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T5 ON T4.attribute_id = T5.attribute_id WHERE T1.catalog_name = 'Chocolate';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique attribute names for all items found within the 'Chocolate' catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id GROUP BY T1.catalog_entry_id, T1.catalog_entry_name HAVING COUNT(T2.attribute_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of catalog entries that have more than one associated additional attribute.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, T3.catalog_entry_name, T3.price_in_dollars FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number WHERE T3.price_in_dollars = (SELECT MAX(T5.price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Structure AS T4 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T5 ON T4.catalog_level_number = T5.catalog_level_number WHERE T4.catalog_id = T1.catalog_id);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the most expensive item from each product catalog, listing the catalog name, the item's name, and its price.

sql: SELECT T2.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Structure AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T2 ON T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T3 ON T2.catalog_entry_id = T3.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T4 ON T3.attribute_id = T4.attribute_id WHERE T1.catalog_level_name = 'Sub-Category' AND T4.attribute_name = 'Yellow';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of catalog entries that are classified as a 'Sub-Category' and have a 'Yellow' attribute.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.catalog_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number WHERE T3.price_in_dollars > (SELECT AVG(price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of catalogs that contain at least one item priced above the average price of all catalog items.

sql: SELECT T5.attribute_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T4 ON T3.catalog_entry_id = T4.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T5 ON T4.attribute_id = T5.attribute_id WHERE T1.catalog_name = 'Coffee Bean' EXCEPT SELECT T5.attribute_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T4 ON T3.catalog_entry_id = T4.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T5 ON T4.attribute_id = T5.attribute_id WHERE T1.catalog_name = 'Lemonade';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the attribute names that are unique to the 'Coffee Bean' catalog and do not exist in the 'Lemonade' catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name, COUNT(T3.catalog_entry_id) AS total_entries FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number GROUP BY T1.catalog_name HAVING COUNT(T3.catalog_entry_id) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each catalog that contains more than two entries, along with the total count of items in that catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T3 ON T2.attribute_id = T3.attribute_id WHERE T3.attribute_name IN ('Green', 'Black') GROUP BY T1.catalog_entry_id, T1.catalog_entry_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.attribute_name) = 2;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T2.catalog_entry_name, T1.parent_entry_id FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T2 ON T1.parent_entry_id = T2.parent_entry_id AND T1.catalog_level_number = T2.catalog_level_number WHERE T1.catalog_entry_id < T2.catalog_entry_id;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing unique pairs of catalog entries that share the same parent and level, showing the names of both entries and their common parent's ID.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_publisher, SUM(T3.price_in_dollars) AS total_value FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number GROUP BY T1.catalog_publisher;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each catalog publisher along with the total value of all items in their respective catalogs.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name, T1.price_in_dollars, T1.catalog_level_number FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.price_in_dollars > (SELECT AVG(T2.price_in_dollars) FROM Catalog_Contents AS T2 WHERE T2.catalog_level_number = T1.catalog_level_number);
result_summary: Returns 9 rows showing the name, price, and level number for catalog entries that are priced higher than the average price of other items within their same catalog level.

sql: SELECT T1.attribute_name FROM Attribute_Definitions AS T1 WHERE T1.attribute_data_type = 'Bool' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 WHERE T2.attribute_id = T1.attribute_id);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of boolean-type attributes that are actively being used for content in the catalog.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_name FROM Catalogs AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Structure AS T2 ON T1.catalog_id = T2.catalog_id JOIN Catalog_Contents AS T3 ON T2.catalog_level_number = T3.catalog_level_number WHERE T3.price_in_dollars / CAST(T3.length AS REAL) = (SELECT MAX(price_in_dollars / CAST(length AS REAL)) FROM Catalog_Contents WHERE CAST(length AS REAL) > 0);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of the catalog that contains the item with the highest price-per-length ratio.

sql: SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 WHERE T1.price_in_dollars > 500 UNION SELECT T1.catalog_entry_name FROM Catalog_Contents AS T1 JOIN Catalog_Contents_Additional_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.catalog_entry_id = T2.catalog_entry_id JOIN Attribute_Definitions AS T3 ON T2.attribute_id = T3.attribute_id WHERE T3.attribute_name = 'Green';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows listing the unique names of catalog entries that either cost more than $500 or are classified with the 'Green' attribute.
